Stepping back in time... There is a time machine & and this is the place with many such experiences. The bridge being just one of many....
Historical and beautiful. Didn't cross the bridge but just from outside you will have a clear picturesque of it.

Iconic bridge of the world This bridge is a thing of beauty and balance. Along with London Bridge, Rialto and golden gate this is a world class site. Visit early before the throng of Chinese and Korean tourists swamp it.

400 year old bridge...a must do! A great photo opportunity of a 400 year old bridged that you can still cross but you might need to fight the crowds if you want a photo alone. Skip it and go visit the oldest house in Hoi An. That’s fascinating and the few coins it cost to go in is keeping that family going.
This ancient beige is worth seeing . On n evening visit to the old town, it is a sight to behold.

Great to see. Interesting and bloody old. We walked through on a guided tour. Each place we went we did a guided tour first to see what is around...then it's easy to go back for closer looks later. Guided tours are highly recommended.

Worth the time connect with the old time village see its culture first hand and experience history , start early to enjoy its tranquility and venture down all the old streets to see it at its best

While visiting the old town, you wouldn’t miss this nice spot. Take a look on the level of water on 2017. Enjoy.

Best attraction in Hoi An ancient city This is number one attraction of Hoi An. It’s beautiful bridge with interesting history. It is going to be the first thing you are going to visit or if you are going to ask people what to see they going to recommend it first!
Small but spectacular I had heard a lot about this landmark that looks great during the day but amazing after dark. Make sure you see it day and night 👍

Excellent , well worth a visit. A must see. A great walking tour place to see the ancient history of Hoi An.
How they kept all this years alive here. Amazing architecture. Most popular place for photos and memories. Everyone will love it.

The symbol of Hoi An (anciently Fai Foo)... Glorious to see, glorious to understand it’s history combining vietnamese, chinese and japanese cultures and trades - without visiting this absolutely splendid spot you will not be entitled to pretend that you have been in Hoi An - the peaceful gathering of traders of different cultures in the past...
Amazing 18th century Japanese Bridge situated in the old town of Hoi An. Beautifil carved wooden bridge. A must see!!
Highly recomanded while visiting Hoi An. It is very beauriful old bridge in the old city of Hoi An. Even the small kids enjoy walking in and out
Just walk over and ponder all those over the centuries that have walked this way before you. Perhaps those that in future might follow in your footsteps.

The Japanese Covered Bridge is perhaps the most popular of the sights in historic Hoi An, and sometimes you feel the bridge is going to give way due to the sheer weight of tourists standing on it. Two to three hundred years ago Hoi An was a thriving sea port, trading with China, Japan and other Asian states. A sizeable Japanese community arose on the western side of Hoi An, and it built this bridge to connect to the rest of the township. Many of the Japanese settlers went on to marry Vietnamese, and in fact some married into the royal Nguyen dynasty, ensuring themselves of a place in history. After the bridge was built an ancestral shrine was built at the back of it, and a pair of Monkey Gods and a pair of Dog gods guard the west and east entrancec.

A good stepping stone to the history of Hoi An Worth seeing this bridge asa part of the historical culture of the town. Better to view and cross it in the morning before the crowds rollin. Absolutely packed in the evening
It is worth visiting this both at night and during the day. it is a little crowded at night but the latterns lit up is beautiful at night
Packed with history. It was crowded when we were there - I would have liked so stroll through and soak up the ambience. There are numerous tiny eateries in this vicinity.

A Piece of Ancient History Located central to the Ancient Hoi An town it is historically significant with it’s architecture and preserved Japanese heritage. Inside the bridge and the temple are markings of flood levels which the town has endured. It seems to indicate a ten year cycle of excessive flooding. The dog and monkey on either side of the bridge support the cultural significance of the Japanese and the temple is a sacred sanctuary. A very good piece of culture of ancient Vietnam.
Nice place to visit in the old town. More beautiful at night. You can take a photo and cross the bridge for free but if you want to get and visit inside you must buy a ticket.
Hoi An is a lovely town but this bridge is something. Great location of the beautiful town. One cannot miss this bridge because of it's central location.

This was great bridge to see in Hoi An There is a lot of history behind the bridge. The Architecture is beautiful.

Beautiful ancient bridge that everyone will come to see and so crowded people around you of course! Wear something light to visit here lol

This is a cute little bridge in Ancient Town Hoi An. You need to buy tickets if you want to walk across it - though the tickets cover you for 5 entries to various areas in Hoi An. Nice to see but over with very quickly.
Well worth the stop to take pictures. It has so much character. And right in the middle of the busy shopping street.

The Japanese covered bridge is probably one of the best known historical landmarks in Hoi An. It dates back to the time when Hoi An was a major trading port for the nations of Asia. Many of the countries established trading posts in Hoi An town in order to conduct their trade. Japan was no exception and it is said that the bridge was built to connect the Japanese settlement to the rest of the town. The bridge today is well maintained, very photogenic and a centrepiece of the historical parts of Hoi An ancient town. It is able to be visited at any time and tickets enabling access to the historic sites in ancient town are able to be purchased at a number of ticket offices around the ancient town precinct. Tickets are not essential to visit the bridge itself but are if you wish to visit the small temple immediately adjacent to the bridge.
Like a quantum leap back in time: well maintained and amazing history behind it. Very pretty st night but beware of the bats.

Wonderfully attractive old wooden bridge Extraordinary old bridge once linking the Japanese and Vietnamese/Chinese communities either side of a small river. It has a pretty temple in the middle of it.

Perfect spot for a photo! This beautiful bridge is at the edge of the Old Quarter and is a very popular spot for photos. It is one of two bridges in this Japanese style the other one is located in Hue. Definitely make a stop and then stroll along the water and explore this beautiful area in Hoi An. Just lovely!
Do stop here it’s a great photo opportunity just be patient especially at night. Plenty to see and eat nearby.
This is a must see while in Hoi An. A magnificent old Japanese covered bridge over a canal. Apparently used to seperate the Japanese side to the other when Hoi An was a trading port.
Not sure what the ticket collector was doing on the bridge, either in the middle or at one of the ends, but we just ignored her and did whatever we wanted!
Historic Japanese Bridge Historic bridge in beautiful Hoi An. You must check it out during your visit, make sure you buy the ticket to Hoi An so that you can cross it.
This is a historic site with wow factor. When you think how old this is... really worth the visit when in Hoi An.
beautiful bridge architecturally pleasing with an interesting history and makes a great photo opportunity

this is really such an historic treasure, beautifully preserved and worth the walk. It's a pleasure to see such amazing places. Very small but you get the real feel for the place and what must have been in the past. Glad we found it

Photo worthy and Unique The charm of Hoi An can be summed up around this Japanese Bridge. It overlooks the big lantern-covered bridge to cross the river and sits nestled on the edge of the old city center. Take a minute to walk through it and have your photograph taken. Best photos are during the day.
Everyone wants to get their photo taken here so its a bit of a scrum. But a very pretty bridge and a good landmark to find people at.
A popular tourist attraction As with anything in Hoi an this bridge is full of history. Take your time to learn the history of bridge and what it represents.
The bridge is a place I visit every time I come to Vietnam. It is stunning, so much so that I bought an oil painting of the bridge! Fantastic!

Visited here with a group and we took in the Japanese bridge with a full description from our guide. We were able to see this from all angles and the workmanship and how time had treated this piece of local history. Many young couples also use this site as a backdrop for their wedding or engagement photographs. It is easy to find, it is within the confines of the old quarter and compliments a full tour of the area well.
The Japanese covered bridge is pretty cool if you go into Ancient Town you will for sure see it. Recommend going during the daytime because there is less people!
A good Looking Bridge Worth finding because at the other end are a lovely selection of eateries, boat tours & lots of other things happening on the river...

Nice little bridge that you walk over in old town. The bridge suits the area perfectly. You can't really miss it if in old town Hoi An

Old Japanese bridge a relic gone by One of the last of the old trading port For Chinese and Japanese traders

So sadly mistreated by tourists This bridge is one of the survivors of the long history of this city. Trải nghiệm tồi tệ khi đến phố cổ Hội An, chúng tôi đến vào khoảng 7h30 tối ngày rằm, tràn ngập người trèo kéo khách du lịch thả lồng đèn, người bán hàng rong lộn xộn tràn ngập khắp mọi nơi và đứng tràn hết ra đường, trên những cây cầu; phố đi bộ bỗng nhiên thành phố bán hàng rong và chất lượng đồ ăn ko có gì để bảo đảm rằng nó hợp vệ sinh; những sạp hàng đồ lưu niệm thì tràn ngập các mặt hàng TQ với giá ko tưởng. Xe máy chạy loạn xạ và nhốn nháo, nhiều xe còn phóng nhanh và việc bấm còi inh ỏi kết hợp đánh võng diễn ra thường xuyên, chưa kể xe máy còn dựng tràn trên vỉa hè cùng với những người trèo kéo chiếm hết vỉa hè. Thật khủng khiếp khi đi trên vỉa hè ko có chỗ mà đi dưới đường thì xe máy gây mất an toàn. Khu phố mới bên kia sông thì đủ các thứ nhạc ầm ỹ. Một khu phố lộn xộn nhốn nháo như vậy nhưng ko hề có bóng dáng cơ quan chức năng giữ trật tự hay quản lý nào.
Hội An đã ko còn là phố di sản nữa!

Chủ cửa hàng thái độ khi khách đến xem nhưng không mua hàng, mình chỉ đến để coi postcard nếu đẹp thì mua, và khi không mua thì chủ cửa hàng bảo: “lần sau em không mua thì đừng có chen vào” :))). Thật buồn khi lúc đầu mình còn nghĩ trong đầu chú ấy trông thật tri thức. May cho chú ấy là mình hiền nên chỉ gật đầu xin lỗi rồi đi. Lần sau phải nhìn từ xa, nếu chắc chắn mua thì mới dám vào :)))

Chùa cầu địa điểm nổi tiếng du lịch ở Hội An.
Nhưng hiện tại chùa cầu đang trùng tu và không có gì để tham quan đoàn chúng tôi đi qua chùa cầu để đến nhà cổ phùng Hưng. Chúng tôi không hề bước lên điểm tham quan của chùa cầu chỉ đi ngang qua tại sao người soát vé lại dí chúng tôi lại và không cho đi qua phải tick vé mới được qua.
Cho tôi hỏi hiện tại chùa cầu đang trùng tu không có cái gì đặc sắc để tham quan tại sao vẫn thu vé của dân? Người soát vé giải thích là tham quan công trình? Nhưng hiện tại công trình có ai tham gia vào sửa chữa đâu mà tham quan ạ.

Về ẩm thực và đồ uống của Quảng Nam cũng vang danh gần xa trên khắp bản đồ chữ S nên không bàn tới. Lần đầu trải nghiệm không được tốt của bản thân tại đây và chắc cũng là lần cuối.
- Tìm được cái thùng rác và nhà vệ sinh ở đây chắc phải kiếp nạn thứ 83. Phải đi ra rìa khu phố cổ mới tìm thấy.
- Quản lý phố cổ không thiết kế để phân làn xe xích lô và người đi bộ tách biệt. Nghe tiếng Bíp bíp mà mình còn khó chịu nữa nói chi người ngoại quốc. Chạy tốc độ khá nhanh, lỡ thắng gấp cái là "Trời ơi,..., con nhỏ Tây này kia". Giá cả không mình bạch cho dịch vụ xích lô, có ông chú tưởng mình người ngoài đưa 2 ngón tay, 1 ông chú sau đó đưa giá 5 ngón tay.
- Bánh tráng nướng thì báo giá người nước ngoài siêu chặt chém. Báo giá 1 người Hàn Quốc 100k/cái. (Không ai quản lý hàng rong+chặt chém).
- Hey, buy 1 get 1 free. Dịch vụ mát xa ra đường chèo kéo khách với cái deal nghe có vẻ khá hời =)). Và nở khá rộ nơi đây, đi đâu cũng thấy.

mà vẫn thu tiền tham quan. Quá lãng phí

Thứ dịch vụ tệ hại nhất ở Việt Nam. Mình gửi hợp đồng ngày 12/1 từ quận Đống Đa qua quận Hai Bà Trưng, Hà Nội, đặt ship TRONG NGÀY thì 5r chiều shipper mới đến lấy và trên app tự động chuyển sang ngày 13 mới giao. Cả ngày 13 không hề giao hàng cũng không hề thông báo gì cho hai bên. Từ ngày 12 đến ngày 17 mình gọi lên khiếu nại 4 lần tổng đài viên lần nào cũng hứa liên hệ giải quyết ngay nhưng chưa từng liện hệ, gọi thêm thì bảo khách hàng tự tìm lên bưu cục Ô Chợ Dừa giải quyết. Không hiểu tại sao loại dịch vụ tệ hại thế này vẫn hoạt động được.
Các bạn du lịch hiện nay kh nên đến hội an. Tình trạng ô nhiễm hôi thối tại chùa cầu và taxi Hội An chặt chém khách du lịch. Kh có grab car cho một thành phố du lịch.

I was very disappointed with my visit to Hoi An. A female fruit vendor carrying goods on a shoulder pole forced me to take photos with her fruits, then demanded money and acted aggressively when I refused. This kind of behavior ruins the atmosphere and gives a bad impression of Hoi An. Prices in many shops were also unreasonably high.

However, I had a much better experience in Da Nang, where the people were much kinder and friendlier than in Hoi An.

As of 2024 the bridge does not exist. It has been completely disassembled, stone by stone, in order to be restored and (I suspect) reinforced. There is no water in the canal, just a bunch of rubble, scaffolding and construction materials. There is also a temporary building covering the entire site so if nobody told you, you wouldn't even know that this was where the famous bridge is supposed to be.

Parking scams, be prepared

Be extremely careful when you park a motorbike. There's NO sign saying that you can or cannot park inside the town. Apparently, there are some speakers telling you what you can do or not but we didn't hear anything. We saw 2 guys escorting our motorbike on a bicycle (yes), stopped them and got the scooter back without paying anything. We saw some other guys bringing another scooter on a small street and locking the scooter to another one. I guess they'll ask you some money if you want your motorbike back (well, if you find it, it's not where you parked).
Second, once we got the bike back, we tried to park on a scooter parking. A woman asked for 10k (it was written : Price 5000d on the sign), she didn't want us to park there once we saw the price and took some pictures of the sign. We left, found another parking. The price on the sign was covered with some tape but on one side only, other side was well hidden, saying 5k. We gave 5k to the guy who looked surprised.
My point is : Yes, 10k is it only what... 0,4 USD. But it's the double that the actual price, keep than in mind and don't feed these tourist scams.

**** Violence trigger ****
I witnessed the worst cruelty of my life today, and I'm happy I'm leaving soon,not to come back

I was driving near the mountains outside Hoi An, Vietnam — a peaceful road, nature all around — when I heard a scream. Not a human scream. A dog, screaming for its life.

Then I saw it:
A man standing in a yard, holding a dog by a leash. But not walking him — strangling him. The dog was hanging in the air, choking, twisting, crying out in unbearable pain.

I screamed too. I begged him to stop.
But he didn’t.
He just kept going.

There was a family standing nearby.
Watching.
No one moved. No one helped. Not for the dog. Not even to stop the man.

I still don’t know if it was their dog, or a stray, or one that had just been caught.
But I later learned the truth I didn’t want to believe:
Dogs — and cats — are being caught, strangled, butchered, and eaten. Here. In this country. Publicly. Legally.

There are gangs who roam the streets and alleys, snatching animals. Many are stolen from homes. Some are poisoned. Some are tortured.
Eventually, they end up in dog meat restaurants — sold as food.

I’ve traveled across the world for many years.
I’ve never seen anything as horrific as what I saw today.

This isn’t culture.
This isn’t tradition.
This is cruelty. This is violence. This is inhuman.

I know there are good people here. I know some are fighting this.
But it’s not enough. It’s not loud enough. It’s not fast enough.

Today, my heart broke.
And honestly — I don’t know if I can ever come back here again.

To anyone reading this:
Please don’t look away. Don’t be silent.
Share this. Speak out. Help stop this.

No creature — not a dog, not a cat, not anyone — should ever suffer like this.

Ever.

The Chùa Cầu Bridge in Hội An is an experience like no other—except that it doesn't actually exist! If you find yourself searching for this mythical bridge, you might be wandering the streets of Hội An for quite some time. Some say it's hidden in plain sight, while others believe it's a tale spun by locals to amuse tourists. Regardless, this elusive landmark has become a bit of an inside joke among travelers. So, if someone points you toward Chùa Cầu, take it with a grain of salt and enjoy exploring the rest of Hội An instead!

과거 무역으로 번성하던 호이안에 장사를 위해 정착한 일본인들이 호이안 시내로의 원활한 이동을 위해 지은 다리라는 일본교(내원교).

오랜 역사를 간직한 곳 답게 구경 겸 반대편으로 건너기 위해 찾는 이용객들로 북적이는 다리이다.
호이안의 상징물인 다리지만 매우 작고 건너는 데는 돈이 들지 않다 보니 미리 정보를 전해 듣지 않으면 유적지라는 것도 모르고 건널 정도로 그리 화려하지는 않다.

다리 내부에 작은 사당이 있는데 이 사당의 입장은 유료.
사당 내부는 아주 작은 방 정도의 공간으로 딱히 볼만한 것은 없고 심지어 입구에서 안을 들여다보는 정도로도 내부가 다 보일 정도다.

다리 양쪽 입구에 원숭이와 개의 조각상들이 설치되어 있는데 이에 관한 설화가 다양함.
다리의 건설 착수 및 완공의 시기가 연유라는 설과 일본인의 왕들이 태어난 시기가 개, 원숭이의 해가 많아 서라는 설 등 다양한 이야기가 전해진다.

호이안의 몇 없는 유적지이자 상징물이니 나름의 의미는 있는 장소.
It was underwhelming. I know it’s popular due to the history behind it. However, I don’t think it’s worth the visit especially in the hot weather.

I stayed in Hoi An for more than 2 weeks and only came to this bridge the first night. The other days I came here early in the morning and late night and it's so much better. Don't recommend if you are a quiet person.

일식당은 선도 높은음식이 우선되어야 하지만
매장분위기에 비해 음식이 저의기호와 맞지않았습니다
ไปเมื่อวานวันธรรมดา แต่คนเวียดนามมาเที่ยวเยอะมากก ทัวร์ก้อเยอะ ก้อรู้อะนะว่ามีคุณค่าทางประวัติศาสตร์ แต่ดันมีหนองน้ำโคลนแห้งๆอยู่ ไม่อินอะ

2023年5月、修復工事のため、橋を渡ることは出来るが、外側から綺麗な写真撮影は出来ない。
チケットを1枚払い橋を渡り、お土産屋を見た後橋を渡って戻ったが、帰りはチケット不要だった。
写真は旧市街中心地から橋を渡った側の門を写した

Völlig überfüllt und wenig spektakulär....
kostet tagsüber sogar Einrtitt! Man kann ein Ticket, das Zugang zu mehreren Sehenswürdigkeiten ermöglicht, kaufen. Abends konnte man kostenlos drüber gehen. Da hat niemand mehr kontrolliert. Meier Meinung nach überbewertet.

Actualmente esta en obras, no se puede visitar 😢
Hay un "andamio" por el que se ve algun rincon del puente pero nada que se pueda apreciar bien.
La excusa perfecta para repetir el viaje a Vietnam ☺️

Packed! There is nothing peaceful or calm on this bridge. Take a selfie outside the bridge if you can manage to be the only person in the frame. We couldn't because everyone was jostling for the right spot

Can hardly see the bridge for all the tourists taking pictures. And it's quite short so maybe it's best to see it as part of a tour to find out what all the fuss is about and then have plenty of other things to look at after. Wouldn't make a special visit just to see this.

Посетили только потому что это символ города. На фото выглядит лучше, чем вживую

Visited only because it is a symbol of the city. On the photo it looks better than live

Well kinda cute I guess. But this really is nothing special. If you've seen a decent picture of this then there's not much more to get out of it. It doesn't cost anything to cross like some people say. I've crossed it several times and I've never had a ticket or had anyone ask me to show one. Take a picture if you are nearby but don't make an effort to see it.
ホイアンに４日間滞在しましたが観光チケットを購入してないので橋の内部は見ていませんので外から見学しました！夜はライトアップされ昼と違う趣がありますが、懐かしい!?ドブ川の臭いが漂っていました😓 …
The bridge is nice, but tiny and with a specific ticket to buy to see it. If you are going to visit many spots around Hoi An it might be worth paying, otherwise just admire the bridge from outside.
秀全工事中で外からは見ることができません。見るためには拝観料12kVNDが必要です。
Bridge has been closed for over a year for reconstruction and it is still in bad shape. Not worth the trip. See photos.
갑자기 소나기가 내려서 사람들이 내원교에서 비가 그치기를 기다렸습니다. 짧은 나무다리이고 특별하지는 않습니다. 다만 올드시티에서 야시장 갈때 거쳐가는 곳이라 생각하면 됩니다. 호이안에서 일본교라는 다리를 보니.. 이곳이 내세울께 없나보다라는 생각과 일본의 이중적인 만행이 동시에 떠올랐습니다.

Just what you see in the pictures. A bridge that connects both sides of the city. Not a big deal and it’s definitely not worth it to pay a ticket to go in.
We could get in free in the morning coming from the outside.

Probably history worthy, but really underwhelming as one of the “must see” of Hoi An. The entire city has been rather “Meh”, truth to be told : a tourist trap / luna park, instead of the charming place described in the guides…
Lieu trop visité. Système de billeterie non claire. (Possibilité de visiter uniquement trois lieux de la ville pour 80000 dong). Gratuit avant 7h et après 21h.

Nichts besonderes. Kostet leider alles Geld in Hoi An.....so auch dort Eintritt.
Die Brücke an sich ist aber ganz schön, weil sie so alt ist.

チケットの制度が変更になりました
5か所の内、
1か所目日本橋と関帝廊、2か所目はサフィン博物館等は強制見学、
残りは3か所のみしか選択できませんので気をつけてください
look…i couldn’t find the place, turns out it’s just in a shed, could be undergoing some renovation? or maybe they’re taking it down?
It was closed and dismantled, maybe they are restoring it I’m not sure

It's featured on the currency. There isnt much inside. Also the ticket checker will demend your tickets as you walk across the bridge. Ignore them if you don't need to see inside of the bridge. There isn't much.
工事中なので雰囲気のみ味わう感じです。
Un pont japonais que je trouve assez banal... apparemment réhabilité récemment, il a perdu de sa superbe. Ne vaut vraiment pas le détour malgré les foules qu'il attire..n
Nothing all that special apart from the history behind it. For a Japanese designed piece of workmanship, this is actually pretty poor. Plus there's a ticket check to cross.

수리 중이라 제대로 볼 수 없습니다.
교량 역할은 하고 있으므로 통과할 수는 있 습니다만...
Se encuentra tapado con una estructura de protección que impide verlo. No debe estar en buen estado pues se ve alguna parte apuntalada.
It was crowded
บ้านเรือนสมัยเก่า มีสินค้าขายคล้ายบ้านเรา แต่ต้องระวังพ่อค้า-แม่ค้าจร ชอบตื้อชายสินค้า หากจะซื้อสินค้าเขา อย่าไปซื้อหน้าร้าน มิฉะนั้นจะโดนไล่
Very crowded. Too many tourists especially the Chinese. Great place for photo shoots.
Just a standard bridge, foreigners have to pay to cross it, nothing spectacular and wouldn't consider it a sightseeing attraction
Lots of tourists... you have to pay to cross it.
Beaucoup de bruit pour finalement pas grand chose. Pont petit et sans grand intérêt selon moi.

의미는 있겠으나 나에게 뭔 의미가 있겠나...
사람 많고 지치고 지나가면서 보세요 ㅎㅂㅎ

2 stars only for the bridge because you have to pay to enter the bridge, but there is nothing much to see inside the bridge. it is good to click pictures from the outside alone. and you wont miss anything by not entering inside. But i am not sure if you can see the shops on the other side which you need to enter by going across the bridge. but again, the shops are just the same and there is nothing different from them and the others. the bridge is very cute from outside and the major highlight is photographing it from outside
정말 사람이 너무 많다. 사람에 떠밀려서 제대로 구경도 못할정도. 역사적인 의미를 부여할수는 있겠으나 관광명소라고 하기엔 부족해보인다. 사진은 다리옆에 다리가 보이는 곳에서 촬영하면 그나마 건질수 있다

本当はチケット買う必要はないです。あれは
参拝？する有料の場所に入る時のものなのでショッピングには必要ないです。ただ橋のたもとで買え買えと迫ってきますが、日本人は呼び止めると止まるのでターゲットになってるとガイドさんから聞きました。西洋人はスルーです！素敵なダナン、せっかくの世界遺産、ちょっとがったりしました。
10メートルの橋で入場チケット無いと入れない。

Attention ce pont est en cours de démontage pour restauration depuis au moins deux ans. Chantier abrité sous un hangar. Les guides meme récents ne sont pas actualisés (Petit futé e français par exemple).

The Japanese covered bridge was built originally by the Japanese and then fixed by the Vietnamese. To go there, you will have to pay an entrance fee which is very common in Hoi An and it can be crowded inside. There is nothing special to see inside. It links one part of the city to another part which is much less crowded but where shop prices are much higher. So I don't believe it is worth to spend money on the entrance fee and it is better to stand in front of it from the market to take pictures from the outside.
2024年1月1日 修復工場中だわん。

This is not a thing worth making a trip for. It's alright to walk by, but nothing of any particular interest. They sell tickets, but if you go in the late afternoon or night they stop selling them and you can cross or just see it for free. It's in the middle of old town so you will see it. Very crowded

The bridge is part of the overrated Disneyland like old town. You pay around five Dollar to get in (some museums are included) and can see tourist shops, one next to the other. The old town is almost always over crowded and you can hardly take a step with out some body offering you something. At least you can find some nice restaurants in the old town. All together: It's not authentic at all, but worth a look.
很普通的一座橋，比起其他會安的景點，只能算普通，不明白大家為何都要在橋前拍照。上橋需要門票，不過我看到很多遊客趁剪票員在幫別人剪票的時候從旁邊大方的就走過去了也沒給票。

그냥 구경안하고 지나가기만 했는데 티켓을 잘라갔어요... 휙하고 가져가 가위로 툭툭...ㅜ
외국인들은 아예 검사도 안했구요
저희도 사긴 했지만 매표소 지나갈 때마다 계속 티켓 사라고 하셔서 샀다고 계속 보여드렸어요
저희는 거리만 구경해서 딱히 티켓이 필요없었지만 그냥 사서 가는 게 맘도 편하지않을까요ㅜ

사람 드럽게 많음.
비싸고 살건 없음.

It’s a cute bridge but from my point of view you may just enjoy it from the outside. The temple itself isn’t particularly special, unless you have an eye for the details and truly care about the history.

Very short bridge despite of its historical value. The ticket office is located next to the bridge. The ticket includes unlimited crossing of the bridge and entry of 5 museums or historical house. The museum on the bridge is simply a historical photo gallery less than 200 sq ft, it is not worth to spend a ticket for this. Other museum spots are worth to see and signifying the historical Chinese societies formed to help new comers and families from the same orient.
It was under maintenance when I went there. Couldn’t see the bridge. Hoi an town itself is okay. Take a boat ride before sunset. Much better light for insta stories!

Ziet er mooi uit maar daar blijft het ook bij. Leuk om een keer overheen te lopen en een foto te nemen. Jammer genoeg is het echt belachelijk toeristisch en als je het mij vraagt enorm overschat. Missy is het iets beter als je dit héél vroeg in de ochtend bezoekt.

As many others have stated, currently under construction so there’s very little to see! Wouldn’t go out of your way to visit this at the moment but there are some nice shops just adjacent to the exit of the renovations.

2019년 2월 28일
베트남 내의 중국과 일본의 정착지.

This natural once unspoilt marvel is being destroyed by loud, inconsiderate tourists from around the world, thrill seekers, Instagram photo op seekers, party people who leave their trash everywhere with no consideration for the environment and people who just come to enjoy the once beautiful nature here. Sad place to visit.

One could swallow the impressive purity, emptiness and magic of the place with respect. It has to be overrun and spoiled, destroyed by noise, poisoned by the smell of gasoline and deeply wounded by morons on quads and bikes. Tourism in Mui Ne flourishes but will wipe out the place in the next couple of years. Even if you ask your tour provider NOT to get you to the offroad park, you'll be spit out there with the morons. One of the vietnamese main tourism rules hits in hard here: „Your perfect holiday is not what YOU believe it is!"

Crowded place with high prices and not much to see Nothing special to see here. So called dunes are basically one sandy hill, where all the tourists are taken by quads. Quad trip is pretty steep and we could not negotiate (asking price was of the alequivalent of the rest of the jeep trip from Mui Ne - 300k dongs per person). Place is very crowded.

Don’t bother, a total rip off. Over promised under delivered. We were taken there as part of our tour. It was the one reason we came to Mui Me. We expected to drive the ATVs ourselves but oh no you have to have their driver. The cost was just over £18. Now for that amount of money I would have expected at least 30 minutes actual riding plus time to take photos at the two stops. Well what we got was a 3 minutes at the most to the top of the dunes. Then photo time. Then a 3 minute again at the most to the lake then photo time and then 2 minutes back to base. The driver just don’t give a dam, I was actually going to use a word beginning with S! All they are interested in is playing and watching their phones at each stop. I had to ask him to take a photo of us. When we returned to base he literally stoped the ATV and walked off. I actually don’t think the engine had completely stopped!!! For £18 for an 8 minute ride is disgusting my husband said it was actually a lot less than that but I’m being generous. I expected to go dune bashing as well as the two stops. I enjoyed the actual experience but not the attitude of the driver or the value for money that this activity definitely doesn’t provide. After all you can get a hotel room, with breakfast and dinner for the same price as that ride. Now that puts it in to context! So driver of ATV number 15 on the 5th Oct 2024, you did yourself a disservice as well as letting your country men down, shame on you.

The jeep was overpriced and ticket places seemed to try and get the most money possible - some friends were chanrged more than me, over the double, depended on person country etc. The jeep itself was OK, no seat belt and blasted music. Seemed to swerve a bit into the middle of the road but generally 'safe'. The driver took me to the white dunes first and there I was confronted with hundreds of people waiting for quads for the 10 minute ride up the dunes. I was asked for more money, friends of mine were assked for the double. There were ticket guys everywhere and everyone as annoyed with me for saying no, the driver went off to play cards somewhere for half an hour before coming back and looking annoyed. Then we headed back and stopping at the red dunes which were wonderful, quite dramatic and where you could walk up. On any future trip I would either hire a motor bike to the red dunes, or take a taxi. The white dunes are a mafia tourist trap and should be avoided at all cost. Go to the red dunes instead!
Noisy and uninteresting excursion It was noisy and sad with all the cars driving around. Also scary. Not at all an experience I thought was worth going for

Over commercialised and over priced Sadly another nice natural sight has become over commercialised by locals preying on tourists. It’ll cost 800k vnd to get there from Min Ne, then another 800k to cover the 15 min walk to the dunes…
Dirty and uninteresting place. There is nothing to do in Mui Ne so we did this attraction.

Unclear pricing and agressive welcome Unclear pricing and agressive welcome, avoid. Changed the price agreed when we arrived there, and then as we didn’t want to pay more they threatened us (a family and 3 kids) to let us there and come back hôte by a walk (more than 15 kilometers). 10 years ago you could go there without the current people managing the area. Another beautiful landscape privatize by greedy anti-tourism companies.

The sand dunes and the sceneries are amazing. But the rental car drivers and operators are reckless idiots. They allow young children to get on the jeeps and no safety instructions. Drivers drive very fast where people are standing. Accidents are waiting to happen. Someone could get run over by any driver.

The Jeep rent for the dunes is ridiculously expensive, they promised one hour ride while they actually just did a 10 minutes ride including the picture time. I had my toddler with me and nobody told us it was extremely windy on the dunes that day so we barely managed to get out of the jeep and it was impossible to open our eyes and enjoy the place. The sand flying all over at wind speed was really painful. When I told the driver the ride was too short and not what we agreed on, he got angry and started driving like crazy on the dunes which was very dangerous for my kid so we had to give up and ask them to go back. 35$ for a 10 minutes ride in a jeep without even a seatbelt with an extremely rude driver.
Masstourism destroy nature Quad is to expensive, guys are unfriendly and throw their cigarettes in the dunes. Sunrise is okay. But too crowded
There were people on amplified cd players creating noise pollution on this beach. The place should be serene, not this noisy cigarette butt covered place.

Waste of time and money. Just skip this part and better to stay at the beach. The ATV safari they offer is a complete scam. They charge saying it will take 40 mins and it lasts like 5 minutes. Overcharged, a trap to fool the curious tourists.
Sand. Fantastic. So boring this pile of sand. Its just a load of sand. I was bored almost to death. If you've ever been to a beach this will bore you to tears.

We went as part of a tour, and having gone to the red sand dunes just before we were looking forward to the white sand dunes. However, there is plastic litter absolutely everywhere. Really disgusting and sad. Would definitely not recommend visiting these

In one sentence: don’t go. When you get there with a sun rise tour 4 am they stop at the bottom and then you have to pay for your own ATV for 800.000 dong to “drive yourself” which means a guy from there sits next to you. The dunes itself are hardly white and many places and i really mean many places have better sand dunes without the scammers and boatload of tourists.

Ça ne vaut pas la peine du tout, quand vous bookez le jeep tour ils ne vous disent pas que vous devez payer 400k dong par personne rendu à l’activité pour simplement vous faire amener en haut des dunes à 2 minutes de jeep. De plus, nous avons vu des conducteurs de ATV s’amuser à frapper les chiens avec leurs ATV sans aucune pitié pour le plaisir. Ces derniers étaient avec la compagnie Du Lich Trieu Trang. Pour couronner le tout, personne ne parle anglais. its not worth it at all. When you first book your tour, they don’t tell you that you have to pay an extra 400k dong just to get to the top of the sand dunes. There was also some stupid ATV drivers from the companie Du Licu Trieu Trang who were trying to run over the dogs as fast as they could just for fun. This is one of the most disrespectful and disgusting thing i have seen. Also they don’t speak english one bit.

Basically a builders merchants back yard. Oh look a sand dune whoopee do. Take a bent piece of plastic if you want and slide down. Gee whiz hold me back. Still shovelling sand out of orifices I didn’t know existed. Want sand? Go to the beach at least there is usually a bar there. And it’s flatter......

You rent a jeep to get to the dunes and when you arrive they tell you that their jeep cant go up ....and u have Three choices rent a quad 800 000 dongs ,pay for a lift 200 000 dongs or walk and die in heat

Mui Ne Sand dunes RIP OFF We paid 800,000 for a 20 minute quad at the white sand dunes. For the 800,000 $50AUD we where told we could drive the quads which would have been great if we didn’t have a local clinging on to the side of the quad stopping us from going to fast ( saving fuel ) and also turning the handle bars in the direction he wanted to go. Very over priced, not worth the 1hr 30 round trip.

Very bad-atv!!!! Worst atv I think they're cheaters. So expensive=ATV. They asked me 40K per person. The worst trip for them. When we went back to the destination we had landed, they did not drive us atv. 평보니까 한사람당 만원은 싼거임 저는 한사람당 2만원 요구하고 갈때 atv하나가지고 저랑남친 태워줘서 심지어 우리가 운전한것도 아니었습니다. 지프투어 가이드 생각해보니 그사람들이랑 한통속이고 오르막길까지 데려다주는것도 아닌 앞에 세워주고 오르막길은 알아서올라가라함 생각해보니 더열받고 . 돌아갈때는 atv는 커녕 차 하나오더니 단체 태워가지구 돌아갔음 . 그거보니까 차가 아예 못들어가는것도 아닌거 같더만 . Atv 5분이요? 2분도안탄듯 그냥 선라이즈 선셋 지프투어 됐고 지프투어 말고 다른 방법으로 가세요 지프투어 하더라도 atv타야된다고 강요하는데 됐다하고 걸어가세요 뭣도 모르고 팁까지드림 ㅎ..

We've been here on the sunrise tour. Totally disappointed with the experience. We have arrived at the dunes at 5am. If you want to see the sunrise from the proper dunes you have to go walking (which we did) or rent a ATV for 600k per person even 2 people go in one ATV. Apart from this ripping off, and the dunes not being breathtaking, you are in a piece of sand with other 100 people. They play loud music without any respect for the other people. Moreover, the people who rent the ATV are the same ones who drive them and they are worst than kids. They drive extremely fast and the noise they make is unbearable, not nice to join the sunrise at all. You can do "sandboard" on a piece of plastic which is supposed to be a proper sand board for 50k!

We just had a ride with one of the motorbikes, it was horrible, they told us we could drive 30 min with a personal driver but actually it was only 1 way up wait 30 min on the dunes and one way down and that was it. We paid 12 euro for this, they set us off...

On our way to the sands 3 policemans stoped us and told us our international driving license is not valid for Vietnam and we are already traveling for 5 months around SE Asia, they ask for 1.000.000 dongs if we ant to continue,be careful if you are going the with a scooter, if not you are gonna have to pay to the corrupted policemans such a shame.....this town is disgusting.

We booked a sunset tour via backpacker village and asked the driver one day before if it gonna rain he said no but better look up yourself. Take a hoodie with you it'll be windy in the jeep. The landscape was beautiful but during the river walk it began to rain and our shoes we kept with us were completely soaked. Better wear some open shoes or ask the guide to leave your trainers in the car. The driver won't let you know. You are able to buy rain ponchos during the walk. The price for the tour was okay we payed 240vnd for two people. But maybe you're better off if you book via a local tour guide with more service included. You pay extra to be taken on the dunes. And you have to pay 650vnd to drive a quat your own. All in all it was a little fresh and it's disappointing when it rains. 1 point for the view

Total Scam: don’t waste your time Unless you have never seen dunes, don’t waste your time here. The area is quite small and there are just a few dunes. It is 40 km away from town and it’s not worth the time. Once you get there, the second part of the scam begins. They will ask you to pay at least $15 per person to take you on the buggy to the dunes and let you walk around. The actual drive takes less than 5 minutes in total. Don’t fall for this tourist trap

Police will stop you and make you pay 1 million VND for "speeding" in the city. They threaten to take your licence and bike if you don't pay on the spot. We demanded a receipt but then they said they keep bike for 7 days if you want a receipt. Complete scam.
Don't go there as said by the other reviews you will only get into a trap and have to pay to get through....

Took a sunset jeep tour to go to the sand dunes. Once you're there (Pam Thieu?) they will offer you an atv for 400/2 pax. They will only drop you and pick you up. The ride did not even last 5 minutes and not fun at all. If you really want to rent an atv, go for Nhu Trung one, the drivers wear a yellow uniform. I find that their rides are longer and more fun based on what I saw.

took us from the hotel then drove 4 min.to gathering point waiting there 50 min,drove 30 min, to sand hills rent tiny atv 25 $ fallow the guide 400 m to the hilltop ,at the way back stop half way for almost an hour when all drivers play and gamble and took us back only after we ask few times,so at the end of the day we wait to the jeep most of the trip. Vietnam beautiful not that trip,that a tourist trap.

Corrupt police booked us too on 29th April 2018 We were pulled over for 49kph in a 40 zone. no speed gun, no police id or number or even his name. Made me sign a form in Vietnamese ("I signed it for receipt only"), & wouldn't give us a copy. Threatened to keep our license when we asked for his name, and wrote on his phone & converted to english we would be arrested if we took his photo, or even a photo of the station which is opposite Eagles beach resort Hon Rom, Mui Ne, Phan Thiet 800000, Vietnam, which is where we were pulled over. Changed my attitude to Vietnamese & other road users instantly, & spoiled our holiday. Fined $1m dong each & dropped to $500,000 for 2 because that's all we had. Keep most of your money in a separate wallet hidden in your bag.

Waste of time and money Two of the attractions on the Jeep tour are slightly interesting: the walk through the river to see sandstone formation and lots of trash; the fishing village which you visit by standing on an overpass road way above a bunch of fishing boats and take a few pictures. That’s it. And both are easily reachable by cab for about 30,000 dong. The third attraction, red dunes, is trash strewn and nothing more than a sand dune. The fourth attraction is the white dunes, well outside town. If you’ve never seen a sand dune this could be fun for a few minutes. However, the dune buggy ride to reach them costs an additional 250000 per person for fifteen minutes or so. The guide provided no information, the cost for two of us in a Jeep with a driver was 1,200,000 dong. A waste of an afternoon and a waste of money. Avoid.

Felt cheated by the people who work at the Jeep rental. They collected 200k Dong per person, agreed to bring us to the top of white send dune and will rent us a sliding board at 20k Dong to slide down the dune. Once we reach the dune top, the staff immediately want us to head back when we haven take a single photo. Attitude were super bad. No sliding board available for renting. Totally over charged for a short 5 min trip up the dune.

We booked a 4-hour afternoon Jeep Tour for 200k VND per person by MR TIEN DAT TRAVEL, (next to the Fairy Stream). Expecting to at least ride some "off-road" on red and/or white sand dunes - we were instead driven by the 4x4 jeep on tarmac roads to the different sights. When arriving at the White Dunes, we stopped at a restaurant and were told to either walk the dunes or rent another 3-wheeler for some extra hundred thousand VNDs, which we refused. What's the point of riding a 4x4 Jeep if not driving off-road? Yes the Army Jeeps look cool but if you are out for some adventure, make sure the off-road riding is included prior to booking, or take a cheaper and much more comfortable Taxi Ride to the different sights.

Waste of money. The bikes don’t work and no one wants to help you. DO NOT RENT QUAD BIKES! THEY DO NOT WORK! We went to the white dunes yesterday excited to see the dunes. Upon arriving, we were quickly greeted with a man trying to rent us quad bikes. We were hesitant as the prices were ridiculous. My brother paid $600,000VD (“discounted” from $700,000), while the rest of us split one ATV between two - paying $350,000 each. Our driver was helping the man sell us the bikes and was no help whatsoever. Anyway, we were excited and though eff it - let’s try. Within minutes, all three quad bikes were broken. My brothers chain fell off and he couldn’t drive it whatsoever; and the other two quad bikes literally couldn’t turn in the sand and would break down constantly. The quad bikes are terrible quality and the maintenance of them (if there’s any whatsoever) is questionable. We were pretty annoyed and went back.. we were in the bikes for no more than 10 minutes and had rented them for half an hour. We talked to the guy who sold them to us (he spoke perfect English), and requested not all of our money back, but a part sum. He refused and said it wasn’t possible as the money had gone to the company (???? We paid cash?!). He got angry, started yelling and just kept refusing us any help. We asked to speak to his boss which sent him quiet as we continued to ask how our money could just be “gone”. Keep in mind he was standing there with a wad of cash in his hand. We didn’t get to speak to the ‘boss’ - I doubt he existed. He agreed to give us 50,000VD back and we followed him to get it. He walked over to a table where everyone was gambling, including our driver. They had zero interest in helping us and it was quiet clear as to where our money went. We had to ask our driver several times to leave as he would not leave the gambling table. Absolutely ridiculous. Don’t do it. Waste of money and if there was a lower star rating, I’d do it.

We did the sunrise tour to see the sand dunes last week and definitely would NOT recommend it. They squeezed 9 of us into a jeep with space for 5. The driver turned up with no lights on then drove like a lunatic. When you get to the dunes you have to pay an additional 200k per person to go on the back on a quad bike to get to the top of the dunes where you can see the sun rise. They told us it’s a 5km ride but it was more like 1, we were only on the quad for a few minutes. If you don’t want to pay the extra you have to stay at the start of the dunes where there is no good view. It was nice to watch the sunrise but was spoilt by so many other tourists. We thought that it would be a small tour but every jeep tour goes to the same place so there are hundreds of people. If you have a motorbike it’a worth coming to see the dunes by yourself but definitely don’t bother with the tour it was just a rip off (we weren’t very impressed with the other stops in the tour either).
I dont know what is the art of seeing large pile of white sand without any mountain or sea to enjoy. They provide atv ride there but i still dont know whats the good

Never felt so scammy before until I came here. The people will rushed you to take their atv for sunrise. Was charged 300k vnd for a super short ride. The driver did not even send me to the top. Asked me to walk up. When I wanted to go back down, everyone was unwilling to send me back, seems like waiting for me to pay for another round or something. Super bad experience and the view was not very amazing anyway. Skip this for a longer time in fairy stream would be better

Beautiful but completely polluted by jeeps and quads The dunes are really beautiful and the sunset was spectacular but the place has lost all its charm because of racing quads and jeeps in the dunes. You constantly hear the very loud noise of these vehicles and we more than once had to dodge quads coming straight at us. They are racing over the dunes without following a specific trail, sometimes not even seeing if there are people behind the dune! It was very dangerous - such a shame!
You can walk or pay for 200k for an ATV when you reach there. Our driver of the tour showed us bad attitude/ service because we choose to walk.

Paid over double from ASIA TRAVEL to go to two sand dunes and a fishing village and to walk up a river. People were paying 150k VND and we ended up paying 350k VND. To top it off. We had to pay 300k VND for a 30 second quad ride, that we were told would take ages and we could ride. Absolute scam. It's not worth the money. You can't see the sun rise, and it's just full of people who won't speak to you. Will rather shout at you, and basically ruin the whole experience that could and should be so much better. Avoid at all costs. Pointless!

We took the chance of riding rented motorbikes to the area as we wanted to save some cash. Big mistake, the police knowingly wait at a stretch of road where they know tourists are heading to and will stop you. Even if you do have a license, they will say you have exceeded the speed limit (no speed limit signs anywhere) or have breached some safety protocol on the roads. Had to pay (more like bribe) 500,000 dong for them to let us go with our motorbikes. We went ahead with our journey thinking we should at least see the sand dunes after paying half a million for an entrance fee. Upon arrival, we were quoted a ridiculous price of 500,000 dong for each quad bike for 30 minutes. In the end, this place is a tourist trap which overcharges you on every possible thing, would not come back.

Complete rip off! We drove 25 km to visit the white sand dunes. For a 20 minute ride they charge you 600.000 dong and 15.000 entrance fee and 5.000 parking for a bunch of dunes. Not worth your time, wouldn't even pay this in Europe.

This trip onto the dunes was part of our tour package. The vehicles we travelled in were old and tired with no safety features such as roll cages or even seat belts. It consisted of a short drive to (almost) the highest point for photo opportunities then another short drive to lake side and more photos, then back to the base. Drivers were abrupt and unfriendly. They appeared bored and added nothing to the experience. The landscape was spoiled by the amount of garbage - mostly plastic bottles - that littered the area. Not necessarily the tourists fault as we witnessed our driver finish a bottle of water and simply discard it on the dunes. The area has huge potential if it was cleaned up and staff were more enthusiastic. We completed a similar trip in Dubai and that was 1000% better than this one. Very disappointing that tour operators don't take more pride in the environment and the comfort and safety of their customers. Not recommended.

Underwhelmed and covered in rubbish Possibly because we've seen sand dunes in other places in the world - Huaccachina in Peru and Swakopmund in Namibia - which were both beautiful and relatively untouched by masses of tourists, we were hugely disappointed on arrival at the white dunes in Mui Ne. There is rubbish everywhere!! It would be impossible to take a photo and for it not to have a plastic bottle or some other rubbish in the picture. This is not the fault of the locals but the fault of the huge number of tourists who seemingly don't care about the environments in which they are visiting. Whilst we were there, the majority of tourists had paid to drive quad bikes up and down the dunes but we chose not to do so, so I'm not too sure on the cost of doing this. Definitely not a magical place but I guess if you've not yeh had the chance to see dunes, it may be worth the visit, just be ready for the rubbish.
400k for twenty minutes including a 10 minute lesson is just far to pricey for what you get. Me and my friends ended up just having a walk round the place.

Guide was late 45 minutes and I missed the sunrise in white sand dunes. Quadbikes are extremely expensive (solo:500.000VND, passenger:300.000VND). Please find a guide, not just a driver! Nothing special, noisy, dirty. Disappointing.

Major accident on quad bike in the dunes Unfortunately I have to write this review in the hopes of warning people to take GREAT CARE when considering hiring a quad bike from the rental office at the white sand dunes in Mui Ne. On Thursday 27 July 2017 I arrived at the dunes with a friend and was instantly pressurised by all of the bike rental staff, including our jeep guide, to rent a bike for the visit. Between ourselves we decided to rent one for the cost of 500,000 dong. I then asked them what safety instructions they would give and how to safely ride the bike, any route to follow, protection available etc... their practically non-existent English skills meant they literally replied with "clutch" and pointed to a button on the left and "gear" to the right handle bar, similar to the basic mechanics as any bike obviously, but no response to any of the comments on safety.. My friend and I mounted the bike with me the agreed driver for the first part and drove into the dunes. It's pretty crazy when you get out into them what with bikes all over the place, no specific signs for tracks to follow, so I thought this meant you could literally bike anywhere. I started to drive up a small slope thinking it was a hill the other side but it wasn't and the bike flew off into the air before it landed and flipped 90 degrees onto it's side, propelling us both onto the ground at great force. My friend managed to jump off in a way that meant she suffered a scratch on her leg, but the injuries were a lot worse for myself. As I got up I looked down to discover my arm was literally hanging off from the elbow downwards and I had lost all feeling and sensation in my hand. My friend managed to call someone over and they helped me get on the back of another bike to get back to the office. Once there, no one seemed interested in helping me despite my screaming, which maybe had something to do with the fact that I wasn't bleeding, so it took a while to locate my original jeep guide. Despite my distress this man was VERY reluctant to start the jeep and drive me to a hospital as I was begging him to do and was more concerned about trying to call me a taxi. I was slipping into unconsciousness at this point and thankfully my friend reappeared to help me beg him to drive. After about 10 minutes of waiting he started driving although he continued to try and flag down taxis en route and continued making calls to book me one; his lack of concern and empathy was astonishing. Safe to say he did find me a taxi and I was hastily pulled out of his jeep and pushed into it. In brief the rest of the story goes looking like this: after a 30 minute drive I arrived at the tiny Phan Thiet hospital where they took an X-ray and discovered I'd severely shattered my elbow into countless fractures. Not able to perform such a complicated operation at this hospital, I was blue lighted for 3 hours to the FV hospital in Ho Chi Minh city. Sometime after arriving, I underwent 4 hour surgery which involved fixing my bones back together using 2 titanium plates and 18 screws... I am writing this review from my hospital bed where I have been recovering ever since and have now had to cancel my whole backpacking trip to return home and recover for the next months. I will need extensive physio to regain full use of my hand and a lot of time to psychologically recover from this intensely traumatic experience, as will my family of course whom I called from the initial jeep journey telling them I was dying... I sincerely hope that anyone reading this will SERIOUSLY CONSIDER RENTING QUAD BIKES from this office - as far as I'm aware there is only one office which is the one the tourguides bring all the tourists too. I really do believe this could have been avoided had the bike rental office provided me with better safety instructions and there had been more signage on the actual dunes as to where is safe to ride and where is not. I now know quad biking is a highly dangerous activity and causes many accidents; had I known this before my accident, I would have been a LOT more cautious about hiring one. I understand that this is Asia and that the health and safety regulations are very different here to the UK, but I could have died in this accident and I would hate to think of someone else going through an experience like mine all because of a lack of crucial safety information. Please also be aware of the importance of having COMPREHENSIVE TRAVEL INSURANCE if you do decide to get on a bike. Thankfully I was covered for all my costs of surgery and treatment and I was able to get to an excellent hospital within reasonable time to have a chance at saving my arm and hand; I dread to think what would have happened had I not been covered.

I attended the white sand dunes today which were very pretty but we got scammed with the quad bikes. We paid over 600,000 dong for a quad bike that did not work could not drive up a hill. Was very dissapointed with how low quality the bikes were and extortionate prices. We also thought we would have ATV to ourselves but we had to share with a tour guide also. Dunes were very pretty but I recommend do not rent ATV
Didn't meet our expectations The place would look beautiful but there are too many ATVs and jeeps. Also 600,000 VND is too much for 15 mins ride with jeep.

I just want to warn everyone who's thinking about driving to the sanddunes on a motorbike. On the way the "police" will stop every white person on a scooter or motorcycle and tell you that you drive too fast (even tho you drive slower than the locals) and tell you that your licence is not valid in Vietnam. He told us that he was going to take our bikes for 7day and then we had to meet in court and pay 2mill dong. We told him that it wasnt our bikes and that we would fly home in 3 day, he first looked at us like "your problem, not mine" but afterwards he said "okay you pay 2mill per bike now and just go" so we did and when we passed them again on the way back they just smiled and waved... i was soo angry. I've met several tourists who experienced the exact same thing! Some even met 2-3 groups of police that day.. (And yes when he first pulled us over we just continued driving as we were told to never stop unless they follow us, which they did so..)
We go there with a taxi and the prices were too high for quad driving. Staff was very bad. if u not a russian tourist dont come to mui ne. very unfriendly place

I don't like to write bad things about the places we visit but felt I had to warn people of the experience we had here. We arrived at the White Sand Dunes and were told we had a half an hour walk to get to the top. We didn't want to do as it was very hot and humid that day, so we hired our own dune buggies to ride to the top instead. We got two buggies for half an hour and paid 1,000,000 dong. Would have been happy to pay this but one of our buggies kept breaking down and we only got about 15 mins on the dunes. They also didn't tell us that the smaller buggies they gave us were not powerful enough to get to the top of the dunes so we didn't even get to make it to the top! Overall a very disappointing experience.
It was nice. We rent a quad and try it. Really expensive, not good for a quality when we ride, nothing amazing to talk about it

Jeep and 4 wheeler rip offs Beware of the Jeep tour and four wheeler operators. First rented a 4 wheeler and had to return it because it couldn't go up the hills. When we returned it we were told it is not large enough for 2 people and not powerful to go up the hills. Decided to try a Jeep tour at a different shop. Were told it was unlimited time, could go as long as we wanted. Went to two places and the driver returned. Told us that the ticket was only for two places to take photos. Both the 4 wheeler and Jeep tours were 600,000 Dong. Buyer beware.

Totally destroyed landscape and loud If you want to see totally destroyed sand dunes crossed by hundreds of Quads and Jeeps, them go there. As a photographer I don't see any reason to go there again. All the nice sand dune patterns are destroyed by different vehicles.

Awful way to spend your money! If you want to replace the classic Microsoft sand dunes wallpaper with something of your own, then you could consider this place. For an entry price of VND150k per person, this is such a colossal waste of time. While it might remind you of the desert safari in Dubai, the experience is nothing close. The locals rip you off for an awful jeep ride to the dunes, have you wait there for 45mins in the sweltering heat while forcefully eye photo ops moments to kill time. Unlike Dubai there are no shacks at the dunes, so your hat and sunglasses will aid your survival. Opt out if you are given a choice.

Just for two person with a ATV driver costs us SGD30 it was just 100 meter to the top and also charged VND500.000 for rent a 125cc ATV(not well maintained) for 20 minutes and we have to pull it out of sand every time it sticks in a sand 😢 not recommended

These people are truly con artists. They only list the prices for their WORST quads online. If you want to ride anything better than an ancient quad only appropriate for a child, you will have to pay twice as much or more than the prices listed above. My partner is Vietnamese so there was not even a language barrier to cause any misunderstandings. I can only imagine how they would treat someone who doesnt speak the language. I'd suggest skipping this activity all together as most of what you will see anyway is littered with all the trash that the locals just throw all over the place. The men at the entrance of the sand dunes also refused refund our parking money after we left without staying to ride the quads. I couldn't have been more disappointed with the lack of professionalism and couth practiced by this whole establishment. Save yourself the headache, skip the Tour De Trash and go rent a jetski or scooter.
An absolute tourist trap with crazy prices for the jeep-tour. It is not worth going there. Stay at the red dunes ;-)

We paid 500k for 30 mins on 200cc quad bikes. I wouldn't recommend it to anyone as they're terrible quality. Our group of 5 bikes all had issues and couldn't make it up any hills without bogging in the sand. There's bigger quad bikes, 500cc I think which are more expensive but they would've been a better option as they could handle all of the dunes. The Vietnamese guy who collected our money was abusive at the end when we explained that one of the bikes didn't work for more than 5 minutes and refused to give us any money back. Avoid hiring the small quad bikes, go for the big ones or get a 4WD car to drive you around the dunes.

What a bad time today on the jeep tour. Not worth your time or your money. Left early to visit the white dunes where we hired quad bikes for half an hour. My bike broke down 4 times. Paid 500,000 dong and when I asked to get some of it back they acted like it wasn't their fault. Go to the beach or head back to Saigon because this place is a living breathing tourist trap.

Worst experience ever Don't hire the quad bikes they're shocking! I got 30 minutes and 3 of my bikes kept braking down. Got 5 minutes of ride time. Then asked for some money back and he told us where to go.

Disappointing not in terms of how beautiful the Dunes are, but the whole experience. We were dropped off in the dark as we came her for the sunrise. After walking a few metres we were herrassed for paying money, only to find out that the young guy was trying to sell us a quad bike. It took about 20-25 minutes to walk up the dunes in the dark. The whole atmosphere was destroyed by dozens of tourists coming up by noisy quad bikes and children selling boards to slide down the dunes. Would never go back!

If you've seen dunes before in your live, it's nothing special at all. The sand isn't very white and you get annoyed by all the quaddrivers. Don't know why people call it "magical", I mean, it's just sand. Get over it.

Real tourist trap. There is nothing more uninspiring than visiting a desert area packed with hundreds of tourists taking selfies....second all the tours take you in a jeep...ridiculous since you have to pay extra when you reach the dunes to have a kind of sand tractor...$16 usd. The jeeps DO NOT go on the sand dunes , so you can go on your own with scooter, taxi or whatever and spend as much time as you want instead of being rush like idiots....half hour they give you...worst experience and waste of half day and money.

Absolute fail - you pay a lot for a van or bus to just bring you there and then you have to pay just for someone who brings you up the sand dunes on a quad. It's just one big tourist trap! The sand dunes are okay - but if you ask me, they are nothing special and nothing compared to sand dunes you get to see at some beaches. You are offered a time of 40 min maximum to stay on top of the sand dunes and then get picked up again by the quad - honestly, what do you do 40 min on top of a sand dune? You take one or 2 pictures and leave. We stayed 10 min and then got a lift back. You can choose if you want to go by yourself with the quad (you drive on your own) or get a lift. I guess driving on your own is more fun, but you get stuck very quick. Renting by your own costs more (you can choose between a small quad and a bigger one with more power and for 2 people). Besides there a loads of other tourists around you, so finding a spot for a pic with no other people on it is not so easy. I suggest, that you rent a scooter in Mui Ne and go by yourself to the sand dunes - it's a 30-40 min drive and easy to get there. At the sand dunes, to save costs, I would recommend to walk up the sand dunes. We saw other people do that and it's really not far (500 m?). Plus, if you rent a scooter by yourself, you can easily stop at the red sand dunes and the fairy river as well - it is all in one way. The offered tours are absolute nonsense - we actually paid for a private tour, as we thought we would be going up the sand dunes and the fairy stream with a bunch of other tourists and we didn't want that. Actually a private tour means, that you only have your private car. At the sand dunes you have to go up by yourself anyway (which we did not know; we thought private tour meant, the jeep takes you up) and pay extra for the quad.
Disappointing. Motorized vehicles and people seeking a quiet experience for impactful photographs don't work well together.
Enttäuschung und Abzocke Cheap travel to the dunes but separate expensive tour through the dunes. Not spectacular as well. Just a loss of time

An absolute tourist trap. 15,000d each to get in the gate, then a man wants 5,000d to park a motorbike, then for 20 mins on a quad they want between 400,000 and 600,000d. Like i hired the motorbike for 80,000d for 24hrs. The individuals were also not very nice. The dunes themselves arent bad.
Mui ne has no tourist attractions and thus places like these have just been randomly invented to show that there it something. Not worth it at all!

Our tour guide had to park his car at the 'designated' point, then the ATV company offered to bring us to the sand dunes (about 5-10 mins walk away, weather super hot blazing sun) but we have to pay $10 per pax! I find it so unreasonable! Definitely a monopoly... Wonder if the guides here talked amongst themselves to do this... They do tours cheaply about $10 (fairy stream, sea village, red dunes, white dunes) but this additional $10... Quite upset about this. With the other korean couple, we grudgingly parted with our money... The ride was just about a minute... You can see the dunes from the starting point but it was so hot! Great fun though in the sand! So clean! Lots of tourists!

Too much effort for mediocre scenery Prices have gone up way more than what is previously posted for atv. Expect to fork out 600k vnd for 20 minutes , half the time the guide will insist on doing some slides for you. While this was somewhat fun , he'll ask for 100k vnd tip. Also for 90% of the time , the sand is blowing into your eyes and it's even painful on your skin when it's really windy. Wear long pants long sleeve and some protective face mask. You would think they could advise you to dress appropriately before embarking on this attraction.

Our hotel recomended a quad bike tour in the white sand dunes for 400.000 dong/30 min...don't do it. They're old, not working properly, were extremly loud and therefore alnost dangerous to drive for the cables and stuff were all loose..the sanddunes themselves could be pretty without the quads though..

We had the sunrise trip. It really isn't anything special at all!!! There is rubbish and huge amounts of people!! The quod bikes absolutely ruin it. Idiots speeding about not really looking for others. The sound of these bikes ruins any peace you might get. The view really isn't that great and the dunes are only in a very small area I.e the bit you're stood on so its not like you see dunes for miles!!
Toerist toerist toerist Not special at all. I just cant understand why it is so popular. The view is okay but it is ruined by al the tourist and locals on quad bikes.

Way from Mui Ne police stops all white people on motorbike and ask for money. It costs 200k to let us continue. Dunes nothing special. Idiots on quads, lot of noise from them. Dont waste your time. Better places to visit around Mui Ne

Ride of my life. Or almost the end of it. So. Being from Canada and being able to travel not far to see sand dunes in the US. Real ones. This wasn't on a list of my to dos! But my friends wanted to check them out so we did. We got in a jeep and stopped at a few places on the way there. Once we were there we could rent atv's or walk up to the top of a "sand hill". Who likes to walk!? I decided I was going to pay 150,000 for an atv. Once I got on it I figure it would be ok but was totally turned off on the idea after I gave it throttle. What piece of garbage machines this outfit is renting. The story gets better.... I take a turn with this atv to return behind to my friends and ask them if they are going to rent one. They said yes so I turned around and proceeded to head up the "sand hill". As I passed the spot where all the atv's are parked and started to go around a slight curve in the sand. The front right a-arm(piece that holds the wheel upright) snapped in half and the wheel folded under the atv. I went over the handle bars and the metal atv rack landed on my back. My small toe was cut and my back was hurting. Very scratched up but other than that I was still alive in Vietnam at some disgusting outfit. I would definitely not recommend renting these machines as they are VERY unsafe and not maintained AT ALL. I had to ask the little scam artist of a boss 5 times for my money back and every time I asked he said yes sir one minute. I had to get stern with him the last time and show a bit more anger before he pulled a wad of bills out of his pocket and peeled off three 50,000 bills. Stay away and beware! ps. If I could rate negative circles I would!
Don't go there unless you are a fan of noisy, dirty adventures ruining already ruined nature attraction.

Quad in front of me rolled over. I hear it happens all the time. They rent quads to teenagers who have never driven on sand before. Drunken people. Anybody. We had a driver..who drove crazy fast...while texting! No place much to hang on, it was dangerous as hell! Just lasted 20 hot minutes. And expensive! Stay away.

We rented ATV and it was a big mistake The thing was so old and patched up together and it showed We spent most of the time pulled it out of sand or restarted the engine and then the chain fell off The shop person had to "rescue" us Do not rent this piece of junk

We tried to go to the dunes this afternoon but got stopped by the police. We drove passed them but they came after us on their police bike. They were not wearing the correct uniform and kept changing their story. First they stopped us because we were speeding (they had no speed gun and only tried to stop us when we were close enough to see that we were foreigners). Then when we asked for evidence they asked if we had a Vietnamese licence. We didn't. They brought us to the "police station" and took the bike keys from the bike while we were trying to call the people that rented the bikes to us. We had to pay 200000 vnd to get it back after a long ordeal. My advice is to keep driving. They will give up once you get to a populated area.

A Potential Gem Ruined by Greed and Noise Do not come here expecting a breathtaking peaceful sunrise in a desert-like setting. Do not come here expecting to escape the crowds, noise and chaos. Hundreds of noisy quads driven by disrespectful tourists who think that tearing up nature and ruining the morning silence is the most fun they can have. Maybe try come after the sunrise tour. Better yet, just stay in bed.
Are the dunes worth to stand up at 4:45 am? Well, if you enjoy noise and exhaust of the little grown ups on there quads and if you don't care that there is trash all over the place: jackpot.

I always consider sunrise and sunset two great moments to contemplate beautiful and amazing Mother Nature and this is what I thought to do in this tour this morning as well. Well, forget it!!! For one hour you will be totally disturbed by the horrible noise of jeeps and quads which will drive all over the dunes in order to make (especially Chinese, Vietnamese and Russian) tourists enjoying themselves, while all travellers like me, are trying to escape from this horrible noise hidding in top dunes hill or just keeping far from them... an useless try, unfortunately with what the travel agencies call "fun attraction" they have destroyed the place, scenery and above all the calm atmosphere of the place. Such a shame!!! Don't loose your money, rent a scooter and go and see the sunrise from the coast at the end of the road and at 6:30/6:45, when all the people has gone, go to see the dunes if you don't want your sunrise will become a nightmare, as it was mine.

Went to the dunes on our motor bike expecting a nice day out, We dicided to fork out the extortionate price of 500,000 for a quad for 20 mins. Wish we hadn't bothered as the quad bike was clearly not strong enough to go up the dunes and we spent most of our time trying to get it out the sand due to it constantly getting stuck. Then by the time we knew it one of the thieving guys came and told us our time was up. So we dicided to walk around observing all the crazy quads and jeeps driving around, that was when we saw a guy fall straight off the back of a jeep driving so fast and the driver continued. When we set off we realised that they had stolen the petrol from our bike!!!! Went to the local petrol station to refule, about to set off again and we noticed the petrol dial had not moved when we asked the guy who filled it up he said our dial was broken?! Once again we had been scammed, as he clearly did not fill it up!!! Do not waste your time or money in the place just a big scam!!!
Nice place to see. But be carefull if you rent a quad, they try to rent u very expensive. They are very stupid and inpolite.

I WOULD NOT RECOMMEND A TRIP HERE We were told as there was 3 of us we needed to pay for 2 quad bikes, but we noticed they allowed others to go on one! They also tried to make 2 of us go ahead and leave our other person to wait for bike WE DIDNT, Then the bike they first gave my daughter didnt steer right, so we needed them to change itm then the other one kept breaking down, leaving us stuck in the dunes in hot heat with noone around to help, then we ran out of fuel. One of us went down to tell them and he was threatened with physical violence and sworn at....... So basically its a rip off and I wouldnt recommend anyone to go here, go to the Red Dunes nearby instead, they are free, walkable and you can see fantastic sunsets/sunrises there
Was. Rey boring . Both red and white was full of people and even impossible to take s photo . Remind me Indian market . Not recommended . I bet if arrive 4-am it would b empty and nice))

Even though your supposed to be able to legal ride a motor bike here if you have the right license at home and have got an international driving permit they don't care. We were warned just before we set off from the red sand dunes to go to the white by the locals that the police had a road block set up as they do every day. This was confirmed by a chinese girl we met who said her friend had been caught and had to negotiate a fine. We again confirmed this qith our hotel owner who said don't go to the white dunes by scooter. Even if you have the right license they don't care unless it's a vietnamese one. BIG TOURIST TRAP SO BE WARNED! Aparently the fine is between 300,000 - 1,500,000VND depending on the police officer, so don't fall victim to this nonsense!
A complete waste of money. The quad bikes aren't powerful enough for the dunes. Expect to be stranded in hot sun. Not an enjoyable experience

The dunes aren't white. There is some white sand in spots. But if you have seen sand dunes before than it isn't worth it. They are partially white but mostly red dunes. The dunes were ok by themselves. We left our hotel at 430am to catch the sunrise there. Once we arrived they wanted extra money to use the dune buggies/ 4 wheelers. DONT PAY. Just walk out to the dunes. The dunes have loads of trash even though there are locals all around making money off tourists.

We tried to get there by motorbike, but halfway the police asks for a "licence" to drive on the road. We heard several stories of people being fined from 300.000 to 1,5 million. We tried to avoid the police blockade, but unfortunately there is no other way. So we only did the red dunes, which we enjoyed a lot!

Got robbed and punched in the chest The sand dunes themselves are pretty great. It's reminiscent of the Sahara and I'm sure riding on one of the dune buggies would have been fun too. However, one of the girls on our tour couldn't find her bag with her money, phone etc anywhere and paid a man to be driven around the dunes to try and find it. After our tour guide talked with the locals for a good 45 minutes and the girl crying over her lost bag with all her possession, we jumped in the jeep and were driven just around the corner to a house next to the entrance of the dunes. It turns out one of the locals had driven their buggy out to the dunes and stolen it from her whilst she wasn't looking. The man who took it asked her to pay 2 million dong to get it back. At this point I was furious at the situation (partly because we missed the sunset, mainly because I hate people being so obviously extorted), I told him there was no way he was getting his money and I went into his house to take the bag back for the girl. This self-assured, lowly excuse for a human, desert cowboy suddenly got crazy at me and ran at me and started swiping at me, and punched me in the chest. We ended up having to just leave without her phone and money (managed to get the rest of the bag back) because we weren't sure if they had weapons or something. This is most likely a common occurrence here, as they had buggies and were situated right near the entrance on the dunes. This situation really cemented our experience with Vietnamese locals. Too many thieves, too many baseless, moral-less operations. This was definitely not the first experience we've had with corruption in this country. Please WATCH YOU BELONGINGS HERE!!!

Whole tour is a scam trip - very loud and and force an "upsell" to pay more money Since we were told that we could not rent a motorbike to visit the dunes because there are checkpoints - this is false, we booked a "private sand dunes jeep tour" The only thing that was private was your ride because everywhere else are tons of people. You are driven in a smoke belching, loud, old jeep for miles in the dark. When you get to the white dunes, they have you and dozens of others wait while you are missing the sun rise and then take you to ATVs and tell you that they are 400,000 dong (~$20). Nobody tells you that you paid $28 (not expensive, but not worth it) to get driven to dunes to pay more money to rent ATVs. The people are rude and don't speak much English except to yell, "you don't pay, you don't go". Half of the people pay the price for the ATVs to go racing around the dunes right behind you making an enormous racket and nearly running you over (no training, no helmets, no safety).
