The whole situation is so noisy, nobody can find anywhere to watch the sunrise in peace. After 15 minutes, they ride around and tell you to get back to your jeep where you are hustled to the next destination. There is no stopping along the way or getting a little breakfast elsewhere. Everyone is treated like cattle. Even the few words of English that the driver understood wouldn't encourage him to slow down or stop for a photo. You are hustled to the red dunes where vendors renting little plastic sleds mob you. There are a few big, expensive, terrible restaurants there that you have to eat at or go hungry because there is no other food that the driver will stop at. The third stop is at a "fishing village" that is really just a few local people on the beach sorting through the literal plastic garbage in the nets to find a few fish to sell to tourists or other locals. I wasn't told how long I was suppose to spend there, but the driver came down after 15 minutes and told us we have to go. It turns out that he had a poker game waiting for him at the next "attraction". The fourth "attraction" is the "fairy stream" which is where you walk up a small stream with hundreds of others that are on similar tours. They even give you the pleasure of paying to leave your shoes behind or to use the toilet. Here they tell you to take an hour or more since it is the last stop and they don't care how you get back to your hotel. I recommend avoiding this tour under all circumstances. Rent a motorbike and drive around on your own. It will be much more interesting.

We went here as an evening venture to watch the sunset, we were warned about police so we're prepared but this trip was rubbish. Firstly we get about 5/10 minutes out of mui ne and are stopped by the police - they asked for license so jack passed it over - 'not fit for vietnam' fair enough he did not have an international license but neither did anyone. If it wasn't for jacks confidence and persuasion we would have had to pay a fine like everybody else. Then at the sand dunes on entrance its 10.000VND per person and then another 400.000VND to ride a quad bike for 2 people for 20 minutes. The quad we got was no where near powerful enough for 2 people and we soon found out it had no brakes that worked either. Half way up a dune, not enough power, no brakes - very very dangerous. Without quad bike experience we would have definitely rolled. We were then stuck, very annoying as we ended up missing the sunset!! We'd had enough and it was too dangerous so we're only there for 15 minutes, when we told the people that quad bike was broken they didn't bat an eyelid. Then finally, on the way back we stopped at the first petrol station from the white dunes and knew that they were probably going to over charge us as we had been overcharged previously. We watched the pump meter as they filled up the people before us and then they got to us - they didn't reset it - they carried on filling ours up half and tried to charge us 200.000VND when it should have only been 30.000VND, we weren't having it so passed over 30.000. The petrol man then proceeded to try and take the key from our moped and stand so we couldn't get away - and argue. We ended up paying 50.000VND, but this isn't fair as everyone out here seems to try and mug you off as much as they possibly can!!

Not worth it, the entry is 10k ( says 5k on the sign but hey it is vietnam!) and it is basically 3 hills with white sand so it is not like a desert as how they sell it to you! If you go by yourself on a moped might be worth a stop, but going there for sunrise or sunset I say don't. The sun is not rising or setting above the sand dunes since it is too small, it is rising above a village far away. And yeah people are going to drive you nuts by driving 4 wheelers all around you. So it is super loud and not cosy at all! The rental price for the 4 wheelers are 300k/20min!

Do not ride on the back of a 12yr old Vietnamese boy with the 'need for speed!' Watched two girls being driven around on the back of two boys, on quads. They were racing around and cornering on two wheels. Next minute the quads crashed and both girls were flung like rag doll about 2mtres in the air. One girl a sports women knew how to land but was badly bruised and scratched on her back. The other girl wasn't so lucky. Both had to go to hospital. If they landed any higher up their backs, their necks would have broken. They boys rode off not giving a toss!!!

It's a long way to ride there. Part of the road is not in a good condition. The dune is big and white. That's it! Nothing very amazing to talk about it. If u already visit red sand dune. I think that's enough.

this is one of the most boring POI I've ever visited. Yes the desert is vast considering its in the middle of a land. But just stare the dune and that's it. You can rent a "slide" to play around, but there's not much slope around. all in all the visit to Mui Ne is boring. The only consolation I have is a local Pho that I have in the one of the residence which our taxi driver took us to, it's very good.

The sand dunes themselves are quite fascinating, however it is completely spoilt by the people managing them in terms of access and quad bike hires. The access is poor, no signage to the entrance. The quad bikes were around 300k VND for 20 mins - they put 2 of us on 1 quad bike and it was outright dangerous. They were clearly not powerful enough for 1, let alone 2. The brakes did not work, and the handlebars were loose. Most people we saw got stuck in the bowls or on the hills, apart from the workers themselves who had much more powerful quads capable of actually driving the dunes. We went straight back after getting stuck three times in 5 mins and demanded our money back, to which they initially refused-they eventually settled on 2/3s refund. The worst bit is - they turned the quad round and put 2 more tourists on it without even checking!!!! Complete disregard for safety, fun or anything else other than the income! DO NOT USE!

We've got pick up at 4.30 am to see the sun rise. We payed USD10 each. We were 8 in the car. We needed to wait 20 minutes for the 2 last one! When we arrived at the white sand dunes we needed to pay an entry... then we arrived so late and no more sun rise! We were so fustrated! Then we went to the red sand dunes. Worst place! Kids are harassing you and you are not even out of the car... please ask before you order anything in those cafe front of the dunes... we payed a tiny coconut the price you will pay in your country! Very expensive! Next the fishing village. Another shame. Some boat were there but nothing to see and so much garbage. The last place would be the best but you need to pay again to see it! We were so frustrated we did'nt do it. But know in that place they are making great cookies and the coconut is 10,000 dong. Way cheaper. This place and only it's a must do. The sands dunes can be skipped too noisy and so much trash everywhere. They look at you as you are a walking wallet!

Nice dunes , terrible access Whilst the dunes themselves are beautiful getting there is torture due to road condition. You can rent a jeep or dune buggy but they are expensive and the noise is horrible in such a beautiful spot. Until road is finished give it a miss.

You imagine something spiritual when you think of the dunes. Well, don't forget to take ear plugs when you go there. Or better yet, save your time and money and don't bother. Nature is completely destroyed by the rental of motorbikes (or equivalent for sand), which make horrific noises all over the dunes.

SCAM!! Please do not go to the quad bikes with the men dressed in orange! Go to the MAIN ENTRANCE. Dont let taxi drivers tell you there isnt one because there is!! They try to make you pay to even enter the dunes... you do NOT have to pay!! The bikes are very overpriced and do not even work, they cut out constantly and are so dangerous. I badly hurt my leg as my bike had NO brakes or power. I was given a "large quad" which broke down within 40 seconds, so the man in orange gave me replacement smaller quad which was supposed to be £100,000 dong cheaper... I asked for my money back as I was no longer driving a big quad and he said no. I set off on my smaller quad anyway.... only for that one to break down too!! The men in orange are nasty and rude an WILL NOT give you money back even if your bike is broken... we had to WALK back through the dunes! Luckily they had broken down within 5 minutes of our ride or we would of been totally screwed as my leg was in agony. My boyfriend has a quad back home (experienced with quads) and he couldnt get either bike to start. He had to push the bike off my leg to avoid my leg getting crushed!! My bike cut out going up a very small dune, my brakes did not work, so I put out my leg to stop the bike rolling backwards So seriously avoid. Walk the dunes. Save yourself money and injury!! I really hope no tourist has to go through what I went through. Please dont be scammed by these men!!! If you really want to go, use alternative quads please guys!!! Safe travels all.

The dunes itself were a great sight but everything around it sucks. The road leading to the dunes are atrocious. The quads were in terrible shape. We had a young kid hopping on for the ride because he knew that the damn thing would stall all the time. And every time he would start it he would go for a spin eating away at our 20 minutes. Really old unsafe dilapidated quads. Not worth the expense. When we left our guide was scolded for taking us to the 'wrong' quad rentals because there are two rental places. Total deception.
over ran with drunken youths Maybe a nice place few years ago. Over ran with drunken idiots, What are you doing here? Please go to Ibiza?

Beautiful place, had fun for about 2 minutes until I discovered my brakes didn't work on top of one of the biggest dunes. After catapulting down a 15m sand dune into a pond and burning several layers of skin off my leg from the tyre (had to go to hospital), I wasn't even given a full refund- not worth it at all.

The sand dunes are absolutely beautiful but unfortunately while we were riding the buggies on the sand dunes two girls in our group were hurt when the buggy flipped (they were not doing anything dangerous).There are absolutely no safety precautions and some of the buggies are old and not very well maintained which makes them difficult to steer. Luckily the girls got away with only minor injuries but it could have been a lot worse. Be very careful if you choose to do this activity!

Really pissed off. Don't bother going on your own I booked a tour and went up to the sand dunes .. When we got to the quad bikes .. I saw a girl who had flipped over the bike as they don't have proper brakes.. I decided to give it a go and when I was up the hill I realised there was a guy on the back of my bike. He said that he should ride it.. So he did .. So so fast .. Then he took me to a lake where no one was and asked me to tip him. I already spent 500,000 for a half hour ride which is so steep as it is. He then asked me for more money when I gave him 100,000 and when I refused he got upset, At this point I didn't want to carry on as it all seems so rushed. He took me back so I only was on the bike for ten mins. It was such a waste of money. So sad and gutted they are all in it together. I overheard a 6 year old kid that lives there say - money money money money money to his mother ..that is ALL they want from you. Such a crappy experience.

DON'T GO FOR THE QUAD!!! The white sand dunes are a nice sight but dont go for the quads! I payed 40000 vnd for 30 min on the quad after 5 min i got stuck in the sand nobody came for help after walking back trough the dunes (35 C) they slowly came for help. 2 min later i was stuck again. I walked back for 15 min again and wanted half of my money back. The guys got really angry and started shouting dident get back a penny after making a scene. This is total ripoff!!!!!! Wasent the only person who got stuck in the sand dunes. The quads were in a terrible condition! Worst experience in vietnam!! Trust me never do this!

Expect a long walk or spending a lot of money on a mini tractor We paid 15$ per person for a private 4x4 tour. We expected it to be able to drive through the dunes and were very surprised that you either have to walk up hill for 40 minutes or rent a mini tractor for 500000 (!) Vnd.

Vietnam government should do something about it We took a jeep trip to the dunes, from far you can see how beautiful the white sand dunes are. I was really excited getting there. But when we got there (BTW they charge 10k dong at the entrance) the dunes was busy with noisy ATVs, that was terrible, you actually cannot speak with the person next to you and always make sure that no one of them are running over you since they drive really wild and dangerous. When we got back to the agency we asked why they let them go drive there between the people? he said the the government haven't got there yet to handle that... any way you cant even take a picture there without some jeep or AVT will get in your way. I think it's only a matter of time until someone will get hurt there, if you do decide to go there - just try and make sure you are not the first one...

Both the White and Red sand dunes are being ruined by unscrupulous locals involved in trying to screw every tourist they see. The White dunes are beautiful and the locals there are ok, but the locals at the Red Dunes are an embarrassment to the Vietnamese people, extremely rude and disrespectful. I would avoid both until the local Tourist committee realise that they need to improve the experience for all day trippers. I can't believe they are trying to charge people to see the dunes.....crazy. They really aren't that impressive, the drive to them is probably the most interesting bit.

I have to admit we went on a rainy morning so the sunrise was rather unspectacular. Still, there were hordes of tourists at the dunes, sliding down. The squads are really annoying as well - the dunes are not as big as you might think, so the squads are very loud and destroy the countryside. The worst thing is the trash which is everywhere. To sum it up: this should not get you out of bed at 5 in the morning. Just stay there! This is the opposite of ecotourism.

Every place you go in Vietnam is filthy. Nothing to it. It is a small white sand dune. Mui Ne is place for beach lover, it likes any other beach in the world but filthy. It is not worth for 5 hours drive from SaiGon There is no public bathroom or shower.

You just can't slide down It was hot and we wanted to sledge down the dunes but as much as we tried we couldn't move!! Not sure if we were doing something wrong but everyone else seemed to have the same issue!
Small area, very touristy, lots of dirt. Average dunes in the Netherlands are nicer On Mui Ne's scooter it took more than an hour and did not find it worthwhile Automatically translated

Although it is true that the place is beautiful, it is not very big and above all a good tourist trap. We found ourselves somewhat obliged to take a quad to climb the dunes, the staff being very far from being clear about the prices and playing on the incomprehension between the price of the trip and the power of the quad. Finally, we find ourselves doing barely 10 minutes of quad for 25 minutes of travel (numerous photo stops).

ATV drivers are rude and aggressive Are these drivers trying to kill tourism here ? It sure feels like it !! They are only interested in $$$$ sure this will help you for the next 12 months but nobody will be visiting in 10 years time. The government really needs to Manage this area properly. We were overcharged and then given half the time on the atv that we were promised. But don’t question the locals because they will just threaten you. Do yourself a favour and visit Hoi Ann or Da Lat. this place has gone to the dogs :(

Just sand dune, no special things there It’s wuite far from Muine(around 15-20kms) , Riding on sanedunes by jeep with driver or ATV car is such a pricey experience , 15-20 minutes for 10 dollars on shred jeep vride, 20$ for ATV ride. It’s quite windy on top the sand dunes , there is also lake down there. Nothing extraordinary here.

We left at 4:45 with a Jeep, we got down to White sand dunes about 5:30 where we had 2 choices either rent an ATV for 800,000 don Per 20 minuts. Or let Them drive for 300.000 Per person wich i Think is very expensive + it was Gilles with turists.

All tours will take you to the Fairy stream (which is a small stream of water with both sides covered in trash), a viewpoint of the fishing boats and the white + red sand dunes. The white sand dunes are picturesque but small, not worth the entire trip. The red dunes are even smaller, in fact it’s just one small hill of sand

when you arrive at the dunes by jeep you will be urged to rent a quad. These are outrageously expensive. we walked, but that was before sunrise. that worked too. Who has never seen dunes, ok, otherwise not worth the money.

It's a good experience The place is very nice to take pictures, however the 4x4 drivers are very crazy and scared my wife and daughter, they do not have any safety procedures in the cars at least the seat belts should be better regulated by the authorities to ensure a safe service. Be careful before you get into these cars.

The white sand dunes were part of a tour offer (besides red sand dunes and fairy stream) we booked at our hotel in mui ne. The actual place is pretty cool since you wouldn't expect to see dunes in Vietnam. The contrast between the white sand dunes and the dark blue water of the small lake lets you get some great pics, so it's surely worth a visit (if you're not in a hurry since it takes already more than half an hour to get there from mui ne). What I'd definitely not suggest is renting a quad to visit the dunes, unless you're into getting ripped off! Unfortunately we fell for their ploy because I did such things in Egypt and Namibia which were really cool experiences, but in this place it was the complete opposite!! They wouldn't let you drive on your own (they care to always provide you an annoying company of theirs), each ride just lasts 2min and you're being charged again to get to another dune as soon as you get off the quad. Those locals also don't bargain at all (which should've been suspicious already for Southeast Asia). We paid 600.000 VND for 2 people to get on one quad for 2min! If you're there and have got some time, just do it on your own (it's for free and you don't need a guide) and enjoy the scenery! It will save you a lot of energy deeling with difficult people!

Got there for early sun rise. SCAM - locals waiting at the entrance to sell u tickets for expensive quad bike ride to top of sand dunes which they claim are 4km away. We declined and walked up which took us about 15 mins so unless you are not able to walk up it's a waste of money and seriously overpriced. As for the dunes one word comes to mind....average!. Scattered rubbish in places and the roar of quad bikes up and down just ruins it! If I could do it again I'd roll over in the bed and turn off the 4am alarm. There are far more spectacular dunes throughout the world.

Do Not get a quad just walk We got ripped off for 600,000 for a 2 minute journey up the dune and then the driver left us there without telling us and we were left up there without knowing what was going on, we thought we were stranded. Big scam. Just walk up there because they are FREE. We were only there about 30 mins. Pretty once there.

We arranged a group tour from a Tour operator in Mui ne, we were told to be in the Hotel reception at 04:30, the driver turned up at 04:45 he forgot us, then he drove like Lewis Hamilton often driving on the wrong side of the road, we arrived around 05:30, we were told to pay k200 vnd each for a Quad bike up the dunes as it was a 4km hike, we were never told we would have to pay 400k when we booked, infact it's a 15 min walk, a right rip off, it was a disappointing sunrise as it was cloudy, not the tour operators fault. We went to the red sand dunes ( rubbish), again Vietnamnamese trying to sell you a sledge for 50k down the sand dunes, the Mui ne fishing village was very good but smelly, and finally the Fairy river, 15k to tourists not Vietnamese, all in all it was a rip off, the driver took every opportunity to try and sell us a quad ride, when we refused he dropped us at the side of the road and said 10 mins, and left, coming back for us later. Would I recommend it NO, would I go again NO. Arriving back at the hotel around 08:45.

The dunes itself is quite alright if you've never seen any. However, any of the jeep tours are not worth the money (even though they are cheap) as you get dropped off on a parking lot where your driver tries to push a quad tour on you. If you don't book this (200k VND) you won't have time to make it to the top of the dunes. It's also a bit annoying to walk around by yourself because the quads are driving with high speed all around you. BTW - If you take a motorbike yourself, be aware of the police checkpoint on the highway there (see screenshot attached, they're always at the same spot afaik) You'll very likely have to pay a "fine" (do some research on this topic if you drive there by yourself).

The dunes are worth visiting, but whatever you do, do not rent an Atv. It is very expensive and the trip is only about 5 minutes. The workers will lie to you that it is 4km of walking, when in fact it is a nice 15 minute walk that you can easily do yourself. The trip would also be a lot nicer without all the screaming brides taking selfies and roaring atv's all around.

I've seen bigger sand dunes at a golf course. Sunrise was blocked by clouds. Skip the ATV ride and walk, unless you've never been on an atv or can't walk. You will meet every other tourist in Mui Me on a small dune top to not take in sunrise together. Very fun to jump off the dune though

I booked a taxi of Dichungtaxi from HCMC to Mui Ne, I hoped that I would see a fascinating view like a real desert but plastic is everywhere. And the heat is intense, luckily we booked a taxi and not rode a motorbike like many people recommend because we could be burnt under the sun. Bad experiences, never comback

We booked the sand dune tour with our hostel and were told the quad bikes were 400,000 vnd per bike. When we arrived the people told us it was 600,000 vnd per bike or 800,000 vnd for two of us to share one bike. This price was only for 20 minutes on the bikes We ended up walking up the dunes. It was really disappointing as they were a far drive from our hostel and we had come to mui nei specifically to go quad biking. The staff were rude and brushed us off when we were not willing to pay their prices.

When you get to the parking lot of the dunes, the 4 wheel-drivers, who are waiting there in plenty, want 800,000 dong (2 people) to take you to the dunes. If you want to go in a Jeep instead then 600,000 dong. All of this would be feasible potentially if we lived in an area with no dunes, but the USA has plenty and these were not all that special. Save your money, take a picture from the road. Also on the property are Ostriches. They are saddled so you can ride them.

We had paid for the bus from Ho Chi Min to Mui Ne without tour as we were going to see the prices for the sand dunes when we got there. First of all, the man in charge of bus was very rude. Couldn’t understand any English and when it was our time to get off the bus, he rushed us off rudely. We then find out he dropped us to the outskirts. We had to walk far but lucky we found a guide who organised us a private tour to the sand dunes. He told us everything was included for a fair price - a private tour with jeep. His friend drove us to the White Sand Dunes first. One of the workers told us at first it was 200.000 pp to use a jeep or ATV just to take you to the top, or walk for free. When we mentioned we were told we didn’t have to pay extra and couldn’t walk as I was pregnant, they got angry and changed the price to 400.000 per person instead. We refused to pay and argued with them for an hour. We were annoyed and just decided to walk. As soon as we walked off the guy called us back and agreed we could pay 300.000 PP. When we came back, other tourists mentioned they were also told they did not have to pay any extra as they paid for the tour and were told the same thing; everything was included. We did not have to pay for the Red Sand Dunes but I feel that these tours are ripping tourists off especially when it comes to the sand dunes and if your on a budget. I’ll admit they were beautiful but for the price, big rip off. Be careful to anyone going.

I was there on May 27, 2019. I was expecting to be a a smaller version of Sahara desert. Only to find out it's not even close, but the most disappointment was the jeep or atv to get to the dunes what a big rip off. 800,000 vnd for the jeep and 600,000 vnd for the atv. that's like over $50 cdn for less than 5 mins trip and then to the so called blue water lake in total 15 mins max for getting out to snap photos. Total rip off!! just walk up on your own.
We travelled by bus from Mui Ne to see this. Really it is just a sand so not special place and after walking five minutes you have seen it all.

Price gougers, dont go here unless you have money to throw away My wife and I went here on a jeep tour recommended to us by the hotel we are staying at. When we got there, we were told it would be 800k for a quadbike ride to the top of the sand dunes for pictures, and even more expensive to rent a jeep. It's nothing but a price gouging scam, as there is nothing wrong with the jeep we rode in, but they don't allow anyone in a vehicle to come to the dunes except for their overpriced vehicles. I wouldn't go back when they are running a scam operation like that. A 1 hour trip to the dunes and lake cost a little less than double the price of renting the jeep to go all around Mui Ne.

They put on a "guide" on each ATV, you are not allowed to use other than 1st gear, so you are 3 pax on one atv. First we were taken to the highest dune where they wanted us instead of driving use our time on sliding down the dune with a piece of plastic (=usin the paid time for other than driving), luckily they understood that we payed for driving, -not anything else. May be good for young kids or elderdy people but nothing for someone who knows how ATV driving COULD be..... No credit cards or euros accepted, even if they state the price in USD they accept only VND in cash
Over rated. Cost extra to get to the dunes once you have paid to ride there in a clapped out Jeep. Ask plenty of questions when booking this.

Visited the white sand dunes, took an hour to get there by bus and when we did it was pretty crowded with quad bikes and off road vehicles flying about the place. If you want to ride a quad around the dunes while having a guide riding with you then go for it, or sit inside a truck as the guide flies up and down the dunes. It was noisy, crowded and over rated, seems to be that with all that Phan thiet and the surrounding areas have to offer is pretty limits when it comes to tourist spots so they all get quite crowded. Maybe earlier in the day may be better time.

Supposed to be one of the main attractions of the area, but like many things in Mui Ne is killed by tourism. Sure, of you're in Mui Ne for a longer period you should probably visit it, but don't expect lush African sanddunes. Instead a few trampled dunes near a lake.

I’ve been to this place few years back when this place is not too many tourists . Everything is beautiful especially ppl there but abit disappointed of this trip as they are not helpful even thou my cap and slipper were drop to the sand . The driver even ask me to climb down by myself to take my stuff ! They can drive down with their ATV! I nearly can’t manage to climb up and tats so dangerous as a tourist with no experience to sand !!!!!!!! Mui Ni suppose to be a nice place but with these ppl makes the place with money face only !

White sand dunes with family We went with a large group of 8 and took a jeep into the dunes. Paid 50k dong to just get in and 1mil dong for about 20 mins in the jeep. We enjoyed it because went in expecting nothing but there were a few exhilarating moments for us driving down the dunes. The driver was the worst. Never offered to take pictures, never offered a word, had to ask him to open the back door for us. The service at the front wasnt much better considering they tried to charge us twice. Just watch out for those guys claiming you need to pay twice. If you do decide to go please take a shawl or something because your eyeballs and hair and mouth will probably be filled with sand! Think Lawrence of Arabia.

Coming from Western Australia, was I interested in paying for a white sand dunes tour? No, but the red sand, well yes.. I have seen more white sand dunes than I care to remember, including living amongst them for a year as a 21 year's old soldier half a century ago. No thanks I told wifey but had to admit red sand dunes would be a first. We passed on the too-long and expensive tour catching a taxi to visit the red dunes ourselves. To be frank, the "red" dunes were err, not red! They were barely even orange, not even the orange peel, more the part of the orange you would eat. The visit consisted of a brief trudge up a vey modest dune to view more of the same orange dunes stretching all too briefly away into a not very distant vista. Even my wife agreed (a bit unusual) that the "red" dunes were at best orange and for the taxi cost of 200,000VND a waste of valuable food and drinks budget. To avoid this wasted money, simply observe the white and orange dunes on the ride into Mui Ne Beach from your bus or car window, its the same colours as on the tour folks, the Sahara Desert it isn't.

A lot of sand, where the locals earn their money by driving the tourists in jeeps up the sandy hills....great view from the top though, but the experience loses value by the generated traffic of sandbuggys and atv's.

Meh - beware the additional charge. Did get some nice pics there but.... ...that additional 500 dong we needed to pay to get out to the dunes (on top of the tour cost) our tour guide or hotel didn't tell us about beforehand. That really annoyed us. Didn't realise we were going there that day as would have looked it up on here. The staff there that day were not very helpful or friendly either. Met many other very annoyed tourists there as well, some who didn't have the money on them to pay the extra fee. Seems a little odd that so many of us who were brought there on tours knew nothing about it.

White Sand Dune is precisely that. A big pile of white sand in the middle of nowhere. You drive or are driven to the top and, after taking a couple of holiday snaps, you return to the bottom and leave. Yawn.

Crazy prices just to get to the dune so only saw them from the road. We took a tour and when we arrived near the dunes we were asked to pay double what the tour cost to get to the top of the dune! It looks spectacular from the road, I can imagine it would have been much better if we were closer. There are quad bikes to take you to the top but you need to be reasonably fit to hold on and not have any back issues which I do. We would not have paid for the tour had we been aware beforehand.

This trip didn’t get off to a good start as we were supposed to be picked up from our hotel at 1.30 pm, but no one turned up to collect us. Three phone calls later I managed to get hold of Active Asia whom the tour was booked through. They rang me back and informed me that the tour operator had forgotten about us. Eventually we were picked up an hour after our appointed time. The vehicle we were picked up in, according to the driver, was 40 years old and it showed its age. There were no seat belts, no windscreen wipers, no sun visors and you could see the road below through the holes in the floor. Our first stop was a fishing village. By our choice it was the quickest stop we have ever made. The smell was absolutely disgusting and there was nothing to see, except a floatation of fishing boats anchored just off the beach. We told the driver to stop, but get back onto the highway, so that we could get away from the smell. Our next stop was the white sand dunes. To get to the top of the sand dunes, you either had the choice of being driven up or you could drive yourself up in a quad bike. Either way, there was an over the top charge for this service. Unfortunately we were not told that we would be charged for this service and after some discussion amongst our group we decided that we not avail ourselves of this service as we did it see it as value for money. We deceived that we wouldn’t do any further sand experience on the way home as they couldn’t offer us anything that we hadn’t done before. I must say that the drive there and back was interesting to say the least. The driving style was unusual to say the least. The driver would put his foot on the accelerator then take it off and the put it back on again. This ongoing action resulted in a very jerky ride. The other thing that he did was that when we came to a downhill section of the road, he would put the car in neutral and coast down the hill, riding the brakes. When we questioned him about his driving style, he informed us that he was ordered to drive that way but his manager. This is a trip to be missed. I wouldn’t recommend this trip to anyone.
I was here with a bike (without) motor from Mui Ne. The trip was exhausting but beautiful. The sand dunes were very loud

Well we were part of a tour to this location and I think in my own personal opinion that it should be listed as a scam. Let me tell you yes I like Vietnam. Ho Chi Minh city has great tours and are polished and have competition on their side. Nha Trang has good beaches and keep them tidy. Hoi An is excellent and a beautiful place. Then Mui Ne beaches excellent near the resorts. This white sands is yes sand dunes where arrogant tour drivers want you to go with the atv vehicles or old land cruisers, purportedly at 600,000 vnd and that is why it is a scam, you arrive with your tour which costs 100,000 dong. Then it is six times your tour price....please smell the rat!We are told by our tour guide you only have forty minutes and we go... Yep is at least an hour we started walking and our tour guide comes up to us on an atv and tells us again 40 minutes, we walk on at a good pace are in our fifties and fit but it is a kilometre to a sand Hill is it breathtaking..no, is it on a natural heritage area...no, rubbish littered on the way to one of sand dunes we took 15 minutes, had the gps on 2kms return, back in 10 minutes. Meanwhile noisy atv vehicles buzzed past. Yes I am Australian we have clean, clear, pristine beaches....sand dunes are the same. Yes if you are from Russia...,Siberia it is amazing but in the end they are Hills of sand with buzzing bikes, and tourists. I read the warnings and had the opinion that MOST people are negative.... It is crap get on the bus to Hoian or Nha Trang. Mui Ne has its good sides but While Sands does not live up to it.

Very crowded and full of trash. The quad rental is unreasonable overpriced. The Chinese people randomly posing are the second attraction after the sunset. During the day I assume the place has not much to offer

400k dong for 20mins (small ATV), 600k dong for 20mins (big ATV). 15k dong entry fee, 5-10k dong for parking. What out for cops during the long drive out there. Until you pass the speed/tourist trap, stay at 40kmh or slower, and stay on the shoulder. You don't get to drive the ATV how fast you want and where you want. They dictate how fast you can go and the path to follow. I gave it 2 stars because when the guide drove (which was most of the 20mins) he did go fast, and it was fun, but I would've rather driven slower, but had me driving. Sept - Feb is the windy season, but they don't provide you with goggles or glasses, so my eyes were closed during most of the time I was the passenger. We stopped for photo ops, even though I didn't care about taking stupid pics, I wanted to drive the ATV and have fun. If you want to spend a lot (for Vietnam) and have your hand held like a baby, then you should definitely visit here.

We went on a morning tour to the dunes so we could see the sunrise. It is an absolutely beautiful location but the vast number of people lets it down as they wiz around on quad bikes ruining the peaceful nature of the location

carefull with the quads As we reached the sanddunes, they told o\us we had an hour and it would take 30 minutes to walk one way. They offered the quads for 500k per person for like 1 minute drive, so we walked instead. It took about 20 minutes and was absolutely fine. The landscape is disturbed by the noisy quads and is not absolutely breathtaking. I wouldn't visit again, go to the red sanddunes instead to watch the sunset.

Beautiful dunes yes, and worth the visit if you need a day off the beach and have nothing to do. But crowded with tourist, four-wheel drive 650 000dong /20min quite over prized and the crew didn’t quite care about good customer service. But our boys where excited about the drive.

We booked a jeep tour to the white sand dunes to watch the sunrise. Once at the edge of the dunes you either walk out or pay more money to be transported by quad bike or jeep. We chose to walk, but honestly, the peaceful sunrise you might imagine does not happen. The exercise was good but the hoards of tour groups & diesel fumes ruined it for me. We did get some fun photos though.

Arrived as part of our tour, was harassed to hire motorbike @ 400,000 vnd for 2 people. Decided to walk didn't take too long around 20mins saw good views but surrounded by hooning quad bikes. Currently still waiting for our jeep to return to take us back. Nice sand dunes however.

Beautiful dunes, noisy crowd We went to the sand dunes to see the sunrise. The dunes themselves would be magic. Unfortunately some people have turned it into a business to drive people to the top of the dunes with quads. So at 6am the place is already packed with people, stinks like gasoline and is noisy. The sand is scarred by the tires of the quads. If I went again, I'd go somewhere around 10am -15 pm. That's when there is the least people.

I had a nightmare journey out there, motorbike broke down and cops stopped me on the way back. There were some dunes that were white. It was a contrast to the red sand but otherwise little to differentiate from other dunes I have seen. Might be fun if you did a Jeep tour and got to let that little sucker loose

Visited today with a group of friends and felt like I got conned out of my money. My group had a relatively good time (minus they don’t tell you the small ATVs aren’t allowed to go the top until you try) but I had a bit of a nightmare. My quad bikes chain repeatedly came off whenever I tried to accelerate past a medium pace which was often required to conquer hills. This must have happened 5 or 6 times before they realised the bike was a dud and took me back for another. In total I only got to free ride for about 10 minutes without having to worry about the bike breaking down. The views were nice at least.

The sanddunes are really beautiful and the sunset too, but there is just so much noise because of the old jeeps and atv's driving turist up and down the dunes. Its a shame that this nice place gets destroyed by loud engines.

The dunes are being ruined by tourism a with jeeps and ATVs devouring the fragile ecosystem so tourists can take bad selfies in a beautiful setting. Who wants to get up at 4am anyway? Sorry, probably not what you wanted to hear.

Boyfriend ans i paid for the over priced ATV. There was a sign on the ATV warning not to have over 2 people riding yet the staff jumped on the back with both of us on. We consistently flipped the ATV and tumbled down a hill of hot sand, the ATV falling on me (luckily not injured badly). This place is not safe or affordable. Slightly fun but mistly scary.
Loud und touristic..just interessting for Motorfreaks.. Its also quite expansive to rent a quad, about 38

Pricey and lacking amazingness Spent half a day here with other 5 friends and rented the ATV for 25 mins. The experience is pricey (600-800K VND) and the ride is fairly limited by the guides accompanying you and directing you all the time. I understand the security concerns but didn't feel genuine given these limitations and the hoard of ATVs and Jeeps going around. Not amazing if you have already experienced proper sand dunes somewhere else in the world.

Lousy, noisy expensive a bit far from Muine Expensive to rent the quads, 300kwith a driver 1200000 for solo noisy and under powered quad for 30 minutes . Drivers are free to roam the dunes and there is no safe place to walk unaffected. They really need to regulate where drivers can go, and at times they should be prohibited.

BAD POLICE ON THE WAY TO THE DUNES If you go by scooter you will have big chance to be stopped by police at around 20 kms after mui ne. They said that you were too fast et asked for 2 .5 milllions dongs ! Very agressive people .We arrange that for one million. If you don t pay we have to leave the scooter and go for the money by taxi ! .

As with the Fairy Stream this is free to visit as long as you can get there yourself. It is a bit of a way outside Mui Ne. There are lots of locals trying to sell you bits of plastic so you can go down the dunes but to be honest they were so steep it wouldn't be safe and getting back up would be exhausting to say the least. Not for me but my husband said he got some good photos of the dunes.
Worth going as impressive sand dunes. Walking is best and avoid riding in 4x4s and other motor vehicles as they ruin both the atmosphere and are dangerous to walkers.

Think the nice 4x4 jeep you booked is going to take you on the dunes? Think again! The jeep only takes you to the car park where you have to pay extra to get into another identical jeep to take you the sands. If you don't want to hire another jeep there are quad bikes that are a much cheaper option. The Quad bike operators are a bit pushy though. The attraction is very busy and no at all suitable for walking. You have to get a quad or hire the extra jeep to enjoy it. We paid $23 for our 4x4 jeep pickup at 5am so we where very disappointed to learn that we essentially booked a very expensive very uncomfortable taxi. Then we had to pay again for the Quad Bikes. The dunes are pretty nice but busy.

Trip to dunes with Jeep was wasting time and money. You have to rent another jeep in dunes or buggy to reach top of dunes. Minimum Price for renting was 600.000 VND. While returning to Phan thiet trip Jeep went Broken so we had to taxi to get back hotel. Luckely taxis are not high prices in Phan thiet area.

We visited the sand dunes as part of an organised tour. The sand dunes are beautiful and unexpected in Vietnam. We arrived in the mid afternoon heat and the reflection of the sun of the sand almost made it unbearable. At the main gate we were asked to pay extra to get into the sand dune park. We were dropped off at the entrance to the dunes where a group of locals attempt to get you to pay extra for 15 minutes on a quad bike or get a lift to the top of the sand dune. The prices were extortionate for Asia. We attempted to walk as far as we could on the dunes but didn't get far before the heat took its toll. We then went back to the entrance to sit in the shade and wait for our time to be up. If you go, go at sunrise or sunset and be prepared to walk!!

If you ever find yourself in Mui Ne or the surrounding areas wich I don't recommend unless you're into kite surfing. You kind of have to go to the dunes since there's not much more to do. The dunes themselves are ok but not very special and polluted by atv's and 4x4's. If you want to go there with a rented scooter I highly suggest to take the bypass and not the road by the beach since there often are police controls that make you pay 1mio dong if you don't have an international driver's license on you (BIG SCAM)
Not a nice place to be... Noisy, dangerous and extremely crowded. Not worth the four hours needed and the 7 US-Dollar needed January 2017.

It was a third part of tour. And just we went out from jeep we were asked to pay 500k vdn for quadbike riding to the dunes (don't do it or pay attention to driver! You can walk there by yourself). It was exciting and funny but only for 5 minutes before we arrived. After that driver said that we have 15 minutes and drove away. We walked around. It's really very beautiful. Take sunglasses because there's very windy. Other drivers had big and extreme tours for their passengers but our driver forgot about us. Despite of that we enjoyed the dunes.

If you get a jeep tour - make sure you ask if entrance fees are included to all stops. Also ask if your jeep will take you up the dunes or whether you have to pay a lot mor to rent a quad bike to get up the dunes. Our jeep was noisy and uncomfortable- no off road terrain so could have simply got a taxi there.

DON'T GO THERE BY MOTORBIKE We went there ourselves by motorbike. Bit you will be stopped by the police on your way from Mui Ne. They say they will take your motorbike for seven days when you don't have an International driving license for motorbikes (A). When you say you will leave Mui Ne within a few days, they say you can also choose to pay ONE MILLION dong and continue your way to the white sand dunes. I asked what if there is more police. They say that there are no more police. After you paid they write down your name and day of birth so that you don't have to pay again on your way back. Note that they won't be nice to you and really try to scare you. The white sand dunes are OK. In Holland whe have better ones. The quads are ruining the silence. I wouldn't go there again. I only recommend to go if it is included in a tour you booked.

Nature destroyed by man Absolutely ruined by quad bikes. There is not one part of the dunes you can stand on without a very LOUD quad zooming past. Definitely not peaceful or relaxing. Plus theres bits of rubbish in the sand. Why can't they allocate an area for quads and a separate area for walkers?!

These are just sand dunes. I guess it could be fun if you have never driven or seen sand dunes before but where I'm from we have loads of better dunes. The scooter ride out there is quite pleasant but even that is ruined by the local police collecting taxes in a fairly aggressive way.

Nice views, but ruined by the quad bikes and jeeps. We arrived at 5am, and were the first ones there, but by 5.30am the tranquility was spoiled by the deafening rumble of quad bikes and jeeps. You can get to nice vantage points without having to pay for quad bikes or jeeps, so would suggest this option. Still worth seeing when you pass through Mui Ne
Collected by 4x4 jeep and taken to sand dunes,on arrival informed our jeep can't go in to dunes,need to hire jeep or quad bike at three times the price of trip
As it names said, it is a white dunes formation. Nothing more than that. You have the possibility to rent a jeep and cross them but I don't see the point as they are not so big.

They ask me for 1,000,000 of 1 hour rent of the herb with the driver to take us around anywhere we want. It end up that he just took us only 2 stop one near the top of the dune and the other close to the lake. Then took us back. The total time spending us just 40 mins. I would say better you go to the red sand dune instead. Walk there further until you are out of the crowded tourists, the facing almost the same atmosphere as you can get in the white sand dune without any noisy sound of the Jeeps and the bikes. Also it is better for the kids. All in all, I would not suggest to go there, the white sand dune, if you don't want to get anything so messy. Go the red sand dune instead

This was included in a tour for us and it was ok, it was sand.. but if your in to touristy things involving old 4 wheel motorcycles and jeeps then your in the right place. This was not for us, try the real thing in africa or in the middle east...

This is my second trip here, my first being 7 years ago have to say I enjoyed the 1st way better, mainly because they didn't have those annoying bikes polluting and almost blatantly trying to run people who don't use the service off the sand dunes, without doubt a disaster is waiting to happen, especially if you have small kids, it's a real shame, cos the dunes are really cool to enjoy and a leisurely hike early or as the sun sets ,is the way to go.its just unfortunate, it's being ruined .

So we booked this tour with a friend to see the sunrise at the white sand dunes because we saw amazing pictures like this Sahara desert (and 3 others places included in the tour) So we arrive around 4:30am at the bottom of the dunes and we had to pay 12$ more to go on a quad with the driver to go up to the dunes and then see the sunrise! Theses drivers were really not nice , we dicided to just walk a bit and then see the sunrise ! So we've never been to this top but it was full of tourist and there is just one top..

We booked a jeep tour with our hotel. We were extremely surprised on arrival to the white sand dunes because we had to pay extra 200,000 VND per person ($ 10) as we had already paid for the tour! It's a scam organized by local tourism professionals. So if you want to do this kind of trip, inquire well and do not hesitate to pay a little more to avoid unpleasant surprises.

We stopped here as part of a day tour - the price to rent a quad was excessive! Even if we were to split it between 4 of us, on a traveler's budget we couldn't afford it! As we were 'stuck' here for over an hour, we went for a short walk - it was very hot and we were worried about getting lost and disoriented so headed back rather quickly, and even then struggled with direction. 1 guy from our tour got lost and it took a lot of people and time to find him! Really wouldn't recommend going for a walk without a guide, but if you do, make sure you have plenty of water and let someone know you are heading out first!! That said, the dunes were very picturesque and there is a small shelter to buy water from near the road.
Good place but people are not good This place is nice but people who take care of this place are only hungry for money and cheat tourist by taking big money and don't serve for paid value .

Do not rent a motorbike to take out to the White Sand Dunes. About 1 km after you leave Mui Ne there are corrupt police sitting along the road who pull you over and ask for an International Driver's License. If you don't have one (no one does) they impound your motorbike until you pay them a bribe. We eventually hired a car to get to the dunes and it was mehh with rather pricey ATV rentals. It is the highlight of Mui Ne which should tell you don't waste your time coming to this town.

We went to the white sand dunes as part of a tour with our hostel. The sand dunes are only good if you hire out the big quads as the small ones don't go up the hills and get stuck in the sand a lot. There are a lot of tourists here so it's hard to get a good photo. I wouldn't waste you're money on this.

As per some of the other reviews, the quad operators at the sand dunes are thugs. Watch your personal gear and don't give them a tip as the prices are already outrageous. For 15-20mins on the quad, you get the operator coming with you. We were under the impression, we would ride our own. Operator gave an exhilarating ride (we didn't really want that) & they were all rude and pushy. After paying the 600k odd dong, he had his hand out and wanted another 100k as a tip. Gave him 50k and operator laughed and said a few words that weren't very nice in Vietnamese. We should've grabbed that money back as he could GFY. Don't recommend them at all. Just pay the minimum and walk up the dunes yourself, much more fun and in your own time. Don't recommend using them at all. Sand dunes are amazing though!

We thought that the quads would be on a different spot then where you can walk but that's not the case. If you don't want to ride the quad and just want to walk the dunes, be aware that you are walking in a quad trail. They don't watch out so you need to be careful. We even saw children riding the quad... Because of the quads riding everywhere you cannot enjoy the view, it should be peaceful and quiet, which it isn't. Maybe it is only worth it if you really want to do ride the quads, otherwise it is a waste of time.

The view is quite nice but I feel there is nothing special about it. The desert is quite small and basically you just walking around it. Beware of the overpriced motorbike service that offer to you for reaching the middle part of the desert. It's only 300m and they charge you for a whoops 8 dollar for one way trip and no return. You have to walk back by yourself. It's better that you walk by yourself since it can be reached easily.

be careful of the people offering you a ride on the quad bike unless you don't mind paying for a stunt we went to sand dunes and rented ourselves one quad bike. we ride to the top of the sand dunes and were asked to come down from the bike by the people who rented us the bikes. they asked one of us to hop on to the bike and said they will bring us to the top as it's pretty steep. i hopped on the bike and the next thing I know the man were doing all sorts of stunts on the bike. they drove to the top and rushed down the hill and zig zag crossed the sand dunes. it took around 5 minutes to finish everything and I thought it was thrilling enough and part of the fees we paid for the bike (300k VND). ended up they forced us to pay a fixed fee by performing those stunts to us. I feel really ripped off because it wasn't even my own will to allow them to perform such stunts. what's more it can be pretty dangerous. it was distasteful to be forced to pay a fee around 100k VND. the bikes were really expensive too for a limited time of 20 minutes

Didn't really think this was worth the trip out here! Expensive ride on 4 wheeler 400 000 for husband and daughter for what sensed like 10 mins. Seemed to have a misunderstanding about time! We didn't want to argue so just left! They rude! Wouldn't recommend ! And not very interesting! But it's a big deal for locals!

Paid (very little) for a dawn tour of the White Sand Dunes, Fairy Stream and Mui Ne Fishing beach. On arriving at the dunes (after our insane jeep driver nearly carried out a suicide pact). Once there we were confronted with a row of quad bikes being rented for the sum of 400,000VD, which was 4 times the amount of the entire tour. It was that or walking up the top of dunes about 500m away. None of the 7 or 8 jeep tours had anyone guiding the people in them, we were just expected to pick one the huge sand crests and walk up it. The dunes themselves are tall crests of smooth sand, but they are swarming with people and the aforementioned rattle of quad bikes. The view was not that great either.
We booked a sunrise tour. The sunrise was unfortunately lame, dunes were not very white, garbage was in an abundance and the potentially serene place was flooded with ATV exhaust and loud engines...

Dangerous!! Stay away from quad bike renting crooks If you consider going there from Mui ne take a cab. A 40-min ride there and back including 1 hour waiting time was 500 dong or 22 dollars. The dunes are interesting for those who has never seen desert. From the top you can take some nice pics with white and red sands and sea in the background. There are strange crooks who want to rent you a quad bike for 40 dollars for 20 min. Those who have ever tried quad biking on dunes know how cool it is if you have a good bike. So, the temptation was big to try it. A massive mistake that nearly spoiled the whole trip. After you pay and is about to leave you figure out that one of those crooks wants to stay with you on the bike. After you ask them to leave they try to threaten you with some additional payments needed if something breaks down)))). And guess what.. They indeed break down after a couple of minutes. After you return the broken quad they refuse to give you the new one. I had to shout on them and threaten them to get a new quad that lost its chain after 5 minutes... The quads are manipulated the way that they stop going after a while (eg no cooling liquid, loose chain etc) to ask you for more money. This was one of the ugliest experiences in Vietnam. Shame on Mui me tourism authorities for tolerating this scam.
Not a peaceful sunrise If you are thinking of a peaceful sunrise at the white sand dunes, forget it. So many loud, annoying quad bikes riding round.

not good if you're not into squats for people who are looking for nature and silence it is not the best place to go. There are many squats passing by and making a lot of noise. Too many tourists and not at all an idyllic place to be. Entrance fee of 10.000.- If you are into driving on the sanddunes, it is a nice place to spent time.
the sand dunes were lovely but the ATV zooming past you is off putting. they should have a dedicated area for people who just want to walk and take a few photos.

Went to check out the dunes, view and just switch it up... The view was great when we were there! That being said it is overpriced for what you get, the 4 wheelers operating conditions are less than desirable. We opted out of the ostrich rides, not really into it. An overall, Meh

Driving a quad on sand was something that i really wanted to do since i got to asia but im bitterly disappointed the way that foreigners are treated as if we are gold merchants.just because we are travelling doesnt mean that we are rich.400k dong for 2 people for 30mins not bad but then a guy who helped us out of the sand and gave us an exhilirating ride down d sand dunes demanded 100k each.i was stunned and should have haggled but just paid up.its unsafe and d quads kept breaking down.wouldnt recommend it!!

We went there for sunrise, which was a really good time since there weren't a lot people, the scenery was beautiful and the sand was comfortable to walk in and oh boy we needed that... We rented a quad bike for 400.000 Dong which was underpowered compared to those the "company" was using. When it couldn't get up the sand dunes they offered to drive it up for us. They expected a tip of 100.000 Dong. Once we got down from the dunes the quad bike's chain broke and we had to walk back. Get there a bit earlier and take the walk up on the sand dunes and I'm sure you will have a good time there and DON'T rent the quad bikes if you don't want to be scamed.

There isnt much to do in Mui Ne unless you eat seafood, kite surf or sun bathe. Hence there is a bit of a feeling that you should do something and the 4 attractions jeep ride seems to be it. Its pretty cheap at around $6US each in a group of 4. It starts with the Fairy Spring and as it is first, the obligation level to participate is high. I read other reviews that said skip it and I agree. Its a walk down a smelly creek which is littered with rubbish. 30 years ago it may have been a nice stroll but not now. There are some interesting cliff type faces where the stream has cut through but that doesnt balance the equation in the positive. There is also an ostrich riding place ( just in case you didnt realise that the locals are 100% into exploitation ) and a small private 'zoo' where poor animals are inappropriately housed . You can enter for free if you buy a drink. Admittedly the kids did like it but they are kids. If you get this far about 50m more will see it out. Then the fishing village, a great picture opportunity looking over the bay at the countless boats which bring in Mui Ne's seafood. Then a pleasant drive along the coast to the whitish 'Sahara' like dunes - not a vast expanse - interesting for a photo but busy and the place is over run with noisy quad bikes - which clearly some with lots of $ enjoy. Then the red dunes - same same but smaller and local kids want $ for toboggans which clearly dont provide the practical amusement that they potentially would appear to. You may be here for sunset which is meant to add something. Home to the pool and a beer and recollections of an Australian beach.

Petrol pump has not been reset, it displayed 86k. We ordered fuel for 40k. After filling our Motorbike it displayed 109k. Woman asked for 40k. "Money" she cried. We didn't pay. Then she tried to take the key away from our motorbike. Her husband came, cried and threatened us with a long stick. We payed to avoid worse at the end. So better keep away from the lonley petrol station on the way to the dunes.

Driver late so missed sunrise We booked a trip to the dunes with a local tour operator on the high street. The next morning we waited nearly 45mins for our driver to turn up, including a call to the tour office (this is at 4.30am!!) When our jeep arrived there weren't enough proper seats so we were put on some fold down seats in the boot which had no back rest - leading to a bruised back from bouncing against the metal jeep. Needless to say the delay meant we missed the sunrise. When we arrived the dunes were far from peaceful with quad bikes racing up and down them, forcing people walking to jump out of the way. The dunes themselves are nice but small and not very impressive. Interesting that they are in Vietnam but not worth the effort.
Not very special, go up there if you are in the area and walk aroudn a bit. Not too much to do there.

Dunes gorgeous, ATV scam not so much The highway robbery that is happening around these beautiful sand dunes is sad. You aren't given enough time if you are on a jeep tour to walk to the dunes, so you're pretty much forced to rent an ATV or pay someone to take you to the top as I did. Others in my group got machines that either didn't have enough power to get to the top or broke down completely. It left a bad taste in everyone's mouth.
We have dunes in Belgium too, so this was not a special experience. You have to pay for a piece plastic trash to slide of the dunes???

The dunes themselves are alright but there are so many tourists on quad bikes and jeeps it's not very relaxing. We hired a quad bike and it had a flat tire and a busted throttle - took it back to get a refund and they wanted to argue with us for about 10 minutes. Pretty poorly run operation and a massive rip off.

We booked a tour and it stated that it is included for the buggy. Apparently when we arrived it was not we have to pay extra plus those kids will bug u for tips and they ask for very high tips!! So make sure u tell them that u want to ride it on ur own. If they ride with u , u have to pay lotsa tips!

Went here as part of a Jeep tour organised by the hotel for only $6usd. We left at 4am to see the sunrise for the dunes. We were under the impression as it was a jeep tour that they would drive us on the dunes however they stop just short of them and you must walk up. If you have seen sand dunes before than this probably isn't for you because they weren't anything special. I am really unsure as to how this made it as the top thing to do in Mui Ne. My advice is your time is better spent enjoying the beach or the poolside of your hotel.

view was not bad but... i recommand traveler would better enjoy only view. im saying about 4wd motobike. when you have some problem, staffs will help you and many of them will ask much tip. you need abuot 300,000 vnd (i don't remember exactly) for riding motobike for 30 minutes. and one staff asked me 20 usd for tip. i felt upset at that time

Came here as part of a trip we came in a private jeep then got charged again to go on the sand dunes 600000 doung to hire a another jeep the same. Check with your tour firm as you can't really just walk up a sand dune
There's nothing to do in Mui ne besides the beach and some extreme sports. The dunes are nothing spectacular and are only passed off as a tourist site because there's nothing else to see.

Sunrise in the ear-splitting noise The White Dunes themselves are beautiful. Probably all tour companies (you find them in every coner in Mui Ne) make trips early in the morning to reach sunrise or in the afternoon to see sunset in Wite Dunes. We took first one. Started 4.15 a.m. from Mui Ne hotel. When arrived, there were already lot of other sun catchers. Local service providers offered ATV for rent. They were like a little surprized when we rejected. As a result of this service was impossible niose all the time. I´ve never had so sad sun rise, because nature has been sacrificed for tourists. All these dunes are plowed by ATVs.

Can be missed - and beware of police racket!! Maybe it was our expectations, maybe the season, maybe our previous experiences; but overall Mui Ne was a big disappointment and totally expendable. The sand dunes were just OK, not a bad excuse for a motorbike trip in some nice scenery. But if you have been in deserts before don't expect anything else. The biggest problem however is the police corruption in the area. They have systematic "controls" exclusively to stop foreigners and extort money from them if they don't carry an international drivers licence. When we were stopped we saw maybe 7 or 8 other motos and during our trip we learnt of many other travellers with the same problem. One of the policemen, with glasses, frightened us until we paid 400.000 dongs. After that it was fine to keep on driving! And we couldn't even keep a copy of the fine -or picture it! Just keep in mind that they know many foreigners take this route and will pocket your money

Overprice ATV and problem We were in a group to the white sand dunes, but everybody paid different price. You need to negotiate about the price. The guy was tried to offer us 300,000 VND for 1 ATV, but in the end we paid 300, 000VND for sharing one ATV, some people paid for 400,000VND We wasted our time there and didnt see much, because the ATV always get stuck and we took time to push it up. If possible, you could hire a guy with you and let them drive you around

It's dangerous and it's a rip-off The white dunes are quite far from the city. Once you (finally) get there, you have 3 options. 1) Walking around - not advisable. I tried to walk, and my youngest son almost got crashed by a buggy (driven by a "professional driver" coming at full speed towards us). Nobody walks, it's too dangerous. 2) Renting a buggy. There are 2 sorts, one more powerful and one less powerful. Expect to pay around 600.000VND for a short time. Nothing, absolutely nothing, is asked when you rent a buggy. You can be unable to ride a bicycle, that you would still get your buggy (with no helmet of course). Now, if already the "professional" drivers are dangerous (see point 1), you can imagine how it is to be surrounded by unexperienced drivers who have no clue on how to deal with dunes. 3) Renting a jeep. You cannot drive a jeep, you have your driver. The price is ridiculous (800.000VND for the small jeep, 1 million for the big, total of around 20 minutes ride), and it's not because you have a driver that you're safe. Right after our arrival, there was an accident with a jeep driven by one of the assigned drivers. And the company still required the client to pay the 800.000VND. I don't know how it ended, but there were quite loud screams for a very long time. So be advised. And don't go with kids.

We visited both the red and the white dunes. The white dunes were maybe worth visiting but the red ones are NOT! We took a private tour. We even paid for the quad for 20 minutes. On arrival they try to charge us for the quad, that we already paid to the tour operator, when we firmly told them that we already had paid they sent a boy to come on the quad with us. So we were 4 persons on a quad. My husband, my daughter, the boy and I???! When we arrived half way into the dunes he told us to stop and take pics. We did. We said we wanted to go further up to see the sunrise, as it wasnt visible from our location, he said no. My husband was really dissapointed, we tried to explain that we paid to see the sunrise. All he said was that we had to go to the end of the dunes to take pictures of the lake??? We did not wake up at 3:30 am to see a lake. We want to see the sunrise on the dunes. There was no way he was going to let us up the dunes where all the other jeeps and quads were. We paid 200000dong for the quad.... for him to ruin everything. I saw other couples drivinf alone on the dunes. I dont know why he came along. Before going back to our jeep he stopped and said that he wanted 100000d-tip; he kept on saying that he wanted tip. We were so annoyed. Not only we didnt see the sunset but he ruined the whole experience. Mui Ne was a big dissapointment. If we had known that we would have skipped this place from our itineray! Glad we saw the dunes but if i knew it i would have skipped Mui Ne from our itinerary. Not worth it all unless you practice water sports.

The sunrise is NOT over the dunes Seriously, there are a few dunes and sitting on them looking at the sunrise over a distant city is NOT a sunrise over the dunes. It could have been okey though if the entire experience wasn't destroyed by noisy four wheelers. A tip for the people in charge: let everyone see the sunrise first, then play around with the wheelers... Also they don't let you walk out until the sun has started rising already as they want you to feel forced to rent a wheeler. Start walking earlier and you don't need to waste money on crappy four wheelers. I would never recommend anyone to waste money until they start taking care of the visitors. However, for most this is one of the things you feel like you have to do while you're there, even if it's bad. Rent a bike and go on your own if you really have to see a few sand dunes..

The combination of the dunes and the lake are actually quite spectacular BUT the quad drive was disappointing. You can only drive a short while yourself, the rest is done by a driver, meaning you can sit on the metal frame when he is doing silly moves in bumpy dunes... My wife still suffers from back pain because of this... The same quad drivers were annoying; asking for tips before you have returned . I would not do this again,

The view is average, but they try to get your money by offering quads from their little shed to the dunes. (Pretty expensive and the only way to get there if you font want to walk a kilometre in hot sand)

Low speed sand sledding on cardboard boxes A popular local attraction for fishing and sand sledding. Apparently the sunrise is spectacular, but I wouldn't imagine it could be that great. Not allowed to swim in the lake but fishing is allowed!! Don't expect too much and it's over priced for what it is.

Beautiful but again not good service The place is really beautiful. Unfortunately the service is bad. The Quad Bike with the guys in orange shirt for bad example. The prices are extremely expensive: 400.000 VND for a Quad Bike which is so slow & can not go up to the sand hills, then they asked us for more money to change another one, which is 600.000 VND. All prices are just for 20 min, just enough to for u to go up to the hill, take some photo and go back. The guys in Orange are unfriendly & complain us the whole time. Why should we pay for that?

Yes, the dunes are absolutely beautiful. BUT, we have gone on the dune trip in a jeep for good access. The 4hrs trip to Fairy Stream & White and Red Dunes was a great value at US 25. To our surprise our jeep only brought us to the foot of the dune, some 500-600 meters from the first top. From there we were told to either walk (on scorching hot sand with 2 kids) or take 2 ATV ($35 for 20 min ride) for or another jeep (exactly as the one we arrived on for $30 for 40 min) to get to and around the dunes. Given the fact that we only had 40-50 min to spend there we went (unhappily) for the jeep. It turned out that the jeep was not planning to spend 40 min driving us through the dunes but we were supposed to choose two spots where it would bring us and allow 20 min at each one of them for taking pictures. We we pointed out third spot where we wanted to go the driver was extremely unhappy.... and to be honest - so were we.
If you have never seen sand dunes this could be interesting. I would just stick to the beautiful beaches.

Beautiful but far and can be a tourist trap Interesting sight but it took a good 2 hours from Ke Ga nay and the the infamous ATVs can be dicey. We hired 2 ATVs at VND300k per ATV and promised no time limit but after going up and down twice they refused to go any further! This is even after one of the ATVs broke down and had to be towed back.

Cool to look at, ruined by quad bikes Travelled to dunes by taxi, felt like the taxi driver might have been getting commission as he took us straight to a guy who tried to rent us a quad bike. Whilst trying to say no I was crabbed by the ankle by a monkey who was chained to a post. The quad bike guy wasn't too happy that we wouldn't rent one and shouted 'they have no money' in English to whoever could understand. The quads are very noisy and speed all over the dunes in a crazy manner. Like the red dunes these make good photos but I think we only spent 30 mins or so on the dunes themselves. A shop offers ice cream and drinks (not a bad price for tourist prices). The whole family watch TV there and a selection of dogs and puppies roamed the place, as I watched the puppies sleep in the shade a child picked up one oif the sleeping pups and threw it towards another whilst growling at them to encourage them to fight. Luckily it ended in the puppies just playing with each other after a few nips.

We were one of the first cars there, and had 10 fantastic minutes there with a beautiful nature, and unspoiled sand. A great quite photo opportunity. But after 10 minutes all the other jeeps arrived and we could hear all the ATV and motorcycles started up. 1 minute after we were in the centre of 20-25 very noisy bikes who wasn't there for the nature, but just to have fun on ATV. The sand was after 5 minutes spoiled by wheel tracks. Call me old (26) but I think it's a gigantic shame that so beautiful nature turns into a Russian playground. I can only recommend to go there early, maybe ask the driver to go there first, and take the springs after the dunes, or just not support the gasoline circus.

I see from the reviews that most people seem to have enjoyed this place, but I am afraid I don't share their admiration. I paid the money to get in, plus I forked out 300,000 for the quad bike only to be told I had to ride pillion because I was not experienced - how did they figure that out, I protested that at home I have an Aprilia 650 plus a Honda trail bike - the driver took me to the top of the largest sand dune, then to another more isolated hill where he demanded 100,000 tip before he would return me, then when I refused he switched it off and said it was broken and I had to walk back. Finally, I relented and gave him 50,000 and he delivered me back 10 minutes into my 20 minutes I had paid for. Having been to a few deserts I didn't find the scenery too impressive nor had anybody bothered to clean up the plastic bags and other litter. I left this scene feeling really ripped off and disgruntled.

We made the mistake of paying for a group jeep tour to the sand dunes. It ended up being a waste of about 4 ours with a driver who was rude, didn't speak English, and simply laughed at us as he knew we were getting ripped off. If you go to the dunes, make sure to go on your own, the tours are a straight up rip off.

I have to admit I'm a little bit spoiled as I know the desert in Abu Dhabi and Dubai, but also if I wouldn't I would say that these dunes are nothing too special. I cycled all the way from Mui Ne to the dunes and then locked my bike at a tree 20 meters next to the first shop on the right side of the street. When I returned from the dunes, a vietnamese woman was coming out of her shop yelling at me. As I wasn't willing to pay money, for locking my bike at a tree that wasn't even nowhere near her shop I denied. That made her even more angry. After one minute of screaming and several attempts of me to just drive of (stopped by her as she was holding the bike), we had drawn some attention to us. Other women form different stores tried to calm her down but she was just raging. Finally there was a little inattentive moment of her, and I slipped away. Advice for you guys: Don't lock or park you bike on the side of the street where the shops are. On the other side there is enough space, too. Also be aware of the little kids with the boards. I've seen some trying to steal money from their "customers" while they where skimming through their money.

We look forward to an exciting day in the White Sand Dunes. We lay by Ham Tien, and the trip by taxi took about 45 minutes one way. We arranged everything alone, without organized excursion. As White Sand is far away from everything else, we asked the taxi driver to wait for us. The taxi ride cost USD 60, - including hold. When you arrive at The White Sand Dunes, are all ready to cash in on tourists. You will be directed straight to someone who rents ATV with driver. This costs USD 15 per person for 30 minutes. You drive out to The Sand Dunes, and will try to slide down on the sand. When you're done with the ATV driving, you get a clear message about paying tips. Are you among those who like to ride an ostrich, you have the opportunity to do so as well. But it also costs a few dollars.

Experience ruined by the noise and smell of quad bikes The dunes are worth seeing, but if you are not into quad biking, the noise and the smell of fuel these quads create, then rent a moto and go and see it yourself during the day before the organised tours get there (2-3pm) Directions are in earlier post. There is a small entrance fee of vnd10k/pers.

Here is what you are in for: You find a tour operator that tells you for 150,000 Dong ($7) they will take you to the sand dunes, but then when you get there, you will pay another 20,000 ($1) on top of it. Then they usher you to a horde of people trying to sell you barely operating quads for about 250,000-300,000 Dong ($13-$15) when there is another place just down to the left that sells them for $4-$5 less. Basically, the white sand dunes are a money grab for sneaky business owners. If you go there, just enjoy walking around the dunes, they are worth seeing and running down, just ignore all the quad-hawkers.

We went here as part of the half day jeep tour. The tour operator neglected to advise us that there was a small entry fee ($1/2 people). In addition, when you get there they neglected to tell us that you can ride on a quad bike on the dunes for an additional fee (about $25/half hour). Since we were not aware of this we didn't have enough money for the quad bike (which looked like a blast, if a bit on the pricey side). We walked up the dunes and watched others having some fun. I am guessing the white dunes are about 50 km from Mui Ne.

After a 40 min drive you get dumped in the middle of nowhere in the dark. no one tells you how far to walk, or in which direction, so you just follow others. Nothing much to see but a few dunes (might be nice if the sunrise is great).
