Locals try to get you to rent a quad instead of walking a few hundred meters. lots of noise due to tourists racing around with the quads.

Long way to travel to see dunes when there are dunes everywhere in the area. The dunes are stunning with contrasting white & orange sands, however our guide hardly spoke English & offered no explanation as to why there were differences in the sand colour. The dune 'ride' was on a cheap piece of plastic matting and after one trip down was not very exciting - Much better to spend time in the area on other trips and mixing with the very friendly locals

it was raining when we got there. our driver asked us to buy a raincoat for 10T dong each. when we were about to pay, the manager said it was 15T dong. even the lady selling the raincoat was surprised. there were 4 of us and he settled for 50T dong for the 4 raincoats. The rates for the ATV were different as well it was 400T dong for two persons instead of the 320T dong from the blogs that we saw. Not only that, before we we drove back. Our drivers asked for a tip and was asking for 100T dong from each of us. They wanted us to give them the tip in the middle of the sand dunes so that their boss wont see them. We didnt know what to do but we were able to settle with 200T dong to be split for the both of them. it was suppose to be a fun experience until we were extorted into giving more money. i hope someone can build a better company to provide a more honest service in the future.

My sunrise tour was supposed to start at 4.30am but I had to wait until 5.30am when the other tour passengers showed up. The guide had no English and could not communicate why or how long the delay was for. Missed the sunrise & instead had sweltering heat - the avoidance of which was the entire reason for the early start. Very frustrating trip, would not recommend it.

Hired a bike and drove down here. Fun trip but what was there was not very interesting. The quad bikes were ridiculously expensive (we didn't pay). The red dunes were better and much easier to get to.

This stop was part of a varied day out, but the cafe owners had two monkeys chained up, that kept desperately trying to bite themselves free, which was distressing (mostly to the English - make of that what you will). The quad bikes available to hire kept breaking down and appeared of dubious quality and safety. Landscape is interesting, but I would not recommend this visit.

Don't waste your time or money Every where they take you is a place where you are going to be conned out of your hard earned cash. Fairy stream is we're kids will try to guide you around then ask for money , ours asked for 200k dong (about £6, not much I hear you say) that can rent you a room in ho chi min city! White dunes is where you will be convinced to rent a quad bike , sounds like fun until you have paid and then realised the gutless wonders can't get up the dunes, so child staff get you going again and ride the bikes for you, even though you don't want them to and then ask for dollars. The dunes are just a beach with no sea!
If you like Sandunes.. Honestly, I seriously wouldn't bother, big piles of sand by a lake.
Trip was only 2 hours instead of 4. Dunes were nice but with lots of people. Driver spoke almost no English. Picked up 45 minutes late.
where has all the sand gone? years ago there were seriously huge tough to climb dunes; now the place is nearly level

Sand dunes too far from Saigon city, nothing fantastic about it, the sand hill not tall/high enough to slide down, and it is so slow to do it. Play ground behind my house can go faster. Beach was so so unless u likes fishing village. Sea water was not very clear. Too many hotel / resort for too few tourist.

not worth it, no 4WD and animal cruelty for all the tourists to see we went on a tour to these dunes and the red dunes. total waste of time. there is lots of rubbish that is buried in the sand and then gets uncovered in the wind. there are lots of kids trying to sell you things (who should be at school). there is nothing to look at and i never saw 1 person manage to wizz down the dune on the board successfully. you go in a jeep but you do not do 4WD. in fact you only go about 10 metres further on the sand than those in cars or bikes. the jeep we went in was definately not roadworthy. plus they have this area geared up for touristy with a chained monkey doing tricks, a couple of obviously crazy caged squirrel that did not stop doing laps round the cage and all the touristy trickets at raised prices. The animals really upset me and I think it is not worth the hassle

Avoid the kids will try to rob you We got to the sandunes around 12 very hot but not to many visitors around so nice and quite. Our tour guide droped us off and then he headed to the resturant to wait our arrival back from the dunes. On the way a kid came and followed us up the dunes with 2 plastic sheets and offered us to slide down the dunes, we did this for 5 minutes took some photos then made our way back halfway back in the middle of the dunes he stopped and started making money motions in his hand, i was going to give him a tip anyway as he was cute i asked how much and he demanded 200,000 dong each i laughed at him and he kept saying you pay now with an aggressive look in his eyes, i told him we was on the dunes for 5 mninutes and slid down them twwice there was no way i was going to pay that amount, he was still demanding this money ( he was only tweleve but if you are in a group of women this could be very frightning) i was ready to slap this kid but didnt i told him i will speak to our tour guided to see how much it should be the tour guide said 20,000 dong for the two of us i payed the little runt thisand he had a look on his face as if hje knew he had been caught out on trying to rob us. To be honest the sledging down the dunes is not really that great any way you get so sandy and only last 2 seconds as the slopes are not steep at all. Overall the dunes are average the kids ruin it you just have to tell them you are not prepared to pay them and also be prepared to hit them if they get frisky !!!!

The visit to the white sand dunes can be pleasant, and it can also be a disaster. The sights and scenes aren't that majestic, but they are definitely something out of the norm - a tiny desert in the middle of a tropical country. Having visited many other attractions around Vietnam, I must say that the ones in Mui Ne have the most irritating hustlers I've ever seen. In some cases, they offer their "services" without your acceptance, and demand a ludicrous sum for it. In others, they over-promise before you purchase their services and later fail to deliver what you had agreed upon. My worst experience was the horse-ride that a bunch of kids will offer you as you proceed to the sand dunes. They will promise you anything for taking their horse for a ride in a desert for 100,000 VND. You are supposedly promised to be able to travel anywhere in the desert on it. After mounting the horse, they would then ignore any of your requests, and take you on the horse through a designated path, which was a short 3 mins ride halfway to the sand dunes. They will then tell you that the horse can't go any further and demand 100,000 VND right there. After which, I had to travel deeper into the dunes by foot to the middle of the dunes where the sights are much nicer. On my way back out, the horse-riding kids demanded another 100,000 VND to take me back to the entrace, which I refused to pay since a total of 100,000 VND was the original price we agreed upon. They became hostile and threatened me with violence. One of them actually took out his belt and wrapped it around his fists, swinging the metal buckle as if ready to challenge me. The same kid then stuck a cigarette in his mouth, trying to look tough. Along the way, another kid told me to pay up or the "gang" out there by the entrance of the desert will beat me up. As I rode the horse back to the entrance, seven kids then stood in front of me, and they then surrounded me in an aggressive stance, waiting for the command from their "boss" kid to have me beaten up. I could see the look in their eyes that they were ready to pounce on me at any moment. In the end, the "boss" came up to me and I explained the situation to him. After some exchange of words, he decided to let me go. In a nutshell, unless you are willing to bow to the whim and fancy of these little kids, paying them whatever they demand, STAY AWAY FROM THEM AND DO NOT ACCEPT ANYTHING THEY HAVE TO OFFER. Shoo them away if they follow you and offer to be your guide. This single experience spoiled my entire impression of Mui Ne. It's just sad that the tourist attractions there are so unregulated and exploited by the locals. In fact, it isn't only about the money. If these kids could offer services that value add to my experience at these attractions, I wouldn't mind paying more than they ask. The fact that they do so little and demand more than what it's worth is completely upsetting.

Too many windmills in this area This is an attraction that is over the top, ride with a quad cannot independently cost pp 500,000 dong. Because it is only a few dunes, in Zeeland they are higher, you are driven around a kind of off-road area with windmills. Before sunrise or sunset, those windmills are right there to take beautiful pictures! Real horizon pollution. Not really recommended but if you have nothing to do it is. Value for money is too much for what is offered. Automatically translated

During a tour from Mui Ne before going to the Fisherman's Village, we had to see the White Dunes, which are located in Bau Trang 23 km from the city, beautiful dunes of soft sand ranging from white to pink, Gold constantly changing, a mini Sahara constantly sculpted by the wind. You can go for a ride and step on these mounds of sand or go on a Quad or Jeep tour, they are very popular during sunrise or sunset, the colors and shape change according to the light of day, many let themselves slide with the toboggan.... At the entrance there are dining options , and many jeeps waiting for visitors, the visit to the Dunes , are often combined with the visit of the Fisherman's Village, at the source to the Tower . Automatically translated
too bad the service is not good need to fix all the staff too ignore the customer's words , had a boring trip
When we went it was a holiday time and there had been many people on the dunes, there was tracks and footprints everywhere. I am sure they would be nicer when not so busy
Ripped off and rude staff Its a tourist trap if you want to rent a quad...40$ for 20min and if tou negotiate the staff tell u to walk if tou are not rich ! AVOID nhu Trung-Đồi Cát Trắng company !!!!

I like the place...so nice. That wild atv ride in the sands is the most exciting one...you have to hold tight. But i have a disappointment with one of the staff. He speak a bad words to us in their local language when we change our mind not to use the jeep but instead an ATV...and not knowing that one of my friend can understand what he said.

We rented 2 four wheelers for 20 minutes, they charged me 70$. My friends rented the same things at a place 2 minutes walk from where we rented from at the same time for 55$. The guy goes with you sitting on the side of the four wheeler so you can’t really do that many fun things.

Price of transport to top was a surprise We had a Jeep Safari and a visit was included. We did not realise that there was additional cost to go up to the hill. The cost was not so big, but we where not prepared and we did not have cash enough for all options. Information from tour arrangers would be appreciated.

Overall, it was worth the experience. We went and saw the sunrise & got a quad bike to get to the top of the dunes. Whilst the bike was expensive (300,000 dong) & the dunes were in walking distance, it was actually super fun. However, be warned, it was very touristy.
You have to pay 200,000 dongs if you want a quad. We went in a tour and we didn't expect it was so expensive. We walked it up in 15 minutes

Worth a visit if you’re in the area On our trip we stopped at Mui Ne for one night just because we heard it was worth it for the sand dunes. If we repeated our trip I’d probably skip Mui Ne as I’m not into resorts and lazy beach days and the dunes weren’t as good as I had hoped. The white dunes are better than the red dunes, by far, they are a little further away too but we had a private jeep tour so the rise was fun. We left Mui Ne at 7:30am as we couldn’t bare a 4:30am start just to see a sunrise, we will save that for Halong Bay. This seems to be a good time to go as when we got there we could only see a handful of people on the crest, but when we left 40 minutes later it was packed! As soon as you get there they will try to sell ATV/quad bikes as it’s “5 kilometre” but this isn’t true so you can walk. We chose to walk to a dune that no one else was on so didn’t go to the “main”part. It was a good experience and well worth it if you are in Mui Ne anyway.

Sand dunes were good fun however they must have changed the policy on allowing solo riding as it appeared to be all accompanied. Many vids on YouTube seem to be solo riding yet these vids were uploaded more than one year ago. The people accompanying on the quad bikes weren’t the nicest and seemed to want you to finish as soon as possible so they can get back to doing nothing and ripping off tourists. MORE IMPORTANTLY, do not travel here on motorbike/moped, take a jeep or taxi as there is a police racket waiting to stop all tourists on motorbike and extort money for apparent speeding or riding outside of city limits. This is on the road just after you leave the red sand dunes. It seems like the police officer who ‘fined’ us appeared to give us discount because we were English. He had to make sure we were not French or American as he would’ve charged more. I’m guessing this is due to events over the last century. Nevertheless, enjoy it but just take the necessary precautions as you may get caught out. This racket/ police scam is mentioned thoroughly by just searching google.

In general, there’re not so much to see in Mui Ne. This is one of the place you can see. I booked a Jeep tour 460.000 VND to reach this place (the other places we visit are fishing village, Red sand dunes, Fairy stream). It took around 50 minutes to reach the White Sand Dunes. When we get there, we got 2 options to reach the top, Jeep and ATV. Because I was with 2 little kids, I took the Jeep. It cost me 800.000 VND. Feels like a rip-off. We could have been walk though, but with the kids is not a go for me. On the top, nothing much. Sand and some litters. Look around took photos, you can try slide down the dunes with some kind of sheet if you want. If you brave enough, the Jeep driver will do some manouvre with you. From there the driver take you to the lake. Took some photos and drove back to the starting point. If you’re on a budget, I’d suggest do your research before go there and rent a Jeep or the ATV. Walk is your best option to reach the top. Early morning is the best time.

Worth a look but not spectacular We went on a Jeep safari and were taken to the bottom of the dune where you can get an ATV ride to the top. They wanted 1 million dong if you took the ATV yourself or 200,000 each to take just go to the top for 15 minutes and bring you back. 2 of us got on the back of a Can Am and the driver took us to the top. It was worth seeing but you don’t need more than 10 minutes at the top. It was quite crowded and some of the ATV’s struggled to make it.

Great views but lots of rubbish Great views across the dunes and to the harbour but lots of rubbish unfortunately. However, on the fun side you can pay to rent a plastic tabogan to slide down the dune.
Nothing special, a big expanse of white sand dunes, you can hire a quad bike for 650000d for 30mins.

Good Fun for a Short Period We were taken here by some local colleagues and hired a quad bike to drive around on. The guides are like a bunch of unruly teenagers but they are harmless!! Overall an ok stop off for an hour or so but I wouldn’t make a special effort to visit it. Saying that, the sunset was good!!!

My husband and i did the half day sunrise sand dune tour which included the white sand dunes for sunrise, the red sand dunes, a fishing village and the fairy stream. We travelled by air conditioned private car which was fab and our tour guide was very knowledgable. Caution for those who love their bed, this trip requires you to get up early - we were picked up from our hotel at 4.30am, however i recommend the sunrise to the sunset as the temperature in the afternoon would be unbearable! We arrived at the white sand dunes not really knowing what was happening. We were ushered on to an ATV and taken to the top of a sand dune where lots of other people were waiting. We watched the sunrise whilst trying to dodge peoples photos and trying to figure out how we were getting down - we were literally dropped off and the ATV man just left! Whilst on the dune, the sunrise was beautofil, but one of the ATV drivers was an absolute creep. He started to take photos of young womens breasts on his phone and calling his friends to take a look. One young lady caught him and asked him to delete the photo - he said he would but failed to, it took her a good 5 minutes and standing over him to make sure he deleted it. It was very unprofessional and very uncomfortable to see. This really soured the mood for us and we didnt really enjoy the rest.of the experience. We were finally collected by an ATV driver and taken for a spin - our driver was very good and took us to a few dunes where nobody else had stopped but we did see plastic bottles in quite a few places which was so sad to see. He even offered to let us drive which was fun. It was all over in a matter of 30 minutes and althought the sunrise was pretty, and the ATVs were fun, the creepy driver really did ruin it. Women please be careful when going and be aware of men taking unwanted photos!

Panoramic white sand dune view is very stunning. Although very hot and sunny weather bur it still a memorable place. However the management of jeep over there is very terrible; the drivers negotiate for higher (than usaul) rental fee.ATV seem to be more exciting but come with very high price.

the experience is so-so although the place is nice, clean compared to most tourist attractions in Vietnam. 1.jeeps used to pick up passengers have no suspension and backseats get all the smoke. As they are only allowed in parking areas, there should be otehr means/better transpo option 2. atv was nice but expensive. Just going up the dunes and a half round and a few minutes of riding cost more than the jeep hired for 4hrs The mini lake with lotuses was a nice unexpected thing
The dunes are beautiful but I was not that happy about all those noisy vechiles driving around. On the other hand I understand that walking in the sand is also challenging

Enormous. Good to come once. Good place to see the sunrise. But you need to get up pretty early like 3am in the morning to get here. No one speak English here so it’s quite difficult to communicate with the local especially when you hv to pay for the ATV. They cost us about 200,000 vietnam dollar per person for round trip. I don’t suggest anyone to walk up to the dune. So, you should pay for the ATV. This place is nice for photograph. And it’s cold in the morning too.

Smaller than expected. If you are in Mui ne, I would go there. But if you are in doubt about Mui ne in general, you could skip it I would say. However, interesting to see the variety of landscapes Vietnam offers

Jeep out there was good. Picked up at 4.30am. Once arrived, another group charged $1M VND for ride in 4wd out onto the dunes. Didn't see that coming. Disappointing result. If you've seen a sunrise and sand dunes before then don't bother. Fishing village on the way back was worth the trip in our opinion. The red sand dunes look more impressive at sunset

we started our tour with picnic jeep tours thinking we would ride the same jeep up the dunes, big surprise not only was the front seat of our jeep to small to hold my 6 foot 3 inch frame my knees rubbed on the dash most of the way to the dunes, we got to the dunes and surprise you either rode two or three on the back of a quad runner or rode in a dune buggy to go up the dunes and of course there was additional charges for either the quad runners or the buggies. We got to the top of the dunes and they had hard plastic sheets to slide down dunes that were at 45 degrees but to get back up you had to use a rope and climb the dunes, not for this 70 year old! A short ride back down the dunes with a little bit of angle driving and small hill and the trip was done.

Nice place,to visit but it is now a tourist trap. The price to hire the jeeps or SUV to go to the top of the dunes was very excessive !! We walked instead and had great fun and got some amazing photos !!

Bit of a tourist trap They are ok. A bit of a tourist trap. First they are quite small. Second you go there on tour and once there they tell you you need to pay more to get a car to bring you at the top. Whatever. I walked. Problem is that tour don’t give you much time to visit so it’s a bit of a rush. Bit overrated. Sunrise was fine. The dune it self , when you find a quiet place, are nice. Just a continuous noise of the engine of the people brought up at the top.
Nice dunes, but the traffic and highway on the dunes is really disturbing. Watch out the police, they fine tourists on the way to the Dunes.

Check the weather before coming Join the tour and book via your hotel reception. Mui Ne Victory Guesthouse is highly recommended for return bus booking 120,000 per person for sleeper bus and the dunes tour at 120,000 dong per person You can walk up there and return in 40 minutes. The bungee cost approx 250,000 per person Its quite a scam considering if say sunrise is at 6.15 am you need to leave the place by 6am. The view is better than the red dunes.. White dunes are better for sunrise, red dunes are better for sunset. Be careful not to get fleece. The tour driver was great just not the management of the dunes people. Pick up time from hotel is at 4.30am. The fishing village and fairy stream are so so too. Take the time and have cheap breakfast along the roadside.

Went by motorbike to the dunes. Is it worth it.. Well. Don't know. But we did and we rented the 4 wheel bike as well. A little dangerous to do because it is difficult to see clearly in the white sand. On our way back the police stopped us. We did not have international license. They told us to buy 1 million vnd to let us go. Not good experience. It was very clear that they were looking for money.

This is a definite must do in mui ne. Great view but we felt we were duped. We bought tickets one a 4 spot tour which included white sand. When we arrived we were told we needed to pay an additional 500 000 dong by the people at the dunes or we would miss the sunrise. If you go just get there early enough it's not a crazy far walk in at all ! Also the police will demand all your dong if your scootering in mui ne.. Just a heads up

Nice sunset but expensive jeep ride Everything is great about the place, the sand dunes, the slide board, the sunset. My family love it especially the little ones. On a downside, the expensive charge for a 2-way up the sand dunes is a turn off.

got there early morning . for a moment i thought it is paradise. great sand dunes... motorbikes and jeeps... and a nice resting place. turns out you pay lots of money for 20 minutes on a bike + driver. jeep tour is also short and only stops at 2 spots for pics. can sand board as well but have to walk a long way. no help to get there from the crew. a little disapointed . still... the dunes are realy nice.

If you've never seen dunes before.. The white dunes are definitely better than the red ones, but nothing spectacular. If you've seen dunes somewhere else you can easily skip this. There's a gang of young men running the business of driving tourist up the dunes with either a jeep or an ATV and they are everything but friendly or kind. Rather rude and intimidating.

Nice to watch sunrise Along with hundreds of other tourists viving for a piece of the sand dune looking for that perfect selfie. ATVs waiting at the entrance to take tourists up the dunes but can easily walk to the top of the dunes within 20-30 minutes....just bring a flashlight. Unfortunately the dunes are not respected by tourists judging by all the garbage left and wonder what the ATVs impact to the environmental. Know it's not positive back in Canada.

Its mainly an outdoor activity. Sight seeing ( sunrise or sunset) ATV riding.To enjoy the activity its suggested that you a lot at-least 3 hours of your time. Bring drinks, ride the ATV ( hire or better yet if you have one bring it and its a good transpo to go around too) Must have: suncreams, water, bring extra clothing incase you need to change shirt after the activity

Good but not reasonable price We went there with lovely friends and decide to took 1 Jeep Car cost 450000 dong per pac then driver took us around feel like roller coaster but safe anywhere with professional driver , i guess haha it’s quite non reasonable price for a few hour for this cost overall good but not reasonable price

The sand dunes themselves were beautiful, I couldn’t fault them. However the whole time we were there it felt like a tourist money grab. We paid $30aud to get driven on ATV to the top of the sand dunes but they just dropped us off and drove away. But of a rip off but it was a lovely sight.

We booked a tour for about 5$ to get there. First we were 6 people in a Jeep for 5. Than we already have to pay there the entrance - just to see the dunes. There we wanted to rent a quad , but they wanted 650 000 dong for each person & the guide is also riding with you. So don’t book a tour. Drive there with taxi & pay the entrance - walk through the dunes & be happy to see some sand dunes in Vietnam.

Once. You get here they will ask you 500000vnd for transport to the sand dunes. Don't. Just come early and walk that 30 minutes. If you don't care about the budget just pay them the money. Otherwise use your feed or haggle the price. It's quite small so it's not worth the ride in my opinion.

Nice but nothing extraordinary. Like most things we saw here it was nice but not extraordinary. We got ripped off with renting a quad that ended up not working and had to walk back to our Jeep. They obviously didn’t want to reimburse anything. Don’t bother renting a quad as the dunes are probably about 20 mins walk.
A bit underwhelming and pricey We found it quite underwhelming and the USD 50 was quite unnecessary to be fair. Unfortunately there is precious little you can otherwise do in Mui Ne.

Beautiful sand dunes - quad bikes a tourist rip off The sand dunes themselves were stunning. Although there were a lot of tourists there so not easy to get pictures without random heads in the background! Despite leaving our hotel at 4am, we still missed the actual sun rising (the main reason the trip was booked in the first place), so get there early!! On arrival there were quad bikes to take you up the sand dunes for the views. It was a LONG way up the sand dune and as the sun was already up we really felt that we HAD to go on the quad bike. We were told that on the bikes we would have 40 minutes. It cost approx £20, which was almost as much as our entire days trip! The ride up the sand dune was great fun and we took the quad back down the dunes at speed which was exhilarating. Unfortunately I would say that in total though we were only on the bike for max 10 minutes so left a bitter taste as did feel ripped off, despite enjoying the experience.
Went in a Jeep, cowboy driver😳, Good value, except extra charge to go up on the white sands on a quad or another Jeep, we did not. Rest of afternoon was interesting. We enjoyed it
Unfortunately when we visited it started to rain so didn't stay long. Was a bit disappointed by the locals who "run" It, ie. Rubbish everywhere.

Once you’ve seen 1 sand dune you’ve seen them all. We did a tour with Mui Ne tourist in Mui Ne, whilst the tour itself was enjoyable we almost missed sunrise as we were picked up late, despite it clearly saying on the booking we had to be outside the hotel at 4:30 am or they would go without us the “jeep” that was actually just someone’s car didn’t arrive to get us until 5am meaning we arrived just after the sun had started coming up which was disappointing as the entire reason we booked the tour was to be there as it went from dark to light and to see the sun rise. When we got there everyone else had arrived in awesome 4x4 classic jeeps which would of added to the experience.
Nice view, but expencive Nice view from the top, but if you want transport it’s gonna cost you. We paid more for 60 sec of atv-transport than a whole day of jeep-rental with driver.

Not as cool as you imagine Too touristy and noisy with people trying to oversell you Safari or quad rides for crazy prices. If you have already been in Dubai desert than you won't bè excited about It. BE AWARE: there are many stories of tourists stopped and asked for money by the local police men. Wear your helmet ALWAYS while driving the motobike and avoid senseless expensive tours by jeep sold by the structures at extremely high rates. 75k is the cost for a scooter per day. Up to you!

Worth a visit as part of the daily tour to see the sunrise. When you get there you can get a ride to the highest dune for 200k and buy food and drinks. Not necessary to go to the top to see a great sunrise and take some cool pictures but view is probably better. Just wasn’t blown away by the experience.

Get a tour out here, shouldn't cost more than 100k VND to get this , Red dunes and Fairy Stream. You can hire a quad for 200k a person to go to the top , it took me 20 minutes to get there. It is hard yakka but i enjoyed the exercise.

Went in the afternoon. Was part of jeep tour we booked. $20 per person for the atv ride was a little disappointing, just a 3 mins ride to the top, some photo time, and then a ride down. Spent probably 10mins at the actual dunes, found the red dunes much better, I think mainly because we didnt feel so ripped off

Did the sand dunes as part of a 4 in one trip from Mu Nei. They are quiet amazing we paid 600 VD for a atv trip to the top of the dunes. It was scary as all get up! My son and husband loved it. I am sure it breaches all kinds of safety regulations.

I joined the local tour and went this desert with my friend yesterday. We both felt very disappointed since we counld not see the sunrise because of windy weather. You must paid around US14 to rent KTV to go to the upper part of desert to see the sunrise. Honestly, the KTV is not safe and teenage driver drove very fast and unsafe.

Great view, along with 4w noise The dunes are beautiful and catching the sunrise is defintetly a nice moment. The place is of great fun if you like riding a quad or a 4w car in the sand. If you hate it or are allergic to the continuous engine noise, consider it twice prior to go there!

Beautiful but a rip off The sand dunes are absolutely stunning and you should definitely go see them, however once there your tour guide will drop you at what I can only describe as a car/quad bike workshop. Once at the workshop you are approached and told your options of getting to the dunes (this is not included on the tour or in the price). You can go on your own quad, on the back of a guides or in a 4x4 it is expensive and they only take you to one sand dune and back. It's a massive rip off (200,000 - 350,000 VND) but unfortunately probably too far to walk so choose what's best for you. Just thought I'd warn so you had enough money etc on the day :)

Very nice, but crowded We came here around 8 am. Already a lot of people there in ATVs and 4x4 which actually disturbs the beauty of the sand dunes. But we went to the smaller dune at the small lake, less people and cars there, so still nice view. Very hot, take strong sunscreen and a hat/ cap. 15k entry fee and 5k for parking (motorbike).
You can take a tour or just jump in a taxi and they will take you there. right on the main road. you can walk up the dunes with hundreds of others

The white dunes are beautiful. More beautiful than the red ones. But it’s way too expensive to go up. When you want to take the squad to the top, costs are 400K (incl driver) for 2 persons. The ride is not more than 2-3 minutes. Feeling a bit ripped off here.

Nice views but very commercialised I wouldn't suggest going on a sunrise tour here, because as it turns out pretty much all tours miss the sunrise and you will have gotten up super early but arrive when the light is out already. You might have seen videos of people sand surfing, but it really isn't that great at all from what I saw. 2 out of 4 people I saw broke their "boards" (which are just made out of thin, soft plastic) on their way down but all four ended up with sand everywhere and couldn't open their eyes the entire time. When you are at the dunes, most people rent either a truck or a foursheelbike to get to the top but both of those options are very expensive for what it is and we just walked it and saw the same. It's impressive to see these mountains of sand in the middle of lush greens but if you are tight on time I'd leave this out.
Beautiful by the sunset! I was there on the sunset and it was really beautiful. To ride a fourwheelbike you need to pay 200 000 dong, not worth it. Take a car and pay half the price instead!

Quad ride on the white sand dunes. The sand dunes were cool & the quad riding was fun. We negotiated a price to drive our selfs on two separate ones. However its better to let them drive, because they do it every day & know the terrain better. Also Its just as fun to pile 2 or 3 persons on one.

Interesting view if you're a city dweller Visited White Dunes on morning of 8 Jan 2018. I came here early so that it still was cooling and light was good for photography. I paid VND200K for what was presented to me to be a 40min ride around the entire venue. What they would do is to drive you to various vantage locations within the Sand Dunes for photography, making a short 5-10min stop at each point. I would have enjoyed it better if I got that experience. Instead, my ATV driver dropped me at the first stop and drove off, leaving me stranded. I then had to call the number on the receipt to get another deiver to pick me. Thinking he would drive me to the next location, instead he drove me back to the reception, thus ending my visit. I would suggest not taking the ATV and walk. It can be strenous, but an hour & a half would have you cover the entire place on foot. Otherwise, make sure it is well communicated that your ATV driver stays with you throughout your visit.

Went for 0600 but with overcast other than white sand, not much to see. Should have arrived earlier! The dunes were already covered with tourists and the parking lot was filled with jeeps. At the entrance to the start of the sand was 20-30 quads with engines running which certainly spoiled serenity of nature. O yes they charge extra to drive you to some dune at very high rates of speed. Walking was difficult so if you have issues walking the quad squad is necessary. Going up and back I saw the typical garbage that is being left by tourists - very disappointing but a way tourists and local greed spoils our beautiful nature. This is certainly a must see if you haven’t been on sand dunes before. But if you have seen one you have seen them all...

It‘s a really nice place. But it‘s a shame that they offer the quad bikes to go right into the sanddunes. First of all the noise of them are so high, second nearly just russian drive the bikes as crazy ...... . And also all the lost items as caps, this breathing masks and and and they just let them in the sand. It‘s not that worse at the moment, but nobody care... The dunes are pretty and there is a restaurant where you can eat and buy some water etc.. very very friendly people.. 😂 no not really

Atmosphere spoilt by vehicles If you want a fast ride up and down the dunes, this is the place to go. We preferred to venture out on foot and savor the time and effort it took to climb to the top of one of the ridges. The opportunities for appreciating the expanses of white sand and enjoying the view from on high are limited, however, by the whine of engines and the screams of thrill seekers.

We paid 6USD to get a jeep that would take us to the white dunes but upon arrival we were told to pay another 8.80 USD in order to reach the sunrise view spot on quad bikes (something that the agency Van Phong Du Lich we booked trip from didn't inform us beforehand). So there was no choice but to pay the quad boys though later on we found out that we could actually walk to the spot (it was probably some 1000 m away but in the morning dusk we couldn't see anything and we were kinda perplexed by this sudden twist in our trip). The spot was of course swarming with tourists, the skies were partially overcast and the dunes themselves do not impress much. It was all right to have a look at them but paying for quads was a bit too much and the fact that we were not informed about it and that the spot was at walking distance spoiled the mood.
the sanddune very nice but every where tou must pay for the bike is cost 600.000 / 20 .to much and the people thet want to tip
To visit it in the late evening was not a good idea :) It was dark, nothing to see, 25 min from Mui Ne by taxi approx. But I would like to see it in the morning.

A must see in Mui Ne but beware of the scam of that motor vehicle We- the family of 2 adults and 2 kids rent a jeep car from my hotel to visit this one and fairy stream. The jeep cost very fair price ( 25$/ jeep/ 4 hours).But the price for by ATV is 1000 000vnd (50usd) for 30 minutes is crazy expensive. I went there to see the sunset and it was truly awesome experience! You should bargain the price if you go to the top by motor vehicle.

The white sand-dunes would be a really nice spot, if not a horde of btw. overprised quads would ruine the scenery. It is loud (because of all the quads and chinese tourists) and there is garbidge every were. You have to pay a small fee of 15.000 (€0,50) at the entrance and for parking your bike. Dont go by one of these overpriced (€25/person!) cheep-transfers - take a scooter! You get them for about 150.000 (€5,60) day...

I recomend to book a jeep tour as it only costs around 100000 vnd. I rented a scooter but as the costline road is controlled by crooked cops i took a longer, inland road. At the gate you have to buy a ticket 15000 and scooter parking 5000. Then you can walk the tunes. With a jeep tour you can take the scenic coastline road and ride on the tunes. Sad to see carbage on the dunes. Around 12am it wasnt crowded.

I personally prefer the red one The white sand dune was surprisingly disappointed. Since the powder was not that great and there were too many marks of the vehicles' tires running around on the sand. It's just worth a quick visit, in my opinion. And btw beware of the scam of that motor vehicle. They always lied about the price at the beginning. I ended up paying 10 times. Avoid it at all cost! Just take a walk. It wasn't that far anyway

Visit this place if you've got nothing else to do.. Great for photo taking and getting yourself dirty. Go off the beaten track far away from the city to have more privacy and less children running around. Make sure your vehicle won't get stuck!

The sand dunes are a must see, very beautiful, that’s about all I can say, I can only compliment the dunes, the four wheelers are over priced USD$30 for 20 minutes, the drivers are very detached and borderline unfriendly, seeing so much trash once you got up high on the dunes is unacceptable, plastic bottles and whatever else you can think of, it would take them 30 minutes a day to keep the place clean. It’s a shame, the dunes would be A+ if the people in charge would get their act together. And that’s a big if. The dunes are worth seeing, bring along your patience.
그러나 발가락 사이 모래알들이 따스하던 곳.. A place where endless desert spreads .. It was a very hot place .. But where the sand between the toes was warm ...

Driving yourself to the dunes is not recommended. Traffic cops wait on the road to the desert and extort passing tourists. The attending officer accused us of driving over the speed limit, stating that for non - Vietnamese drivers it is 40. Obviously trumped up bs. He charged us some 2.5 million dong but was happy to take whatever cash we had in our wallet then let us go. If you're driving yourself to the dunes I'd advise not carrying large amounts of cash in your purse. These guys are simply after a quick bribe. Apart from this inconvenience, the coastal drive to the dunes is incredible and the very pretty. Entrance is 15,000 dong followed by 5,000 for parking. Renting ATVs is very expensive: 600,000 for a measly 20 minutes. With that said it still fun. Sandboarding is overpriced: we thought the guy was charging us 5,000 to slide down the dune on a piece of plastic but when it came time to pay he insisted on 50,000! Complete rip off. The dunes themselves are enchanting and like no other place in SE Asia, though the area is a complete tourist trap. If you don't mind burning cash at western levels go ahead. If not I'd stick to the red dunes.

Something you should see First time in any dunes. Really expensive to get a buggyride etc to the top of the dunes... They really know how to screw you there, but the area is so huge that I wouldn't recommend to walk up the dunes, unless you have a lot of time and are in a very good shape. Fun ride a nice views.

Went early for the sunrise-tour, but due to the weather we didn't see anything, thought we were going to sand-surf as well, but didn't do that either.. Passed by the dunes in better weather later on though, looked a lot prettier with sun and blue skies!

Much bigger than Red Sand Dunes but unfortunately quite a lot of ATVs & jeeps zipping around. We were taken by ATV up to the top of the dunes by the driver. Just wondering how safe this place is as there is not fixed route & those who are unfamiliar riding on the terrain may not be aware of the dangers of accident if the vehicle tumbles over the dunes.
im here with my friend for half day tour in mui ne. so many coat bike here and you pay alot of money for this
Very sunny and hot out there. I rented the motor with drivers 400.000d for 30' but i just take some pictures and then go down because sunny too hot.

All tours include the white sand dunes and offer transport here. Unfortunately the sand dunes are overrun with tourists being driven around on quadbikes and 4x4s. Therefore it is quite noisy and a trip on the quads costs quite a lot for only 20min. I didn't enjoy theses dunes so much, but they are definitely very majestic! The red dunes were much better!
Pretty place for photography U'll need to ride on ATV and pay 200,000 dong per pax which i find it too expensive to go & fro the middle part of the sand dunes.

It was a fun experience but I found the hire of quad bikes rather pricey. We walked the dunes. They are relatively well looked after although people did leave some litter on the dunes. Be sure to use sun block it was very hot even at 9am.
Fun to see but not a must see for me.We had a awesome quad ride and some good fun and photos.Bit of a hectic crazy place.

Little bit too noisy and crowded We booked a private tour with a guide who dropped us there. You can borrow Squads (which we didn't, because they are quite expensive for a short time - around 40$ for 25 minutes) some of them you can drive alone but on the big and fast ones you'll have a driver next to you. You can easily walk to the top and have a nice view over the landscape, but the squads make a lot of noise and are a little bit ruthless on the people who walk. Furthermore, It is a little bit dirty but not as dirty as the red dunes! Still we had fun, because a friend brought his drone and we took some great shots.

Wind + sand = not fun Was very windy when we went, the sand was unrelenting, I swear I still have sand on me today. If you pay for the 4x4 or dune buggy then it's worth going but to just walk around probs just go to the red sand dunes there closer and basically the same thing. Also we rode motorcycles up there, be careful the police set up road blocks on the road up there. So be ready to pay the fine.....
It's okay. The RAV rides seemed a little overpriced based on the standard living over there, but it's a tourist attraction...

It's my first time and really excited,a good place to take photos..i want to recommend the white sand dune more than red one..when the wind blow,it carries a lot of sand and hit my face and body as well..feel pain and itchy..so mind on me 😣

Tbh i wasn't that impressed. In reality, it's just sand. Be sure not to rent a sandslide, you will be disappointed. And also. If you don't have a international drivers lisence and plan on renting a scooter to go there. Think again. We were stopped twice by police officers claiming we were driving way above the speed limit and we had to pay them off a bit.
Yes, white dunes are nice, but there are a lot of mess and the company which provides jeep for 1 hour is terrible. It is just 5 min riding to place where you have to do photos...

Commercialized, overcrowded but still part of the tour This place is way crowded compared to the red sand dunes and i dnt mean in terms of visitors this places is filled with kids selling sliding carpets and adults selling foods not much interesting things to visit here as compared to red sand dunes people came here to slide down the sand only and you've to purchased those carpets from them 1st not recommended to come here but its part of the sand dunes tour

It's nice but Expensive service and short time we have checked in here with group of 6 in May. white sand dunes are really beautiful but not much place for taking picture, also the service here is very expensive, 600.000 vnd for a Zip car hiring in 15 minute hiring and you control it by yourself. i advice you should get it for hire and they have their driver take you in the white sand dunes.

This place is bigger, better for photo as compared to the red dune but it's much further. I thought unless you never seen or experienced a dune there is not much about it. There is no activity individual can participate. The place is rather disorganised and you see foot prints n track marks all over.

A desert in Vietnam (Part 1) Nobody knows that there are desert-like sand dunes in Vietnam. If you have seen sand dunes, than it's not a "must-see", but if you stay in Mu Niet /Phan Tiet, you should go to the dunes. If you don't find it to odd to ride around in the sanddunes via quad... Have fun.

Corrupt Police Officer On the main road to the White Sand Dunes we were pulled over by a police officer, who was sitting with a group of other officers on the side of the road. They asked to see an International Drivers Permit, which we didn't have, we only had a British permit. Because of this, he said we needed to pay him 1 million dong (equivalent 50 USD!!!). We pretended not to understand....explained we only have the bike for 1 afternoon etc, but to no avail. I've read that if you refuse to pay the first price they will often increase it, especially if other officers get involved (so they all get a slice of the pie!) so we very begrudgingly paid up. This was obviously absolutely gutting. We are backpackers on a budget and to be honest, if it has just been 100,000 dong I wouldnt have been bothered....but 1 million seemed pretty mean. Perhaps we were naive in not obtaining an IDP...but from everything we'd read it is rare to get stopped. Also, I think if we had produced one, the officer would have just said we were speeding. He would have got his money one way or the other. So, if you want to go to the White Dunes, I would strongly suggest booking a tour. The White Dunes themselves...they were nice but I wouldn't say they were stunning. And the fact it had cost us in excess of 1 million dong to get there, we kind of wished we hadn't bothered! Live and learn. Good luck!

Nice dunes, but honestly not very impressive compared to for instance Whitsunday Island. Even the Dutch dunes may be better, and at least don't have idiot quad riders racing by visitors on foot at a meter's distance.

Too crowded, too many cars, not private anymore You don't have to get the people there to drive you, you can walk up the sand dunes by yourself, which is actually much nicer. Unfortunately the sound of the machine steals parts of the romantic atmosphere. Furthermore you're not alone there anymore. It's a huge tourist place. Don't be disappointed.

As we noticed how little there is to see in Mui Ne, we booked a tour at our hotel. For 20 USD we would get to see four sights with a private jeep. For three people this felt somewhat reasonable. White Sand Dunes was one of the four sights. Our driver dropped us off far from the sand dunes. Here we were asked to pay 500k VND (~25 USD) for 20 minutes use of a buggy. So more than we paid for our tour for the day for just one person. The other option was a jeep that would drive us a couple minutes to the dunes for 150k VND per person, so they asked for 450k VND for the three of us. We said we can walk, and eventually managed to drop the price to 300k. The third option would have been to walk, but it's quite a distance in the sun. The dunes are a nice sight. Walking barefoot on top of them is a relaxing experience. It's a shame the experience is ruined by the aforementioned extortion. When tourists' only option is to walk quite a bit in soft sand, in hot weather, paying for a ride to the dunes becomes an entrance fee. Had it not been for this, I would rate it higher.
Well this is cool to behold. But hardly required paying the money to get taking up on a jeep of atv. Do yourself a favour and walk. It's a drive out of town. That was part of the joy frankly.

The White Dunes are some 30 kms or so North of Mui Ne. We rented a jeep and driver at very reasonable cost to travel along the scenic coastal route to the White Dunes, but felt somewhat trapped on arrival, when we found that to access the Dunes we would have to leave our jeep, and pay for a local vehicle to gain entry. This cost was larger than that for our jeep, although the distance travelled was relatively neglegible. The White Dunes present quite good photographic opportunity. The coastal trip in the jeep trip itself was most enjoyable. However the Red Dunes are much closer to the Mui Ne hotel strip, and access is free!

It's a sand dune, with trash and noise The dunes are nice, but the noise of the quads and the plastic trash everywhere make it depressing, so it's a nice walk if you are fit, or you have to pay again for a jeep or quad, but the bottle tops, empty bottles, and no effort to clean anything up, spoilt it all, not to mention the crazy whining noise of the quads .

The sand isn't white it's a light brown but white compared to the surrounding red and dark sands. I suggest you consider paying the a 20 minute ride by quad or jeep. There is not many dunes here so it can be walked.
If you are an ATV rider, this is the perfect attraction for you. I was with friends so really didn't care for this. My young male friends had no problem. They enjoyed it.

If you go by moto •watch out• We decided to go to fairy creek and white sand dunes on one day (you can easily do it) by moto. After 15 km driver where you see all the kite surfers on the beach you have to watch out for the corrupted police 👮‍♀️. They will pull tourist over because you need an international drivers license. If not they will show you a fake folder with a photo copy of a international drivers license and if you don't have one they will ask you to pay between 800.000 & 1200000 dong thant you can go and as if nothing happend. If you not do that you'll have to leave your moto for 7 days. There were other people there and they ask 1 million from them. So I made a big scene cause he tried to keep our keys which I pulled out of his hand. Called our rental guys and he fixed us 300.000 and I fixed that the others had to pay the same. If you see them drive by don't stop and just act stuppid. They are to lazy to chase you. They tried to pull over other tourist but they did not stop and just waved 👋🏼☺. I wish I did the same. This happend the day before lunar new year so I hope that karma is coming for them in their new year. • photo 3 is bringing one of the moto of the other guys to the police station 500m back. If you pay you'll get it back. But I did not hand over my keys.

We took a tour from the hotel, but we didnt know we have to pay extra to get to the dunes. Once we arrived to the place, driver was upset about our negative answer to rent an ATV or SUV or JEEP with a local driver, so they left us on the road to go walking to the dunes. Trip was long, we missed sunrise unfortunatly. In spite of that, dunes are very nice. Try to anticipate and ask every detail of your tour before purchasing.

White sand dunes and crazy quad accident The dunes are beautiful!! However they will want you to rent a quad or Jeep to take on them. You are welcome to walk however the vehicles for rent our encouraged. We ended up renting quads for 400,000.00 dong or $20 US each for 30 minutes(plenty of time actually). Anyways we took the quads out explored a bunch on our way back riding on flat ground my husband's quad had its tire break off and all of a sudden he was underneath the quad he landed head and shoulder first in the sand at a very fast speed. He was lucky to not have broken anything however for the remainder of our trip and even now 3 weeks later his back has been in a ton of pain. And the owners of the quads had zero sorry and quite a lack of patience for the accident. It was an unfortunate event and ended up leaving him in alot of pain for the rest of the trip. We would caution renting quads and to ensure you inspect them thoroughly before riding. Still fun time but a scary experience overall.

Being an Australian, we have these environments in our country, so for some and not for others. Many tourists visiting, with the option of quad bike, which I recommend to get the best overall experience. Otherwise the walk is quite far in sand.

If u wanna see white sand dunes in Vietnam, here is this place. It is far from Mui Ne around 1 hours drive. They have local bus from Nguyen Dinh Chieu to Hon Rom( bus 1 or 9) then get the jeep from Hon Rom to White Sand Dunes. They make price expensive there for the quad bike and jeep to get on the top. The staff attitude are not good for the service. Orchard bird ride one time is charged 50000 vnd.

Socialism + tourists money = bad service Very nice place, but because it is very far from Mui Ne (26 km) you don't have a choice but to spend here some time if you come. This is the core thing for local business - no customer service at all, higher prices and bad food. When arrive , you have some activities to do, e.g. Quad bike, ostrich ride. Bikes which cost is normal - have lack of power - don't take them. Also don't eat here - you can shortly get stomachache. There are lots of hammocks - that's great. Conclusion. If you girl is not sportive or risky, come here alone, with lover or only for sunset. And don't forget to bring some food with you.

It's ok but absolute chaos The sand dunes themselves are quite impressive and it could be really nice. But they pull you up and you have quite a walk over or pay again for a quad. We walked and honestly with so many people on quads, mostly people who had no control, it was chaos and at one point I literally had to dive out of the way to stop myself getting ran over. Really spoilt it, but in general still a little average.

If you take a tour, they will take you to 3 or 4 places: white sand dunes, red sand dunes, fairy stream and Mui Ne fishing village. The white sands dune is sort of a scam. They drive you to the bottom of the dune and then ask you to pay another 500,000 VND to take you up the dune in a quad or in a 4x4. And once you are up there you wait for them to come back and take you down. Or you can walk. It's only a sand dune and nothing spectacular. If you have been to dune du Pilat in France then its really not worth it.
We had not the best weather and so we had to do someting and this was funny to when you are some together it s even better;)

They are dunes all right, about five of them. Not white actually, sort of yellowish more like. Anyway, you won't see much of it through the omnipresent jeeps and 4-wheels. I can imagine it would have been quite a serene place if you had been there alone in the sunrise, but that cannot happen to you any more. There is also a petrol station close by in the direction of Mui Ne, where they'll try to overcharge you for a petrol, so be on guard.

40 minutes drive from ham thien, this area is more for the adrenaline junkie with jeep and ATV for rent which you can ride to the top of the dunes. There is an entrace fee af 15k VND per person. Nothing much to see or do accept to walk to the top and look at the views. Most people go during sunrise for the lovely views.

Cool views but don't get Quads Really enjoyed the dunes - something different in the heart of Vietnam. Really great views from the top. Felt the prices of quad bikes was a rip off and they struggled to get up hills. Get a group and get a driver in a jeep. Does some cool driving and get good views.

If you take a scooter as a tourist, be prepared for a roadblock by the cops where you will be forced to pay a fine. We went in a jeep with a local driver and were fine. The dunes are large. Its a bit sweaty to walk to the top, but doable. (Plan to take your shoes off - the sand is clean & soft). You can pay to rent an ATV and drive it yourself or they will drive you. The day we went was 150,000 per person to ride, or 400,000 to drive your own (seats 2 people). It's interesting to see such an unusual landscape, but its covered with people and vehicles, so not exactly peaceful.

Not as great as I thought and highly expensive The sand dune is not as beautiful and big as I expected. We went for sunrise tour but couldn't see any sunrise due to bad weather. The business people over there charged super expensive Jeep rides, but the service attitudes were really bad. The guy was busy playing with phone and didn't do the exciting rides at all. Not worth the mobey at all.

The dunes itself were ok, in holland we've seen them many times so for us not that spectacular. On our way to mui ne (with our own motorbikes) the "police" stopped us. We had no international drivers license and we were driving to fast (which we didn't and they had no meter to test that at all). Soon they said we could pay 2 mln dong so we wouldn't be kept in custody. We had almost no money in our pockets so they took us to the "police station". It was all very suspicious that they wouldnt be real cops. Although they had the police bikes and uniforms. Eventually we negotiated and I contacted a vietnamese friend who told us to pay absolute nothing (keep saving no; after a long time maybe pay 200.000 dong max), because they never hold you (they threat to keep you) and it's very corrupt. They got really mad at us so we became quite scared. However, when they were all inside the (police) building and were not doing much, we quickly took off on our bikes and they even didn't try to follow us. Advice; just continue your way and don't stop for these men. This didn't affect my rating though for the dunes :). Hoping to help some others in advance!

nice but ruined by the noise of quad bikes for sun rise Our guide forgot to give us our boards, and there was confusion when we asked him.. make sure you have your boards pre booked as the sand dunes are quite high and a lot of fun. We were unlucky that it was cloudy so missed the sun rise. We still enjoined watching the skies go from dark to light. You can rent quads and jeeps here which we planned to do but opted out as it was so chill and peaceful before sunrise. this was quickly ruined by tourists arriving late and then spending 30 mins driving like crazy around the sand dunes.

Worth a visit but don't pay to go in if you just want to have a look at the sand dunes . You can get a good photo from the road and also the sand is very hot to walk on! If you do want to go inside you pay a small fee and then can pay for a jeep / quad bike tour .
The fairy stream is a nice morning tour with a bit of walking along a small stream normally included with a visit to the sand dunes

Nice, hot and overpriced We stopped at the White Sand Dunes as part of a half day trip round Mui Ne. The Dunes where nice and our little one (3 years) was realy excited to ride a Quad for the first time. However, when negotiating with the staff, we where given no chance. We paid 700.000VND and just knew that this was way over price. However, they did not give in so we went for it as a special treat cause our little one just wanted to ride one so bad. A few minutes after the deal was made, another group of people (Asian) came and the same Quad was offered to them for only 500.000 VND. Needless to say that we where Pixxxx. Of course they ignored our complaints. The trip to the Dunes was nice though but would have been better with a smaller Quad for a Single Person. Since it was 11am, it was crazy hot, we would reccommend going there early in the morning. But we where told that it can be very noisy and crowded in the morning.
Yes, it in nice, but at the end only some sand dunes. The Quads you can rent are 600000dong for 20 minutes. This money you can save. The area is way to small to have real fun with it.

500k to get there and 200ak a person to get up. Rang gardens did not tell us this. And it's not sandboarding. You lay down on a piece of plastic and move slowly down the hill if you're taller than 5ft

Be aware of the police "mordida" Nice for a half day excursion from mui ne, but if you go on your own bike be aware that police loves to spend the day in the shadow getting some dongs from tourists based on many different excuses like too fast, no international driving permit or anything

Nice dunes, but with to much tourists and dirt The white dunes are really beautiful, but they turned this place into a tourist trap. Parking is quite far from the dunes away, so they want to sell a Quad-tour (too expensive). On the dunes itself there are so many jeeps and quads. It's so loud there. And there is too much plastic waste in the sand.

All together an ok experience! It was a special scenery and some nice photo opportunities although we weren't there sunrise or sunset. Rented a "tired" motorbike for 500k Vnd. Noisy and shaky but a good way too get around.
Was ok bit of a rip off for the prices they wanted for 20 mins on the quads $30 but apart from that was ok nothing special in my opinion.

Nothing extraordinary It has become a bit too commercialised and it was all about money and how best to get it off you. We managed to stroll around and met some kids with skate boards to be rented to slide down the sandy slopes. It is located very close to the Fairy Stream and is a matter of just getting across to see the place.

There's not a big amount of things to visit, but if you are already here in Mui Ne go to take a look, unless you want to spend your entire staying inside your hotel. We had the chance to choose between the 5am tour and the 1pm tour, but probably depends on the tour agency. When you get there, something like a dozen kids try to sell you slide mats and after couple minutes walking other people offer you a quad (300k) or a car (600k). We decided to walk and enjoy the sunrise at 5.40am, the problem was that all tourists who rented a quad were making so much noise and the worst thing was that I saw many locals throw trash like bottles or plastic bags!!
Red dunes is better in term of view. Here is no atv passing by, so more quieter. Happy with the view and be careful with personal belonging

My Fiancé and I really enjoyed the sunrise here. It was a beautiful way to start the day. We rented a quad to try to get to the top of the dunes before the sun came up. But was severely disappointed with the bike. The wheels on the front weren't parallel with each other and the tyres were so bald I couldn't even make it half way up the dunes. If I were to do it again I'd just walk from the car park. It's really not that far.

Beautiful dunes with a view over the lake. Good opportunity to take photos BUT too much rubbish and the staff renting quads and jeeps were driving dangerously near people! Authorities must take steps to regulate this attraction!

The Desert of Vietnam Overall the dunes are pretty impressive, can easily see the sunrise there. Just spend some time and walked up the sand dune. Bad thing is the monopoly of the ATV business here. Suppose the hired jeep/ 4wd can easily drive in that area, but due to this business, tourist must pay extra to hike up to the sand dune. Bad smell and noise pollution by these little machine.

Bunch of white sand dunes. That's about it really. I went on a tour in that driver bought you there for 45mins then on to red sand dunes. You can hire quad bike between a few of you but very expensive so I didn't bother. Bit of fun and nice to do something in Mui Ne.

Great place ! Very few tourists !! So I went to Mui Ne with my family and friend during the holidays. I was expecting it to be packed with tourists since that was the place of choice for beaches. However it turned out to be almost completely alienated. At any given time of any given day at any of the sights and tourist attractions, there were like maybe only 20 other people ? Which is great because Mui Ne is quite beautiful. The beaches aren't great, the water isn't blue and the sand isn't white. It's just your typical beach. But you get to see people in giant baskets zipping on the water which I felt was quite interesting. However the white sand dunes are where you want to be. It's truly a sight to behold. You'll need to book a driver to get you there and it's quite a journey depending on which hotel you are staying. My hotel was over an hour away so you'll need to plan ahead. Once you're done with the white sand dunes you may not wish to check out the red sand dunes as it's pretty much the same thing only with different colored sand. But I'll say check it out anyway. for some reasons it just feels different. Hotels are reasonably cheap I got a room 10 meters by the sea for under $60USD. There were plenty of seafood places with a decent variety of live seafood ranging from crabs, lobsters to scallops but sadly the chefs don't quite do justice to the ingredients. They basically just put everything on a grill and depending on your luck, it may or may not turn out too dry. The fairy trail was a little bit disappointing. it's basically a walk down a tiny river accompanied by some interesting rock formations. However most of the natural formations are gone. but it's still worth a look. You'll have to either go bare footed or bring slippers but do take note that they charge a dollar to take care of your shoes. It's fine I guess but I figured I should give you all a heads up. overall my 3 days in mui ne was excellent. I really enjoyed the dunes and the sea breeze. I would say it's best to go with family and friends, the more the merrier.

Nice to see, but horrible noise pollution The dunes are pretty impressive, and they allow for great views at sunrise. You can see pretty far out into the expanse. Walking around them is no problem at all, it's not a far walk to the highest point to catch a fantastic panoramic view. However, if you don't like noise pollution, then you'll want to skip this. I get it - I've lived in Vietnam long enough to know that if there's a way to make a buck off of a foreigner, they'll figure it out. But the ATVs/quad bikes are pretty obnoxious. I'd rather them ban those and have to pay an entry fee than to put up with the noise and tourists who are driving them when it's clear it's their first time doing so. A great place ruined by noise and generally obnoxious behavior.

We had a epic time visiting the dunes! Please go and rent a quad bike. It is not really cheap but it is a cool experience. We went there to see the sunset and it was a little crowded. (i think some tours from hotels and there). We did no such thing as a tour, simply took a picture of the tour its highlight rented a motorbike and took off. A little rude maybe, but we like to take the time we need!
Motobike parking 10k vnd. Coke 20k. Coconut fresh 30k. Others... wow. Eerything 3 times expensive. But honestly, fresh cold coconut there is worth to try. I luv it.

I was expecting a larger area to be covered by the actual dunes but it was very beautiful regardless. We went on a 4:30am tour as we thought we would see the sunrise but were too late and missed it. There are sooo many tourists here at these times for the tours and the noise from the buggies going up the hill was very loud and also expensive! Id recommend finding a way outside of the tours to go visit the dunes as its worth the visit but not the lack of sleep and the crowdedness.

Sand Dunes themselves are a beautiful sight to see but those "operating" the ATV and Jeep rides ruin the experience for those wanting to just chill and take it all in. You can definitely hike to a peek of the dunes and just look around without renting a jeep ride. It took my girlfriend and I about 10 minutes. The sun searing down on you and heating up that sand which bakes your feet makes the trek difficult but not impossible. Nevertheless, it was quite astonishing to see the juxtaposition of the dunes and lake. After we left the dunes, we rode our moto through some back roads to get better photos of the lake with the dunes in the background. CAUTION FOREIGNERS: Police Trap On our way back from the white dunes, I was riding my moto on the road/highway around 45kph... ok ok, I'll be honest I was going closer to 50kph when I noticed a group of bikes parked off to the side of the rode. Out of nowhere a tan uniformed traffic police officer waved his white baton and gestured for us to pull-over. He asked if I had my "international motorcycle's license". Oh! Heeeere we goooo. I told him I didn't so he told us to get off the moto and come with him. He escorted us over to a group of 4 other "police officers" who were sitting on tiny plastic chairs smoking around an equally tiny plastic table. One officer again asks about the license then tells me I was speeding because the limit on the road is 40kph. He said that he was going to impound my moto and then we'll have to see a judge in a few days to pay the speeding and driving without a license fine of 2,000,000VND. I knew when we got pulled over that all they wanted was MONEY so asked if we could just pay the fine now and be let go. He said, we could go if we pay 1,000,000VND. Some bribe money to be set free? Sure why not! Enjoy your day officers! I drove under 40kph all the way home :)

Nice to see the sunset ruined by jeeps Really nice seeing the sunrise the views are amazing however the people on the quad bikes and jeeps just ruin it with the noise , not what you want to hear at 5 am in the morning! Worth going to though

Beautiful Shock to the system 10, 000 dong per person to enter. They're a beautiful shock to the system as all you can see is sand for miles and miles then a beautiful lake at the front. I would definitely go to see however, they have become extremely touristy. Restaurants in try to get you to eat there, quad bikes all around, an emu you can ride (I love animals and this upset me as they clearly didn't want to be there carrying around tourists for a laugh- as with many animals in Asia). We hired a moped from a local person in Mui Ne (100, 000 for half day) which was great fun, each to find (if you've done your research before) and drive past others like the fairy falls and red dunes.
White sand dunes can reached from Ho chi minh city by bus about 5 hours. The bus depart in the morning, afternoon or evening

where the horizon made by the wind its the biggest white dunes i've seen the first time. There is only three main color are white sand, blue sky and some green bushes. The shape of sand hills changed every day depend on the wind flowing. Its smooth and too much sand. Comfortably, you walk without shoes and feel cool sand cover your foot. Some activities in the dunes and you can rent cars, motorbike and ride over the hills. Should be there in the dawn and the sunset. Bring some food and drink.
The sunrise was beautiful and so were the dunes but all the noisy quads reduced the ambience. I'm glad I went but it's not as fabulous as the descriptions because of the quads.

Just like any other sand dune place... I admit, the views are lovely from there. But the thing that annoyed us most was the complete oblivious manner of the staff renting out the quad bikes there. You hop on, they show you how to use it via sign language (no English skills to speak of whatsoever) and they send you on your way. We got stuck on a rather dangerous and steep dune and thought it best to leave it there and walk back to the quad station for help. Absolutely no staff or rangers around whatsoever. On trying to tell them that after, they absolutely couldn't care less. The views are nice, granted. But I wouldn't recommend the quad bikes. Also bear in mind that to get to the dunes from Mui Ne, it's the good part of an hour or so, so plan ahead. Best advice is to go with a group tour which is well priced.

Alright for a few minutes unless willing to pay masses for renting the quad bikes We paid for a trip to the White and red dunes and expected to be able to rent a quad bike on arrival - they tried to charge us 400,000 for half an hour. No thank you. Disappointing.
Only stayed aprox 15mins to take photos, unless your riding the sand buggies you dont need any longer.

A few hills of normal sand, nothing more Best time to see the sand-dunes is early morning for sunrise. Usually a bus or jeep gets you there. At the entrance you get the chance to rent a quad, if you like. But it's also possible to simply walk to the dunes (roughly 15'). The dunes themselfs are not white at all. It's normal yellow sand. And there are bushes and small trees everywhere. If you've seen dunes in other places (Australia, Africa, Peru etc.) you won't be awed by the dune in Mui Ne.

We didn't take place in any of the powersports while we were here, we were told that the rentals were fairly low powered unless you rented the more expensive ones. For myself personally having grown up on ATV's and dirtbikes I didn't find it worth the price. The scenery was great and it was nice being the only people there to walk around and explore. Definitely worth the moto drive, roads were pretty rough getting to the dunes but that is to be expected.

The main tourist attraction in Mui Ne, as advertised by most hotels. I found it not that special: It is a nice place to go, but I've seen many more beautiful dunes elsewhere. Maybe I've been to too many places and changed my standard. Nice for a short trip away from the beaches and restaurants of Mui Ne, but don't expect a miraculous site.

Remote ride to dunes in a scenic area of Phan Thiet, gets a bit bumpy on the soft sand drive there but looks like they are tarmac the access road. Not sure great idea as takes away the adventure. good fun day out. Better at sunrise or sunset for low light and less heat

Worth a look if you are already in Mui Ne Worth a look if you are already in Mui Ne but not travelling to the town just for this. The beach is the main reason to visit. The dunes are quite impressive and similar to any you may have seen in other places. It is worth the early morning start but if you take a tour make sure they give you enough time to walk up and relax. Probably 90 minutes is about right. Private jeeps go for about 550-600K Dong in any high street agency so it's still a good option between 3-4 people and you can decide how long to stay. The other reviews sum it up well re the ATVs / Quad bikes being noisy, disruptive and not worth the money. Easy to walk up if you want the scenery and not just the thrill of a ride.

Only if you have time Over an hours drive by car from Mui Ne town. I went there in the afternoon and unless you want to ride in a 4x4 beach buggy or hire one , it's a bit far for what you get to see . If you have limited time in Mui Ne , forgo this and just visit the red sand dune late for sunset .

Quart bike fun - but exspensive We went there on a rente scooter to realize the only thing to do is ostrich riding og quart biking. The quart bike where like 30$ for 30 mins per bike. Ostrich riding was like 5$ for 2 mins, but hey! you can say you rode a ostrich!

The sanddunes themselves are quite amazing! I enjoed the walk. But the fuss they make about this place is a bit exagarated and I was dissapointed about the tour we took. The quad bikes there are in super bad condition, they just want you money, don't do it! As for the red sanddunes, it would be a much nicer experience with less filth everywhere.
We went to see the sunset in dunes. Better go in the morning, so you will able to see lotuses. Enjoyed the view. Also you may take a ATV.

We heard good things about the sand dunes. It was good and me and my friend rented an ATV and drove around for an hour. It was 400.000 for one hour which is pretty decent. I think our expectations were a little bit to high, but it is still worth a visit.

We went here in the afternoon as a part of a tour. Immediately it became evident that the dunes are for quad bikes first and anyone else second. If you don't want to take the quad bike option (which is pretty pricey as we found), you can walk to the top of the dunes and take some photos. The scenery is ok but nothing too special and the constant drone of quad bikes speeding past you all the time dampens the experience a bit.

No longer a hidden gem I've been here three times in total. However, the third time was truly disappointing. The entrance was full of trash and some people even brought a karaoke machine to have a little party! I hope that this place will be preserved and that people will stop littering!
This would be a beautiful spot to watch the rise of the sun over huge pale dunes, and get lost in your thoughts. However the rip off priced quad bikes racing everywhere spoil it. Shame.
Very expensive but nice to visit.far from phan thiet take a cab and ask him to wait for u .take scarfs and glasses

The view was great, but the people there would push you to rent a motor bike, saying it would take only 5 minutes to the mountain top while it takes 40 minutes to walk up. They would keep saying it, standing in front of you, no matter what you say exept YES! It's really annoying. So, coming earlier, take some time to walk instead of renting a motorbike!

This place is a 2km expand of sand dune that give you experince for a dessert. You can walk up the dune and watch the sunrise from the top of the dune. You have to arrive early at the place about 5.00am and spend time there not after 8.00am otherwise the sun will be too strong to visit this place
