Our travel agent booked the jeep for us to visit the sand dunes.. Pick up was on time.. But a bit dissapointed with the ATV ride.. They say will bring us around for the price paid.. Upon paying, they whisked us up the dunes.. Pick us up upon our request, bring us for a ride and thats it.. No stops at the lake.. Perhaps if you want, you must tell them.. But then again they dont understand English, so a bit of a problem.. The dunes itself is beautuful.. No sunrise though as it was cloudly..

Beautiful desert located 220km away from the City. The entire landscape making you feel as if you are in a desert. It is extremely photogenic view during sun rise. When you arrive, you may choose to use 4wd, jeep or ATV. Extra chargers incurred.

Nice place. Only thing is that the quads and jeeps are a bit annoying. But the area is wide enough to kinda like ignore it. The Quad costs 500k for 20 min which is ridiculous. So we went by food which was the better idea even though it’s sometimes very loud. I booked a private Jeep tour with a stay of 40 min. Could have stayed there much longer, so going on your on is recommended by me. Problem is That the police checks for foreigners with motorcycles or scooter from Mui Né coming. If you don’t have a Vietnamese driver license they try to fine you with 1-1,5mn VND otherwise they keep the scooter. But they are open to negotiate the price :) (at least that’s what I’ve heard) ... but you can stay at the dunes for hours. Really nice and clean! So if u go to Mui Né then go there. But basically I wouldn’t have gone to Mui Né if I knew all of the information like how trashy the beach and the water is... so maybe think twice :)
Visit early morning with family and friends. Nice experience Even though the sun never showed up. The 4w drive was really anjoyable and an adrenaline kick.

While vacationing in Saigon. We booked 3 days in Mui ne to visit the white sand dunes and the fairy stream. We were not disappointed. The white sand dunes was beautiful! It's like a little dessert. Sad thing is tourists litter everywhere.

Beautiful and the lake right beside adds to the beauty It was amazing but we didn’t bother renting and atv or jeep we just walked. It was quite a windy day so it wasn’t as hot although you needed a mask and sunglasses so sand doesn’t get every where and it still did. We had fun but I’d recommend hiring a taxi for a pre negotiated flat rate for 4 hrs rather than a big tour group. We just beat the tour bus rush for the sunset tour and got wonderful pix with no one to photo bomb us. We paid our driver 620,000dong for two of us for 4hrs where he offered to take us up to 4 vantage points. I felt it was a great deal! And he was really nice!

Is a very beautiful place, the white dunes still are a great show of our planet. Unfortunately human “footprint” is everywhere. At the top of big dune you can find hundreds of water plastic bottles, without considering the ones sand already swallowed . Local people have their business , they charge you 600.000VND ($25) to bring you to top . They are rude and only talk to you to ask you money. They don't care about the treasure they have and they laugh when you ask them to collect the garbage. Without mention the concept of “safety” , they really don't know or care about your safety. They ignore all except money $$$. I was wondering with the views but extremely sad . You must go but be prepare to face indifference ... don't create big expectations. Wear confortable and closed shoes, careful with sun burns cover your shoulders, neck and face. Take lots of water (no plastics please)
We chose to go on a tour to the white dunes and got an ATV to the top of the dunes! The view is pretty spectacular and it’s not too hot as there’s a nice breeze up there.

Better to hire a tour guide who can fetch you to the white sand dune from your hotel. You can contact this jeep owner by virtue to his contact as shown in the picture below ✌🏻his photographic skill is very good 🌹he will bring you to 3 places, which are white sand dune, fishing village and red sand dune. Renting one jeep is 400000 dong, I think it can fit 6 people. Thuong jeep mui ne =WeChat Thuong jeep tour = instagram

Good place to see the sunrise. But you need to come earlier maybe at 4am because if you late that will be too crowded when you want to get the best picture. I suggest to rent ATV that cost 200.000 VND because if you walk will take a long time.

We payed tour with jeep thinking that tickets are included in price. When we get there, our guy parked next to road and we had to buy ticket to go there with other guys.If you want to go by yourself with their jeep than you have to pay a million dongs, If you want to go with them than 200000 dongs, or you can walk, but you can't manage to get there and back in an hour. Otherwise it's nice place.

Visited the White Sand Dunes while in the area doing research. The place was remote, but was pretty busy on the day we were there. Riding the dune buggy up the dunes was a very unique experience! The only thing I didn't like was how strong the wind was and how the sand blew all over me! As I said....unique!

Beautiful but be prepared to pay We loved the sand dunes and walked to the top instead of paying the extortionate prices being charged for renting an ATV or being driven to the top of a dune. Free for walkers but hot and tiring! So just be ready to either pay a lot for the ride up (which looks like a lot of fun by the way) or stick to your guns and walk up.

Almost an hour drive from Mui Ne, much of it along the nice coast (could make a nice stop). Three basic options when you get to the parking place: walk, take a car or ATV. We chose the ATV for 400,000 VND. It was fun. In a crazy ride we went to the highest dune. There we had time to walk around, take photos and enjoy the views. Then we took the ATV back and had another crazy ride. The attraction is pretty expensive - OK, But everything depends on what you expect. A good setting for photos. Some places to eat and have a dring at the parking lot. We were satisfied although on a cloudy day after rain the sand is not as white as some photos show...
A nice area to see. We didn't rent the ATVs. They wanted a lot of money for those. The keeps as well were a lot of money. Ticket to get in was 15k each. Spent about 40 minutes here.

If U stay in Mui Ne go to see it. U can feel like in desert for a moment. Hot air white sand around U makes unforgatable memory. I recommend to go there early in the morning before others. Later is quite crowdy and noisy of ATM and jeeps

The white sand dunes were amazing to see and it’s great to experience actual desert. Would recommend. My only complaint (which is why it’s only getting 4 stars) is that the price to rent quad bikes to go up the dunes was extortionate. It was ridiculously expensive so we decided to walk instead.

They charged for everything.. Bought a tour from hostel for 120,000 and thought it was a good deal. White sand dunes 200K minimum Red sand dunes you could rent your own crazy carpet for as long as you want but smaller hills
We had a really fun experience at the white sand dunes. We chose to pay for going with the ATVs. It was quite expensive, but for us it was worth it.

Good sunrise spot but..... This is a good sunrise spot but it's cold in the morning before sunrise, and the ATV up to the hill is costly but is hell of a ride down the hill. Do take note that you can walk up the hill.

We went early in the morning and drove on ATM's there, it was so much fun and then we just waited for the sun to rise. It was a little cold even though it was around 25°C, but I would 100% reccomend this experience if you're in the area.

The white Sand dune is about 40 mins drive by jeep from beach area of Mui Ne. We left at 4:30am to reacg there at about 05:30 to wait for sunrise. They offer service for ATV , I did not ask for the price, but I walked up to the dune by myself which was ok, about 20-30 mins. No need to go to the top of the dune, too crowded, I chose to be in lower dune, but facing the east to see sunrise (however it was too cloudy that eventually nobody saw its rising haha) The ATV was very noisy engine, this needs to be improved ugently for environment un my point of view. I would say White Sand Dune was a fun experience to walk up to those dunes, but not the best place to see sunrise for sure. (I based on my experiences for my many sunrise viewings) Please go to enjoy walking up the dune (no hiring ATV) then take a sunrise as a bonus of this, then this place is lovely.

We were not expecting much. We went for a tour by our hotel where we paid 32 usd for 5 pax to go to 4 places - fishing village, white sand dunes, red sand dunes and fairy stream. The white sand dunes shocked us as the moment we arrived, there was a group there already waiting to greeet us and asked us to choose between a Jeep(200,000/pax) or atv (350,000 per pax) We asked if we could just walk but they said it was too big. We wanted to atv after further consideration since it would be fun but we found out we had to be the pillion riders since it’s too “dangerous” to drive alone so we chose the Jeep. It was so sandy + windy it was really bad. He drove us to the TOP where again they asked us to pay to take this sandboarding thing down (100,000 dong) it seemed fun but the walk back up wasn’t. You have to be prepared. In the midst of the desert and harsh winds slapping and cutting into your face, you have to climb up the steep hill and it was a real challenge for me. I had to ask my Friend to hold my board before I could properly struggle to climb albeit I got tired halfway. The 100,000 dong was for numerous times of sand boarding but it was too much pain we only did it once. All our bags were sandy and my face was covered with sand it was bad. They drove us around to get some pictures tho the wind was so bad my hair was flying everywhere. We went to the red sand dunes afterwards and the wind was slightly calmer and the sand dunes didn’t look that steep. We should’ve done the snowboarding here instead :’) Everyone was there to await the sunset and you should really try to climb to the highest one closest to the sun to get the best picture - the best. Overall it was the highlight of our trip for better or for worst!

It was part of a tour to the white and red dunes as well as fairy stream. Been there for approx. 45 min and had a quad bike ride of 20 min, which was fun. I only was confused because a guide sat on the bike with us at all times. Would not recommend to go there without the quad as it is very loud because of them. Red dunes are more relaxed (although very crowded as well). Be careful: some guys try to give you a toboggan on top of the dunes. As soon as you go down they will charge you.

Great tour guide and must go A great tour guide Mr Thuong in Mui Ne for Sand dunes trips. He is a driver and also a very great photographer. No regrets to book him. He is very professional. He can be contacted via wechat (by english text is fine) and phone (+84905555512). Pls try to book him earlier as his schedule is quite tight must of the time.

Great adventure activity. Enjoyable adventure activity at the dunes and bigger and more impressive then first anticipated. Throughly recommend getting a quad bike or buggy for the experience to zoom around in!

We enjoyed the dunes. You can walk up. They also rent dune buggies but we found them too nousy. You can also rent a magic carpet to slide down the dunes but remember, you have to walk back up again! There is a lotus lake at the bottom but not too many lotuses.
Had a blast with the groups here with quad bikes. It was really fun and pretty intense. My groups had pretty amazing experience.
Make sure you go early morning..can get really hot later. Sunrise view is amazing. We walked up to the top. Quad bike available in case you don't want to walk. Must visit when you are in mui ne
Big attraction in the area One of the best attractions in the area, you can also rent a jeep with the driver and go up by 4x4. Or you can just walk, the dune is big enough.

In case you know the way to drive scooter and head out with the dunes. The final km is gravel so physical exercise caution, though the other 30kms is a good paved road. Be sure to have a little cash since there's a tiny entry fee at the dunes. Additionally, you need to lease a quad at the dunes since the dunes are massive and it is the most effective way to see them BUT do skimp out on the quad. We purchased probably the cheapest 1 plus the tires had been just bald that we kept getting stuck. Pay for the fundamental quad and also you are going to have a blast. Be sure the tires have a great deal of tread left or maybe you are going to spend the time to find out caught in sand. we wish we'd. Enjoy! you are able to speak with folks from about rent for a jeep. People extremely frendly. else you wind up spending additional for another jeep and entrance fee to help you move right around the dunes.

Beautiful but expensive sunrise The sunrise was really beautiful but I advice you to take a motorbike. The tour with the Jeep to do the white / red sand dunes + the fairy river and the fishing village is 120 000 d which is correct but you have a short timing to enjoy and the driver will go slow to see the sunrise and arrive on time for it but you have to climb the dunes. It’s why the propose you for 200 000 d per person to take the Jeep and go in the top ! You will not miss it ... not fair but was beautiful !

Amazing views at the White sand dunes. If you only have time to visit one place here, go to see the white sand dunes. Even though it is further away than the red dunes the views are so much more spectacular. A must see when visiting mui ne

It can be quite windy at the top of the Dunes. Take care with prescription glasses as the fine particles of sand will etch and scratch the lenses. Hold onto your hat as we saw a few blown away on the sand dunes. We enjoyed this tour.

The sand dune is beautiful but the four wheeler is over the top stressful! I rather walk up by myself but was hustling into paying an extra 500 thousands dong for 10 minutes of hanging on for my life dusty ride io the hill. Take taxi here and walk up yourself at your own pace.

We booked our trip online through the Sinh Tourist and they were spot on. The dunes are impressive but be prepared to keep handing over money. When you get there the option is to walk (it’s a canny walk) for free but you might not make the sunrise (it was a cloudy morning with some rain the day we went 🙁 so we didn’t get to see it). You can pay 600k dong for four of you to get in a jeep and get taken - it was actually quite fun. Or you can pay 650k dong for 2 of you to go on a quad. 2 in our group went on the quad, the rest went in a jeep. The views are spectacular just a pity we didn’t get a sunrise but that’s life! There was someone at the top encouraging you to surf down a slope which some of the group did - they had great fun but be warned you get sand in every orifice - what they don’t tell you when you get to the top is that will be 55k dong - granted this is to go as many times as you like but after one go our group said one dose of sand up your nose was enough. It did feel a bit like they were constantly trying to rip you off - has they said there was a charge the group all said they wouldn’t have done it. You just want people to be upfront.
Nice to experience but not that spectacular for me as in Peru. This area is much smaller but eventhough I enjoyed it very much.

Mui Ne boasts of its spectacular white and red sand dunes, eroded by oceanic wind. The winds have carved out mind-blowing formations, such as Barchans & Sieve, and display amazing desert topography. As one of the most fascinating Vietnam tourist places, it is crowded during the peak season. Yet, it is quite challenging to explore the entire area by trekking. Hence, people need to hire sledge, quad bikes, and buggies or take an ostrich ride. Don’t miss: Quad biking, ostrich ride, sunset, and kite flying

Mui Ne White and red Sand Dunes are two tourists'attraction one cannot miss. The scenery is awesome and breathtaking. I was quite surprised that such an amazing place exists in Vietnam. Despite the bouncy and bumpy experience of travelling to the place, it was worth it.
Very nice place. We arrive around five in the morning. We walked about half an hour. There is not much else to do. I saw more beautiful dunes in the world.

It is obviously a wonderful palce to visit. You should visit it by sunset time. It is more than words can describe scenery. We just don't refer the ATV vendor trying to fish tourists with expensive renting and there seems the only vendor there to for rent those ATV & Jeep

A short drive from town, this offers a unusual desert experience in the middle of Vietnam. Nice white sand dunes slightly spoiled by the hoards of 4 x4 vehicles for those who are unwilling or unable to walk.

Lovely landscapes and view . Exciting Quad Bikes rides Amazing views of sand dunes and beach in one place. Do take the 15mins quad bike rides by the professionals. Thrilling and exciting driving on the quad bikes. Much better feel than the 4WD..WOWWW

The landscape around Mui Ne is amazing with red and white sand dunes dotting the area for miles together. A visit to the white sand dune by an open jeep from Mui Ne for the sunset is worth doing during one’s stay. The quad bike ride adds to the thrill at the dunes though the service provider lacks the professionalism to make the experience even better.

Great fun on the quad bikes in spectacular scenery. A guide rode on the side of the bike to stop us going off piste. This is needed, when you see the huge drops off the lip of the dunes! Also good to keep teenage daughters upright on some steep off cambered slopes. 650k Dong for 15 mins seemed a lot, but this would be cheap in Europe and you get the guide. We also had the trip for at least 20 mins. A bit of a luxury but worth the journey for a treat. Slight regret that we didn’t stop more for good pics
My wife and I went there early in the morning to watch the sunrise, we just find a good spot to take pictures. Great experience.

Go Sunset tour in the afternoon, not the morning one White sand dune tour is good to tour in the afternoon. Weather in Vietnam is usually cloudy in the morning, but clear in the late afternoon in August. You can take good pictures then. Recommend Walk up to the top so you can see them from different angles.

Reminded me of Little Sahara on Kangaroo Island South Australia Only difference being it was filled with ATVs and people rubbish Having said that it is something to see. If u haven't got much time, do what I did, the 5 hr tour combing red dunes, fairy stream and fishing village..150k... approx $9..

Very scenic location. Nice surprise dunes and lake in what seems like the middle of no where. Lots of jeeps, trucks and ATV’s available. Staff is friendly and accommodating. Some refreshments are available. I would recommend an afternoon/evening visit for the amazing sunset photo ops. The ATV tour comes with a guide. You can drive it to the top of a dune but after that they take over and go in a fast circuit around the dunes, almost like a roller coaster. Being an American biker I was not thrilled riding on the back as I was led to believe it was going to be me and my girl in control of the machine. I just went with it seeing how we were all the way out there on top of the dune already.
This second times be back at white sand dunes love the ride, fun but its cost $600k vnd for jeep 10 minutes and for 4 wheels motorbike 20 minutes if you like little challenge

Strong experience with pickup & sand dunes Only 50km from PhanThiet city. The road is good, along beach and through dessert. Please come to Bau Trang (White sand dunes) to have strong experience with pickup & sand dunes. You'll ride pickup Jeep or Toyota 4WD to pass sand dunes. The is large lake in this dessert. Beatiful lanscape

I didn’t see the lake I wanted to see the lake and was sold a 4 wheeler trip that didn’t take me there. The sand dunes are beautiful. I have heard the lake is too. When I complained I was told to get a driver who speaks English. It’s a money making kinda place and don’t expect any kindness.

Very nice Jeep experience Recommend to use the jeep / ATV service. Will bring you to upper part, 1 million VND for 5 people (1 jeep). Nice place, worth the visit. Unexpected nature sight seeing in land of Vietnam. Best to come at 5PM when the sand is not hot and wind is calm.

Significant dunes with a catch. To get to the top of the dunes you must pay a rather steep 200000 VND per person for a round trip to top on the back of an ATV or in a 4WD. 5 minutes each way although on the ATV it was quite a thrill.

We went in the afternoon when the weather tends to be more windy hence got sand whipped, But this only added to the adventure! The bonus was a magnificient natural lake amogst the sand dunes. it was very beautiful

Best sunrise experience We took a private Russian jeep with tour guide. The tour guide was so lovely and he spoke English. The sunrise in white sand dunes was breathtaking but in order to enjoy the sunset at a greater spot u need to take the quad bike which costed around 80k VND per person. It was pricy as the riding distance was less than 5 mins. You may consider walking but it was not recommended as it was pretty far by foot.

Pretty dunes - no need for a quad Lovely sand dunes. It's easy to walk at the top of it, so there's no need for a quad - it's actually not helping the preservation of it. Indeed, there is a lot of plastic bottles and trash everywhere which is a shame... But you do get nice views of the surroundings. Go down to the Lotus lake after that, you might be able to see some buffalos (loads!)

This is about 20k from the town. There are lots of areas to walk without seeing others plus there are tours to the dunes as well. So if indeoendant drive on a little and you will find seclusion. It is free but there are also buggy rides around if more adventurous
Jeep tour took us to this location. The drive from Mui ne lasts approximately 45 minutes. The sunrise was spectacular. Great place to make some excellent pictures, although it was quite windy.

Great view but difficult to take a picture It was a good place to visit in Mui Ne. I purchased a half day tour (300,000 Vietnamese dong) which the jeep driver picked me up at the hotel and take me to the White Sand Dunes and other places. I arrived there around 5.15 am. And I have to pay for another jeep for taking me to the top of the dunes (200,000 Vietnamese dong per person). I was a overprice for this tour as someone reviewed that it should be 7-12$ per person for this tour but I paid 21$. Moreover, there are many people there so that it’s hard to get a good picture.
It's advisable to ttavel there very early morning or late afternoon to avoid heat. Also, wear comfortable clothes and bring spare ones to change after visiting.

A must if in Mui Ne. We booked with our hostel, were picked up at around 4:45 AM when we were told to be there for 4 AM. Unfortunately, we missed the sunrise but got to see it on our way there. Walked up the dunes (12 minute walk.. did not want to pay 200k dongs per person for the ATV) and in overall, the view was nice. The rest of the tour consisted of the red dunes (not as impressive), the fish market (fun to go see the locals & what they picked up!!) and the fairy stream (actually very beautiful). All in all, fun experience and would recommend!

Don't miss to take 4 wheel jeep drive Taking the experience of 4 wheel jeep drive at White Sand Dune is one of the best experiences in Mui Ne. I really like this and very exciting when jeep drive to the top of white sand dunes.
Really cool in the morning It’s really beautifull to see the sunrise. Also they make you stop at different places so you visit Mui Ne with juste 1 little tour of 4 hours

Interesting to see these massive dunes. Like they were randomly placed there. Nobody could tell us the geography though. Took a 4X4 ride up the dunes. If you take the time to come here you need to do this.

We went here as part of a tour for sunrise and was very nice, however you have to pay quite a bit extra for a jeep to drive you up, you can obviously walk it's not that difficult but bit nicer to get higher up for better views

We booked a jeep, told we will be part of a 6 group(100,000d). We were due to leave at 4.30am , got collected at 4.50am then had to pick up more people, 3 were no shows which lead us to long waiting times. Sun came up while we were driving (5.20am) Walked to the top instead of paying 600k for a quad bike (you dont get to drive either) it took us 20mins to walk. Overall, nice views and was very clean.

ATV in the sand dune is a MUST! This is really amazing, I seen this somewhere in Australia but didn't expect it could be in South East Asia and this place won't disappoint you. You must engage the service to go up to the hill with jeep / ATV, I found ATV is more thrilling. Strong wind up on the hill and you got a full view at the hilltop, love this place. Everybody please go and enjoy yourselves.

It's a good place to visit by spoiled by the local operator. My advise is don't take the jeep or ATV, just walk. Not about the 200k VND but is about the great experience to walk at the sand dunes while enjoying the fantastic view. Stop wherever you want to take nice pictures and don't believe the local operator that's VERY FAR!!! just ignore them and walk follow the trail, you will not get lost because you are not the only one walking around. Enjoy!!!! Thank you for visiting our nature place and write this reviews Wishing you a wonderful day Written April 17, 2018 This response is the subjective opinion of the management representative and not of Tripadvisor LLC.
This response is the subjective opinion of the management representative and not of Tripadvisor LLC.
It was cloudy day, and we haven't saw all beuty. But we are happy, because this journey was interesting.

Beautiful but expensive We went on a jeep tour with a local driver which cost 600k and included visiting the fairy stream, fishing village, white dunes and red dunes. The white dunes are furthest away from Mui Ne (approx 15-20 km east) and you are left with few options when you get there. You can either walk but it is probably a 45-60min walk to get up to the top of the dunes (and then you have to walk back) or can pay for a jeep/quad. The prices are high (they have a captive market) so we paid a further 600k for a lift on the back of a quad up to the top. The views were good but not exceptional. As with other areas of Vietnam there was still litter up there which was a real shame. Overall would recommend but just a warning that it will cost a further 600k (at least) to get up the dunes.

One of attractions in Mui Ne where u can watch sunrise or sunset. we opt for sunrise so we wake about 4,30 am but the weather was about to rain but we did have good time in taking photo . we took tourguide from Mrs. Khanh VN Lotus . so transport n hotel is provided.

Big Sand Dunes in Tropical Vietnam-Amazing The sand dunes here are amazing. Inland from the sea and very high. Phan Thiet is the windiest place in Vietnam and when we went there it was extremely windy at the high point. The trip up in the 4 x 4 was a bit hairy but a lot of fun.Good photo shoot opportunities.

There is a lake nearby if i'm not mistaken. Unfortunately we didn't hv a chance to go there. View from the tip of the white sand was amazing. Atv costs 200k dong per person. The driver took 2 passengers at once, but when we need to get back to the station he took all 4 of us.... Omg! That was uncomfortable ! Some people would just walk but it was quite far. Still ... A good memory as a token 😍

Really good experience It was such a great trip to see he white sand Dunes. Be prepared to pay 200.000 VND for a 30 minuted jeep ride. If you don't want to pay you can walk to the top of the dunes and take amazing photos there.

We went to the sand dunes at 5:00am on a weekday to avoid the crowd and watch the sun rise. Walked across from one hill to the next while the kids were happily sliding down the hill on their own. It was not as bad walking up the big hill (the legs need a massage at end of day). We had a great day at this beautiful attraction. Can't stand those tourists who left their trash on the sand.

Beautiful nature but to many cars/quads/humans We did the sunrise tour wich I can really recommend. It cost us 100’ d per people. At these time the sand is not hot, it has a pleasant temperature. The sunrise was ok, because there were to many people/quads/cars and it was a little bit cloudy on the horizon so we saw the sun a little bit later (well that’s nature). In the end it is nice to sit there and enjoying the view. It is a good variety to the rest (nature) of vietnam.
Sunrise in the desert A place that are really beautiful and a must visit when you are in Mui Ne. But it is a attractiv activity and it’s going to be a lot of people, so be there so early as possible.

Beautiful sand dunes! Hesitated to ride with the locals driving ATVs because of back pain and walked to the top of the highest sand dune instead. It was a VERY hot, hard and long walk! I was close to giving up a couple of times, but pushed on and made it to the top. Here I was rewarded with a spectacular view! An awesome experience - and good exercise for me!

Nice sand dunes, one of the prettier things in Mui Ne. Be aware that you need to pay to get to the top. Quite a site though and even the crowds didn’t ruin t. Was fun to see and recommend, much better than the red dunes.

The white dunes are easily accessible with a scooter (about 25km from Mui Ne). They look impressive and are worth visiting. But prepare for crowds especially during sunrise and sunset. We went in the middle of the day and it was OK but don't forget the sun tanning creme. You can walk to the highest peak (about 15 minutes walk), rent a quad or they take you on top with a jeep. Especially the quads are completely unnecessary as they are noisy and dangerous (to people on foot and drivers themselves, as they are not used to such driving conditions) Overall it was a nice, hot and sandy experience.
Good but a lot of tourists We went there by an organised tour with seven other people. It was an nice to see place but not that special if you have seen dunes before. A lot of tourists around...

You don't get to ride the ATV. Waking up early in the morning at 4.00am for the tour to White Sand Dunes to see sunrise. You can choose between group of private tour for this. Of course when you are in group do watch out for the timing when your tour driver tells you what time to be back for the next destination. Once you reach White Sand. You will need to decide to walk or take Jeep / ATV. Personally after experience it myself I will not recommend taking Jeep / ATV. It pretty expensive cost $200 000 dong per person and the ride is as short as 3min - 5min. Just walk and enjoy the white sand while waiting for the sun to rise.
The wind killed us all 😂😂 Super windy when we went up the top of this sand dune, and it charges $10 usd per person for return ticket. The views are amazing but the sands got into our whole body lol.

Beautiful scenery! Was a really fun ATV ride. At first we were quoted 400,000đ for one ATV but we managed to get it down to 250,000 because we didn’t have enough money with us (we spent it all on the taxi there!)

We booked a private tour and a driver. He picked us up at our hotel 04.30.. Thats freaking in the middle of the night! Pitch black and a couple of cars in the street. When we arrive its maybe 20 other people there. We pay a vietnamese who take us up at the top of the sand dunes. The time is around 05.30 perhaps when we come up. So its we and around 50 chinese people who is wating for the sun to come up. I dont think a can explane the sight or the feeling but it was magical! The one downside is that its other people around you who is laud and noise but its worth every minute and second of it. If you are in Mui Ne. Go there for christ sake 😀
a lot of pester the Vietnamese offer from the virgin to the tears of the Virgin Mary. walk for half an hour will go.

Great views of sandy hills, a pond and tropical forests on the background. They offer buggies and jeeps, we took a jeep because it was our child’s choice. The walk isn’t far though, we saw people wandering there on foot, and thought we could walk too, if not for the kid. They also offer a slide down on some sort of a sleigh - not much fun, your hair will be stuffed with sand.

Great time on the 4 wheelers, especially with the boys that drive it. Quite aggressive on the deal once you get in, Blunt $ offers and very expensive for the local market, but nonetheless, a great time. We went on a moped, quite a bit away from Mui Ne so maybe a jeep tour would have been better, but they ask you for entrance fee and parking fee. Though its not that much, you never really know if they are just making a free business out of it or if it is really legit. The plastic sheet for 100k was such a rip off for what it is and felt taken advantage over as tourists but oh well.

Beautiful place and landscape The white sand dunes were beautiful, climbing up wasn't too hard and the view there was amazing. A very big place and if the sun is shining it may be too hot. Worth seeing.

To really enjoy the dunes, get yourself in a desert walking experience, it is really breathtaking! It is free so you don’t have to pay anything to access to the park. It can be hot and hard so take your time, avoid the horrible and super noisy ATV or jeeps or boogies. No go with your motorbikes! First it is faraway and second there is a police control in the way, read more about it! Rent a jeep for 20US$ for 5 hours, he will take you there but not on the dunes! Even with jeeps they cannot drive on the dunes, then you walk. Go to the dune in front if the lake (atv are not getting there) and gi down to the lake side, it is very calm! Explore the dunes between them, lot of hats lost and some trash, like everywhere. If you rent a jeep, tell him to get you directly to the dunes, he will try to stop you in the fairy stream so he can get his breakfast, remember YOU decide.

Sunrise is the best time to visit It was nice visit, but we got trapped by our tour because need to pay more vnd 400.000 for ATV to pick and drop us in the top of sand dunes ( which we already paid vnd 600.000 for jeep) I thought we gonna use the jeep to go up the sand dunes. So i feel little bit upset rent the jeep because we can go there with normal car or motorbike. Anyway it was nice experience.

Amazing place to view the sunrise. I did sprain my ankle sandboarding as the ground was very rough at the bottom so would recommend that sandboarding is done at the red sand dunes instead. The dune buggies spoilt an otherwise fabulous experience as they were so noisy.

Went on a sunrise private tour, with White Sand Dunes being one of the places. Peaceful watching the sunrise sitting on soft sand. ATVs zooming here and there. Very pricey but worth it I guess? 900,000 VND for 30 mins. Wish I could teleport my ATV. But absolutely loved riding the ATV on my own up and down the sand dunes. Comes with picture opportunities, near the lake and green trees etc. Highly recommended.

The white sand dunes are wonderful, we hired a driver in a jeep he was an excellent driver, on arrival there was some pressure to hire an ATV or take a 4 wheel drive ride we walked to the highest dune no worries, while all around us ATVs and 4 wheel drives were blasting around shattering all calmness with no regard to safety ride at your own risk,lots of lost hats lying around and the usual rubbish.My daughters wore there swimming goggles to keep the sand out of there eyes it's very windy with fine sand,we had fun.
Beautiful spot. Have to pay to get a jeep ride up or walk free. Very windy the day we were there but fun

Was on a surprise trip which brought me to Mui Ne. Our guesthouse host arranged our private tour on a Jeep to the usual spots and the White Sand Dunes was one of them. Loved the morning Adrenalin on the ATVs, recommended to go there for the sunrise. Tip: Head to the lake area after where it’s absolute peaceful, at least for the time we were there!

We visited this place in a group of 6 on a tour. When we reached there, we had a choice between walking/ Jeep/ ATV. We got the Jeep at 1.2mil dong, where the driver would take us to the top of the dunes and to the lake. Not sure if every other day is as windy, but when we were there the wind was crazy STRONG. Having a pair of shades is a MUST. It was not the best experience having sand flying and hitting hard against your skin. Please take into account the wind if you intend to walk to the top... (which is not advisable because it’s really far). Read other reviews about the noisy Jeeps and ATVs everywhere but surprisingly when I visited there were at most only 3 around us that come and go to pick people up, it was not disturbing at all. We were there at around 1pm on a Monday and it was rather empty.

We wanted to go on the ATVs but they charged a lot for a 10 minute ride so we passed on it and decided to explore and take pictures by ourselves. It is still a pretty amazing place, definitely something you should see while you are here.

We were here yesterday and my girls left bed it. I was initially worry about hiring the sand buggy because they were going down the dune probably at an angle of 70 degrees but it was safe and very fun. It’s a bit of a drive, probably about an hour drive from the main area of Phan Thet but was worth the trip.

Myself and my wife got a private sunrise jeep tour during our stay in muiNe. We were picked up at 4:30am from our hotel and got to the dunes around 5:15am. Our driver kept dozing off which was concerning but none the less we made it. We opted to pay the additional 400,000dong to get a quod bike upto the ridge which I feel was a really wise choice as the walk was not ideal. The sunrise itself was a little disappointing but it was nice to beat the crowds and there were not too many people around. The sunrise was probably not so good as it was a cloudy day. Regardless, I feel the red sand dunes were better in my opinion. Still definitely worth the visit.
Very nice little dessert in Vietnam. Would advise to take early morning jeep tour, the tour starts about 5am, beautiful to watch sunrise here.
White Sand Dunes is interesting place to go. It is very close to city, just few minutes. You can rent a plastic board and go down the hill. It is fun.

Better than the red ones If you have the time, walk around these dunes. The views are lovely and far from what you would expect in Vietnam, but more o some desolate landscape in North Africa. ATV and Jeep hire is expensive and in my opinion not worth it, if you have the time. We opted to walk and had a nice time

Take Jeep tour is better than you go by yourself, some Jeep tour included white and red sand dunes and fishing village, it’s more worth. But the when you reach white sand dunes, there are another charge for transport to send you to the sand hill, you can choose to walk, but it’s quite far and hot. There have ATV bike and Jeep, same price for both, charge by pax, each pax VND 200,000. Both with driver. But ATV bike only bring you to the top sand hill, Jeep will take you to two point. The view there is nice. You can play sand slide there which charge for VND 5,000 for each slide. Better apply stronger sun block if you don’t want to get tan skin. Recommended to bring along your sunglasses to avoid sand get into your eyes. Wear shorts is ok, but when there have strong wind, when those tiny sand pass by your leg will feel scratchy, but still ok since it’s not hurt.

I booked morning jeep tour and visited there in the morning. It’s unlucky that I could not see the sunrise since it was cloudy that day and the night before it was rains so the sand was a bit wet and that make I can see ATV wheel footprint. It’s hard to take a photo. If you’d like to visit here, I’d recommend to visit in the morning because it’s not too hot. And try to visit in winter season (mid November - April) because there is no rain and the sky will be blue and clear.
600k for a bike is a rip off in Vietnam - also disturbes the peace and serenity of this place. Overall 4stars
Nice place for teenagers to fly about on a quad bike through the sand, not the cheapest ride but both enjoyed the freedom and exhilaration of off road sand quads. Worth an hour of your time.
should go if having time Nice sand dune. Should go earlier in the morning, remember to bring your camera and sun hat. Tour on a jeep is recommended.

Suitable Desert for Photograph! I joined the $5 morning tour to go to this site. The location is far enough from my hotel, about a hour by jeep. About 5.30am we arrived there and people came to us for offering ATV rent, I thought it was expensive (about VND 300.000/people), little bit annoying, so we decided to walk inside. This is the first time I came in desert, and it was wonderful experience. Morning Sun made a perfect scenery to capture. Actually there were a lot of tourists when i visited, but if you can find the perfect angles to shoot, it doesn't matter. As the place is wide enough, not too difficult to find beautiful spots for capturing photos.

Desert in South East Asia Stayed in Mui Ne for less than 36 hours. There's actually nothing much to do except the sand dunes and fairy stream, but as I've never been to a desert before, I wanted to visit Mui Ne during my trip in Vietnam. The white sand dunes was vast, and its good fr photo taking. We signed up for the afternoon-evening tour and spend about 45 mins here. There's 2 types of ATV ride (30min), the smaller one (500k vnd, 2 person to drive and ride on your own) and the bigger one (800k vnd, the staff will have to drive u). We took up the smaller one as we wanted to try driving ourselves up, but we were not aware that our smaller atv is unable to reach the top, and the staff had to come help us when our wheels got stuck in the sand. Overall the place is nice, and worth visiting. PS: sunblock is a must! Selfie stick too if u are visiting in small groups

I'd prefer it before they invested ticketbooth and the motorbikes and jeeps. The sand dunes is still what it's been, tremendous view, rare desert look in a tropical country. However, comparing to what I visited back in 2009 which is more quiet, no tour services with motor roaring, jeep driving ON the sand dunes, it's getting worse for me. We had to pay for the entry which we hadn't before. Or we could've driven off to another entry where they don't control the entry because we could enter it by just going a bit further. The photos are still great but I hate the tracks that motor and jeeps leave on the sand.

This was my first trip to Mui Ne, and all I heard anyone talking about was the Sand Dunes. There are two Sand Dunes, Red and White. The White are bit further away from the city but worth visiting. We visited both as part of a tour package, but I enjoyed the White more. You basically have to pay to ride ATV/4-wheeler, but it's not outrageous. I didn't see anyone walking from the entrance. Plus, the drivers go really fast and it's fun going to the slopes. It can be a bit crowded but the views from the top are superb with sharp contrasts in the landscape. Definitely worth checking out.

Nice scenery, but loud. Dunes themselves are quite impressive but very noisy due to tourists on quad bikes. Our tour guide (essentially just a driver on our $10 private tour) refused to drive up the dunes although he had driven all the way there in all old jeep 4x4. We walked up the left dunes, which was very nice. Best sight in Mui Ne by far!

white sand dunes very nice, go up and down with the ATV is very very shock,but its very expensive for the ATV fees! the view is very very beautiful， very suitable for take a lot of nice photo， morning will feel very cold!
A long ride out to the dunes Lots of fun driving the quads a little expensive for the short time they let you ride but overall it was a good time
We went for a sunrise tour, bit cloudy and drizzling but the sunrise colours were still good and the dune buggy experience worth it!

Really stunning!! Must do when you visit Vietnam! Lot better than red dunes about 20km south of the white dunes. Less crowded but bigger! ATV's driving all the time, so they destroy the "chill and nature factor" a little bit.

If you're after peace and quiet you won't find it here. A rode out by scooter (took an hour-ish) and walked up the dunes. The walk is a mission but worth the savings as the quads or rides are $$$$ if you opt in, male sure you bargain!! Nice lake and a few good spots for pics. Note: we left late enough to miss the hoardes of visitors chasing the sunrise. As we were nearing most were leaving in their jeeps. No police were enroute but on the way back, they were just setting up. Before they knew we were tourists and whistled at us, I had already passed and did not acknowledge the fact they were there so we were VERY lucky!!! They Were just after the left hand turn nearing the large mountain as pic below. Enjoy and ride safe!!!
Its good place to see sunrise if no clouds. Place is nice. Not to far from Mui ne. But i think just for one time.

Nice place to visit but keep safe We have rent a small jeep for the full familly, 800 000vmd is not cheap but still worth it. It is nice to see the whole place from the top as well as to put your feet in the warm water. Be carrefull with the quads that are driving all arround with unconsiderate risks. 2 women were on a big quad with a driver that tried to impress them with high speed when 1 of them were kicked out of it. It took the driver about 300m to the understand that the girl behind him was asking him to stop as her friend was on the floor... luckily she stand up and were just covered with sands but not injuried. Nice place to see, if you want to enjoy jeep or quads it is a nice opportunity but keep in minde it can be dangerous !

Went there for sunrise and I love the journey there by a Jeep! It is not as crowded as Red Sand maybe because the place is bigger and we went there really early. Take the ATV ride if you prefer not to walk, it's relatively cheap so just hop on and the driver will speed his way (hold on tight) to the peak! After that you can either take the ATV back to the entrance or take a walk back. Overall, I would recommend this place.

Cool place to see, great place if you have money to burn. I went to the white sand dunes with my wife. We rented a motorbike in Phan Thiet and drive there ourselves. It turned out to be much farther from the city than we anticipated... 35 kilometers, so it was a long drive but still enjoyable to see the countryside. On our way there we were unfortunate enough to hit a police stop (which of course was only pulling over foreigners) and they made up some bogus reasons why I needed to pay a fine to them. Most locals will tell you this happens often and regardless of how well you were driving you will still need to pay to get out of it. Make your case but in the end you WILL end up paying them something. We finally arrived at the white sand dunes and it was honestly very beautiful. Being a warm sunny day, we only stayed for about 30 minutes -- we climbed up to the summit of one dune, reflected on the sights around us, took some photos and left. Why so quick? Because we weren't ready to spend as much money as all the fun stuff cost. At the dunes, they offer 1-person ATV's for 300,000 dong ($13 USD), 2-person ATV's for 600,000 dong ($27 USD), and pick-u trucks for 900,000 dong ($40 USD) per 20 minutes. They also operate hot-air balloons near the dunes, which run from 5am - 8am (5am pick up, 6-7am balloon ride, and 7-8am breakfast and return trip) but will cost you a whopping $310 USD. As I said, lots of options if you have the money to blow. If you're like us and are budgeting, at the base of the dune in the shade of small wood are many shops and they even have a section where you can ride an ostrich (for 1 walk around its pen) for less than $1 USD. The White Sand Dunes are worth seeing... for the beauty if you're budgeting or for the fun if you're not.

Lots of jeeps and quads Beautiful white sand dunes. Nice to climb a dune and watch the view. The sand is very hot, so wear good shoes. You can also drive through the dunes by jeep or quad. It makes it crowded, noisy and dangerous.

The jeep driver who brought our group was running a bit late as he made a pit stop to see some acquaintances. Please ensure that you do make it to the location before the sun rays start to brighten the sky. You will still need to make it out further to enjoy the view of the first sunlight flooding the expanse sand. We unfortunately missed the sunrise but still got a view of the sun hanging low in the horizon. Sadly, the jeep driver (so-called guide) kept rushing us and giving us tight deadlines to view the place. As such, he insisted and colluded with the ATV renters that we go out to the center on an ATV. The quote was for VND 600,000 each person.. So preposterous it was laughable! Even after trying to negotiate, they only went as far as VND 400,000 per person. I think they wanted to take advantage of us since we were all females. The driver even pressured that walking would take us 3hrs to the highest dune and back and we only had 40mins. In the end, we firmly said no and started hiking and semi-jogging on the fine, soft sands. My thighs were killing me but we had covered quite a distance that was acceptable to us to take in the views. I was happy enough at that spot as the higher points were crowded with other visitors. What made the journey bad were the lawless ATV riders. I know that the desert is open and wide, but with such frequent commuting, there should be a particular set route and direction that the ATVs are allowed to go. We could even see tracks that stayed for a long while. Nevertheless, we made the best of it and took videos and photos to capture the moments. Took in some quiet reflective moments too. Great for a variety of poses. Get creative or just be natural. Sliding down the sand dunes was the other favourite activity of visitors. We were next to a steep slope and so watched a few people trying to sled down the sandy slope. You may rent a board/mat from the local youth/aunties roaming around. But if you'd like to go multiple times at multiple locations, it is best that you come with your own mat. From our vantage point, we could see the nearby lake. It looked like a beautiful oasis. Finally, try to trace back the same route you took to avoid getting lost. To me, all the 3 entrance points looked similar and we wasted time searching to exit out. The consolation prize was that a moile vendor was around and since our driver was nowhere in sight (even had another driver, whose car he was parked behind, sounding his horn to notify our driver to move his vehicle so he could leave), we bought a couple of baked goods and together broke bread for breakfast. That desert may not come anywhere near the great deserts around the world, but standing there already made me feel like one of the grains of sand in this desert world.
Kids loved jumping off the dunes...and actually...so did I! No need to get motorbikes or jeeps...just go on foot to enjoy it.

Beautiful scenery of sunrise and desert If you can't go that far to Dubai to see the real desert, then you can come here. Many review said that its not worth it etc. But after all its up to you. Did this half day tour together with Red Sand Dunes, Fairy Stream and Fishing Village from 4.30 am to 8/9 am, and this White Sand Dunes are the best among those three. Here are things that you need to know: 1. Its not too far from downtown Mui Ne 2. dont wear shoes if you dont want sands go inside your feet. sandals are better but maybe not flip flops. 3. when you come to the entrance, locals will offer you to go to the top with ATV and the price is 850.000 vietnamese dong for 2 people in one ATV, with the driver (so 3 people in 1 ATV). Not sure if they raised up the price on us because one of my friend is white. 4. they drive very fast and scary up and down the desert. for those who like riding on a scary roller coaster can be very exciting. but no helmet was given. be sure dont hold any loose items, as it might fly away and you will never find it again in the desert. 5. its quite windy, so expect the sands will be all over your face, mouth, eyes and body. sands was soft too. 6. you can take pictures up there, enough time. 7. light clothing with darker color is better (as it'll be dirty because of the sands) 8. I went on sunrise and so many people were there already. the good thing is, its not too hot, and you'll go to Fairy Stream after, so the river in Fairy Stream will wash off all the sands you have on your feet. 9. when it is almost the end of trip, on top of the desert, the ATV driver will ask you for additional tips 100.000 or 200.000 vietnamese dong and they force you to pay. 10. private tour is better especially if you travel in a big group.

Was quite an interesting experience. The dunes are amazing and absolutely fantastic contrast compared to the rest of Vietnam. The ATV was crazy (choose the bigger one, since it is able to go on top of all dunes unlike the smaller one). However, I would strongly recommend you to go early in the day or take the morning tour since the dunes get overwhelmed with tracks of the quad tires...
Was a nice sunrise tour we did. Got lost on the way back to the truck which made us spend heaps less time on the the rest of the tour. The dunes were pretty sweet ae

Quite a distance away from town, but it's a nice journey by motorbike. Do make sure you have a valid licence though as there is a roadblock along the way. The place itself offers some amazing views, and plenty of nice photo opportunities. There are ATV and Jeep rides there, but they are overpriced even for tourists. Do go at sunrise or sunset, as sand and hot sun makes an uncomfortable combination.
Saw the white and red sand dunes for the white the quad bikes were rather expensive but the view was stunning enough, also red sand dunes unfortunately had some litter still worth the trip!

When you are in Mui Ne, you have to visit the sand dunes. There are tour available to get you here (and Red Sand Dunes) which you can enquire from your hotel. If you want to go to the top you can rent ATV (it will cost, price is for 2ways), the view is beautiful, love it!

Catch the sunrise via motorbike coastal drive at 5.30am All over Mui Ne, you'll find this famous "Sunset" and "Sunrise" tour which are guided tours that covers the main attractions in Mui Ne. Our biggest mistake was giving Mui Ne only 1-day, and we had an odd timing coming in via HCMC and arriving after dinner, taking the bus to Dalat first thing the next morning. It was one of our worst decisions in this trip as we hardly covered any grounds (only the white sand dunes, but barely!) The most rewarding trip was biking there on our own via the coastal drive. The views were stunning and it's a must-catch if you are taking a self-guided tour. Don't miss it! Google maps will easily show you the way and do make sure you're travelling along the coast instead of the other highway. Catch the sunrise when you reach the white sand dunes. You'll find that you won't be alone as many of the tour groups of the "sunrise" tour will be with you!

Reading reviews only gives an insulated feel of the White Sand Dunes in Mui Ne. The 4.30am ride in an open jeep jolts all senses awake. After a second visit within 4 months, it allows me to give a good perspective now. During the cooler months the jeep ride can be quite chilling. The short ride on the ATV to the top of the highest sand dune was thrilling but save the best for the downward ride back..... hold tight. Being the first up there was fantastic as the sand was untouched except for bird footprints after an overnight rain. All round views are awesome but pray hard that there are no clouds to musk the rising sun. Trod on the fine sand without footwear and its therapeutic. You can rent a slide to go down the slope but then regret when you struggle back up. It's quite surprising that such a unique heritage place exists so close to habitation.

Took hotel guided tour to both sand dune. White sand was more interesting than red. Depart at 430am just to catch the sunrise. The sand buggy motor was fun. A must during the visit. Could try sand surf if possible.
Put on your sunblock, bring your hat, and have fun! Consider to rent a jeep or atv to go up the sand dunes, it's too huge to walk!

vast area of dunes. The dunes were large and majestic. It is quite far from the city and would be expensive if you are taking a taxi there. There is also an entry fee. the ATV ride is expensive but worth it. They prefer to ride without instead of having you having free roam with the ATV.

Went in the afternoon and witness the beautiful sunset. I'll recommend to visit during weekdays so it's not really crowded. We rented a small atv for 40mins to have an easy access. It's worthy rather than walking plus the man who drove us was friendly. Literally experienced sand bathing during this trip! But it's really cool. Experience dessert safari in Asia.

Wake up early to get the 4 am trip out here for sunset and you'll be happy you did! We had second thoughts when our alarm went off but its so tranquil at this time, I couldn't imagine being here in the heat of the day but when the sun was rising, though the clouds covered our view, it's almost magical being out here, among the flour-like sand, with the water looking out beyond the sun. A beautiful experience.

Breathtaking view when you reach the top of the sand dunes. I went to the sunrise tour and it was awesome, except the fact that we had to pay another 500k vnd for the ATV. The jeep wont bring you to anywhere near to the top. The only Choice is rent the ATV because you will have to walk for 30 minutes to reach the top. I m not sure if other jeep tour company provide services that reach the top by using jeep, maybe you can ask around.

Really cool but would be better without the quad bikes I walked this and it was cool. Would be 5 stars except for one guy driving the quad bikes around like an idiot. Would be better imo if the quad bikes were not there. Also note that the quad bikes are kind of a rip off, approx 400k rental for less than an hour and a lot of them can't get up the steep hills because they're only 2 wheel drive. So you'll end up walking a lot anyway.

We went on a day trip and the white sand dunes were included. When we arrived you have the option to rent a quad bike which wasn't much money at all however the quad bike we were given was useless and we couldn't make it up the steep sand dunes but they soon swapped it! Would definitely recommend visiting as part of a day trip!
Interesting experience! Interesting...A large white sand hill is spreading but at the edge, there is a blue river beside. Sitting in the Jeep car and drive over the sand hill is so stimulated

We came here by scooter around 7 am and the place was deserted (no pun intended hehe). The sand is not smooth like in the advertised pictures, but it was still an awesome sight and worth the trip! Just rent a scooter, no need to join a tour unless you strongly prefer those. We got some cool pictures!

If we talk about Mui Ne i'm sure the first place we'll think about desert !! And white sand dunes is so beautiful like a dream. But you must hire motorbike for go to the top even if you must walk a little bit far but the problem is the weather is so hot!! You'll get a lot of beautiful photos from here sure!!

First time seeing Sand Dunes Very exciting place to ride 4 wheeler bike. If you can't drive, give some tip money to staff and they will drive for you and it have more fun. Price is a little expensive for 4 wheeler bike.

So hot I almost turned into dried squid The view was nice, I enjoyed and pleased that I went there eventhough it was too much sun out. They have a jeep that will take you to this white sand dunes, red sand dunes, Fairy stream, and fishing village. With 100,000vnd per person. But the price is only for the ride on the jeep, other services are not included. You will need to pay seperately for other services so make sure to ask carefully and specifically for all services. When you get to the White sand dunes, there will be cars or sand motor-bikes (that 3 wheels thing) for rent so that you can get to the top of the sand dunes and to the lake side next to the dunes. It was a good place to go but it was too hot I that didn't remember much. The pictures we took there were pretty nice, still.
Well worth the early start at 4am. We visited the white and red sand dunes, the white dunes are better

Amazingly fun and beautiful place We were wonderfully surprised at the size of the sand dune and the beauty of it, especially during sunset. The ATV rides down the steep sand dune was definitely the highlight of the visit. As was sheer heightened adrenaline throughout the fast and steep ride on the ATV. A MUST if you visit this place.
Definitely worth a visit, we booked the white sands, red sands and the fairy stream together via out hotel and i think it was around £5 each!

Good for watching sunrise I joined a local tour to there on 2 April 2017. It took an hour from my hotel in Mui Ne. The tour dropped me at the entrance. The tourist may choose beach car instead of walking. It took me about thirty minutes to walk to the top of the dune. It is beautiful for watching sunrise there. It is a must if you go to Mui Ne.

We visit in the early morning, see the sun rise in the east..better weather, the sky is open...Nice view and very interesting. Second time to visit here at Mui Ne. Enjoy with the sandboard..The entrance is free. It is natural.

There' re many paid activities here such as ATV, jeep, sandboard. You should prepare exact money (so you won't be cheated) if you want to play sandboard. The guys offer us so high price to play unlimit time but we bargain them just 40k for 4 of us for unlimit playing and they agree. After we finished playing we paid them 40k for the deal and they want us more money. We are lucky that we paid them exact money, so no need to change, otherwise, i'm sure they wont give us changes. So be careful.

Nice sand dunes like deserts Awesome dunes to visit Mui Ne. You can also enjoy stunning sunrise and sunset views at the dunes for great photos. The entrance is free to all but you may spend a small sum of money on some games or activities here. It's a little bit exhausted to climb the dunes, so remember to bring along some fresh water.

We visited at sunrise which was nice as the sand was still cool underfoot. The dunes are very photogenic. It's a little walk up the dunes if you want the best spot, or you can take a ride on the back of a quad bike if you want an adrenaline rush.

Had to take a jeep/atv to enter the sand dunes. 1 000 000 dong/hour for a jeep (able to sit 5-6 pax) , 600 000 dong/ 1/2 an hour for a atv (able to sit 1-2 pax) - didn't know this before going there.. This price comes with a driver. You can try negotiating with the driver, managed to bring the price down to 800 000 dong for a jeep. Best to visit the white sand dunes to catch the sunrise.. PS wind is strong, so the sand will feel like micro cuts on your skin if you are baring some skin 😂 long sleeves and pants advised Looking to see sunset or do sand surfing, head to the red sand dunes, white sand dunes isnt the right place for that.. 😊

It was an unfortunate day. I left red sand dunes around 4.30pm in order to reach white sand dunes before sunset. Unfortunately during the last 7km, my rented motorbike's tyre blew up. We were quite lucky to find a repair shop nearby but overcharged us for 300000 dong just to change the tube. Then we reached white sand dunes slightly after 6pm. It was already dark and cloudy, which didnt give a good scene. My gf said it was ok as atleast it was burning hot and there were no people at all.
But only really worth it if you never been to a proper desert or are stuck in Mui ne for a long time. Btw you can get yourself beer/coffee/Vietnamese food on the entrance.

My friends and I arrived at Mui Ne in the afternoon by bus from Dalut. The first thing you should be reminded is not to trust any special offer or touring promotion provided by the travel agency at the bus stop. What you should do there is to ask for the lowest price only and keep that in mind to ask for even better discount at the hotel. We asked the receptionist to help us rent a jeep car for 6 to travel to the attractions around Mui Ne and she gave us a good price. At White Sand Dunes, we found it wonderful and photogenic spot to take photos with your friends. The weather would be nicer without the rain, though. Next thing we would like to suggest is that you don't need to pay for a quad bike to get to the top of the dune. (Why don't save money for enjoying your seafood dinner there instead?) You'd better walk yourselves and could find many beautiful spots along the way within 30 minutes. But if you still want to take the bike, don't forget to ask for discount! My last recommendation is that you should wear blue or indigo clothes because that would best match with the scenary for your photoshoot! Plan a little bit about the time you go there. Avoid rainy season and don't forget to bring one photographer in the your team! Enjoy!

We went for sunrise which was amazing only downer is that there is a loads of people flying around on quad bikes which can feel uncomfortable at times considering your relying on a complete stranger at the hands of a quad bike to see you over a dune doing stupid speeds not to kill you !! But was worth the 4:30am get up to go see !

We had the moon on the one side and then the sunrise on the other side. It's very crowded but still beautiful It's not really cheap to go up there with the quads, but walking takes long I guess. Nice and for sure a must-see

Hidden paradise in Vietnam. If you think Vietnam just have Halong Bay you must visit Mue Ne 😂 Prefer go to go this place with private tour out rent a bike, because if you following open tour, they just give you 1 hour for explore in here.

These dunes are lovely and beautiful, although upon entry remember you do not have to go for a jeep or quad bike ride. It does require entry into the park so bring some spare cash (our tour did not warn us)
We woke up at 3:30 am to see a sunrise. Views are beautiful. But road was a bit difficult. It took near 1,5 hour at night. Take sleepers with you, there are a lot of sand)

We visited the White Sand Dunes which are beautiful and vast, and hired a quad bike for a family of 3 and rode to the top of one of the biggest dunes with one of the helpers. We were told one price by the booking agent and then charged more than double on the day, which was very annoying but couldn't say no as our son was really looking forward to the experience. Also, be warned that I doubt you would be covered by your travel insurance if you were to have an accident! as there are no forms signed, just pay and ride!

Overall a nice attraction - if you are in Mui Ne area and have the time and means to get here (it is about 45 minutes from resort strip, maybe 20-30 minutes from red sand dunes/Mui Ne proper) you should go see them. Better than the red sand dunes (much cleaner with less trash) but still an issue with too many tourists, and ATV's flying all over at too high of speeds. A worthwhile visit with some good photo opportunities, but so many nice things to see in Mui ne area that if you cannot make it you should not feel bad about it.

The White Dunes are a good place to visit either for sun-rise or before sunset. It is a 45 min drive from Mui Ne hotels. Once there, it will make sense to rent a jeep with a local driver (there are many of them there) and take a ride up and down the dunes. The view is fantastic. Wear sunglasses to protect eyes from the sand blowing in the face with the wind.
Pleasant trip and visit, but after the dunes in Namibia - not a WOW. Beware of the quad rides at the dunes - they just give you a lift to the top and thats all.

Quad bike is fun but DO RENT THE BIG QUADS Quad bike is fun but DO RENT THE BIG QUADS We join a day tour in the afternoon, and they'll jeep you here by sunset time to enjoy the view. White sand dunes is beautiful like a small desert, you can either walk and take your time, or rent a quad bike to ride for 400,000d small one or 800,000d for the big one (45mins). The small quads were all in a terrible condition, got stuck easily while challenging the bigger dunes, and the seat is literally disconnected to the bike, thank god my boyfriend knows a bit about bikes... somehow we still had fun though.

It is some 50 kilometers from Mui Ne, but it is very nice. You can rent a jeep for reasonable price, which will drive you on a dunes with few stops. There is a nice lake, so you can sit on dunes and enjoy the lake view. WHen we visited dunes, it was national holiday, so there was too many people for our opinion, but... what can you do? It is nice half day excursion from Mui Ne, combining with red sand dunes.

We did the early morning sunrise trip which included white sand dunes, red sand dunes, fisherman's market and the fairy stream. Once out at the dunes you can either rent a quad bike or another Jeep at 150,000 pp and they drive you out onto the sand dune and up onto the ridge. Since that was far more than we had paid for the whole trip we just walked up as we had an hour to look around and it happened to be plenty of time, even found sound untouched sand for some awesome pics. Great experience and something I definitely did not expect to be seeing in Vietnam.

My Southern Vietnam Trip Wow, this is the real desert! Create your own creative photos there. The desert is widely. You can rent the ATV Car there or the tour with Jeep Car. You can go and see the sunrise, but I'm not sure how beautiful it is 'cause I cannot make it. This is your 'to do lists' when you are in Muine, Vietnam.

Went at 430 AM Atv lift not really necessary. Took a private jeep for 2 for 550k VND... we were picked up at our hotel at 430 am. When we got to the white sand dunes, the jeep driver told us to get on the atv with a driver and the driver would take us to the top of the sand dune and pick us up in an hour for 200k VND each. It was dark out and the spot where he was going to drop us off seemed quite far. Maybe it was the lack of sleep or whatever but 12 dollars canadian for a 1 minute lift there and a minute back on an atv isna bit excessive. All in all, it was a beautiful sunrise and it was the perfect temperature. We only got an hour so we didnt really go far. Also because we went at the time we did, we didnt get to rent any atv's. Not sure if this is an option during the day but it would be sweet to go around and see more. It s quite beautiful though
They'll charge you 150K to ride a quad or jeep (regardless of how many people the put on it). It's nice to see taking into account they Mui Ne doesn't offer much to do besides water sports

We had a great morning on the White Sand Dunes. From the reviews it said it was 400k to hire a buggy, but we found that it was actually 900k to hire a larger buggy that would take us up all the hills. Although the price was a little unexpected, it was still a lot of fun and we didn't regret spending the money!
It's funny to see those dune in the middle of nowhere but nothing amazing and the huge amount of tourists does not help it ...

The larger of the two dunes in the area. ATV's can be rented for approx. 300k/half hour at the time of the review. I heard there were motorbikes available but didn't see any either for rent or on the dunes whilst there. Be warned, the ATV's are speed capped (for safety) and do bog regularly as a result. You can also pay to go on an ATV with a local on a non capped vehicle and this looked very fun (they'll go off the edge of the big dunes and take you over jumps etc. if you like). If you are going to do any sand boarding, do it here. These dunes are much larger than the red ones. Food and drink available at the base.

Sunrise on the white sand dunes Very touristic tour, but still beautifull to see, it's one of the things dto do in Mui Ne. You can do some quad to go to the highest dune to see the sunrise but the amount of people to the top of that sand dune have discoraged me. I enjoy walking around and taking pictures...It looks like a semi-desert...

We loved the sunrise trip to the sand dunes. We took breakfast and watched the sun go up over the dunes. We read reviews before alerting us to the cost of the quad bikes not included (200k pp) they were fun but we probably could have walked in hindsight. The tour included the red sand dunes, a visit to a fishing village and a walk along a stream. Our driver was nice but wasn't a tour guide, having said that we had a lot of fun and overall for everything thought it was worth the money.
We need to pay about 200,000 VND to ride the jeep up the sand dunes but we think the view worth it. You can easily take picture like a pro with view like that...

My bf and I had fun running down the sand dunes. Getting up them again was more of a task but it was good exercise. There wasn't very many tourists so we were able to get some nice pics with just the dunes in the background. Be aware that the speed limit before you come to the white dunes is only 40km and if you exceed this it's highly likely you will get fined.

Beware of scammers - renting a jeep is not necessary The sand dunes in itself was mesmerising but we were disappointed when we realise later that we had paid 800,000 VND for a short ride to white sand dunes - not worth the money we paid . Like my partner , if you have been to other sand dunes prior to this one then a visit to this place might not excite you , but as it was my first experience I enjoyed it . Be weary of touts trying to scam money out of you by offering pricey jeep ride and try your best to cover your legs as the sand accompanied with the wind tends to prick your bare skin . Carrying a scarf can be very helpful .

Good place to visit..but crowded.. The white Sand dunes are a good place to visit to view the sunrise. Also, a good option to try quad biking. On the con side, it was crowded even when we went early in the morning. Felt like the place is being exploited due to tourism.

I believe there would be quite a number of entities running/manning the white sand dunes. My family and I opted for a half day tour (fairy stream, fishing village, white sand dunes and red sand dunes in the package) from the hotel and jolly rode on a jeep to this destination. Upon arrival, after more than 40mins ride, the tourists have options; either to take the All-Terrain-Vehicle (ATV) or another jeep to enter the dunes area with additional cost, if my memory didn't fail me; the jeep (can take 7 pax) is 1M VND and the ATV is 400k ATV. These are fine prints, so watch out and bring sufficient cash. Just check with the source/place you make booking to avoid shock and complication. The place is worth a visit, really, apart from the fine prints portion. Nice place to take photos, try out the sand sliding and at least experience the sahara.

The dues are stunning and very dramatic and beautiful in photos. We were told when we arrived that we had the option of taking jeeps (with drivers), small ATV's, and large ATV's. We should've taken one of the jeeps or the large ATV. The small ATV we rented leaked oil all over our legs, couldn't make it up the main dune, and was super uncomfortable for me as a passenger. I was basically sitting on a metal grill as we bounced up and down the dunes. We saw some people just walk up and that seemed a better option as well.

Afternoon well spent! I feel that the White Sand Dunes are better than the Red Sand Dunes in the sense that there are no locals bothering you. The ATV is expensive so my friends and I just walked around the sand dunes instead. We just sat anywhere we liked and enjoyed the silence, compared to the locals bothering you all the time over at the Red Sand Dunes.

We got a wild quad bike rollercoaster type tour that unfortunately was over all too quick. Its not cheap, but if your budget can stretch to it, then its a blast. For the more sedate, take a jeep and get a little longer tour but without the thrills. Certainly impressive sand dunes.The little eating place is ok if you like speedway, the noise. We got there on scooters from Muine, about 25 km . Beware traffic cops, have your driving license with, and or some $s for their favourite charity.

The quad bikes are fun!! We didn't end up seeing much of the white sand dunes, we hired a quad bike from the entrance (my first time!) and the whole rest of the tour here was just an adrenaline fueled blur. If I had my time again I would give this site the attention it deserves and jump on a quad bike somewhere else. I had to look back on our video to see what the sand dunes actually looked like! Also, something to be mindful of - our quad bike got stuck at one point and a kind local offered to help us get out of that situation. He encouraged us both to jump on the bike, and naively we thought it had something to do with weight balances, but then he took us speeding up and over the dunes for a joy ride. Once he was done he asked for a tip and was quite persistent to give more and more - we ended up giving quite a bit more than what it was worth!

The dunes and lake are very pretty and worth a look, Especially as it costs nothing to see them. I would say however to be very careful. There are quad bike hire places so quad bikes everywhere. I rented one myself and soon realised it was a bit of a free for all. There are no designated areas for bikes and walkers, just all mixed together. I saw some scared walkers as bikers rode dangerously close. I remember going up a dune thinking that if a quad was coming the other way there would be nothing that I could do. The quad bikes are maintained to work rather than to be safe. I did an easyrider tour and my guide told me that there are many accidents and injuries such as broken bones. Go see it but be careful. If you ride your own moped here to see them be warned as the police almost every day have a check point to pull over people to see their liciences etc which most holiday makers do not have. A payment of about 15000000 dong is enough to pay them off.

A little fun in the sun The drive out tof the dunes was as beautiful as the hills themselves, just be wary of getting a quad. Although fun and not too expensive, many of themy do not work well and cannot even make it up the dunes. Bald tires and leaking gas tanks seemed a little too common unfortunately

We visited as a couple. We booked a jeep tour round Mui ne for $25 and visited both the white dunes, red dunes, fishing village and red canyon. We got a jeep and drove to the top of the dunes. We then got taken at high speed up and down the dunes one at a time then together at break neck speeds. It was terrifying but good fun!! I was a little concerned someone might get hurt but it was a good experience. The dunes themselves are interesting and worth a visit to take some arty pictures.
