Never seen a sand dune before so it was really beautiful to me! Saw the terrible reviews left by other travellers thus we didn't get any noisy quad or jeep up. Hiked up wasn't tt difficult. 10 minutes top and you'll get rewarded with a beautiful view! And the lotus lake at the end of the dunes is as beautiful as well!
A nice and fun place to visit! Great view and fun riding the quad bike.....going on a hot day will be better for sure.
Amazing place, Sahara-like red sand dunes overlooking Mui Ne, with ocean on one side and jungle on the other.

I have read up and its true that to enter the tour in the morning as there is lesser crowd and tourists. I have book the private jeep tour with Hai Tuan Travel. The driver was okay as he dont know english that well so basically we didnt communicate much. However, when i send an email down asking for confirmation, the agent responded very fast, giving us assurance that the jeep will come at the allocated time. Tour started from 4.30am and ended around 9.30am-10am Arrival at the white sands dunes, do be prepared to pay another jeep transport fee to arrive at the top of the dunes in a short while. You may also walk into the dunes but its a long way walk and pretty tough walking in the sand. I saw tourist walking there but im gald that i took the transport. Basically, you are paying to ride on the ATV as well.,

Beautiful Place in Vietnam The place is located at Mui Ne, left side the mainroad if we start from the Mui Ne city. The place is beautiful but it's dirty one if compare to the red sand dunes, as all people can access the area. The area is free of cash, everyone can stop by from their trip and walk to white sand. Its many hill and good for the photo background. Good time to visit the place is in the morning or early afternoon. On the right side of mainroad are many food shop with cheap price.

The place is very touristy, a lot of motorbikes and go-carts. But very nice sunsets. Next to is a small lake which looks really nice. Be careful if you go there by motorbike and you don't have a international or Vietnamese driving license the police might stop you. Between Mui Ne and White Sand Dunes they stop all foreigners.
It is worth going there but dont expect a peaceful atmosphere. Hundreds of tourists rent motorbikes and trucks to drive around the dunes. Really sad that they dont stop this stupid rentals.

Cool & Spacious Desert This place is huge and filled with extreme amounts of sand. The location is a bit far off from the main city and requires private transportation to get there. For some fun action, ATVs can be rented to experience the ups and downs of this 'desert'. On a windy day, the sand can get really irritating, flying into everything that you have. The pictures taken here will surely be great for every angle is just right. Though there were many who mentioned that one can do sand-boarding here, there weren't any renting out sand boards when I was there. This would be a good place to do sand-boarding for the sand dunes are steeper than those in Red Sand Dunes. Be careful of locals charging exorbitant rates for the ATVs.

Very nice place to see We went to Mui ne for 2 days and by around 11am we rented bikes and we head to this place and did the tour by ATVS it's a short trip but we loved its very beautiful place, dessert by the lake, they were asking for 700k but we drop down the price to 500k I think you can go a bit lower than that and we were lucky with the weather it was cloudy not a hot day so u might better go early to avoid the heat if it's clear day. And in the way bck we were stoped by the police and it's a scam u can't escape just pay money to go away or hide ur money and said you got nothing on u so than they will let u go after they search u. We knew about the police scam but we wanted to ride a bike going there it's beautiful country side road.

Bit of a scam really. The place is quality itself but you pay 15k to get in and then you have to either get a quad bike (the smaller ones don't get up the hills, the bigger ones are 1m!! Get yourself a group of 6 people and get in a jeep for 600k between you. He'll drive you up to the top and around for a bit.
Place was so and so, but I rented ATV and this saved the place visit....that was fun....even I seated back and local guide drive down (but he was very good).

The tour guide only gives you 40 minutes so you have no other option but to get a quad or you won't make it back in time ( it all feels like a set up to get more money). They try and charge you 400,000 dong we were not happy with this and walk off they followed us up and gave to us for 300,000. The quids get suck and don't even make to the top! But its still very beautiful and worth going.

Beautiful scenery but spoilt by the jeeps and ATVs The white sand dunes are absolutely beautiful and unique in South East Asia. The area is not big, you do not have to be afraid of getting lost but big enough to take some great pictures witn nothing but sand dunes. Went there for the sunrise tour. The sunrise over the sea and the colours the light creates on the dunes are magnificent. Sadly, the experience is ruined by the jeeps and ATVs. When you get to the camp at the entrance to the dunes, the drivers will tell you that you have to rent a jeep or ATV because it is too far away to walk. If you arrive in the dark to wait for the sunrise, of course you cannot see the surroundings. Don't be fooled by these drivers. The dunes literally start right behind the camp and you can easily walk around the entire area in 1-2 hours including ample time for taking pictures. Of course, if you want to have some fun going up and down the dunes on an ATV (with a driver), go for it. No matter if you rent a jeep/ATV or opt for walking around, the noise and smell of the jeeps and ATV is atrocious and spoils the serenity of the place.
Feel like real deserts When come to Mui Ne you should come here. Can walk o pay 300k VND per person to ride ATV or jeep. The view is very awesome it's not feel you in Vietnam.

Very beautiful pláce.nice view.you can have a lot of fun.taking the board and slide the dunnes...and pay the girls who borrowing it.you can walk there or a pay kind of a lot to those quads drivers who will také you to the Hill and after one hour back....Very sadly those quads are destroying the nature heritage.which i guess they will regret in future..
Great place for sunset/sundown. Really white sand, but many many tourists. Despite it you can take there some really nice pictures :) you can ride with quads on the top of the dunes!

Lovely Dunes But Rdiers Be Aware!! Great coastal road to drive to the dunes. Arrived and decided not to get a quad as the prices were too high so we walked around them which was awesome, great views and got some good pics. How ever on the way back police were pulling over just tourists on bikes and making us pay up 1000000 Dong! Just ruined the whole day for us! Other people were paying different amounts and it all seemed very corrupt! Shame on you Vietnam!
If you get a chance to visit the dunes you won't be disappointed. Reasonable prices but like most things in Vietnam there is always haggle room.
Quad bikes are quite annoying - during the sun rise go to the left dune,at least there the quads don't drive directly past you

Overpriced four-wheelers, beautiful place This place is worth a visit. The best way how to get there is to rent a motorbike. There is a parking lot in front of the entrance. The ticket to the dunes costs around 25k VND and the parking 10k VND... The price of the four-wheelers is worse. We were looking forward to rent one and ride the dunes but we assumed it´s really not worth it. It costs 600k VND for 2 people (they are willing to give you some discount but not very big) and the worst part is that the guy is riding with you so you really don´t have any chance to freely ride the dunes on your own. We choose to take a little walk instead and that was amazing. I just recommend to wear some "fit for desert outfit" like long sleeves and trousers because if its a little windy you will have the sand everywhere :) Also bring a lot of water and a good sunscreen, the sun is burning hard!

Private tour by Hai Tuan T&T (day trip from Saigon to Mui Ne) I didn't get enough reviews about this company, but I went ahead anyway. We are 4 adults, picked up at 7am sharp from Saigon to Phan Thiet town. You can choose not to do lunch at the resort where they transferred you to a private jeep, We explored Fairy Spring, pass by fishing village, and spent average 1 hour on each white and red sand dune. It was Monday so there weren't much people and noisy ATV (very expensive). It was quiet and pleasant to walk on the white sand and make great pictures.
I recommend to travel in the morning. If sky is clear you will get the best view. The time up to your tour jeep.You have time in this place about 30-40 minutes.
It's a nice place to see and driving around with the quat is huge fun. I'd definitely recommend to visit.

Impressive place to see. Lots of companies offer the trip to the sand dunes, which means you won't be by yourself out there. Also there are locals renting out quats and jeeps so you can drive in the dunes. Most of them are pretty expensive but it is worth the fun! It also means there you need to be careful walking there, might be a crazy driver appearing when you're not expecting it. The view is amazing tough!

Its not the highlight from Vietnan but if you are in the neighborhood its well worth a visit. If you do this excursion from mui ne then u also pass by the red sand dunes. You can skip those. Dirty and a pure waste of time
You can walk cross the sand dunes or take 4x4 drive cross. ATV is alternative choice. Sunset and sunrise is the best time to visit. Very very hot at the Mid of the day
Nice view and it's fun if you rent a bike and drive to the dunes, but be careful with the prize. First it is very high, but you can get it half of prize they say.

Try to get there as early as possible. Do a sunrise-tour but make sure they start very early. Extremely touristy and the quads etc are probably fun (didnt do it myself), but disturbing the peacefulness of the place. Beautiful place and a mustsee when you're in Mui Ne nonetheless.
We went at sunrise and i think thats the best option if you can deal with the early wake up because the heat is pretty crazy mid day. We paid to much for a atv but it was super fun.
amazing.. feels like you are somewhere in Africa.. worth it to come.. i came here with my friends, and we go to this place by book the jeeptour from online, and this place really amazing..
U need to pay for ATV if u don't want to walk till the top of the sands. Or u can rent a car which they will give u a ride to the top. Nice view.
Well it has a great view. Considering use ATV as a transportation its much helpful. The price for atv can be negotiate down to probably 400vnd. Overall its good though

Great scence and activities Just droped by White sand dunes in Phan Thiet, Binh Thuan province- Vietnam. Large sand dunes with great landscape. There is big lakes within look- like desert. You can have motor sport on the dunes. Service is not much. However, recommendable to visit, where you can have a feeling like Far- West of US. You can stay in Mui Ne- beach front small tourist town (4 hours drive to HCM city or to Nha Trang city)

We did a tour which included the fairy stream, fishing boats, white and red sands was 4 -5hr tour cost with quad bikes and tips approx $100 which is good value for an Australian family. Was a lot of fun quad biking.
Excellent for adventure seekers.. Very good for adventure seekers, lot of activities. Sand bikes, lotus lake. Good for MICE travellers.

we enjoyed a half hour quad bike experience for two on one bike. Cost was 500,000 dong. despite person insisting he would budge below 600,000 until i said we were walking. very noisy but saved me having to walk in scorching sun. im 100 kg and my wife and i had fun. some orbers going crazy and airborne. i was impressed the place we rented from sent a 'marshall' out tolook after us

I visited the dunes on a tour from a local hostel and was quite surprised by them. More what you expect to find in the desert than on the coast in Vietnam. There are opportunities to hire a quad bike when you enter, these are underpowered and prone to getting stuck in the sand and seemed overpriced. You can easily walk around and explore the dunes. Definitely a recommended trip if you are in Mui Ne
Located not far from Mui ne, interesting place to visit, especially if you know how to drive motorbike :) a little bit dirty, but worth seeing

Whilst it is very touristy and crowded at places, it is still a very beautiful part of Vietnam. After all I booked this trip with a tour company so I can hardly expect to have it all to ourselves (though it would be nice). Depending on what time you go it will probably be quieter (not sunrise or sunset). we booked the sunrise, however, we missed sunrise completely because of the jeep picking us up 45 mins after they were supposed to arrive (4:15 am) this was annoying!! Never mind though, it was still very beautiful and my mood soon picked up to the point that I wasn't that mad anymore, mostly cause rising in the back of the jeep while the driver drives like a nutcase is fun!! The shop we used to book the tour was PTK travel, for 110k vnd each we booked the group tour ($5 each) which is a good price, I would say the more expensive private tour is probably not needed as they will try to tell you it will be uncomfortable and crowded, but it's really not. I would suggest NOT!! using PTK as they did not deliver on the premise that we'd get to see the sunrise at the sand dunes, it had fully passed by the time we arrived. I don't know what the hold up was, but I got the impression that they really don't care if they deliver or not as nobody will be held accountable for bad service and people will just keep coming anyway. It may be like that for all the tour companies that run this service but I can't really speak for them. It takes about 15 mins of tough walking to get to the top of the sand dunes, there were other options of quad bikes and a lift in a separate jeep to the summit, these were being offered to me before I even got out of the car. These other options are extortionately priced and the people are pushy. I enjoyed the walk anyway.

Went here with friends. Quite far from town, but the journey by jeep to the sand dunes is worth it! Not too crowded. You have to walk about 20 minutes to reach the top of the sand. They also have ATV ride for people who are too lazy to walk. The view is breathtaking. Recommended if you're in Mui Ne
Nice for the sunrise and cool to see but as everyone else says the quad bikes Ruin it and you don't need them, it's an easy 10 minute walk and saves you £10!

We took a sunrise sand dunes tour with our hotel where we left at 4am for an hour's journey to the white sand dunes by jeep. By the time we reached the white sand dunes, it was already getting bright. We regretted not getting the ATV as it was quite a walk to the peak and by the time we reached the peak of the dune, the sun was up. It was still a nice view nonetheless and you could just see sand for miles away. The wind can get quite strong here so you will get sand all over your face and even in your mouth! There are also quite a number of tour groups there so the place will be relatively crowded as well but worth the effort :) You can also walk to the lake on the way back to the entrance/exit.
Quite unusual scenary of this natural site...but the charge for ATV and the jeep going up to the white sand dunes is quite expensive compare to a tour of 3-4 hours over this 4 major places in Mui Ne

We have been to many sardines and were still glad we came here. Unlike dunes in North America, there don't seem to be areas specific to ARV's versus hikers versus sled riders but it all seems to work out. If you have a good sized bottle of water you can hike the dunes to get away from the crowds. Or if speed is your choice, tenant a dune buggy. Make sure your hat is on tight though, many can been seen half buried by the dunes. We simply hiked around, some of the designs in the sand created by the wind were lovely. We came to the white sand dune as part of a Red, White and Fairy Springs tour which was a great way to see all 3 as well as a stop at a flushing village. Our tour was only 6 ppl (a max of 8) and we were driven around in an old army keep which added to the whole experience. Our driver didn't speak English so it was up to those of us on the tour to discuss how long we would stay at each stop. Note, you walk through the river at Fairy Springs and in deep sand on the dunes so choose your footwear accordingly. Entrance to the dunes was included in our tour price.

Stunning views changing with every hour of the day The White Dunes are much further away from Mui Ne than the Red ones, but worth the logistical effort. (We happened to have a car so it was easy.) First the bad news: the operators of the site are greedy and in a hurry to milk visitors for all they’re worth. Quad rental prices are outrageous, you are firmly invited to sit down at the restaurant – we declined - and signs here and there mention charges for photography - it seems to apply only to crews shooting wedding pictures and the like, but it does not say. The good news is that the site is definitely worth multiple visits, so different are the views depending on the time of the day. We visited on foot during the worst possible time (around noon), which is definitely not recommended and even hazardous unless you take skin and heat protection seriously. But the views were stunning anyway, with the high white dunes framed by the blue lake, lotus pond and dancing clouds.

Awesome setting of dunes against the sky, ignore the noise of quads The white sand dunes are popular among local tourists, most of whom hire a quad or jeep. I went walking, which is doable if your physique is not too bad. Thereby you see more small details of the dune area (and its a good physical exercise) and you can get everywhere (even the quads can't). I was there when dark, ominous clouds were rolling in from the mainland, which presented a wonderful, almost apocalyptic scene. Just beautiful. Forget the continuous noise of quads and jeeps, they are a necessary evil (and fun to many visitors). Fortunately there is much less litter than with the red sand dunes, but some public rules would be welcomed for those too ignorant to behave.
Did a tour to see the sunrise at the dunes. The view is amazing but the experience is slightly ruined by all the quad bikes driving around. It is a lot of fun to drive one of those though.
Nice for a teenager to have a quad experience. Parents can walk and see him ride. The dunes are nice to see.
Try ostrich ride and quad drive! Avoid the typical rip-off-foreigners guys... And bargain. Near to Muine, and provides a good half day activity for family
Amazing place to see the sunset !!! It took me 2 hours by motobike to get, but all I can say is its really worth to try !!! Hot but the activities here are very cool :))

This was worth the visit. The quad bikes weren't particularly great if I was to be fussy as they needed more power and as a result we got stuck numerous times. That aside it is really good fun. Not something you do every day so, worth doing.

The most amazing activity here is riding by moto quad. That's why I tried to book 1 via muine-explorer. The staff was following up, they called me to confirm and again to give instruction when I started to come White Sand Dunes. They had 3 types: mini moto quad (for 2 people), moto quad (for 3 people) and Jeep (for 5-6 people). The duration for renting is 20 mins, 30 mins or 45 mins. You can choose between riding by your own or hiring the driver. At first, because I rented mini moto quad, I chose riding by myself with guide (another expert will sit next to me to help when I need). Then I decided to let him drive because he would take you to some high sand dunes and go down. It was like roller-coaster and the driver was just awesome. He could drive very skillfully in such a difficult condition like sand. And the feeling was crazy. You can scream but make sure do not let your hands out the moto quad, you may fall down. The scenery here is really good though, you may take some photos here. The best time is around sunrise or sunset. Actually in muine-explorer, they provided a tour to go 4 places including White san dunes, red sand dunes and Fairy Stream by Jeep for tourist so you can check the information on the website. However, because they will have some people tried to sale you jeep rental or moto quad rental with higher price, please be careful (especially when you're foreigner, so you should book on the website first for advance and guidance and they had fixed price.) White sand dunes has a beautiful lotus lake, you can ask the moto quad driver to take you there. They are very pleased to do that. Try some thrilling experience by quad, it actual helps you to release.

First time sand dunes visit Its my first time visiting sand dunes and im excited! Prefer the white dunes to the red one actually. Alot of tourists here.. choose to walk instead of taking the jeep. Walking around and taking pictures is fun! very desert feel-like. Better to walk ard with someone than alone cause can get lost easily as the surrounding looks the same. haha!

Because of the staff and the manager (Phi) at Shades apartments the stay in Mui Ne is Fantastic. The "strip" is quite touristic and (in my opinion) there are to many rood Russians, but if you stay near Shades and go to Phan Thiet et nice and local :-) Super trip !!!!!!!!!!!!!

Worth doing while in Mui Ne, but don't come all the way just for this Dunes look great, desert-like feel, and you can walk around or rent quad bikes. Lots of quad bikes around so may not feel very relaxing. Probably best 'thing to do' in Mui Ne.
Fatasy we like stay on jeep car and fly ..... hahaha i think we fly realy ..)))) nice time here not so noisy we will back in next time for sure .
Had a super climb, great views expecially in the morning! It was even fun to watch the screaming Chinese groups on the jeeps...
If you have" young "men- they will love racing up and down sand dunes on 4 wheel motorbikes! Fun for all but special treat ,so different for them .Feels like standing in middle of desert,incredible.
Got up early for the sunrise. Very peaceful until the 4-wheelers started racing up and down the dunes.

Hired a jeep and driver to bring us to the sand dunes, included in the package was the tour to the fairy stream and the market by the sea. Second time at the sand dunes, however this time the place seemed to be much more crowded and had slightly more thrash. Rented the ATV however we were driven around on it, was only allowed to drive it for a short while. Weather wasn't too good when we went cause it was raining but overall, it was a good experience, wished there were lesser people though.

I went to the White Sands Dunes to watch the sunrise when I was in Mui Ne, which means getting up @ 4a.m. to reach there! We must climb all the way up to the top of the dunes in order to watch the sunrise. For those who are too lazy to climb, there are motorcycles available to drive you up there! The sunrise was rather beautiful. The scenery is also rather beautiful. This place is worth a visit, providing you are willing to wake up!

We booked a tour from Mui Ne that included the White Sand Dunes, Red Sand Dunes, The Fish Market and The Fairy Stream. It cost 50000 VND in total which is the equivalent of less than $4 Australian. For the price, it's well worth it. I would recommend doing a tour and starting there for sunrise. I image during the day it would be very hot. Also weekends are generally busier so if you have a choice go on a week day. The sand dunes are really pretty. If you walk to the top you'll get a great view or the river that wraps around half of it and the sunrise on the other. It's an early start from Mui Ne (4am). We bought our tickets from Mui Ne Hills Hotel and they took a group of us by bus. You can pay more and go by 4x4 but these vehicles aren't allowed on the sand dunes. You can hire an ATV at the white sand dunes but we are glad we didn't. Quite a few got bogged. They don't drive very fast and are very expensive for the short time you can hire them. You can walk up to the same spot that they drive to. I think it's a shame the ATVs are there as they take away a bit of the magic of the sand dunes.

Went at sunrise which meant getting up at 3.30am! Was well worth it as the sun rises over the the dunes and the lake. Didn't rent a ATV and was glad as they clearly lacked power and several people were stuck as we walked up the dunes. We went on a Saturday and wasn't that busy at all, ATVs were annoying and extremely loud. Almost seems disrespectful. However would highly recommended, best attraction out of all that's on offer in mui ne!

The sand dunes were meant to be the highlight of mui ne, but they were just like any old bit of sand. That being said, if you climbed up the higher ones you got a good view but the experience was tarnished somewhat by the sound of all the quads. At only 10,000 entry it's worth a trip but it's a bit of a journey from mui ne. They were nicer than the red sand dunes so if I had to pick between them I'd choose the white ones.

Great half day trip from Mui Ne The White Sand Dunes impressed me more than I was thinking. It is really a unique and beautiful sight. Contrary to most people, we walked up to the top and enjoyed it a lot... Frankly it is not very far. We rented bikes in Mui Ne and drove to the dunes. Good road, and there is a petrol station not too far from the dunes. There were Police checks on the roads, but they never seemed to stop foreigners, as long as you wear a helmet. Access to the site is vnd 10'000 per pax. A big quad was offered to us at vnd 600 thousand for 20 minutes, which we turned down. Definitely a must visit if in Phan Tiet area. I would avoid the red sand dune, which did not seem of much interest. Great place to stop for a bite or drink on the way is Jibe's Beach Club...nice setting on the beach.

We did the 4WD together with our kids and they are thrilled with excitement! My daughter even requested for faster speed! Big 4WD rental is at 800k for a 30 mins ride. The professional driver will follow our 4WD but we were the one riding it. When reach the summit of the sand dune, the driver took over and drove us downhill and uphill again! This was so fun! Suggested one to wear gloves for this activity as your hands will be hurt by grabbing the metal tight as you don't want to be flung out of the 4WD when at a high speed.

Have to pay 600k dong for jeep or 400k dong for ATV if you want to go up . If not just walk all the way into the sand dunes which you will find it hot and not fun . We choose the ATV was pretty fun ! There's some locals in the sand dunes will help you or offer to ride u around give you a crazy and awesome ride around the white sand dunes but you have to pay them tips after the ride .

My partner and I went for sunrise tour. Even though we woke at 4am we missed the beginning of the sunrise sadly. However we got some nice pictures on the dunes. You can hire a buggy if you want or get the locals to drive you up the dunes on one. Also sliding is available if you dare to try! A famous part of Mui ne that's worth a trip to for a picture and a buggy ride but that's about it.

We went with a tour group and rode up the sand dunes in a jeep. At some points when the jeep turned corners round the dunes, it can be very scary. Not sure if this place is suitable for sand-boarding as there are vehicles moving around. As it could be very hot in the day, do wear a hat and apply sun-block.
Magnificent scenery for photos. Quad bikes were fun but a little steep for 30 minutes(500,000VND). Not so bad if you're not on a budget otherwise look elsewhere.

Went with a tour group as we didn't get to rent a bike to get here. The ATV was VND 400K, but it was quite alright as the guy was friendly and brought the two of us on many rounds and to different parts of the dunes. The sand was soft and didn't irritate the skin, nice to sit on. Nice place for photos and its marvelous scenery, but nothing much there really.

Awesome scenery, but make sure you walk We did the Jeep sunrise tour from our hotel, unfortunately for us it was raining and overcast and we didnt exactly see the sunrise. Would recommend to just walk the distance, literally takes 10mins and not very hard, it's quite fun! The quad bikes aren't worth it for a 2minute ride nor are the Jeeps that drive up the sand dunes to drop you off 3/4 of the way up..
We came here to watch the sunrise. Amazing view. Pretty fun to just walk around and enjoy the view. It does get pretty hot when the sun came up.

Nice sand dunes, best to visit during the early morning The white sand dune is worth a visit, but to avoid scorching hot sun, it is recommended to visit during the early morning. You will need to pay an entrance fee and parking fee (if you are riding your own bike), and a short walk to the start of the dune. At the entrance of the dune, you would be approached for either a jeep ride, an ATV ride or self-ATV ride. I would recommend that you take an ATV ride with a driver, i.e. the person will ferry you to the top of the sand dunes and leave you there to explore and to take pictures. The ATV driver will leave and return to collect you back to the base (at an agreed timing). During the trip back to the base, the ATV driver would ride around the dunes for a short tour, speeding down slopes for some adrenaline rush. Fun! We paid about 200k Dong per person.

If you've never been to a sanddune, it is worth a first visit. If you are in relative good physical shape, you can walk up the dunes to a higher vantage point to see across the bay. For a faster way (and more thrilling), pay to have someone drive you up the dune on an ATV. Try to reach early in the morning like 6am to catch the sunrise and when it is not as hot

Good experience, but annoying kids who demand the tips at first, i had put too high expectations on the tour. My friends and i had rent the ATV for 300k Dong, but the kids request 100k dong for tips, so total 400k dong for the ATV 30mins. Other than the annoying kids request the tips, its a nice tour to there. For who want to rent the ATV, just ask for it 300k dong for 20mins, and need not to give the tips. They are too demand for the tips.
Good place for nice pictures and views! Jeep tour with drivers are available, ATV! Wonderful looking view, soft sands. Super in love with how the sands touches our feets.

This was definitely one of my highlights of my trip to Vietnam and would recommend this for adventure seekers! I would give this 5* as my ATV worked perfectly but one of my friends ATVs apparently kept turning to the right spoiling their experience a little. Two things to watch out for: the huge scary bee like insects at the peak of the dunes and the ridiculously cute puppy at the beginning of the dunes. For half an hour 500,000 dong was pretty pricey in my opinion but very worth it. I'm not sure if prices vary or whether you might be able to haggle. I would also recommend getting a private tour or having your taxi driver wait for you to take you back if you go at 5 am to see the sunrise, which I would highly recommend as the views are beautiful.

Feels like desert in South East Asia Very cool! The dunes are very impressive and at times are the only thing you can see. Drove the buggies which was fun but when you are walking around it can be pretty annoying hearing them (they are very loud).
from 05:00 AM highly precise visit the white sand dunes . feel like in the desert . and should try to drive a quadbike . a pleasant experience

Expensive but worth it! We had a lot of fun riding the quad bikes on the dunes. You can walk up to the top of the dunes for the view if you don't feel like getting on the quad. Paid 300,000 VND for 20 minutes, not the cheapest activity for backpackers but it's not something you can do every day.
Amazing place to visit in mui ne... Well white sand dunes was on my bucket list.. And finally i was there... Which is sooo amazing... But too tiring to walk around hahaa

Good fun if you get the quad bikes! We paid 300,000 VND for 30 minutes on the quad bikes. I was really glad we did because it's pretty much the only way you'll enjoy the visit otherwise because it's far too hot to walk around there in the open sand dunes. Our trip left us there for 50 minutes which was about the right amount of time. It's the best thing/ only thing worth seeing in Mui Ne so if you don't fancy it, don't come to Mui Ne.

Mini dessert experience The white sand dunes are around an 1hrs drive aprrox from the beach town of Mui Ne. Tour pick up is at 4am-4:20am definitely worth the early start you are back at your hotel by 9-10am so plenty of time to catch up with lost sleep if you wish. Tours vary between $4-6 which include white sand dunes, red sand dunes, fishing village and the fairy stream. If you are in a group i would suggest a private tour as you are packed in a jeep with other people, which is cool if your on your own. When you arrive you are immediately approached by teenage Vietnamese boys offering you to rent ATV's. I opted out as I fancied the walk they are also very loud and made it hard to appreciate the tranquility of the dunes (negative point). I would not recommend a late visit of 7:30am-8am visit as the sadn would be too hot to walk on.

It really was worth getting up at 4.00 am for this. The only thing I would do different is probably have a private tour as there were always that one couple that wouldn't listen to the times we needed to be back by, so we were always left waiting in the heat for them. Like nearly all tours in Viet Nam, you just don't go to the one place, you go to many places. I think next time, I will hire a dune buggy and ride to the highest peak and take in the sunrise
It was a great experience. We paid 500k VND for an ATV for 2 pax. 30 minutes. Probably the highlight of our trip.

If you want to feel the dessert in Asian, this is your place. To enter this place, you'll be charged 10,000vnd. You can rent jeep for 600,000vnd and ATV for 400,000vnd. But i prefer walking much to feel like a 'traveler' LOL.

Renting a motorbike and driving there yourself is definitely the way to go, it's an easy drive with not a lot of traffic, and an hour tops away from the backpacker area. The red sand dunes are a lot closer but not nearly as amazing as the white sand! When we got there the admission was 10,000 each and another 5,000 to park your motorbike. There is a nice lake with benches along it for you to sit and look out. Many people offering to rent you ATV's, which we did. They all offered 500,000 for 20 minutes, but we got the time up to 40 minutes. Good thing we got the extra time since we spent almost half of it stuck inside the sand and not actually driving. The second time the ATV got stuck it was in super deep sand and there was no employee around to help us. When we finally got ones attention after walking up a sand dune, he told us this area was out of bounds - we were not informed there were out of bounds areas. The sand dunes are great but I wouldn't suggest renting the ATV's. Do it somewhere else where you won't get stuck and it doesn't cost so much. You can easily walk through the sand, and there are also herds of cows to see!

Hot and so hot there, but you can enjoy driving ATV. However, the ATV rental is very expensive. I was not an experienced ATV driver, so I found that rent the ATV and led the staff drive for you was extremely fun and stirring.

Worth the visit. We woke up at 5 am and went straight for the dunes to catch the sunrise . White sand dunes are truly very beautiful . The only catch is a quad company at the entrance to the dunes . They try really hard to make you rent them or jeeps for quite a steep price. The quality of their quads is not very good either . We decided to pass and just walk. It turned out to be a very pleasant walk and I believe we got to appreciate it even more because we were on foot . So glad we got to see all that beauty !

Feels like being in the desert It's a crazy mother nature wonder. You can walk through the dunes and while you can imagine how stranded people must feel. The jeeps and ATVs are pricey, wouldn't recommend to rent them. If walking is possible do it. They built a new road to go there, so it's easy to reach.

Even though the ATV was fairly pricey, we paid 600k dong, it was well worth it as it could sit two people with the guide driving or you could drive it yourself. The guide took us up to the peaks where it was absolutely beautiful and he also drove us right down a 90 degree drop along the dunes, it was thrilling! I thoroughly enjoyed the breath-taking scenery and adrenaline rush from the ATV ride.

Desert experience on fishing village Our hotel man at Mui Ne Bliss (located in Mui Ne hills) offered us an early morning jeep tour (started at 4 AM) priced USD 6 to White Sand Dunes, Red Sand Dunes, Fairy Stream and Mui ne fishing village. It takes about an hour to the White Sand Dunes. The white sand dunes was stunning at morning, catching up sunrise and walking around to find the best spot to photograph. The weather bit windy and cold, but started to warmer along with the sunrise. There were a lot of ATV that you can rent, but we prefer to walked around since the desert area as not big as the real desert and to be honest the ATV's sound is just noisy :)

Beautiful views, rent fourwheelers for a steep price The dunes themselves were magnificent and beautiful. The tour guide brought us in a jeep, but parked quite far from the dunes, advised the group we could stay for 45 mins, the dunes being so far away we negotiated a price for the four wheeler to take us to the top, and around for 20 minutes, then back down, for 200,000 dong, 2 people. Our driver took us 1/3 of the way, 3 minutes total, dropped us off and disappeared. We hiked the rest and walked back. Fought with the driver and got half our money back. To do it all over again, we would rent the quad and do it ourselves, but that cost is 400,000 dong. Not easy to pay when you are a backpacker.

We paid 300k dong ($20) for an ATV, split between 2 people. they gave us a quick lesson, and then we headed into the dunes. Stunning way to catch the sunrise and also not have to walk as the dunes are massive. We stopped at the main area to take photos and I noticed a few Vietnamese guys sitting on our ATVs. I thought it was weird but they told us to get on then proceeded to drive down the steepest parts of the dunes (literally a rollercoaster). I had a lot of fun but its definitely unexpected so beware. We asked them to let us drive on our own (I still thought it was weird they were driving with us) and they left. We later got stuck in the sand and the guy came to our rescue. He asked for tip and said people usually gave 100k ($6-7), I was like hell no but my smallest change was 50k. $3 tip for a ride I would've never taken otherwise and for a country where people do anything to make money? Sure, I guess

If you're in Mui Ne then you definitely have to do this. My tour dropped me off at an ATV rental place and I was told the dunes were not within walking distance and I would have to rent an ATV for 500,000₫ (or more). This was not true, and I had no trouble walking to the dunes in about 5 minutes. This was definitely the "hot" way to do it, but I really enjoyed being able to wander the dunes and also knowing I didn't get ripped off by the rental agency.

Worthwhile to visit, warning Different surroundings compared to red sand dunes, worthwhile to visit in one day trip with red sand dunes together. !Warning! If you go by motorbike - AVOID the red DUONG DONG PETRO STATION, the LAST ONE BEFORE WHITE SAND DUNES on the RIGHT SIDE, DON´T GO THERE, they will DEFINITELY RIP YOU OFF. If you really need petrol, pay after filling up your tank and tell them before how many litres you want (recommend you just 1 litr it´s enough to go to another station, they will tell you that your tank is totally empty, don´t believe them any single word), watch if they reset everything and watch VERY carefully how many litres you get. The worst people in Vietnam so far.. They rip you off with mock to your face, don´t support these "people"...

-ATV renting was just a bunch of youngster giving you different prices. A bit risky to rent ATV. No instructions of any kind. You can actually fall over with it or even drive to your on death since the dunes had crazy cliffs where you could have fallen over 20 meters.

I booked a half day tour using a jeep to go there. I would said it is one of the place you shoud go if you come to mui ne. You can see the wind blowing a sand like in the movies. The wind is very strong, better tie your hair. Also don't forget the sunscreen and sunglasses.

Amazing sunrise but very noisy We decided todo the sunrise instead of the sunset as we have seen many sunsets. We got picked up at 430am so make sure you get an early night the night before. The sand dunes where awesome we walked to one of the highest ones to watch the sunrise which was beautiful. The only thing that ruined it for us I would say is all the quad bikes it ruins the peaceful atmosphere and spoils the views. I also don't think they are worth the money many of the quads didn't make it up and of the sand dunes and we also seen a guy fall of the edge of the sand dune and the wheels fell off, so also very unsafe so be careful.

Bring some sunscream, a couple of bottled waters and hire a scooter! Let's spend a whole day for the trip to this place, you'd never feel regretted (i promise), it's around 40 - an hour ride from Mui Ne, along the very beautiful landscapes as you might think that you're not in Vietnam any longer. There's a lot to see on the way. So, things you must have for your trip are: water, sunscream, sunglasses, and then an exciting mood will welcome you. Cheers!
Sandunes was great to go and see the sunrise quad bikes are a waste of money tho as they can't make it up most of the big dunes
Very pretty, came for sunset but left before that. Very windy and try to wear shades to protect your eyes. Can rent ATVs for 500,000/30 mins. Good for thrill seekers.
Be prepared to get covered head to toe in sand. However, it's pretty amazing to look at and good for half hour or so. You can rent a quad bike but it's quite expensive so we walked.
We did the jeep tour from Mui Ne and it was worth it - great day out - we called to fishing village on the way and took photos - dont miss it.
Beautiful sanddunes much better than the red ones. You can hire a quad for 350,000 for 20 minutes. Quads aren't powerful so be careful up steep hills.

Beautiful dunes, too bad about the ATVs! The White Sand Dunes are beautiful and worth the trip along the coastal highway. The dunes themselves are gorgeous, however the ATVs and jeeps for rent are quite noisy, so be prepared. Folks renting them will try to convince you it is far to walk, but it is only about 12-15 minutes. Many people who rented the ATVs had troubles with them getting stuck in the sand or breaking down, so if you are debating it, I would recommend walking, as you may save yourself some hassle in the long run.

My thoughts after 3 trips to the dunes White Sand Dunes 4 out of 5 The white sand dunes are a must if you ever decide to visit the city of Mui Ne. However I feel like there's a lot one should know before going. I've visited the dunes a total of 3 times during my stay in Mui Ne. I've gone by taxi, private jeep tour, and on my own via motorbike. First time I went was with a taxi. We had just gotten to Mui Ne and right away wanted to check it out. We didn't expect it to be a 45 min drive away from Mui Ne! It's not too expensive but it was just unexpected. So be prepared for that. And also somehow our driver seemed a bit lost going there. He might've been new (or drunk). There's a 10,000 dong cover charge per person upon entering. Pay this to the guard. It's like 50 cents USD. When we first went we took a path to the left of the lotus lake I think. There they had an ostrich ride if you were into that. Honestly it was kind of a fun experience but the birds looked abused so I felt bad. Once inside we rented their biggest ATV's 500cc's for 30 min. It was about 1 million dong for each and a guy has to go with you. It was pretty fun though. The ATV's were able to handle the dunes very well. At times the guides who go with you will take over and take you for a spin around the dunes. HOLD ON! It was like a roller coaster and I thought I was gonna fall off a few times. So much fun but just be prepared especially if your scared of that. Overall it was a pretty fun day. Oh and last note, if you take a taxi make sure you have them wait for you because you'll have a hard time finding a way back all the way out there. The second time I went I took my wife and kids through a tour we booked through a hotel. The tour was only $30 for all of us and it included a private jeep left at 1:30pm and took us to the fairy stream, fishing village, white sand dunes, and red sand dunes for sunset. This time after paying the guard we went to the right of the lotus lake to a different building with ATV rentals. Here's where my opinion of the dunes changed a little. The dunes are awesome. Except it's hard to find a spot not covered with tourists and four wheelers. Secondly, the people who run the dunes seem shady. First we were considering having them drive us to the dunes (which is a few hundred meters hike from the main building) with a jeep. They wanted around 800,000 dong. It seemed a bit pricey just to drive us a few hundred meters. But maybe they'll take us wherever we want to go for 30 min? Nope. One family left and came back 5 min later. The jeep literally drove them out 500m. Allowed them to take a picture and drove them back. The family was furious and arguing with them the whole time. The driver and staff seemed to ignore them. We scratched that idea and decided to just rent the ATV's they had. My wife and daughter on one and me and my son on the other for 400,000 dong each. These ATV's weren't as strong as the 500cc ones I rode earlier. In fact they were a lot smaller. The ATV's could barely go anywhere and kept getting stuck in the sand. I can't remember how many times one of the staff had to come to our rescue and push us out. My wife's ATV broke down and they had to get her another one. Which took awhile for them to get. We finally said screw it and walked. We rented one of their sleds for 50,000 dong. Not worth it as they don't work. Even on the smoothest steepest dune you'll slide nowhere and end up scooching yourself down the hill like a fool. We just took our pictures and then headed back. Here's one more thing to watch out for. I got stuck again and one of them came to help me out. He then proceeded to "take me for a spin" around the dunes. While yes it was fun to go up and down the dunes again like a rollercoaster I didn't ask for it. He then demanded a 200,000 tip. What? Seriously? No f'ing way man. 200,000 tip? That's just wrong. I told him it was a tip which is something I don't even have to give. I gave him 20,000 dong and told him to go away. Watch out for these guys and remember you don't have to give them anything and 200,000 is way too much to be tipping anyone in Vietnam. Last straw was when we came back with our ATV's. They were trying to tell us that we didn't pay and that we needed to pay for exceeding the 30 min. Um hello your stupid ATV broke down on us and you spent 20 min getting us a new one. I told them to go F themselves. They may try to back you into a corner but you just gotta get aggressive back and keep walking. Most of them are just teenagers. My third time back was on my own. I heard that the white sand dunes was awesome at sunrise. This I wanted to see before I left Mui Ne. Like I said the sand dunes are amazing it's just the crew that runs the dunes are some shady a-holes. Anyway I digress, this time I went on my own and took a motorbike. Motorbikes are cheap in Mui Ne, just find a sign for motorbike rental and you can pick one up for $7 a day no questions asked. Just make sure you know how to ride one and do so responsibly otherwise your screwed if you hurt yourself or damage the bike. The ride to the dunes was pretty long. It was also kinda crazy going at night but I'm a pretty good rider and I was real familiar with the way at this point. Just know that if you go this way on a motorbike to follow the speed limits (no more than 60km) and stay in the bike lane. There's also one point sometime after you pass the road along the ocean where they always have a speed trap set up (even at 4 am). Don't go more than 40km in this zone and stick with other groups of motorbike riders. Try and blend in because I think they target tourist and they'll try and take all your money. Luckily I noticed this trap the first two times we drove to the dunes so I was prepared for it this time. Getting to the dunes before the sun came up there was nobody at the guard gate to pay so I just drove on in. Nobody was there at 5 am so I was the first one. Tour groups usually depart on buses at 4am to the dunes but since I came alone I was the first one here. I hiked out to the furthest dunes which was a lot more tiring than I thought it would be lol. Remember to bring a flashlight. I didn't so it was kinda nerve wracking trying not to fall off a dune in the dark. In about 15 min tour buses and jeeps started pulling up. I could hear the engines of ATV's coming to life and the place already started getting crowded. One second I was the only one on the highest dune and the next there were about 30 people up there with me. The sun came up to the north just over the ocean's horizon. As expected it was stunning. I liked this trip because I enjoyed the dunes and was bothered by no one. If your physically up for it hike up there yourself, don't bother with the crappy ATV's. Well there you have it. A detailed description of all three of my trips to the sand dunes. The big take away from this is that the white sand dunes are a must see if you are in Mui Ne. Hell if you're in Vietnam. Most people never realized that there are sand dunes in Vietnam and are always amazed when you come back with pictures and show them. I recommend doing the Sand Dunes with a morning tour. The sunrise is beautiful, the dunes aren't as disturbed and there aren't as many tourists as later in the day. Also the sand is cool to the touch. Just be very wary of the idiots who work there. Most of them seem like their just kids who don't go to school, they just hustle, ride ATV's, and get high all day (Yes one of them actually toked a bong in front of me). Things are overpriced and it's hard to haggle them down (but not impossible!). Just don't fall for any of their scams.

Fooled by a tour but nice dynes We took a tour from Mui Ne to visit Red and white dynes. The dynes itself are nice specially the white ones but there are a lot of people trying to take profit out of the tourists here. The tour was useless. We only had a driver who took us from place to place and then dissappeared for 40 minutes and told us to take pictures. So if you have a map and can rent scooters or motorbikes I would definitely recommend doing this on your own.

A bit out of the way (we were staying in Mui Ne) but worth the visit. A surprise funding this, but peaceful and tranquil. You have to go on a quad bike to get the best views, which is great but not always to everyone's taste. Best time to visit is at sunrise. Less busy then.

We woke up at 4am to travel to the sand dunes and climb to the top whilst the sun was rising. The view from the top was spectacular, absolutely breathtaking. The weather was also really good at that time of day as there isn't a lot of wind so you don't have to worry about sand getting into your eyes and noise and mouths. Would recommend !

Just walk! Not so hard to climb up sand dunes. Sadly I missed the sunrise, however view at the top of the dune was amazing. You can see sand dunes, river, mountains and ocean at the top of the dunes. The fact that you can see all of this at once is really cool.
Incredible experience Was able to catch the sunrise. I rent and drive ATV for 400k VND for 20min, 500k for the bigger ATV (Can fit for 2 persons).

Great location witnessing sunrise me & my group of family members visited this majestic place during the last weekend of Jan2016.. we got there very early morning when it's still dark (6am local time) but there's already a big crowd which got there first before us, all for the sake of witnessing the sun rise...

Though it's further compared to Red Sand Dunes, the view is better since there were not many tourists. I love to see how the sand moves as the wind blows. The jeep will cost around 600,000VND and more suitable for family while the terrain bike is cheaper and more fun. Come around 4:30-5PM so it's not too hot.

We booked a private jeep for a tour to red and white sand dunes and fairy spring of 30 USD. The whole trip took 4 hours. Even though I have seen sand dunes before, it was still good fun and a beautiful place without trash littering the place as you will see driving there. We took a bug ATV (600k dong for 20 min). While expensive it was worth it with small kids, as it is a long walk to get to the big dunes. The costal road going there also has beautiful scenery.

Although you really need a Vietnamese driver's license, you can rent a motorbike and make trips on your own in the surroundings. You should however avoid police controls. Very nice are the sand dunes just about 30 km to the north. Shortly before the sand dunes (before you turn on the new road to the right) there is a gas station. The operator tried to cheat us with the billing, but we could avoid this. Therefore, take good care of liter and costs display isn't not pushed away too fast. Better yet avoid this gas station.
Beautiful white desert sand, with great views if you get to the top. You can rent a ATW at the entrance to get around faster and easier.

Sand dunes were great- but beware of petro scam on the way!! Had a great time on the dunes. ATVs were a bit pricier then expected but all in all it was a lot of fun. Please be aware of the gas station on the way to the dunes! We have spoke to multiple tourists now who have said they have been ripped off by this same station. The lady will switch the meter off after filling your tank and give you a MUCH higher price, more then your motorbike can even gold in fuel. She will also take the key out of your bike until you pay! Fill up before you go or tell her how much you want to fill your bike by holding the "only" bill you have.

Fantastic fun to ride the sand dunes in an ATV We went here without much expectations, and decided to rent an ATV to get around. We opted for one of the big ATVs, and had a driver join us (2 persons plus driver). We drove around for a bit before leaving the driving to our driver. He was driving furiously over the sand dunes, bot uphill and down the steep edges. It was so much fun, like riding a rollercoaster. Definitely recommended.
Nice to see and to watch the other people on theit quad bikes :-) The lotus lake had unfortunately no lotus on.

Once u arrive u will be asked which ATV you want but it's not free or included in your tour. I found the guys very pushy. Walk! It feels great to walk the dunes. If you decide to do the ATV, ask to be alone if not the guy will hop on as well. Make sure you go to the side where you can see the view with water.

My first Sand dunes experience Great experience walking on sand dunes, catched the sunrise and take early morning tour. Better go there in the morning so that it wont be too hot. Try walking on barefoot. Enjoy the sand and the scenery.
We went there with a tour. Of course when we arrived they propose us to take some quad but it was really expensive. You can go walking maybe 15min. It's pretty nice look like you are in Sahara

We got up early to see the sunrise there and even though there were a LOT of other tourists and an annoying ongoing sound of quads it was the most beautiful thing to see the sun rise over the sand dunes and the sea. The tourists spread out so you could actually pretend they weren't there. It was cool but the light was just mesmerizing and it was magical even though you had quads howling in the background!

Resembles the sand dunes that we have here in the Philippines, but the sand is finer and whiter. :p We rented the ATV which is good for 3 people which cost 600,000 VND. I am afraid of heights so when I saw that we're going to a slope which I think is more than 20ft, I got off the ride. The driver wants us to experience it, so he didn't leave and turned off the ATV. So, we're left with no choice but to ride with him. I'm glad he did that coz he took us to the top with a very good view of the lake. I think the drivers are not allowed to be given a tip because before our session ended, the driver asked us for one. We gave him 200,000 VND for the 3 of us sincrewe enjoyed it. Though later on I think that we gave him too much.

10,000 dong to enter the dunes and I would suggest going with a jeep driver. The dunes were spectacular, especially the one that looked over the river! For approximately $18USD you could ride a four wheeler for 20 minutes, which we did! Certainly got the heart pumping!

This attraction took us close to an hour taxi ride to reach. The cost was 500,000 dong. There is a visitor fee of 10,000 dong each too. There are a few options for visitors to this attraction. For the thrill-seekers, I highly recommend the ATV ride, choose the medium range as that is more powerful for the terrain. The dunes are steep and you could create alot of inertia from your turns and downward accelerating motion. We tried to drive the ATV on our own however, the wind and sand proved too much and we ended up having a driver instead. This turned out to be a better choice! You could also choose to walk the slopes, except it would take a longer time as the sand is pretty fine. He took 2 of us as passengers and confidently maneuvered the vehicle through the slopes and lakes. He stopped at strategic places and helped us take pictures too. One alarming thing about this place is the way they insist on having tips at the end of the 20 min journey. We were harassed consistently from the time we off boarded the vehicle, had a drink and boarded our taxi. This was really uncomfortable and uneasy as the men followed us around.

The dessert along the sea We passed the white sand dunes on our way down with the motorbikes to mui ne. The scenery is beautiful but it's not worth it to just book a tour for it. Rather get a scooter and drive along this beautiful brand new cost road and enjoy the dunes and the view of the ocean and the white lake.
quite a miracle that such a place is located just less than 500m from south china sea. it is amazing to take a walk inside.

Fool your friends & make them think that you are heading for an adventure in the Sahara! Unless you have really been to the Sahara! Personally having been to 2 deserts, the Mojave (USA) & Jhansi (India), this trip is advisable only if you haven't been to a real one, & only if Mui Ne is en route to your next destination. It's not too impressive (Smaller than what the photos show) but can be a really interesting destination for Southeast Asian natives who are not intending to head out to real deserts anytime soon! Tours include red sand dunes, fairy stream & the Mui Ne fishing village & can go as low as US$6! I went for the sunrise tour, which is from 430am to around 9am! Consider a crocodile for dinner while you're at Mui Ne!

The place you must visit in Mui Ne This is the afternoon tour to the White sand tune. Wonderful Scenery with good weather. What a pity that cannot rent an atv (coz all are renting out). Still good enough to walk with bare foot.

Made a visit during a stay at Romana Resort and Spa, arranged a "Jeep Tour" which took us to this area. Took a ride in a 4x4 ex yank jeep, still going strong after at least 40/45 years - don't know how the locals keep 'em running. Part of the ride took us down and up some rather steep declines and inclines, very good. Great views of surrounding areas.
It is a really nice place and peaceful. Good be better if it a bit more clean and if people were enough educated to Not throw rubbish through the windows of the Jeep.
From lounging to 4-wheeling Fun for a couple or the whole family. You can ride 4-wheelers over the sand dunes or just hang out with a picnic basket and watch the sunset.

This can be pretty good if your with friends and want to go wild, there's plenty of kids there that try to jump on your bike and give you a tour for money! Don't let them on!!! Bikes are a trashed but who cares just go crazy and enjoy yourself!!
It was good, a nice site! Definitely enjoyed it. But I wouldn't spend money on quad bikes unless you have a lot to spare, they seemed to break down a lot

Tough for me to give this attraction a 5/5 as I have travelled into the Sahara on camel back and I'm comparing what I visited today to this experience. But putting this aside, the dunes themselves are impressive. White sand blows and bellows as your walk up and down the dunes. It has a surreal feeling as you can look off into the distance and see a fresh water reservoir oasis next to these seemingly out of place dunes. Needless to say, parts are breathtaking and for an entry fee of 10,000 DONG it is good value for money. Beware that there are hawkers stationed at the entrance of the dunes who will hound you to ride their quad bikes. 300k DONG for twenty minutes. We didn't partake in this extra curricular but we saw many people get stuck (with no help) and the bikes are quite old and beat out. Also, two people on one = no more than 10km per hour. So be weary!
If you have like 2 to 3 hours to kill, you can visit this sand dune. It gets really windy up there and the view is pretty nice. Good for those who's never visited a desert/sand dune before

It is a new experience for me to ride a bike at the desert. A guide is diligent and take care of me during raining. They are generous with traveller. This place is near my country so i think i am sure to visit here again.

We did the sunrise tour and came here first. The dunes and view of the sunrise are fantastic. We rented ATV's and I have mixed feelings about this. When we were renting them we assumed we would just go and come back 30 minutes later, so we decided to share one. But the ATV guy insisted on coming with us. This made some of the hills more difficult to do with 3 people on the ATV. When we left the biggest dune and were going to the lake, the guy was driving and took the hill too fast. We fell off and I went head first and had the ATV land on me. I was okay and they got another vehicle to take us back to the building, but I would make sure you be careful! Do not do more than 2 people a vehicle!

Awesome when you rent a VTT My experience from the white sand dunes is definitly worth a 5 because I have enjoyed the VTT in the sand dunes, which was an awesome way to kill the time we had with our tour. Though, if VTT doesn't interest you or if you don't want to pay for this, you might find this place a little annoying.

Don't expect too much We got disappointed when the booking agent informed us about the dune buggy rides then wjen we got there the rides wasn't included and we have to pay for it separately. Ride was very expensive for 30 minutes. The place was amazing and it's one of the things you should not miss checking out while in Mu Ne
We had a fantastic time here driving around the sand dunes on the quad bike. We rented one for 300,000 and shared it. The view is beautiful at sun rise and well worth it.
Nice but have similar at home. Atv bikes were fun. Went early to beat the heat and had a nice time. ATV was fun. We have similar dunes in Australia so not blown away but definitely worth a visit.

I really enjoyed the white sand dunes! They were something different that you definitely cannot see everywhere. It almost gives you the feeling of being in a great sandy desert. Chose to walk around and I think this gives you a great sense of the place, as you see and hear a lot more. Although the sound of the quad bikes can be quite load depending on how busy it is at the time. You can also hire plastic sleds if you wish to slide down the dunes. One of the nicer places that I got to see on my time in Vietnam. Note: there's an extra 10,000 vnd price to get into the white dunes, that isn't included in the $4 price of tours booked from hostels.

Visited here as part of a 1/2 day tour through the Fairy Stream, the 'fishing village' (gross!), and the red sand dune. This was definitely the highlight. Paid $20 for 20 mins on an ATV but it was a blast. Did not feel safe but quite a thrill!
Beautiful but very hot!! Go early as it can be pretty unbearable in the afternoon. Worth the trip, but sunrise is best.

A short distance to the east of Mui Ne are very large sand dunes. There is a large fresh water lake below the dunes. One can rent a dune buggy or motorcycle for exploring the dunes further. There is no fee to visit the dunes. It is worthwhile visit if one is in Mui Ne.
got the tour cost USD 5 from the hotel included white sand dunes, red sand dunes, fishing village and fairy stream. the tour start at 5 am until 9 am

Great a must see if in Mui Ne Fantastic scenery and best if you go early morning (sunrise) or sunset due to the fact it can get really hot after 9 AM until 5 PM. The disappointment are the Quad used which make noise and smell in the otherwise spiritual and poetic atmosphere.
Interesting if you want to walk in sand or ride quad bikes. Ideal if you want to hoot around on quad bikes , which are at a fee. Great for taking photos although quite some distance to the dunes.

Great trip to sand dunes and fairy stream We went on a half day tour of the sand dunes, 2:00pm - 6:30pm. We were picked up by a jeep which first took us to the fairy stream which was nice to walk through, then the fishing villiage, then the white sand dunes and then the red sand dunes. We rode a quadbike around the white sand dunes, 20 minutes for 300 vietnamese dong. This was good fun, although we got stuck in the sand twice so spent almost half of our time pushing the quad bike out of the sand. The trip itself only cost 6 USD, so it was good value, and this can be booked at most of the tourist centres along the main street. If you want your own private jeep it is around $25 per person, which would not be worth it. It was nice to share our jeep and make some friends. Beware of pickpockets at the fishing village.

Nice lake and some dune bashing! Nice lake when you get there new road being built so by year end will be easier access. Went by moped which was ok if Quad bikes are all shot take a 600cc model Rest are underpowered Dunes very impressive and was very quiet midday mornings zinc evenings much busier Such a pity the locals dump al their trash everywhere - plastic bags need to be banned !!

It's very beautiful and fun for the first time. But if you've been to a desert like the Sahra, it's not so exciting...and the ATV's are in a bad condition but still fun if you've never driven one before!

Reach there abt 5am in the morning. Was drove to a station where there is rental of ATV. Was charged 200,000 each for a 5 mins ride to watch sun rise. Was too cloudy tat day, so didn't manage to watch the sun rise.. Enjoyed the spectacular view of the White dunes.. It's just amazing..
Quite far from travelers' gathering place, but you can ride a motorcycle to there. Very beautiful and worth visiting, sand motor riding is most interesting thing.

White sand dunes and Qua. A good place to see some hill of sands where you can do ATV. You will felling fun on the ATV but the cost was expensive. I asked the motorbike taxi from Ham Tien where the tourists called Mui Ne just 10 USD for a tour white sand, but the cost 20 USD for the ATV.anyway it was fun. The good motorbike guide in Ham Tien who was Qua.if you need to see some things in Mui Ne, you can see him on the road or call to him 0918643592. Qua was a good guide and friendly.really recommend.

Amazing sand dune formation. We went her very early in the morning to see the sunrise but unfortunately it was cloudy.... This is an amazing dune formation on its own...... Unfortunately it quickly gets spoiled by all the quad-bikes. In no time tracks are formed in the " untouched" sand, turning it into a very noisy "speedway".

Sunrise at the white sand dunes. We got here very early when the sky was just turning blue. When we arrived, there were around 15 people in total. We quickly climbed to the top of the dunes to snap our pictures with the untouched sand. After about 5 minutes we could hear the ATVs coming. We initially wanted to rent ATV ourselves but I'd definitely recommend walking instead. Around 10 ATVS riding around everywhere. I don't mind the noise but they want to climb every hill and they ruin all the untouched sand with tire tracks. No idea how this place would look during the sunset as the ATVS would of turn the sand into beach sand.

I preferred the white sand dunes to the red dunes although it was quite a walk up to the to Top but was worth the effort as the view was lovely, there was also a beautiful azure lake , all in all a very nice trip

Anthon in Mui Ne and Phan Thiet, Vietnam A wonderful and MUST place to go if by any chance in vicinity of the place. It takes less than 4 hours in own car from HCMC and something like 4,5 hours by Bus. The Sand Dunes are spectacular and the Fisherman Village and their landing Beach is a perfect Picture taking place. Indeed every cent worth visiting.

you wont expect that vietnam has a desert! The sand are soft and the view is amazing. You can rent a ATV or motorbike to reach the top with a reasonable price. However, if you wish to walk with own foot, go on, I did it. Tiring but awesome. The desert is limitless , you could just see the sky and the sand.

ATVs at White Sand Dunes We rented ATVs for sunrise. Fun time going up to the highest peak, off to the side where there were les tourists, and down to the lake. Very beautiful. It costs 10,000 VND for entry and 300,000 to rent to ATVs. The ATVs were fun but a bit sketchy. No one fell off, but we had to press to wires together to turn it off and the plastic on the front was all busted up.
Beautiful sand dunes! It is most beautiful send dunes in Vietnam. You should get there in the early morning as day time is too hot. You can slide down the big holes - that amuse the kids a lot

Up Up and Away on the sand dunes Visiting the white sand dunes we knew we wanted to take a quad bike tour, although a little pricier than most activities in Vietnam it was definitely worth it for that little rush going up and down the dunes. You are paired up with a "guide" who will navigate you through the ups and downs of the dunes and will take over for the difficult climbs and descents. They will also offer to take photos for you knowing where all the good shots and backdrops are. All in all a very memorable experience.

Went with a half day tour and we started here for sunrise! Beautiful but if you only choose one then go to the Red Dunes for sunset. Renting an ATV is extremely expensive so I skipped it. I hung out with some people on the tour and we took in the sunrise. We even watched people getting married on a few dunes over. Bring your camera!

The tour began at 4:30 am, we were almost the first ones to arrive so we had really nice pictures. The only thing is that between tourism and local people are making the white sands look like a garbage place. :-(
It's such the most funny time in Mui Ne. But it's quite expensive to rent a car there. Early morning is the best time to visit but if you can not wake up early, from 5PM is fine.
The white sand dunes are very pretty. You can walk through them. If you've never been to the desert, it's a must visit! Great for photos too.
Get up early is a big problem for me but it's worth. It's a real natural beauty. It looks like a desert of totally white sands and there is a Lotus lake in the center.

Discover the Land and Sea place Unexpected that this place have desert and sea allocated. You also may enjoying the Sunrise and Sunset in different place of viewing. Seafood are also found in the near by restaurant or cafe and also in the market. You also may rent a motorbike for self bike to the places. If you are getting personal trip, please do get a map or advise from you resort or any travel center. All the tourist spot are free and you also can find out many nice and good resort & hotel next to the beach. Ladies and Gentlemen are encourage to bring along of scarf or cloth to cover the facial due the sand wind especially cover up the camera or video camera to avoid the sand blow in the device.This place that no Airport but you can have a sleeping bus or tour bus to there from the agency. It take 5 hours bus journey to capital Ho Chin Minh City.
Beautiful sand dunes in the middle of Mui Ne. I preferred this one to the red sand dunes also in Mui Ne.

A bit of North Africa I took the organised tour offered by the La Marina hotel. The sand dunes are amazing, they came as a bit of a surprise in Vietnam, a land of Paddy fields, but beautiful non the less. The price of the quad bikes was also a surprise, but only if its too hot or you can't be bothered to walk. Take a hat, sunscreen and make sure to keep your camera under wraps, (I am still blowing dust off mine). Altogether a good experience.

Big beautiful sand dunes We went in the morning (left Mui Ne resort at 7:30 AM) before it got too hot and windy and the light was good for photos. Road there is pretty rough - dirt with lots of pot holes (chicken holes as the locals call them) but looks like they are starting to build a paved road. We hiked to top of dunes rather than pay 400k VND for a 20 minute Quad ride. Hike wasn't bad. The dunes are much bigger than the red dunes. The lotus pond next to dunes and the big lake were interesting backdrops for the dunes. This is a must see when in the area. It's a ways out of town - about 20 to 30 minutes but worth the trip.
Something a little different. You can take a bike or jeep ride but at 3000D and 5000D i thought a bit expensive and not for an older person like me.

Beautiful and interesting nature; very thin sand, you can walk on them, slide as well and enjoy the view. Great fun, this is fine for an afternoon. Not far away from the hotel, but you need a car (taxi).
Pleasant to see dunes in Vietnam, but nothing very exceptional. Can rent a quad on place, but quite expensive,..

Feels like the Sahara Awesome stop on a day tour I took featuring this mini desert, a fishing village and more. The dunes are like being in the Sahara desert... take a walk on the edges of wind swept sand dunes and grab a cold beer at the pit stop before heading back to your hotel, or resort... awesome photo opps and a fun trip through an awesome country-side. I loved it!
Had a fun time driving around the sand dunes on a quad bike. Very interesting to see these sand dunes in Vietnam.

Stunning place, but the only time to go is at 4.30am! Any other time would be too hot (hate to think about the burnt feet), and as the wind tends to get up as the day goes on, I think you would get sand blasted! Also if you go by jeep, don't expect a tour guide. They almost verge on the point of being rude (mine was anyway) & it was noticed by everyone, but ignoring that, it was well worth it!

Massive Dunes, watch out for the quad bikes! We thought the dunes were cool. We went as part of a $7 tour from the hotel which also stopped at the fairy stream, fishing village and the red dunes for sunset. The main detraction from the dunes, as another reviewer points out, are the quad bikes. We thought we may as well get involved rather than have our peaceful dune time ruined by others, but sadly the bikes are falling apart and drastically underpowered (50cc) for getting around on the dunes. We actually had two that both broke (chain came off, battery went flat), before getting our 300k dong for 20 minutes back. There is another quad bike place around the corner that does 250cc bikes which would be OK, but not sure how much they were! In summary - very nice big sand dunes, don't go if the quad bikes are going to annoy you, and if you do go don't bother getting the quad bikes! Regardless of the fiasco with the bikes, we had fun and would go back. 10k dong in.

Awake to think that this in VIETNAM, very unique. I hired the quad bike for 300000 for 20 minutes but they are crap add the tyres are not meant for sand. If you do get them make sure you get the smaller red ones that have more power

The trip to the white sand dunes from Mui Ne takes some time, and the last part of the road is quite unpleasant, but the dunes are beautiful and surely worth a visit. They are much more impressive and beautiful than the red sand dunes.
Off-road drivers do not rush you can stop at will to take pictures in every place if the weather is nice there are beautiful colors Automatically translated

The White Sand Dunes (Đồi Cát Trắng or Bàu Trắng) offer a more expansive and dramatic desert-like experience. Located further northeast, access typically requires a vehicle—such as a rented jeep (around 800,000 VND for a tour) or an ATV ride (approximately 600,000 VND)—as the area is vast and it's nearly impossible to walk to the best panoramic viewpoints for photography. Once there, visitors can admire the stunning contrast of white sands against the deep blue of Bàu Trắng (White Lake), enjoy thrilling rides across the rolling hills, or try the simpler pleasure of sliding down the slopes on a rented mat. It remains a premier spot for witnessing spectacular sunrises over the surreal landscape.
Fabulous nice place. Big and beautiful . View is fantatsic. Recommend a jeep drive through the dune driven by vietnamese. With strong wing and sand blowing at your face. 👍

White Dunes Recommended Riding ATVs here is enjoyable because the area is spacious, offers plenty of sights, and provides great photo opportunities. If you have limited time and can only visit one desert location, the White Sand Dunes are generally considered a better experience than the Red Sand Dunes.

Be sure to perform individually! Pleasant, Impressive and much cheaper. We already had certain concerns about a group ride through the dunes on arrival, as we had already talked to travel-experienced acquaintances in advance. They had advised us not to because of their own experiences. Upon arrival we probed the situation. We checked with some of the providers' offices and then also sought contact with people who had only just taken part in such a tour. We heard several voices from people who had made their bookings in different agencies. The result was not pleasing. There were only very critical voices regarding the course of their ride. In some cases, the reports were upsetting and the providers would be prosecuted in other countries.
