We therefore decided to follow a procedure that we had successfully practiced in Vietnam several times during transports: we stopped a taxi, spoke to the driver a few words and then received either contact or address of a person who can make such trips. We can only report the best of this ride: We – 2 people – were transported leisurely through the countryside in a pleasant vehicle, received comments and explanations in a very friendly form in some places and were also allowed to ask for stops. Also: The price for our company was dramatically below the amount we would have had to pay with one of the numerous tour operators. One more point, why avoid groups: We left our vehicle several times in beautiful places with great views and flair. Sometimes we also went from one point to another while our driver was transporting the car there. This flair that surrounded us here – single – was grandiose and indescribable. Automatically translated

Great view of sunrise rise ar the dune Wonderful landscape and view of sunrise. Book a jeep tour and a guide cum driver who tell you what you want to know. We started out around 415am and as soon as we are in the colourful jeep we were told it is equipped with Bluetooth. At the destination, we got ATV with driver waiting for us. Glad to come during low season. May till end of year will be quite busy. Sand boarding is for the brave at heart and climbing up the sand dune is 100 per cent bad for those with heart or health problems. Take a stroll and take photos of the view

We had the best day with Mr Kim. We were picked up at our hotel and stopped at the fairy meadows and got a free drink which was really appreciated after our walk. We then stopped at the roadside and Mr Kim helped us get photos on the jeep. The next stop was the sand dunes which was soooo fun. Our final stop was the red sand dunes for sunset. The whole day was amazing and Mr Kim really helped to keep it fun and entertaining. Would highly recommend to anyone!!
My husband and I went to the White Dunes on a motorbike . We really liked the views. There were a lot of beautiful photos taken. opposite the white Dunes, there is a very beautiful lake with lotuses.

Thank u Mr Kim for awesome tour ! He was a very good photographer. I love all the photos. His service also top notch! Please use Mr Kim services when you want to go to Mui Ne. He also provide others services to other places.

Đồi Cát Trắng – A Majestic Desert-Like Experience in Vietnam Our family trip with parent's colleagues to Đồi Cát Trắng was an unforgettable experience. The vast white sand dunes created an incredible backdrop for adventure and relaxation. One of the highlights was riding an ATV across the dunes. The thrill of driving the ATV on the soft sand was exhilarating, and it added an exciting twist to our visit. It was a fun activity that allowed us to explore the dunes in a whole new way, while enjoying the stunning views around us. Whether you’re looking to relax and take in the scenery or seeking adventure with activities like the ATV ride, Đồi Cát Trắng is the perfect destination for a memorable family outing. The combination of natural beauty and adventure makes it an ideal spot for creating lasting family memories.
The is new my experience here which I could tried on quad bike, Mongolia’s dress. The staffs smoke cigarettes on duty, the price was f quad bikes so expensive. The sunset was very gorgeous.

white sand dessert in MuiNe i joined an half day sunrise tour (thuong jeep tour MuiNe) three weeks ago when i revisited MuiNe.white sand dunes is the first stop. that time, the driver drove a yellow colour jeep to my hotel in the early morning. then we started our journey. when reach to white sand dunes, you have to buy ATV ticket. then the driver will take you to the top of the white sand dunes, it is quite like dessert in the middle east, although the real location is in vietnam. unfortunately, that day, i cant see the beautiful sunrise. the wind here very strong, so do remember to bring your hat. there is a lake in the white sand dunes. my driver took me there, and helped to take lots of photos there. very beautiful photos. after that, he drove me to the double road, taking photos there again. what impressed me is he asked me to stand on the top of the jeep, and take photos of me. actually it is the second time i came here. the first time i started the journey in afternoon, not for sunrise or sunset, actually it is the same, the most important for me is my driver can help me to take photos, cuz i travel alone. highly recommended for thuong jeep tour and also white sand dunes.

Quad ride in the sand dunes. A sand dune as far as the eye can see and a quad bike ride for those who want it. Hold on... The quad will make you experience beautiful sensations. Automatically translated

Stunning viewsStunning views and a lot of fun. We are booked in advance for all services, so it is so free and easy. We had a quad with a driver. We ride to the top of the white sand dunes hill. And it was fun, a lot of taken photos, and safe. * Sunrise is very nice but too crowded.
Quite a nice pile of light sand. It is possible to ride the dunes on four-wheelers, which is a nice experience... :)
Who knew Vietnam had desert-like landscapes? Sandboarding down the dunes in Mui Ne was a surprisingly fun and unique experience.
The quads are great fun. 600,000 VND for one person, maybe not cheap but it's worth it. Even teenagers can drive them. You can pay on site with a credit card. Sand slides 50,000 VND. But also great.

Fantastic experience! An experience I will not forget, seeing the white dunes, and not least the nice camels that we took a short trip with. We chose to walk instead of quad bike/car. It was very nice to wander around the countryside.
Morning or day trip. Fascinating landscape, less touristy than Red Sand Dune. It's better to take a taxi and explore the area on your own. You'll have more time then ✌️

White Sand Dune Adventure We reached there in the evening to watch the beautiful sunset. We ride a 4WD vehicle to go to the top of the white sand dunes hill. It’s thrilling and adventurous. The wind is so stong and you can feel the sands chit your face. Adviseable to wear mask/scarf. My kids love it so much.
Amazing, amazing, amazing. One of The best time and experience of my life watching the sunset and on the quad bikes over the dunes. Would recommend to anyone and wish I could do it again
They have quad bikes and camels. Sand is very clean unlike red sand dunes which are a like a garbage dump.

The Better of the Mui Ne Dunes The White Sand Dunes are a curious bit of geography near Mui Ne. Look in one direction and you may as well be in the Sahara; look the other way and you see a deep blue lake with tall grass and lotus blossoms. It’s a nice bout of exercise to climb up and down the dunes, and you can romp around on an ATV if you’re into that. If you’re in Mui Ne, go to the White Dunes. They are significantly better than the Red Dunes.

Fun experience in jeep It took almost 40 mins to reach from Mui Ne centre. It costs us VND800k to rent the Jeep. The area is not that big and could covered by walking but part of the fun is getting in the Jeep or quad bike for the adrenaline rush when going off the dune edge at almost 80 degree like roller coaster!!! As it is windy the sand would be blown to your skin and hit at speed which hurts a bit. So it is advisable to wear long sleeve and pants esp for young kids.

Stunning views and a lot of fun, even for us oldies!! We had a quad with a driver, and it was fun, and safe. The views were wonderful, very windy up there. Due to the pandemic, only local tourists, so a bit more expensive than usual. But worth it!

처음에 레드샌듄을 보고 이쪽을 가서 그런지, 많이 작아 보였습니다. 다만 짚차 투어 하시는분들은 레드샌듄 보다는 확실히 사진찍기엔 좋으실거에요. 레드 샌듄보실땐 너무 어두운데 화이트샌듄에서는 오히려 인생샷이 더 많이 나왔습니다. 다만 풍경보고 정적인곳은 레드샌듄이 훨씬 좋아요. It's not as big Dune as Red Sand Dunes. Take a photos at White Sand Dunes if you take a Jeep Tour. Red Sand Dunes is too dark to take a pictures, so you can take the best shots at the White Sand Dunes.
Our local guy took us here. You can walk up the dunes or get in a truck with others for a price or indeed hire a quad bike to ride the dunes. Take some water, its very hot and exposed.
Go there and go with quadbikes!!! Bet time ever. Lovely sunset, wonderful views and you are not gonna regret going there for sure

We did the sunrise tour to the white sand dunes and arrived here at around 5:15am ish, after using the tour through our Mui Ne Budget Hotel. We arrived and opted to take the quad up to the view point for 200,000 VND each. Yes this is an absolute rip off, they say it’s 5km and you wouldn’t be able to walk it but you absolutely can walk it. However when we got picked up by the quad the way back was down hills and they took adventurous routes. This half makes it worth it I suppose, but if you’ve done Huacachina in Peru then this doesn’t even come close. The sun rise is beautiful and I recommend taking a good camera to capture it. Would definitely recommend doing.

I was here by 4.30 AM and the view was amazing, you can go to the sun-rise spot with 4wheel quadbike and pay 200,000dong which I don't recommend you to do. You can walk and still get the best view just in time. Many people are walking too. I was with my hostel and I were travel with my own schedule, I will stay there longer!
Dune sloapes, way to go! Sloaping down the dunes, with the madress or the atv. Seeing the whole view, feeling the joy to be present here, the sunset and the happy people, delighted to be here.
Breath taking white sand dunes. Huge piece of nature available by ATV (quad), jeep or walk. Pure joy of view and wind. Hope you will enjoy as much as I did:)

Must see is you visit Mui Ne. Make sure you have an international drivers licence with you. If not the going rate is 1 million dong in the morning when using the costal road. Afternoon the cops where gone.

The dunes are amazing and going there to see the sunrise or the sunset is worth it. Be careful with the excursions they offer to you and what they Include: ask for the info written down. They will tell you it is mandatory to take a quad or Jeep to go up: it is not. You will have to walk a little bit and it is steep: but you can make it to the smallest one: which is better than. The bigger dune, as you will be in the front line.

Wonderful morning on the white sand dunes We went here two times. One with Pandanus Resort were we stayed. And that was the best visit. We were alone that morning and the sun was shining and it was quite. We took Done amasing photos. And the second time was with every one else. Lots of people and crowded. It was nice to. Many cars and more drivning around on the dunes.

Once in a lifetime experience We went there on our way back to Ho chi Minh city from Da Lat on our tour van (20th Dec 2019).We rented 2 jeeps costing VND600K each. We took lots of pictures and the scenery on top of the dune is beautiful if most of my eyes half-clised due to the glaring sun.It is advisable to wear a mouth cover&dark shades where the wind blew very hard and the sun is glaring if you visit in the afternoon (I learn my lesson when my mouth filled with bits of sand). The most exhillarating moment was when the jeep-drivers took us on a sharp descent down the dunes where we screamed and laughed like crazy.Everyone was happy eventho exhausted, including our bedraggled looking tourguide (it's her 1st time,too🤣🤣)

Stunning View. We went their to see the sunrise. though we did not the see the perfect sunrise because of the clouds, it was still beautiful. The unexpected thing was the ATV ride.. the ATV driver drove us all the way down to the steep sand with a full speed.. it was like riding in a roller coaster. it was actually fun.

Cool Desert in Vietnam! You can choose to walk, take a jeep or ATV up to the top of the desert dunes. The transport are pretty expensive but it is worth if you want to cover all Mui Ne attractions in a couple of hours. The view of the dunes is simply breathtaking. Do the desert jump shot!
It is beautiful to walk in the white sand dunes. It is like in the Sahara. The lake closeby is beautiful and the view and contrasts of both are just splendid.

A mini Dubai jeep ride in Mui ne. Taking a jeep ride to the peak is very enjoying yet crazy. Great picture opportunity but careful of high wind as the sand might get into your eyes. Also few hours drive with bad road condition from Da Lat

My trip to Mui Ne was 3 days 2 nights. On the second day, I booked a jeep with 500,000 vnd for whole day explore 4 places (fairy waterfall, mui ne harbor, white sand dunes, red sand dunes) with my friend. The driver sent us to white sand dunes and there us a station sell ticket as 200k to the hill and 300k vnd (go to the hill and lake). The landscape is awesome, driver also friendly.
Really nice views. Some say it is just sand and, well, it really is, but a very nice one, can't argue with that. It os worth a visit.

We arrived at 8am in the morning. The blue sky and the beautiful white sand dunes made us spent quite a long time here to capture beautiful scenery! The sands are soft and smooth. On the other side, there is a beautiful lake!

1300 years of history The dunes were really pretty as well as really enjoyed seeing them, nevertheless, I would not point out they are a must see plus you are missing out just in case you don't see them as we have all witnessed dunes before these basically ultimately be truly big. There is the chance to lease a quad bike which were pleasurable although they did show up as providing they'd been going that fast it was a bit of pricey and also were definitely really loud wrecking the peace on the spot completely.

Really impressed with the sand dunes, drove atv's to the highest point where the view is unbelievable, our 2 boys did the plastic sledge's down the hill which they enjoyed too, quite expensive for the atv's but worth it for the view and experience

Must do when in Mui Ne Highly recommend a sunrise tour at the dunes. It is spectacular to sit and watch the sunrise. You need to pay for an ATV to get up and back. It is 200,000 per person for one stop, 300,000 to go to the top then across to the gully. The ATV's go quick and our ride up was a little scary. We realised once we go there people were requesting them to go slow, so if you are not there for the thrill of the ATV flying up the dunes ask for them to go slow. We went in a 4WD after that as the the line up for the ATV's was long.
A must visited place in Mui Ne A beautiful sand dunes by the big lake, driving the big bike on the sand hills, feel the win, enjoy the sunset, what a place, love it and will come back for sure.
I really like how this place looks like a desert, but not literally desert! It's fun to ride ATV (quad-bike). I really enjoyed it. :) Make sure to notice the lake beside the sand dune.

Walking in the white sand It is another must see place in MuiNe.walking in the white sand just like walking in the white desert.before you go the the top if the dunes,you must take jeep or ATV car to the top,or you may wasting time if you walk up to the top.some travel company do offer jeep service to the top,If not,you must bargain with the operator here,and it is not cheap.be noted that the ATV car driver drives very fast,so be careful,must hold very tight,or you may fly out of the car.when you want to go down,just wave your hand,the driver will take you down again (but not the same man,sometines have t queue up),cuz i am on tight schedule,cannot stay longer,will come again.
This was such a fantastic sight to see and they take you to the top of the dunes on a quad which is very fun! Not many people there and a wonderful sight to see.

Booked it through out hostel was a good day morning trip. Got a private jeep and visited the red dunes and fairy springs. Paid 580,000dong between 5 people. Also went to a fishing village, not worth it stunk of fish and fish guts everywhere was hungover and didn’t help. If you book the private tour avoid it and stay longer at the dunes. Even thou we only spent 5 mins at the fishing village more than enough time unless your into that.

Make sure you go to white sand dunes not red and definitely pay for the quad bike tour. The guys in the yellow uniforms only. They were so much fun. Value for money. Thrill seeking adventure. Would highly recommend to anyone.

White sand dunes was the first destination on the sun rise jeep tour we booked with the tour agency right outside our accommodations (tour agodaa outside Bo Guest House). 500k VND for 2 pax from 4.30am to 10.30am to visit the White Sand Dunes, Red Sand Dunes and Fairy Stream (after negotiations). We got on our jeep at 4.30am, and it took about half a hour to get to the white sand dunes where we were promised a breathtaking sight of the sun rise. Sadly, we were a little late and missed seeing the sun rise on one of the sandy peaks; we witnessed the sun rise while waiting for an ATV. It costs 300k VND per pax to ride the ATV, which proved to be exhilaratingly bumpy! It was my first time riding on an ATV and I found the experience amaaaazing; just hold on tight to the side as they caution you to do. Once you got on an ATV, they will drive you to a peak and leave you there. You can then take your own sweet time to admire the view and walk and climb around, or pay 20k VMD to slide down one of the steep slopes. Remember to keep your receipt for the ATV ride so that you can get another ride down later; we spent more than 2.5 hours playing around with the lovely white sand and walking up and down the peaks before we finally waved our receipt around and got a jeep to drive us down.
This is well worth a visit and the best way is to take a sand buggy ride it is great fun costs about £25 for 30 minutes.

VIETNAM 12 DAYS TOUR ARRANGED BY FAR EAST TOUR , HANOI I worked out an itinerary for visiting Central and South Vietnam with the help of Esther Tran an experienced travel consultant with Far East Tours . With a lot of input from her we were able to work our a rather tightly scheduled itinerary covering Ho Chi Minh, Mui Ne, Dalat, Nha Trang, Danang, Hoi An , Hue and finally back to Mui Ne. After arrival at Ho Chi Minh we left the next morning for a three hour drive to Mui Ne which is a very quiet and serene beach resort in SE Vietnam where we spent the day on the beach in preparation for a very early morning tour starting at 5am to the White sand dunes to catch the sunrise. The sand dunes were really an awersome sight and something you would never expect to find in a lush tropical country like Vietnam but in fact the dunes were there and very grand giving you visions of the sahara desert . From the foot of the dunes we changed from a jeep to sand buggies up to the top of the dunes and after sunrise which was a grand sight we moved to the red sand dunes and the fairy streem followed by a visit to the fishing village. The next destination was Dalat in the Central highlands where the scene was totall different with cool and dry weather and a very European feel to the city where we spent two nights visiting the sights and attractions and then moved on to Nha Trang with the beach spawning for as far as the eye could see . We visited some attractions and had a mud bath which was a speciality of the city Following on we took a flight to Danang and was driven to Hoi An, an ancient city which was very serene and peaceful and stayed at the Ancient House Resort a very cosy and small resort but with very friendly and sociable staff and a very cosy feel . From Hoi An we drove back to Danang in transit to Hue the Ancient Capital and a hub of culture and history . Finally from Hue we flew back to Ho Chi Minh and joined a Mekong Delta tour before ending our very hectic but rewarding tour of part of Vietnam. All this was made possible only with the hard work and dedication of Esther from Far East who took pains to get all hotels and tours set up despite being the most busy season for travel in Vietnam being the Tet New Year and also the Chinese New Year and an influx of Chinese and SE Asian tourists . Despite the fact that it was her holiday and that of the company she was following up on the tour progress daily from her home and made sure that I was informed in advance of the following day's schedule , the tour guide's contact and timing so that we had a hassle free vacation thanks to her hard work. I would feel that Far East Tour is a reliable and dependable tour operator in Vietnam and would recommend them to any of my friends or associates who plan to visit Vietnam or for that matter other parts of IndoChina . Likewise if I should plan another trip for the area they would be my first choice

Who would know that there are beautiful, untouched sandunes in Vietnam? I went as part of a tour with my hostel. They provided transport to the red and white sand dunes, the fairy stream and the floating village. To access the white sandunes, we went to a company that offers quad biking and sand bashing in jeeps. I shared a bike with a friend because they are 800,000VD (£26) for one quad bike for 20 minutes. I loved the experience. Driving up and down the dunes at speed was liberating. I loved being at the top of a dune, with a deep blue lake to look down at and being in the face by loads of sand. You can just sit in a jeep and dunebash for 150,000VD. I really recommend seeing this. It’s once in a life time.

A must see in Mui Ne, Vietnam I'm staying at Longson Mui Ne Resort and they offered a $5 (£3.76) tour to a number of places including the White Sand Dunes. It was around a 30-minute drive to the dunes. We pulled into a small garage-like place that had a number of quads. There were a number of small ones but I got a big one that cost me 400,000 VD (£12.97). The other option was to go up to the top in a jeep for 150,000 VND (£4.86). One of the employees came up the dunes with me on the quad and directed me. It took me between 5 and 10 minutes to get to the top. It was really windy and sandy. I would highly recommend sunglasses and a cap (make sure they're on tightly). You'll also need sunscreen, lots of water and some closed shoes. I chilled at the top and took some pictures. I had a view of the lake right at the end of the dunes and some greenery immediately behind it. I then lost my sunglasses because of the fierce wind 👍🏽 I then went down on the quad for around 20 minutes. It was so much fun riding the dunes. If you're in Mui Ne, you need to check this place out! Just make sure you're prepped.

Or VND300,000 for a drop off at the peak + a visit to the lake + a drive back out. Or if you don't to pay at all, you can also just walk in. We booked with "muine-explorer.com" to get us to the sand dunes for sunrise from Ho Chi Minh city. We were a bit unsure about the whole thing being 3 female travellers and having to get into a car at midnight to drive for 4 hours, but it ended up being fine and the driver was noticeably more cautious than any other we've experienced in Vietnam. He drove us to a resort (Ca Ty Resort) where we waited in the abandoned lobby area until right on 4.30am a jeep turned up to take us to the dunes. Communication was zero the whole time, but again, all worked out fine. We were also driven to the red sand dunes and the fairy stream. No tour guide and very limited English, you're literally just paying for the ride. We paid at the very end when he dropped us back to our hotel in Ho Chi Minh. I'm just writing this particular info because I know it would've been helpful to us before doing this tour ourselves, as there was very little info on them and they seem to be the only ones we could find who do a day trip from Ho Chi Minh city.

Pretty much the reason why we decided to go to Mui Ne was the White Sand Dunes and we had a blast. We rented the ATV for 20 min to drive ourselves and afterwards they dropped us off at the top, we stayed there for another 20 min aprox, took some pictures and came back to the starting point. We paid 600K for the 20 min, but careful here because the guys in yellow tops may give you a higher price; they give the owner the 600K and then they keep the difference. Also keep in mind, that the 20-min driving is always accompanied by a guy that will help you to drive around as they know exactly where to go, so it was three of us on the ATV. We found 2 places were we could rent the ATVs, but at the end, we went to the 2nd place on the left-hand side if you are coming from Mui Ne Town. It was very busy, they had so much business that I would say it was going to be impossible to haggle, the owner was busy walking here and there, and people just wouldn't stop arriving. I understand some people may think that the ATV are loud and break the whole calm and peaceful atmosphere of the sand dunes and its true, so maybe if you don’t want any ATV around go very early in the morning. Also, I heard that the ATV were not powerful enough, I don’t think its true, they can go really fast even with 3 adults on. We paid 15K/person as entrance fee but not everyone was paying, we got stopped by the security guys, anyway we were not going to argue. Parking was for free.

Make sure to wear sunglasses so sand does not go in your eyes and buy a nose and mouth mask for 1 dollar there so sand does not go in your nose and mouth. They sell refreshments and fresh coconuts too.

Lovely place but go early before it gets too hot We did both red sand dunes and white sand dunes. IMHO, the white sand dunes are much nicer. The dunes are larger and the peaks are higher. Go early because you want to be there early before the crowd arrives and before it gets too hot. And of course catch the sunrise. We stayed outside muine and took about 30mins to 45mins to reach the dunes. Go by a cab. The jeep is utterly gimmicky because it won't be able to travel on the dunes. That's if you want to experience a jeep ride and have the wind blowing in your face. We took a taxi over. Any timing after 9 to 10pm will be too hot. We got there around 530 to 545 and took alot of pictures. The dunes are vast enough for a quiet spot of photos. Oh. Not to forget, do remove your footwear when walking on the dunes for the complete experience.

The sand dunes were very well maintained with little to no trash lying around. The sand dunes were untouched as we arrived early before the sun rise. When the sun cast its golden glow on the dunes, it looked beautiful. Must see in Mui Ne.

Very interesting destination Our last trip to the White Sand Dunes would have been a delight. The sand dunes were perfect, no entry fees without too crowded. Now they provide sand buggy thrill tours as well as the sand dunes are littered with trash. At any rate, try to visit quite early in the morning prior to the tour bus crowd as well as the thrill seekers!

I was pleasantly surprised to think it is. When I initially arrived, I feel it was a little dirty, however when I put the foot of mine on, it felt extremely pleasant. You simply keep on walking in the water (very shallow, simply by your ankle) and the perspective will keep getting much better. The spring is in the middle of something I believed was stone, though it seems it was in addition a sand dune because I stepped on it to have the photo and sands began falling down. Extremely cool.

relaxed and beautiful! It was a genuine thrill to get the quad bikes above the sand dunes. The scenery was beautiful as well. Do not make use of attempting to glide on the dunes though. Despite looking high, they're not rapidly enough and most you should do is get talked about in the sand.
Would recommend to go in the morning to catch the sunrise where the weather is still cooling. Trust me guys, you will love the view of the sunrise
It’s pretty cool place to visit make sure you take atv up to the top specially for sun rises worth the money

This place is beautiful! go on a sunny day, Be sure to take a camera. You will wind up investing aproximatelly 30 45 minutes driving around though it is certainly well worth the trip up there. The lake along with greenery is gorgeous and the drive,..
Loved the white dunes While not an early riser, it is certainly worth getting up early to watch the sun come up over the White Dunes. The sand is super fine and is so lovely underfoot. Magical!

These sand dunes are extensive and I think unusual to not be directly on the coast. It is about a half hour's drive from the town. Once you arrive your choice is to walk up the steep dunes or else hire an ATV. We took the ATV which was an exhilarating ride up. The ride up is fast and a little bumpy but just hold on and you are OK. It is breezy at the top but you are afforded nice views both of the ocean and also of the dunes stretching into the distance. There are good opportunities for picture taking. I read reviews of boys who rent you round discs upon which you can sit and slide down the dune. I was warned that they often will steal your possessions. They were there but I did not see any issue. To be safe just have a member of your party hold your camera, phone etc. It probably is opportune to arrive in time to see the sunrise of sunset. We did not do so but this as still a fine experience.

COMMON WRITE-UP (Here’s my common write up for 4 sights) Took the tour which is a must do in Mui Ne unless you plan to go free and easy yourself. It’s a USD 25 for 2 persons covering 4 locations, namely the White Sand (5 stars), Red Sand (3 stars), Mui Ne Harbour (or Fishing Village – 5 stars) and Fairy Stream (4 stars). They have 2 options, a morning 4:30am to 9am or afternoon 1:30pm to 6pm. I would highly recommend the morning session for the following reasons: 1. It is much cooler in the morning and makes the whole trip so much more pleasant. Imaging going to a desert like place (white / red sands) in the heat of the afternoon. 2. You get to see the catch in the morning at the fishing village. The fishermen closes at 9am so I guess there will not be much to see in the afternoon. 3. You can extend your trip (cause I think trying to cover the 4 places in the allocated time is too much of a rush) by asking the drive to leave at the last stop and taking your taxi back from the last stop which happens to be the Fairy Steam. The trip back to my hotel was just US$5.50 (I was staying at Anantara, you are likely to stay nearer hence even cheaper fare) so it was well worth it. The tour first takes you to the White Sands where you get to see sun-rise. From there they will proceed to the Red Sands, then Fishing Village and finally Fairy Stream. The time allocated for each is not long, I told them upfront I will take my time (which I did). By the time I reached Fairy Stream was 8:30am, I told the drive to leave and that I will find my own way back (i.e. US$5.50). I had breakfast, then proceeded to Fairy Stream. By the time I left Fairy Stream it was 11:10am. So all in all it was great that I took the morning tour as it allowed me to extend my whole sight of the 4 locations without rushing and at the same time enjoy the morning air. SPECIFIC WRITE-UP (White Sand Dunes) Enjoyable trip. Brought my drone along to catch the sights. I would recommend paying the VN 200K per person to take the 4WD. It beats having to walk up and down on your own which you will be doing anyway at the Red Sands. Spent about an hour up on the top of the white sands (the guide originally suggested 40 mins).

Good for start the day of tour It's good place for visit for sunrise, nice view and not too hot. I was booking half day tour with company and visit this place. You can walk or you can use their local service for go up the hill. As I am going with my mom we use motor for go up but when we go down; they give us a ride on Car. Very Good....

We booked Hai Tuan travels to visit White sand dunes and It remained the top exciting places we visited in that jeep trip. There is an entry fee for White sand dunes ( this was covered by our trip cost ). Once you enter all you can find is the Quad bikes and Jeeps that will take you to the TOP dunes. Please remember if you dont visit Top dunes ,there is no point visiting sand dunes at all. The Jeep ride(Operated by driver ) to-and-fro for Top dunes ( 6-7 persons) costs 300000 Vietnam dongs(for an Hour ) and Quad bikes costs 700000 to 900000 Dongs( for 20 minutes ) . Whether you ride Jeep or Bike depends on your adventure style but I strongly believe the Jeep never fails to give you the same excitement that the quad bikes deliver. Once you are at the Top dunes, You can rent (50000 Dongs) a Slider ( to sit on a plastic sheet that you will be pushed down the sand dune slopes ) .You can visit Sand dunes in the early morning for Sunrise or afternoon to evening for Sunset views. White sand dunes has stolen my heart and always stands in my heart across the sands of time. Travelers visiting Mui Ne , pen down this beautiful place on top of your Must -do List.
Fantastic trip, white sand with contrast blue winter behind Must visit. We went there during afternoon and it was breezy. The blue water with fine sand gives perfect picture views.
So much fun. Go early to watch the sunrise on the top of the dune. Make sure to rent an ATV.....makes all the difference!

Like a dessert in the middle of vietnam Totally unexpected when I first googled tours to do in Vietnam! Who knew This country would have its own mini Sahara dessert. There is the option to hire quad bikes or take a jeep ride up to the top or just walk up. The pictures you get are amazing. Very random but also on site there is an option to ride an ostrich lol which my husband found hilarious

Expensive but beautiful Paid 620,000VND to ride a quadbike on the dunes for 20 minutes. A little pricey, but the experience was worth it, very liberating to ride around freely on the dunes. And there was a bunch of puppies at the shops, not sure if they're always there but it made our day!
Giant sand dunes made by wind from the sea, a must see in Mui Ne, a jeep ride is recommended for this attraction visit.

This is a place of great natural beauty. You can spend an hour or so riding an ATV or buggy on the dunes but the best activity is to just sit on one of the taller dunes and just enjoy the scenery. You will spend around 100-150€ for vehicle to blast over the dunes for 30-60 minutes but it is well worth it. Totally recommend visiting this place!
It's a beautiful place. Do rent a jeep when you come here. Highly recommended when you come to Mui Ne
we had a great afternoon here, mind you to go on the quad bikes was not cheap so be warned. But it was really good fun & my GF just loved it.

White sand dunes is a unique place in Vietnam. A great place to enjoy sunset. Go around 1-2 hrs before sunset and sit and enjoy the beauty of nature. Jeep and bike disturbs the place but select a location which is not on their route.

Nobody should visit this area without going to the sand dunes. There are plenty of options for accessing this place. We chose to rent a motorbike and ride there ourselves. about 14 km from the city. The sand is beautiful and the views from the top of the dunes are classic

We joined jeep tour paid USD 25, our driver pick us up on time around 4:30am It was about 45 minutes ride from our hotel, wear your wind breaker with hood, Jeep doesn't have window, if morning cold and wind could make little uncomfortable during whole ride. Pay for ATV, well worth to pay, ATV rider straightly send us up to top of sand dune for sunrise, It tooks less than 5 minute to top, but if you choose to walk, imagine walking on a soft sand and a little up hill climb, it going to make you spend lot of energy. The best part, ATV include way to top, and return to where you come from. On return was about 40 feet from top steep roller coaster ride down to flat ground. Best part of the trip. Unfortunately we did not see sunrise clouded on that day, but an early morning breeze on top of sand dune does pay off.
Do not miss.even even if get up early in the morning.recommend for any who visit muine.do not miss and take atv just for experience most fun.

Beautiful sunrise over white sand suns. Be aware the quad bikes/4wd utes up the dunes is expensive but worth it! Sand boarding was expensive but kids had a ball. Would have loved to have had the chance to walk around a bit more but we were on a tour so time was limited. Suitable for kids to elderly.
A very interesting location where you can rent a 400 or 600 VND quad bike for 20 minutes and ride along the dunes ... nice pictures

Unreal experience with great views at the top! Great spot to visit in Vietnam 🇻🇳 We visited as part of an organized tour. There are a few options: 1. Driver + quad bike (2 passengers); 2. You can drive the quad bike (driver as passenger + 1); 3. 4WD + driver (6 passengers) All options are the same price at $600,000 dong approximately AUD$33. We chose the 4WD option as we had 6 pax. If you are an adrenaline junkie I would recommend the quad bike as the driver can take you down steep sand dunes. From observations looked like a lot of fun! Unreal experience driving through the sand dunes and when you get to the top breath taking views. A must visit as part of your Vietnam trip if this is of interest.
impressive dunes, but best to avoid the busy times (sunrise/sunset) when most of the tour groups arrive. it's better to go by yourself by jeep if you can

We really enjoyed our trip to the white sand dunes. Cheapest way up is to rent a jeep. Don't walk, it's too hot and too far and you won't get to other areas. We also rented a couple of sliders and had a ball with them. Careful - wear a hat - it's very hot and sunny!

Must do if you’re in Mui Me We rented a Jeep that fits 6 and a buggy that takes on 3. They charge 600,000VD for 20 minutes. The scenery is amazing and the fun of riding the buggy was the highlight for our teenage kids. If you’re in Mui Ne, take half of your day to include this activity. We didn’t do the sunrise because it was too early. But we were there for the sunset and it’s marvelous.
Cool IF you rent four-wheelers You have to rent the four-wheelers to get a decent experience - otherwise it's just a beach without water! :)
We didn't regret the quad ride - it was crazy fun. 20 minutes adrenaline when riding up and down the sand dunes.
Nice experience. We drove both the jeep and the atv. Wear sunglasses or else you will have a dune in the eyes

The quad bikes are a massive thrill, but don't think that this is the only way to get to the top of the white dunes. The white dunes are massive and are super fun to run or slide down. They are at least twice as steep and long as the red sand dunes
My first time at White sands. Need to wake up at 4.30am , but the sunrise is awesome. Even walk to the top of sands not takes so long, deserve to visit again.
This place is blowing your mind away! In the middle of the (green) nature, you will find a little desert!
Cloudy morning to see the sunrise but dunes are great fun for family with young kids. Absolutely stunning white sand to play for hours and be a child again and again.

With Quad drove in the white sand dunes Very nice to see. Bigger than the red sand dunes. Its possible to walk or to drive with a quad for 30 minutes to the top of the dunes for 25 Euros for one Quad , we drove with 3 persons and one guide
First experience in white Sand Dunes Muine . Took a jeep with 45degree fly up and down . Really fantastic! Love it .

It is beautiful place with magnificent scenery of sand dune. The sand felt so soft when walk in barefoot. I will definitely come to here again since we failed to catch sunrise on our 1st attempt. It was cloudy at that time.

Excellent location at the end of the beach, away from all the activity but easily accessible by a quick taxi ride. The rooms are nice but could use some additional pest control. The beach front location is beautiful and quiet. The bar and restaurant are excellent. Pool is beautiful and well maintained. We will return!

It’s a vast expanse of barren land. Undoubtedly more pristine and breathtaking compared to the Red Sand Dunes . About 30 mins drive from Mui Ne town . Adrenalin junkies are recommended to rent the ATVs from the rental company available on site . From there One can explore the Sand Dunes just like in Dubai . There is a lake nearby but no swimming is allowed for safety reasons . A very picturesque location for nice photos .
Avoid renting the ATV (600k VND for just 20min). You can simply walk as the place is not too big. Strong winds and beautiful sand dunes. It overlooks a lake and makes it great for pictures.

I went there to see the sunrise and it was truly awesome experience! We actually walked up the hill - took us like 15 minutes (not 40 as I heard before), but either way is good - on foot or by ATV (was around 200 000 dong per person).
Touristy but worth it We visited here as part of a guided tour. We quad bikes through the dunes (I would recommend paying extra for the better quad bikes if you want to go up high) which was fun.

Had a great time watching sunrise here! We didn't book any tours as we didn't feel it was necessary. Went there by ourselves instead. Left hotel at 430am, took a cab and we reached the White sand dunes in half hour's time. Was able to catch the beautiful sunrise around 515am. If you want to ride ATV you can actually pay on the spot. We just took a lovely morning stroll on the dunes, took some nice photos and left. Overall nice experience!
Big and white sand dunes. Cool to rent a cruiser to ride around. Should prepare your hats and sunglasses
So pretty and the whole day tour was great with the fairy springs and red sands included. A must see in Vietnam.

Beautiful! Its a must yo visit in Mui Ne! The tour which I book did not include the transport in white sand dunes. You can choose to walk (freaky far! lol!) or spend 200k dong for ATV or jeep to bring you to the higher peak of the white dunes. (Worth every penny!)
Never thought & visualized till you see it ,especially the white dunes & the lake which offers a serene view of the entire place at sunset !enjoyed the jeep ride & the sand !!

Booked a private Jeep to go for the sand dune tour. White sand dune is really beautiful, and you should not miss the ATV rides. You can choose to drive ATV (of course it is more expensive). It is not easy because the sand is soft, and have to climb up and down. Paid to get ATV drivers give us a ride to the top of the sand dune. Trust me. Siting behind is nothing lesser than driving the ATV. It was so cool and fun! Sand slide is another activity in sand dune which is pretty fun too. Please make sure you wear pants/short if you want to play all these “xtreme” game. Of course if you just want to take great photo in sand dune, you can dress nicely too. Saw many ladies dresses up and took photos. Must try if you love sport!

-ATV couple cost 600.000 VND or 30$ Single cost 400.000VND -I see jeep 4wd for rent too. If you want come with 5-6 persons -You can ride by urself or ask for driver to do. -Area is very large around 5km, staff said. -You can walk around, until you feel satisfied If you go by motorcycle make sure you fill the gas full tank. Parking 5.000vnd Enter fee 10.000/person -Raining season not recommend. If you want take a photo without crowd of tourist, early morning maybe the answer. Afternoon will full of tourist from travel agent.

I visited this place in Sep. I highly recommend you to visit here :) If you didn't have been desert before, you can feel it in this place :) Using a jeep tour is normal for the tourists, and if you visit here on rain season please just make a reservation for group tour. In this season, there are not so much tourists are here in Mui Ne so you can take a jeep usually total 4 person. You also can do it with a private tour, but it doesn't really different with a group tour. Lastly, if you are a person who likes walking or tracking, it doesn't matter to climb up by yourself. But if you don't, please use a buggy (ATV) You should bargen a price for this!! And it looks really funny :) I regret to didn't take buggy in here.
The place is beautiful and the ATV ride was thrilling. A place worth going. I persoanlly would visit this place again during my mext trip to Mui Ne.

Super fun, surprisingly not hot We went on the 30 minute jeep tour in the middle of the day. I thought I was going to die about 5 times. This is not the same type of sand dune tour you go on in Dubai. It's more like a roller coaster ride. I loved it! Don't let the heat scare you away. It's really windy on the dunes so you don't feel the heat. I would definitely stop here if you're looking for something to do.

The white sand dunes is a must, u simply cannot miss it if you come to mui ne. Its beautiful and he most exciting thing in my tour in mui ne. The ATV ticket costs 200k vnd if u let one of the guys there ride you up. Its a beauty u will not want to miss at all. Be careful about ur bags caps and sunglasses tho. It can fly off when on the atv.

Bring a change of underwear if you intend to go on the quad bikes. Awesome big sand dunes set alongside an unusual lake. Great cafe and refreshments on site. Rent a car or quad and go crazy on the dunes. This is the only time i ever closed my eyes on a thrill ride. Crazy drivers!!

We went to the white sand dunes for sunrise and what can I say besides this is absolutely amazing. Well worth the early 4.30am morning to watch the sun rise and have some fun on the quad bikes. For $40AUD we had the time of our lives and one we will not forget
it is romantic place to visit here in early morning to see the sunrise.. wow fantastic!!! and every excited to drive on ATV car..

Well worth a visit to see the stunning views. We went as part of a jeep tour of mui ne organised through our hotel. The downside was that our driver did not drive us up the dunes but instead suggested that we either got expensive quads for hire or walked. We did walk but it was tiring and extremely hot. It was however, worth the view once we made it to the top of one of the dunes. Beautiful scenery and hard to remember that you're in Vietnam. Make sure you are firm with your driver and make them drive you up for a better experience!
Thrilling Ride and Views Take the dune buggys/ jeeps. Thrilling roller coaster like ride with beautiful lake and sunrise views.

If you are in Mui Ne, be sure to visit the White Sand Dunes. This small desert landscape I can recommend to everybody. You can do a walk up or also enjoy a motorized trip by a Quad or a Jeep. Ask for a higher motorized Quad, which is more expensive (600'000 VND for 20 minutes total time) but gives you more fun.

It's quite a drive out of Mui me but worth the effort! You can either be driven to the peak on a quad bike or drive yourself around but not to the peak since the quad bikes they lease are not powerful enough to get up there.

Experiencing sand dunes here is amazing, i went here during sunrise it's so beautiful, after pay about usd 38 for jeep and half day tour when u arrive here they will asked u to pay around vnd 600 per couple to take you to the highest dunes to see the sunrise with atv motor. After 10 minutes they will takes you to another spot for 5-10 minutes before they take you back. They will offer atv rides that feels like roller coaster.. amazing experience. Other option is you can walk to the highest dunes but seems like everyone use atv motor. Tips for driver additional vnd 100
We did the 4wd landcruiser option kids loved it so much we got them to take us for another round, 500,000 dong per circuit

Good fun but expensive Great fun. They do quote 400k dong for quad bikes at agent but if you wait to pay there it's 1.2mil dong per bike. You can have 2 on the bike. You also have the option of just going in a jeep as a group. The dunes are good fun but a quick experience.

MISSED THE SUNRISE DUE TO JEEP DRIVER BEING LATE! Better to book private tour so can stay there longer and leave earlier! There is ATV rides which is amazing however it cost 200000 VND for just a short ride! There is sand boarding too however that day I was charged a hefty price of 50000 VND! Not enough time so I only get to slide down once. It was difficult to climb up too!!

Very beautiful view of the dunes. Went for a sunrise tour to see the dunes and it was well worth it! Such a rare sight to see, and can't really be found anywhere else in Southeast Asia (from what i know). The sand was soft and cool under your feet as you hike up to the tallest peak to take in the whole view. There is also ATV's available for rent if you prefer, which looked fun but can be a little costly. You can book a tour that includes both the white and red sand dunes.
Its a beautiful place to visit,the scenery is good to take photos,but too many tourist when I visited there,maybe if you visit in the morning is not to crowd.....

You need to hire a jeep to get up the sand dunes (600,000 VDN) but it's a huge part of the adventure. The drivers are fast and aggressive but if you hold on it's a fun ride. Sand dunes themselves are beautiful.

I stayed at Mui Ne Hills and they provided a tour for 100.000vnd to 4 places and white sand dunes is one of them. The tour started very early at 4:15am so we can catch the sunrise. It was such a beautiful moment.

Nice place to enjoy sunrise We book a Mui Ne half day tour to enjoy sunrise at the place. The tour starts quite early (4:30 AM) but it was worth as the sunrise was so nice. The best thing to do here is sand surf.

We went here in July and we loved it so much we visited three times! We walked up it twice which takes about half an hour but it means you can take as much photos as you like to explore and take pictures! We did the ATV once which was expensive but I'd say it is definitely worth it for the rollercoaster spin they take you on around the dunes before going back to the base! Be aware of the guys who take you on the ATV though as it is expensive enough and our guy demanded more money of us for a tip.

Most people take the jeep or the 4wd bike then click and go. I didn't. Walked it for hours and found a spot where there's no footstep. The wind forms nice dunes that are beautiful with the green/blue backdrop of the sea.

Great experience with Sand dunes special car! Great view of nature with sand dunes & lake - amazing wonder of nature! We also enjoy so much the ride experience on special vehicle to conquer sand dunes in high speed!

Great morning start inorder to see sunrise! Ride in Jeep towards white sand was fresh n smooth route. Went up in ATV , thrilling n yet awesome feeling going up on the wild ride. Once at the top, view of sand dunes n lake niceee... A place not to be miss especially the morning bf sunrise.
the experience of ATV at the white sand dunes is exceptional! Advise you to visit the place in the afternoon with enough sun light, the photos look so good with the reflection of sand dunes!
If you have chance to visit Mui Ne, you should go there. Amazing experian. It will give u little bit of scary but lots og fun and so many nice photo

Splash out and hire an ATV. If you're a motorcyclist you can negotiate to drive yourself with one of their staff accompanying you. Wow! Fantastic fun whizzing up and down these towering dunes. Set your own timer tho so you know when your half hour is up, I think we got a bit shorted here. But, the views and photo opportunities were second to none. A must do!

As Wide As Ocean, As Smooth As Powder 😍 Come early to see sunrise!! And after that me must try fast sliding with ATV, OMG Its so scary for me, but its a Amazing experience with beautiful landscape. So shiny 🍺🍦
Came here as part of a tour, loved the dune buggy ride through the white sand dunes. Just amazing! Well worth the trip and a must do!
Atv roaring thru the dunes OK so I know we hugely overpaid but we had an absolutely blast spinning all over the place through the dunes at 5 am, watching the sun rise!
Beautiful place , exotic place , interesting for traveling , family treveling , nice tour to Dunes, Quadrobike , Jeep 🚙 safari .
The scenery is amazing...a small lake,desert and pine tries. you can even walk on the send untill the top of the dune where you can enjoy the view.great experience.

It is a must to visit when you travel to Ho Chi Minh. Just took 4 hrs sleeping bus from HCM to Muine and take the day tour. Its really amazing experience and the view is awesome. Recommended to go before sunrise. It is cooler and you can see the awesome sunrise view.
This place is beautiful with lot of photo points. You can either take a jeep with friends or ride a sandbike.

Hire a motorbike and do it yourself The white sand dunes are a must do in Mui Ne. Instead of booking a tour, we decided to hire a motorbike and make our own way to the dunes, which were about 45mins from our AirBnB, Papaya. The best time to see the dunes is at sunrise - just make sure you give yourself enough time to get there and find a good viewing point. It's a bit of a hike to the top, so you can hire a quad bike with or without a driver (200,000d or 300,000d for two people).
The view is superb and is very good location for photo shooting. ⭐⭐⭐ must try for the jeep tour is really different. 👍👍👍👍👍

I booked a private jeep tour with my boyfriend in Mui Ne and the white sand dunes was part of the tour. It is INCREDIBLE to see something like that. At some parts, you can also see the water right besides the white sand dunes - the contrast between the color is EPIC. We also paid for the ATV ride to go up the dunes. It was 600 000 VND (approx.26 USD) for 20mins and it was totally worth it! They make you ride it down the highest spot. It's not something you can do anywhere so we did it and don't regret it!!
Awesome. Like we expected. Nice white sand. Very hot , but so much fun with the ATV. We did take a lot of pictures there. Nice view
Had a ball on the quad bikes and the views were amazing endless white sands the kids loved the jeep ride would definitely recommend a must do
Adventurous ATV ride that comes with a guide... Mui ne is a location not well known to many people, but it is abundant in natural beauty and not to be missed one

Why is everyone hating? The sand dunes were amazing!! We weren't too excited since everyone's being bare miserable in the reviews. We got an ATV tried to drive it ourselves but we weren't great so we asked the guy to drive for us and it was better than any rollercoaster and worth every penny (around twentyish dollars) we went in the late afternoon, it was cool enough and hardly anyone around. Dunno why most the reviews are negative, it was beautiful and one of the best things we've done so far in Vietnam! Ah yeah and we got stopped by the police on the way back but he was sound when we showed him our international license and let us go with a handshake.

Beautiful desert. My favourite among 3 more destinations in the tour programme We asked a local tour agency to help arrange our trip to Mui ne because during our visit to Saigon ( January 2017 ), a new year festival was going on " Tet Festival " & it is hard for us to book a bus on our own to get out of town. Paid around 39 USD for everything ( return bus ticket, hotel & Private Jeep Tour ) and the experience was Awesome !! if you were to visit this place by bus, be sure to ride a sleeper bus for an add on experience. Price may be a little bit ++ than a normal bus, but the experience is worth it. some tips during your visit to WSD : 1. Bring your sunglasses along to get the best view of the surroundings.The weather's quite windy !! 2. You'd have to rent an ATV to get on to go up the dunes. you can walk too but it will take some up some time & energy to get to the top.

The best exciting place Recommend to pay for ATV or Jeep in VND is better than in USD. If you choose ATV, recommend to let staff ride for you and tip them after game finished, you will have more exciting feeling than you ride by yourself.

Need some strategy to enjoy this place I have always want to visit Mui Ne after seeing many beautiful photos from my friend. White sand dune is one of the appealing place. Imagine you can get a scenery similar to those desert in Africa or Arabian country in just a few hours from Ho Chi Minh City. According to my experience, the best time to visit white sand dune is before sunrise. I took a tour package from Sinh Tourist. Initially they offer me for a package which leave HCMC in the morning, so we take afternoon tour in white sand dune. However, I ask for morning tour. So, I left HCMC at 8 pm and arrive around 1.30 am in the morning. I took a room in their hotel also to get sleep around 3 hours. I recommend to take jeep private tour instead of bus/minibus tour (if you go there with 2-3 other people). It is more flexible to arrange your time. I believe we need extra time to take photos or other selfies things! Don't forget to prepare 250k VND for ATV which will bring you from the edge of the road to the photo spot in white sand dune. It's quite far if you walk there. Jeep tour will pick you up at 4.30 am. When you return to HCMC on the same day, I recommend to take ordinary bus (non-sleeper bus). Afternoon trip with sleeper bus are tiresome (based on my experience from Dalat and Mui Ne).
Try to be here in the morning to be the first to see the sand dunes. Also good place for sun rise here. You could try sand sliding with a board or strong feeling with ATVs
Wake up so early for the sunrise !!! It's worth it! If you have the chance to come here, must visit it.

A relatively short drive from our hotel in Mui Ne, the dunes are rather impressive. The lotus lake was not that impressive. We opted to walk in the dunes, though some visitors obviously enjoyed a drive through them.

The white sand dunes have been included in our Fairy Streams Tour. It was a great experience. The landscape is incredible and the drive with the Jeep at the coast was as well worth the trip. We booked at the white sand dunes a quad tour and that was great although quite expensive. The ride was thrilling and we had great fun. Ok the guide took the lead and we raced through the dunes with a speed that was incredible.
A beautiful and surreal place! We took the ATV up and the driver was really skillfull.. a must visit when in Mui Ne.
4 wheels bike was so exciting!! Funny this is..I still can't understand........why i paid 1million Vietnam dong even i have International license...
we have rented quadro cycle at the station below the dune. the driving of it was so great, even better than rollercoasters. it was breath taking. you can rent a jeep with driver as well.

We were thinking about booking a tour here, but in the last minute we thought it would be fun to go there on our own. So we rented a motorbike and drove there. It was a bit further than we thought, when you think you have driven to far, just continue. It was really worth the trip, and fun to motorbike there. Just think about having an internationall drivers license if you go there by motorbike, we got stopped by the police and had to give them all of our money. Luckely we didn't have that much money with us. We came there at 17.30 and walked up the sand dunes, so we saw the sunset, it was really nice!

We did the afternoon tour. Great experience but (of course) very sandy... You have to pay an extra 15,000 dong to enter but they tell you in advance. To get to the top of the sanddunes, the 8 of us took a jeep which cost us 100,000 dong each. Pretty sight to see!
Amazing sand dunes, i will come back for more photos. Everyone is recommended to visit this place. Early morning should be better.
We had a trip here in feb, pretty place, you can take a quad up the dunes or walk but it is heavy going in the heat so early morning is best. Worth a visit though

I went for the Sunrise tour, was at the White Sand dunes for about an hour. We took the ATV up and costs us 300,000 VND per pax. The view was great but by the time the place gets crowded, it might be hard to get 1 perfect shot. It's quite chilly up there so would recommend to brink a jacket or so. The ATV ride back down was awakening, there will be a steep slope about 85 degrees which i might be exaggerating but damn it was the best adrenaline rush I've ever got at 6 am in the morning!

Me and my boyfriend took a taxi from the hotel to the fairy stream (less than 10 mins from 4 oceans resort) and took a morning stroll by ourself. It was so beautiful and calm and I will urge you all to take the stroll without any guide. We took a comfy pitstop at a nice little café centered at the fairy stream Where there was an mini zoo with monkeys etc. Very friendly and very very cheap. The fairy stream are free of charge so go by yourself and explore this hidden amazing river. Just a perfect way to start the day.
We went a bit late but arrived just in time to witness a beautiful sunset! At that time of day the sand had also cooled quite a bit so it was wonderful
I visited here many times and would come back again. Natural, beautiful view, lake here also. Thanks God to be here.
Take a 4 wheels bike and explore the sand dunes. The cost is around 50 dollars for 30~40 minutes but the satisfaction is more than that.

I went around 120 pm. Barely any tourists on the dunes. Got some amazing pictures ! There was a bit of trash but honestly not even enough to complain about. More broken flip flops than anything (I went to the sand dunes on the left. Not the main touristy one on the right) By about 3 pm there were soooo many more tourists . We left around 3:15 and on the way back there was a check stop, pulling over only tourists. I've read that it is a scam but it isn't. They asked for my international license. I showed it to them and I was on my way! Highlight of my asia trip. My friends ar super jealous of my pictures that I took!

Great Natural feature and quad bike fun. We hired a taxi for the morning to do a round trip of the red sands white sands and Mui Ne fishing port. We got to the white sands around 10am and we were the only ones there. Once you get to the top with the quad bike or Jeep the views are spectacular. Take a good camera. Hire the bigger quad bike for 600,000VND (you need it's higher torque for the steep bits) and drive yourself. Let the 14 year old driver they give you ride on the back except when you need his guidance. Look well ahead to gauge the terrain and keep the steering adjustments small. Keep the revs up or you will bog on the steep bits. It's great fun when you get it going. If you let the 14 year old drive keep him off the really steep stuff otherwise you will have good reason to be scared when he shows off his very good driving skills ! Not cheap but worth the money.

Exhilarating Experience Riding a quad bike or a jeep on the white sand dunes was such an exhilarating experience! The wind was so strong that you could feel the sand on your face! Riding further on, you could see a lake with trees which was a refreshing sight after seeing white sand dunes all around. Great!

White sand Dunes surrounding. We booked a jeep for 6 pax. The jeep took us to hills of sand for taking photos and sightseeing. I remembered when the jeep came down the hill and suddenly turn left, felt super fun. Just felt like playing thrill game in Universal studio Singapore. A must see when you visit Phan Thiet.
A must see must try place to visit! Thrilling and exciting to ride the quad bike and wrangler jeep to go around the sand dune.

Tiring Climb but beautiful view! Stayed at a hostel and they offered this tour on a jeep. Quite fun, you can see the beautiful view. White Sand Dunes is much cleaner than the Red Sand Dunes, much more spectacular too. Between Red and White, choose the White one, I'd say.
Huge sand like Sahara! The tour drop us at entrance of sand so we need walk or pay to go on top. We couldn't see the sunrise but still beautiful place!

Me and my friends were originally going to skip Mui Ne due to the poor reviews of the white sand dunes. However we had some extra days on our visa so thought we'd give them a visit! We did a trip with our hostel in a mini van: white sand dunes, red sand dunes, fishing village and the fairy stream for 60,000 each (+10,000 for white sand dunes and +5,000 for the fairy stream). We arrived for sun rise and although it was cloudy it was still very beautiful and the sand dunes were more impressive than I originally imagined! They are a definite must-see!!!! It requires a little exercise to reach the peaks but it's definitely worth it - the pictures I took do not do it justice! However you can pay for a quad bike to take up to the top if you do not want to climb. There is a very small amount of litter however not as described in some posts I've read and it is more on the climb so does not ruin your views and pictures at all. I definitely recommend going for sun set as it is cooler and apparently less busy! We paid 200,000 each for a guy on a quad bike to take us down a few of the steep dunes - a treat for us as we are travelling on a very tight budget but honestly worth it! I laughed so much and you do not get sand in your mouth as I was initially worried! Highly recommend - so glad I didn't skip this place!!!!

Well worth a visit in the AM or PM We did both the sunrise tour and the afternoon tour (white sand dunes, Red sand dunes, fairy stream and fisherman Village). The sunrise was definitely worth the 4am wake up, we got some great footage but arguably the the afternoon was the better trip! You can do the quads and jeeps at either but the sunrise is too dark to get decent videos. The quads aren't cheap (we paid 650k for 20 mins on the back of a large quad with a driver) but well worth it! Taking us down the big steep dunes and to the untouched parts. Just absolutely exhilarating. We got some super videos on the GoPro. Definitely recommend.
We had a great time . Wild nothing like anywhere else. We rented an ATV And did few few dunes. Do not miss if you vist that area
Nice place to visit in the morning. Better to rent scooter then go with organized excursion. Sun protection necessary.

We visited the white sand dunes early enough to avoid the tourist and rented an ATV for 450 vnd (20 minutes) but before that we stood and watched them drive the ATV's and make sure they're working well. One guy rode with us and while my bf was driving it on the way to the top we got stuck and the guy immediately jumped off and pushed and helped my bf with the stearin and we finally made it to the top. It was beautiful over looking the lake and the rest of the sand dunes. We took photos and enjoyed the scene. On the way down the guy said that he will drive and he wants us to hold on tight. My bf just let him since he wasn't experienced enough with quads and the rest of the ride was adrenaline rush fun! It was an unforgettable ride! Another thing is that we weren't stopped by any Police back and forth but on our way back to town the Police officials were there with all the foreigners being stopped. See the sand dunes in the morning if you plan to go by yourself in your motorbikes.

We rented a motorbike to drive here which was a nice drive. There is police scam, pulling over foreigners and charging them fines or threatening to take your bike. We went in the morning and were able to avoid it, but on our way back we seen them stopping a lot of foreigners travelling to the white sand dunes. There is an entrance ticket of 15000D per person and then if you wish to rent a quad after that is additional money. We rented a quad bike and we had a great experience, we also got one of the guys to come with us incase we got stuck but it didn't happen. There is a lot of negative reviews about the quad bikes but we didn't have these experiences. You can also walk around after and get some great photos of the place. This is definitely worth a visit if have the opportunity to do so.

Here is something I never would have thought to see in Vietnam of all places. But standing at the top off a massive white sand dune with jeeps and quad bikes flying around all over the place, I could see, the sea, a beautiful pair of lakes, a forest and of course, the rolling sands around me. A surprise addition to my holiday during Tet. A must see for sure.

Booked the sunrise tour with the hostel and was picked up at 4.40am by the Jeep. Jeep driver dropped and told us to take the ATV up the dune, having been told by the dormitary mates that ATV is not a compulsory, with the nice morning cool air I saved 200k dong by walking my way slowly up. The walk was somewhat tedious as the sand is soft and it's a hike up. While I was regretting the walk I saw many ATVs and a jeep drove past me, as it was still dark I could not tell what was the different between the jeep that was allow to be drove on the sand dunes and the jeep I took from the hostel. It was our first stop and we were the first few groups up there and was able to get a good spot, seated and waiting for sunrise, crowd start to come in and some tourists decide to stand right in front of you, because everyone want the good spot, hence had to move again in search of a good space. Sunrise wasn't the best because it was a cloudy day so we did not see any sun, but the moment the desert light up slowly is still breathtaking. Was tempted to try sliding down the sand dune but not in the mood to haggle and got putted off by the greed of the vendors asking for 5 times the price the dormitory mates paid the day before. Although not much money, but the greedy will become greedier. I walked down the slope and had the my feet dipped into the soft and loose sand almost knee deep, which was somewhat a delightful experience. But I bet the sliding would be more fun. This attraction is obviously a tourist trap and the vendors are only interested in getting the most money out of the tourists. Nonetheless I enjoyed my first desert visit, the spectacular views. Would be looking forward to another visit at the non peak hours when I know of a way to avoid the traffic police ambushing tourist without a Vietnam driving license.

The red sand dunes is one of the most memorable experience i had in Vietnam as I didn't expect anything before I travel there. The view is beautiful and it is fun walking along the sand dunes and I felt like i was in a desert. The site has many children selling the sliding sheet, so you might want to try it. It is free of charge, so enjoy and take as many pictures as you can ! It's beautiful!

Come really early, beat the crowds and get a quiet spot to watch the sun rise. Stay longer here if you have taken the jeep tour - this is truly the best part. wear the right shoes - the dunes aren't that easy to walk on in sports shoes.

As we drive along the coast the scenery gradually changes .It becomes very arrid with scrubby shrubs and soon we come upon massive white sand dunes.It reminds of scenes fromthe Egyptian desert! We climbed only a short way as the sun was beating down and the going got tough. Braver (younger) visitors explored the big dunes with buggies and some even slid down on plastic boards.

White Sands was meant for just a few pictures, but ended up kind of falling for the place. You'll see people stopping early on the side of the road on the way in, but keep pushing on to the further destinations. Much more quiet and much less people. Here, that means better pictures. Freezing during the winter, but just pretend you're walking over snow.

Never thought Southeast Asia has a place like this! The sand dunes are just beautiful and impressive. The sand is very smooth though sometimes the strong winds will blow the sand to your face. I felt like I was somewhere in the Middle East. The view is just breathtaking and undeniably amazing. Well, of course you can pay for the ATV/Jeep tour ride but we preferred just walking on our own finding spots to take pictures in (guilty) and simply enjoy the moments. A must-visit!
Fun tour for those who like speed, excitement and any kind of motor equipped vehicles. Stated to be dangerous but we did not see the point. Great If you are familiar with snowmobiles.

Beautiful sand dunes, worth the visit Impressive white sand dunes close to Mui Ne. It is worth the visit. You can rent a 40 minute jeep ride or a quad bike at location. This visit can be combined with red sand dunes and visit to the fishing village. Tip: wear sun screen, a hat and a mask. The place can be very windy and fine sand will get everywhere. Also wear comfy shoes that does not trap sand.

We have been to many sand dunes back home in Australia so I expected these to be very much the same but I was pleasantly surprised. They are quite high and the fact that you can hire a quad bike and take these things on makes for a very fun day out. Excellent views from atop!

The sunrise and sunset are marvelous, be careful with the locals they will try to rip you of. I would recommend to hire a quad for an 1 hour, it will give you an adrenaline rush. Great photographs too :) . Avoid the dunes between 11 and 3 as it is too hot.

a tiny desert nearby the beach the desert looks amazing, even its tiny but you can enjoy walking on sand hills and great for taking pictures. we only walked on the sand and explored the area on foot but you can also join some activities there. to enjoy more for taking pictures or video, you can take your drone along with you or a go-pro with sticker.

Exciting and Unique - the Driest spot in Southeast Asia we were a group of 9 (1 child, 3 teenagers and 5 adults) and we went to the white sand dunes as part of our tour of Mui Ne (we will be back for an extended stay in the future). We arrived here at 5am...ish, and observed the sunrise, sadly on a cloudy day there was not much of a sunrise to observe, but as daybreak wore on, the views and scenery were superb. we booked a jeep for all of us, and this took us not only to various parts of the dune, and the lake, but the ride was exhilarating and this kept everyone excited while enjoying the serene views and scenery. There were quad bike tours also available and it was amazing to watch people riding down the dune's "cliff face" which we chose to walk down (slide more like it) This is a nice mix of excitement and scenery, lots of photo opportunities and if you come early in the morning, you also get the pleasure of making fresh steps on pristine dunes.
Is was kinda cool! If you are a bit crazy do the slide from the highest point. Otherwise, the 4wheels was cool! If you have money for it, do it. The cars was cool to just like a crazy rollercoster.
start a good day from sunrise Although i need to wake up very early, this place is worth it. Walking in a sand dune during sunrise is very romantic.

Sneakpeak of being in the dessert We went for the sunrise, unfortunately it was rainy so we did not see the sunrise but it was a great experience anyway. You can rent a scooter for 400 000 VD but we decided to walk from the base camp to the Top of the dunes. Good workout and you with the sand between your toes you have the real experience.

Very cool place, pretty realistic. Especially if the weather is hot. But since this season is rainy seasons, so it's pretty hard to to predict if when will be the best day to come here. But I do enjoy the trip here, the atv ride and the slide. You can feel the adrenaline rush. For sure it's gonna be much enjoyable if the weather is good, I would suggests that travelers can come here by wearing flippers only and of course by summer time would be really awesome.
We booked a jeep to bring us to the white sands dunes. About half an hour to reach here from mui ne town. we walk up because the atv is so expensive. The sunsets was so beautiful.

Tips: tag along the Jeep there super early in the morning and enjoy the awesome sunrise scene. Renting a cart there is ok but kinda pricey. Some practice and you will master the beast! Also from there you can take the new high-way to Phan Ri Cua city. The ride is just unforgettable!

I love this place so much!! Had lots of fun riding on quad bike. It's really nice to visit. Unfortunately, we got scam from the driver on the way back. He stopped and ask for tips, he made the atmosphere turned into awkward.

Definitely a must visit when in Mui Ne! The sand dunes were magnificent and not too crowded. We hired an ATV and had so much fun riding up and down the slopes. ATV is recommended if you have the budget, otherwise it is nice to walk around and enjoy the scenery as well.

If you are in the vicinity of Mui Ne, the spectacular sand dunes are a must-see! Who would have thought that a desert landscape like this would exist so close to Vietnam's shoreline? The view from the top of the dunes is fabulous and while it is possible to climb to the top on foot, most people prefer to be driven up in a jeep or to zip up and down the dunes at top speed on s quad bike, which is great fun!

You have definitely got to visit the white sand dunes, they are spectacular, I had flip flops on as you do and the sand is so hot it really burns your feet, so be warned. You can hire a sand sledge and slid down the dunes and you can hire a dune buggy which is fun. Great experience, definitely do this.
Went up in a brand new and powerful quadbike.. a bit scary but quite an adrenaline buzz! the views are extraordinary and spectacular..
My husband and I have really enjoyed driving in the dunes. Beautiful sunset. All trips recommended .

Really fun on the atvs We found this place after renting a scooter and just getting lost really, we did run into roadside police who will try to get any amount of money out of you especially if you don't have a international license, so if you do get stopped just ask how much they want and pay or they will take you to the police station and it's just not worth it, we paid but heard stories from other tourists who refused and ended up having to pay anyway, the dunes were really cool atvs were super fun ... some really good photo ops too up there.
It is a beautiful place for tourist visit and take pictures. I love going on motorcycle if you have time.

We booked the tour through the backpacker village and it was super cheap at 3.50 usd. This took you to both white and red sand dunes, fishing village and fairy stream. I'd recommend doing this early morning as we didn't pay entry and the view was stunning and not busy at all. I didn't think it was average like others are saying. Don't rent a quad bike though it's so close and the walk up is nice as well. Waste of money in my opinion...
Great place to go on motorcycle if you have time. Half day is enough. Area is quite large, but not necessary to take jeep to go around. Definitely nice pics from the area.
The Sand dunes are a lot more impressive than I expected. But it is pretty noisy because of the quads. Still very worth a visit!

We went there for the sunrise view and got pick up form the town at 4:30am, but unable to witness the sunrise still though. But the white sand landscape was beautiful. Makes u feel you're in a desert. You may either choose to ride by Atv or walk all the way to the center of the place. It was pricey for a short ride, but it's still a fun experience to try. There are locals offering like sliding board to try for sliding down the sands. You may also wana try to pass by the Lotus Lake just next to the entrance of the white sand dunes. It's a pretty view as well.

It is amazing to see sand dunes in the tropics. You can easily forget you are still in Vietnam! The dunes are huge, maybe over 40 m in height. There is no need to pay for ATV or quad to get you there, if you have both legs, you can easily walk there. It is no further than 300 m from the parking.
