This place is fantastic, but think twice before going there around noon in sunny day, it is really hot there!

Definitely worth going here, the views are spectacular and if you get a quad bike it's even better, they are quite expensive but totally worth it as you get to see so much more (we only had 30mins here) and it's great fun riding it on the dunes. Didn't really get anyone pushing things to buy on us. We also stopped at the red sand dunes which was nice but not quite as good probably still worth a visit though.

Took a hired jeep for for around 800000D from hotel. driver was good and dropped us at each venue and slept in a hammock waiting for us. We hired scooters the following day and rode 50km to the sleeping Buddha in the hills which was our highlight of trip. Lucky to be alive with traffic but fun on own scooter. Point is you can hire a scooter for like $4 a day and go to Fairy stream, fishing village (which is just a stop for a photo of the water and boats) and white sand dunes yourself. Make sure you take two wallets and hide one with all the money in it and just show corrupt cops one with little bit of money in it if they pull you over. Or just to get around for a bit of fun. If not take a bumpy jeep or car for comfort. Anyway fairy stream worth a look and white sand dunes definitely. Red not worth stop with kids trying to get you to hire a board (plastic) for sliding down dune. Two companies at White dunes, we went to second one along road and had to pay 15000D each to get in. We hired big ATV with a young teenager driver. He let me drive for a little bit but not long of the 20mims at 700000D and at a slow speed. But when he took over he flew up and down the dunes and it was sure an adrenaline hit with me holding on the back. Little bikes did look slower but obvs cheaper. Driver wanted a tip once done. If you don't want a bike it's like a 5-10min walk up the dunes and a nice view. Stop in and say hi to Liam at Mooney's Irish pub for me and say hi. He's a character.

Rollercoaster ride in sand dunes Do rent those jeep or buggies and do not regret walking to the top. I visited this place sunrise and sunset. I prefer sunset in terms of the view. But sunrise is also beautiful and it can be better because the sand is not blown too much; better for your hair and skin.

We went on a sunrise tour booked through our hotel and it was superb. It was 60k and the bus picked up at 4am and took us to the white dunes, red dunes, fishing village and fairy stream. About a half hour bus ride from the hotel, one hour at white, 30 min red dune, 15 min fishing village, 45 min river and got back around 9am. The white dunes were pristine. Went to the top peak to see the sunrise and it was gorgeous! A few ppl rented the quads and weren't very happy with the quality of the machines for how much they paid. They couldn't get to the top of most dunes which you'd hope spending 400k or more for 25 mins would do it. Additionally, we heard about a few ppl getting fined very heavily by police for not having a Vietnamese drivers license if they took their own bike to the dunes without a tour. There is only one road in and out they stop you on.

Great change after rainforest of SE Asia :) We visited the dunes at about 2pm in september 2016, was quite hot and the sun was shining. Anyway it wasnt so bad. You have to pay 15.000 VND / person and than 5.000 for motorbike parking. If you dont wanna to use the jeep, you can walk. So we did. I had only flip flops and the sand wasnt so hot like i expected. Dont know how is it possible. We walked about 1 hour. The dunes are really fascinating, especially you are in Vietnam. The lake is also nice, I recommend to go to he part where the sand goes to the water. Nice place. Dont be silly to pay Jeep. We were the alone.
dont miss this place and make sure to rent a quad bike for 25 mins...i went around 2pm but sunset is also a good time to go

Sahara of Vietnam - really cool We rented a scooter rather than going through a tour guide as it's simple enough to get to and much cheaper. If you go by scooter you have to pay a fee to park, can't remember how much but only a few pounds. We got there before the tours as they go to the red sand dunes first I believe. While we were there the tours turned up and the serenity of the dunes disappeared as people flocked in. Try to get there before others, definitely worth it. Quad bikes were a good laugh so give it a go.

we are going with a small group from our hotels. very good adventure. lot activity can do but be careful with the scam which is organized by the local tourist guide. you can see the sunrises at 5a.m in the morning. Very less tourist go there, so you can take a lot of pictures of the dunes.
I went for the sunrise trip. Was so chilled. Some guys driving around with quads. Quite noisy but ok. Was awesome to see team sun rising.

If you are expecting a quiet walk up the sand dunes then think again!A stream of quad bikes and jeeps career around the dunes which is great fun if you are on one but not so nice if you are walking. Definitely A recommend if you are up for a bit of risk.
Atmospheric and beautiful setting. Recommend going early in the morning to avoid the heat of the day.

This sandunes is much cleaner compared to the red sandunes. The view is just so amazing. There are lot of locals offering you to hire quad bike and jeep, but i would highly recommend to just walk around to the top. It's much nicer and you can enjoy the view from different part of the sandunes. The location is far away from Mui Ne town, and the best way is to rent a bike and have a short road trip. The views along the road are so romantic and nice and you can see the beach, waves and so windy. Be careful not to get caught by the police.
Well worth a visit either sunset trip sunrise or just a solo trip The contrast of moving white sands blue sky's and the lake make for awesome photos

Great place where to spend half day! You can reach it with 40 minutes by motorcycle from the city center! You can play beach volley with local people! Pay attention when it rains because it's dangerous

Our family have been here for an afternoon siteseeing. We rent a motorbike and went their by our self. We enjoyed the time here. My daughter loved this place very much. You should park your motor bike down the street and walk to the top of the hill. We would love to come back.

Booked a tour with our hotel to watch the sunrise on the White sand dunes and it was incredible one of the best experiences of my life. Well worth the hour or so drive and getting up at like 4am. We didn't rent a quad due to bad reviews and glad we didn't we saw loads breaking down and we found they ruin your experience a little would be better if the dunes weren't covered in tracks and they were just quiet because the quads are very very loud. We ran to the top of the hill to make the sunrise it wasn't too crowded and was just amazing. People try sell you slides but in Asia they try sell you anything so we were used to it. It's was only pennies to rent one and it looked pretty fun sliding down on them. Amazing views though but soon as that suns up it does get hot so the walk back was hard.
Romantic place with beautiful sand dunes and lake. Tried exciting motor activity there, just great! Slide on sand with a plastic board made made me nervous but so fun.

We all love visiting this spot coz there are a beautiful sand dune and a beautiful swimable lake. On the sand dune, we are able to ride dirt jeep uphill and downhill. Riding is so amazing, so i will never forget.

Since it's my first time being in a large area full of sand, I had the time of my life replicating instagram-worthy shots. We visited the White Sand Dunes during the morning tour and arrived at 5am. We received warm tea (cool morning) and walked to the center. It was magnificent during sunrise. Came with my siblings and we were just rolling around without a care in the world. Definitely recommend my fellow Filipinos to visit (if you haven't already visited the Sand Dunes in Ilocos Norte).

We visited the white sand dunes for sunrise! We left our hotel which was central Mui Ne at 4:30 and arrived at the dunes by 5:30 for sunrise! Most people organise this through a tour but we simply hired a jeep for the 4 of us and it was us $6 each. Once at the dunes we hired quad bikes, I think we were ripped off as we payed 400,000 vnd per bike with two people on each so (200,000 per person) If you can get the bikes down a bit the whole experience is cheap and worth it. Riding the bike is pretty hard over the sand but once you arrive at the big dune and climb in time for sun rise it is absolutely amazing. We then returned to our hotel by 9 am for brekky and started the day :)
Better to go in early morning, otherwise it will get too hot. I liked it. All these sand dunes and a beautiful lake just next to it. Try it, breathtaking!!!
Sunrise was spectacular here! Gotta arrive a lil earlier than other tourist before the place is being polluted with ATV noises!
Walking up at 4 so we could be there for sunrise was well worth it. We loved the quad bikes, was really fun! A few people there sure but worth the visit.

We heard that many of the tours didnt reach the white sand dunes in time for the sunrise, so we wanted to take a xe om(motortaxi) to the dunes. Unfortunately we didn't find any at 4.30 in the morning, so we took a taxi. Cost around 500 000vnd for a taxi one way. Sunrise was amazing. Also when all the tour buses left we were free to stay. We were transported by some locals back to Mui Ne for 300 000 vnd. We think it was worth the cost, but its probably cheaper to rent a driver for a day/rent a Jeep. If you are on a budget, the cheapest is to take a tour.
Got very covered in sand but it was really good fun! We went for the big quad bike... a little pricey but probably worth it for the experience.
It is worth it to do the early morning tour to watch the sunrise from the white sand dunes. You have a the perfect view of the sun coming up and once it hits the sand it looks really pretty.
Good place to try Drive Moto on sandune We arrived sandune very early morning to see sunrise. it was clearly. After We have tried drive moto here. it was very funny and good experient.
We went to see the morning sunrise. Nice and big place. We book the quad bike ( quite costly US$25 per bike ) - sits 2 only. Recommended to sit on the bike to see the whole view of the dunes.

Although missed the sunrise, it was still GREAT with quad-bike Our local guide was late so we missed the sunrise in White Sand dunes. It was a little bit sad but able to touch the soft and silky sand. It was all worthy to join the tour. I have played the quad-bike and it was very funny!!! It is totally user-friendly and no licenses required. All you need to know are "START", "STOP", "TURN RIGHT" and "TURN LEFT". Quad-bike is highly recommended for all who do not have sufficient time in visiting White sand dunes. In contrast, it is entirely walkable but a little bit tiring only.
Brilliant View ........ even there has many tourists :D Have to go there if you visit Mui Ne

What a nice experienced there , we came in the afteroon time i thought i was iN Sahara dessert without a camel but have many motors . Really enjoyed the sands blowing with you because of strong wind .. Perfect place to go if you dreaming to go in sahara dessert
Why not see a good sunrise off the dunes? Nice for the sunrise, get up there early to not miss the sunrise and also get a good spot at the top. Can get crowded FYI.

We hired a quad bike each. It was 500,000 VND each for 30 mins. This was so fun though and the only way to go see the dunes in the scorching heat! People were walked around but this would be very hard!

White sand dunes was located quite far away from my resort however it was totally worth the experience! ATV are available for rent however I chose to walk and it wasn't that bad. The sounds from the ATV was beyond annoying. It is definitely a must visit when you are in Phan Thiet. The Red Dunes is not enough! Totally worth the travel time there.

Beautiful dunes! Walk away from the crowds! Took a group keep your @ 7USD/pax which covers 1h @ white sand dunes, 20mins at fishing village, 30mins @ red sand dunes, 30 mins @ fairy stream. You can easily book a tour from any desks along the main stretch in town. Alternatively, you can rent an entire jeep @ 25USD, and take up to 6 pax! Absolutely love the white sand dunes! You could take an ATV for 400,000VND up to the peak. Or you could take a walk to a different peak for some peace and nice shots (no photo bombers!). Spend some time enjoying e sunrise view, and sitting on e soft sand! One hour wasn't enough. Will definitely be back with my own plastic boards to slide down e dunes, and to do the ATV rides once the sunrise crowds have cleared off! About the trash, it wasn't as bad as I thought. Hopefully one day, residents here will realise the impact of littering e area, and things will change.

Possibly the best attraction here. Reached at about 530am to catch the sunrise which was amazing. Was offered to get the atv to reach the nearest highest dune at vnd400k or usd20k (what ridiculous translation) for 1h. Did not bother and walked up another dune that was a tad nearer to the sun. Still had an amazing view and feel of the entire place. Wished we had more time here and that we skipped the red sand dunes entirely. Paid usd7 per person for group jeep to the 4 usual attractions (does not include atv).
We took an ATV tour of the dunes which was perfect since the sand tends to be very hot. We rode to the top of the dunes where we took some really sweet photos!

Definitely a must for mui ne visitors This was definitely one of the most fun things my gf and I had the pleasure of doing in mui ne. The sand dunes are extremely beautiful and allow for some great pictures. We also decided to rent an atv which is an absolute must. It was $20usd which we found expensive for Vietnam but that's only because everything else is so cheap given our standards being from Canada. The atv was an absolute blast to rip around on and as long as you aren't too foolish you likely won't get stuck (which we did see happen quite often). You will hear a lot of horror stories about the atv's not working. We didn't encounter this personally although ours was spitting quite a bit of oil onto our legs, it wasn't that big a deal. We highly recommend this trip and definitely spend the extra 20 for the atv
Clean and beautiful sand White sand and clean , I love at first sight in clean dessert. Not hot when you walk with no shoe . Everyone must visit here if you come to Mui Ne

When the first arrived was very exited with the desert was very wide and the sand is very nice unlike the red one althought there are kinda lot of rubbish but i like it and make sure if you remember the entry because i forgot it and get lost for a while because i walking very far but you can rent a 4 whelled motor bike for 19$ for half an hour which is i find quiet pricey

We came here on VND100,000 jeep package from our hostel. Don't take a cab here, it's too far and expensive. 4am was really cutting it close, since the sun starts to come up once we reached there at 5am. The dunes are harder to walk than they seem, but very much worth it since you get to stop and get great shots.

Highlight of our trip Very cool place, worth a visit, we skipped the red dunes and only visited the white dunes, which was good cuz most of the tourists were doing the full tour and left us alone on the dunes after the sunrise :) sunrise definitely best, wasn't hot at all
You should be here... Love this sand dunes very much. Need ride around 20 km from Muine city. Never knows, Vietnam has this beuatiful sand dunes like this. Worth it to be here, buddy...

I have been here before. But this time, it's very hot at 10:00 and hardly walk on the sand, really hot. So we check with the Jeep guys, one Jeep for 6 persons is VND 800,000 for one hour. We rant two Jeeps for 12 persons. The Jeep will take you to two top points for view and take photos. It's worth and fun. We also ask the Jeep driver to drive around to feel the up and down at the sand dune, that's too exciting.

Amazing ! Just couldn't believe dunes here Took an army jeep to the dunes with friends. Amazing, drove buggies around the dunes for about half ab hour, then there is an area to relax and get refreshment.
Good place to visit and super fine sand with atv ride white sand.. very recommended place to visit must go bring shades

Show up EARLY for sunrise We arrived at 5AM and were the first ones there - totally worth it. The colours in the sky were incredible and the fact that nobody was there was a blessing that we didn't fully understand until the hordes and noisy ATVs took over the dunes. Seriously, nothing worse than the horribly underpowered 100cc ATVs ripping it across the sand. Find a dune without anybody on it and get the pics you need. It gets hot from 630AM onwards!

I took a half-day sunrise tour. They will pick you up at 4.30 am. It was worth it because it will be away hotter if you take a sunset tour. The jeep driver will stop at the entrance of the white sand dunes. You don't need to rent ATV or motorcycle to go to the top, since it wasn't that far if you walk. It was great experience since it is my first time to visit sand dunes.

Very beautiful place to visit I ride bike from Mui Ne beach to this White Sand Dunes. It takes around 1 hour to go there. Just use your gps to go there. The view on the way there were very beautiful. It is superb and amazing. The sand dunes is quite large. You need to pay for entrance. There are person offering ATV ride and jeep ride. The price are quite expensive. You can also get here by take any tour from your hotel, it will cost you around $7.
Did the sunrise tour, unfortunately it was too cloudy to see. Really cool desert. The quads are $20 to rent once you get there and the experience wouldn't have been as good without them. Lots of fun

This is an awesome travel destination! Although I find it quite pricy, dont miss out on the ATV! It costs 300K vnd for 20 mins & 400K for 40 mins. Don't worry if you dont know how to drive one because you can also someone to drive for you for free!

100% worth a scooter ride Visiting these dunes was the best thing I did in Mui Ne without a doubt. I don't feel like I gave them nearly the time they deserved and still they were the highlight. Rent a scooter and make your way out there. I had a local sim with maps and GPS - easy as pie if you've got this. Maybe a bit tricky without as there's getting through the main town of mui ne and then a couple junctions you have to navigate but they are all big/end of the road junctions on the way out. You'll go past the red dunes and the hundreds of tourists and hawkers who may tell you the white dunes are "closed" or too far. They're not. Allow an hour to get there and get there well before sunrise or sunset to walk away from the initial few dunes that everyone goes to (everyone = maybe 40 people both times I was there) and you'll have a beautiful rolling landscape of pale windsculpted feather soft sands pretty much to yourself. The dunes even roll in to a lake. It's incredible. We ran out of petrol on the way home and a local pushed us from his scooter to the nearest petrol station (where they charged us 5x what the locals paid - watch the gauge on the pump if you fill up there and flat out refuse to pay more than you agreed to pay before they put the petrol in!)

We book a jeep for our family to visit white sand dune in the early morning. We like climate in Mui ne, warm and sunny nearly every day. We hear a quad to drive around the dune and play with sand sliding. We have enjoyed our time here very much. Strongly recomment every one visit this place. A jeep for 6 pax USD 28,00.
Great dunes in the Sahara of Vietnam! Took a jeep ride out to the dunes, very nice! Huge dunes but its very, very hot, bring lots of water and a hat!

We took a private jeep to see the sunrise on the whitesanddunes at we had a good time. There was no clouds and we could see the sunrise perfectly. We walked up on the sanddunes, took around 20 minutes. There are the option to pay a guy on a quadrabike to dirve you up the sanddunes. But we recommend walking! Private jeep coast us 25 usd for white and red sanddunes, fishing village and fairystream.
lovely amazing place less litter than other places good sugar cane drinks in cafe toilet very very basic
Mui Ne is famous for its enormous red and white sand dunes. The white dunes are the more impressive.

Enjoying sunrise in white sand dunes I went to this place with tour ( it costs $5 for the tour package ) at 4.30 am. I enjoyed the sunrise from this dunes, It was so amazing and spectacular. You can walk to the top of the dunes to have a great view, but if you do not walk there, there will be ATV and then you can pay 100.000 VND ( actually it was 150.000 VND, but I bargained ;) )

Lobed this trip. You can do a sunset or sunrise tour. We chose sunrise as we had already seen so many sunsets but whichever you prefer I'm sure the sunset is just as beautiful. We were collected at 4am and drove to the sanddunes with others, we did this tour as a group ad it's cheaper. Upon arrival you have an hour free time in the desert, you have the opportunity to be ridden on a quadbike to the highest point but you have to pay extra (rather a lot) and everyone seems to do it so it's not as special. We just walked through the desert and found a spot on our own and we personally found this so much better! We had views you can only dream of and in complete silence. Something we'll never forget.

A little bit spectacular! Definitely worth a visit. All my family enjoyed it. Whitish sand and more spectacular formations than the red sand dunes. Much cleaner too, thankfully! Fun to go in on the buggies but if you're fit you could certainly walk. We went at from about 7-8:30am and it was lovely. Took some very nice photos.
We were taken to the white sand dunes by a private guide and we walked to places where there was no one else. It was like being in the Sahara desert. Beautiful sunrise, worth getting up at 4.30 for!
Beautiful. The highest point is a long walk from the drop off, about 30min (I walked). Take the ATV if you're not used to walking on soft sand. If I remember correctly it was VND200,000 per pax.

Sand dunes with strong wind My 2nd visited destination after 8years. Just got to be excited to walk on white sand dunes. Spent lot of effort and energy to walk on the sandy hill. We had fun and enjoy the strong wind of the day. Took many pitures and end our trip within 90 minutes of fun after much exposure to the hot sun.

I traveled a lot but I have never seen something like this, especially in Asia. You feel like you're somewhere in Africa. Absolutely worth seeing. I walked around there but you can also use a Jeep service or rent an ATV. You have to haggle with their prices.
Gorgeous. A small sahara Best place to visit for sunset and sunrise. Take a bike over here but bewarw of police. There is polices waiting for you. Fortunately i have my driving license with me.

Do the sunrise tour of the dunes, fishing village and fairy stream. The temperature is perfect at this time to accomplish all of these activities without over heating. You do not need to take a quad to get to the dunes they are very walkable.

The white sandunes are well out of the city, so you'd have to get there by bike or arranged bus. The drive to and in the sanddunes are great, the views are spectacular. All in all expect to pay at least 30 dollars for the whole trip (rent, gas, entrace fee, parking, quad rent). We had rented the biggest one available with some extra time so we could pass the wheel (cheapest option we'd guessed). An safty driver had come with us (just so you don't flip over, you just drive yourselve), and at one time we found ourselves with him behind the wheel, screaming, almoste falling to our deaths, on a slope of a sanddune... So yeah, well worth the thrill ;)
Sand dunes just like the middle east...its a must while visiting pan thiet Around 4:00 afternoon would be the best time otherwise its too hot
ATV was charged for VND300.000/person. If 2 person per ATV, VND200.000/person. Sunrise at this place is so beautiful, I just can't move on with the view!

It was pretty far from the center of Mũi Né, yet the scenery on the way was worth it! Just keep driving about 30kms ahead, you can't get lost! The sand dune here was much more maginificent than the Red one. I was taken aback by how enormous and stunning it was!

An Oasis inside an oasis Walking there we cant stop to admire the beauty of the place. You dont get much place that offer both desert and also beach. It is now possible to go there by motor bike as they have repaved the road with tar. It takes about 40 minutes from Mui Ne centre to White sand dunes.

The white sand dunes were the first stop on our tour, and by far my favourite! I definatelly recommend paying for the wuad bikes, it was a great way to explore. Be wary though, as you will get stuck trying to go up the big hills!

These dunes are amazing. In the middle of nowhere they assemble and are surprisingly high. You can rent a quad bike for 400000 vnd (30min) which is ok but totally worth it as you wouldn't be able to walk to the viewpoint. It's simple to exhausting. Truly beautiful to visit though!!

Beautiful, Picturesque Beautiful, photogenic area for lots of great pictures. Great fun, but be ready to be GOUGED if you want to ride in an ATV. $10USD/10min for small 1 person ATV or $15USD/10min for 2 person ATV, then get harrassed to return it 5-8min before its due.

Best to visit on early morning to catch sunrise, avoid the crowd and take pictures. Walk and run on the sands on barefoot. We chose to walk instead of renting vehicles to take us on the sand cliffs. We came from Ho Chi Minh and took a sleeper bus on the evening. We arrived past midnight in Mui Ne and started our sunrise tour to white sand dunes at 5am. Went back to Ho Chi Minh at 1:30 pm and arrived the city at past 7pm. Great backpacking experience with friends.
It was the Best Part Of My Vietnam Trip I enjoyed playing and taking pictures in the sand dunes. It was so surreal. Mui Ne instantly became one of my fave destination . Would definitely go back here!

Go with easy riders , do the quading , dont be a moaner So many moaning yankeee here , its a 4 hour round trip and with easy riders its approx 500-600k plus if you want a quad the guide can advise best take the bigger high powered quads and the quad driver sits on the back , and guides best route and tells you to slow down if you are a wild child ;) do the easy rider tour , white and red dunes , fishing village , red canyon and fairy stream , these are natural wonders and dont litter here , be nice to locals but firm learn a few words , dumb tourists deserve to get a bit fleeced

Huge white sand dunes along the coastal road of Muine. You should try driving the ATV up the dunes. It may be a td dangerous but the experience is truly out of this world. The sand was so soft that I was so tempted to roll down the dunes. A really beautiful place to be.

Really fun time riding the dunes We had a blast riding the quads all over the dunes. The price was reasonable and they will come get you the minute your time is up. There is plenty of places to eat, have coffee, rest in a hammock. I recommend making the trip to the dunes for sure.

You will feel very small walking along in the middle of this Viet Nam's Sahara, the best time to go should be early in the morning to see the sunrise or late in the after noon because it can get very hot, hat and sun glass should be prepared in advange, good luck guys;)

Interesting experiment The white sand dunes, so you cannot miss it. Just be sure that while exploring the region you visit the the White sand dunes. It is one of the most beautiful places in Vietnam. Book jeep tour at muinego.com to get you to this white sand dunes as well as other attractions

Sunglasses. Bandanas/scarf. No footwears, if possible. Sunscreen, aplenty. Took the tour package from Mui Ne Backpacker Hostel for just $4 (Fairy Stream, Red & White Sand Dunes, Lotus Lake and Fishing Village). It was such a great experience. Mesmerised by the beauty of the white sand dunes. Best bare-footed. Just remove all footwear and sink your feets into the sand. Go to the highest peak of the sand dunes and feel the wind wiping off your feet. Personally, wanted to try the quad bikes but it was too expensive. $30 for 30 minutes just not worth it. I was in Cambodia earlier and they only charged $20 for 1 hour. So the quad bikes in White Sand Dunes highly not recommended. In general, had a blast, even without quad bikes.

The 5 star grade I am giving at that review is mostly for the ATV ride in between the sand dunes. It's a great ride in a nice view. 1. The price is for a 20 minutes ride. 2. Small ATV is 300,000vnd 3. Big ATV is 600,000 vnd 4. I did not like the ATV staff service. 5. Because of the short time, split the ride time between the lake and the D sand dunes.

Don't miss out on dune bugging at White sand dunes. The sand dunes appear out of the blue away from the coast. They are majestic and snow white right at the edge of a Lotus filled lake. It is fun and the view is spectacular

Lovely place to visit See the sunrise from this place is one of a wonderful moment that I've done when I was in Vietnam. The desert was so vast and beautiful. It's better to come here at morning or in the late of afternoon.
Good experience ride ATV on White sand. Watching the sunrise or sunset in the white sand dunes is surreal.

Must Visit if you are in Mui Ne Watching the sunrise in the white sand dunes is surreal. Also driving in the jeep or riding the dune buggies is too much fun and if you are not up for adventure then just sitting quietly next to the lake can be very relaxing

This place was simply magical. How can be that amount of sand placed in the middle of land. It looked like the white sand was randomly there. I took the half day tour in the hostel I stayed. The destinations of this tour were Fairy Spring, Fishing Village, White Sand Dunes and Red Sand Dunes. Our tour group spent most of the time in the White Sand Dunes because it was simply magical. At first my boyfriend was little bit skeptical about visiting Mui Ne, but after he arrived in the dunes he couldn't stop wow-ing this place. This place is a must go place to visit if you go to Mui ne. Anyway, please keeping the place clean, since we found some plastic bottle scattered near the lotus lake and in the sand. Kindly be responsible visitors
Good excursion from Mui Ne..white sand dunes with a blue lake in between. Specially the ATV is worth experiencing.. the best way to visit the dunes
The white sand dunes are much more impressive than the Red Sand dunes. They have ATVs and jeeps for rent starting at 300 000 dong but we just went walking barefoot and enjoyed the scenery instead.

Very amazing place...better join a half day jeep tour! We went there at sunrise time, and the scenery was really great, and air was quite comfortable, sand is cool for walking! Really deserve a visit! But the fee for motor was incredibly high..a return ride from bottom to top of the sand dune (and pick you back after 1 hour) was 100,000 VND (5 USD) per person!
Well worth the drive and admission fee. Take the jeep tour for great scenery and photos. Food was edible but not great. It was cheap though.

One fine dunes at South East Asia I went there with my husband few years later and we can't stop to feel amazed while we're there and everytime we remember the place we'll be still feel amazed because it's amazingly great view and experience for us.

Wow Dramatic contrast This is great place to see in a little bit off Mui Ne. There iis many ways to get there, many hotels would see you a jeep trip to go to the dunes, it is the fast way but you will miss most of the scenery on the way. The best way is to go on a scooter, you rent one and it is about 32 KM from Mui Ne. Make this a day trip, you make a stop on the beach on the way there, stop at various snack bar for food and then you will be arriving at the Dunes. The white ,blue and green are stunning to see. To access the Dunes, turn right it is the best cafe of base before heading up the hill. To go on a bike its 600 000 dongs and to go on a jeep up there is 300 000 to 400 000. To make it cheap if you have chosen the the right shop to the right, there is a path that you can follow to access the best point of the dunes. Take your shoes of and just climb that hill. It is the best view point and its free. ON your way back i suggest that you try to time yourself with sunset. The sunset is pretty much at the same time all the time. Just stop at the dunes at the entrance in Muine and follow the crowd. Its pretty fun place to be and watch an amazing sunset with friends and sands.

Sunrise over white sand dunes...priceless definitely go for the sunrise. Was a little weary of the quad bike drivers as read bad reviews on them. However they are actually very good at what they do (yes even though they do look like they are 12 years old and they do go extremely fast). We had a fantastic time.

Must go see if in Mui Ne. We went on a clear day to the dunes. We booked a tour with our hostel for only $4 USD that took us to Fairy stream, Fishing village (cool to look at but don't stay to long), White sand dunes, and then Red sand dunes. It was an extra 10,000 VND to get into the white sand dunes. They offer 4 wheelers for 20 min rides for 300,000 VND but I chose just to walk around. I have been to sand dunes in Peru but these were really different as the sand was packed solid that made them easy to climb. Really awesome views from the top.
The white sand dune is amazing and worth a visit. Going there in the afternoon would be better as the sunlight should be more beautiful to take some photos. The ATV rental is a bit costly though.

Amazing sand dunes. We were on tour and were not given much time to explore so paying for an ATV might be wise since the sand dunes are very large and to walk through it goes through quite a bit of effort. This sand dune is better than the Red Sand Dunes and I advise going for the sunrise tour as it will be more cooling to explore.

Vietnam has a Sahara? This is like a mini-Sahara desert with sand dunes, a picturesque lake, and a beautiful sunrise to bask in. First time I ever been to a sand dune so this was a unique experience for me and my fiancee. The tour we were on was free, but we had to pay extras so for 400k VND for 40 minutes on an ATV driven by one of the local boys. If you're a thrillseeker you can rent your own, but those hills are slippery and gets dangerous if you're not experienced with sand. We got along with all of them and they helped us take pictures, offered rides for the two of us, one at a time to each hill, then drove us to a special lake where lotuses grew that no one else really knew about, and gave us an extra 15 minutes because we were all having fun. The only mandatory fee was paying 5k dong to get in to the park, other than that, the ATV fee was optional, you can choose to walk up to the dunes, but if you haven't worked out in awhile, you'll be huffing and puffing by the time you get to the first hill. You can also rent your very own ATV, but probably expensive and not safe to do.
the only white sand dunes in Asia! hot but fun experience!! ATV is a must but its little expensive for me
A great nature! but too big to walk. Hiring a jeep is recommended, we hire at 700,000 VND for a big jeep ( 7 people).

Stunning scenery with white sand dune Mui Ne is the amazing place that i never been, place, beach, food, dune, fairy spring....fishing boats....,,,but what i was interesting the most is white sand dune, so stunning and amazing to see like a very big sand with shinny, it catching my heart to say Wow............but it really hot to walk with your shoes and it will be burn you ,must be apply for sun block as my opinion :) or you can take a electronic car as they rent there 20$ for 15 minutes you can bargain anyway.
The best way to see the sand dunes is to rent an ATV but beware of the local vendors over-quoting you and also tip them only if you want to, you're not obligated to tip them.

Visited Muine through Sinh Tourist for their 2D1N package, but we altered our package and saved over a million VND (trust me, we saved almost more than half the price of the package). Anyways, that's not related to the White Sand Dunes. My point is, we avoided going with the tour, which in my opinion was the better option. We rented a motorbike from Mr Tuk, who has a motorbike rent shop opposite of Muine Resort by Sinh Tourist. He was very nice! Highly recommend him, we paid 130,000 VND for one day, and he came to collect the motorbikes at the resort the next day. We rode for an hour or so, less than an hour if you're an experienced rider, from Muine Resort to the White Sand Dunes (WSD for short for now). We set off at 4:30am and rode to WSD to catch the sunrise. The journey there was very smooth and not as scary as expected for two non-riders. I would recommend a jacket as it gets pretty chilly in the morning. We used GPS and trusted Google Map to guide us there. A few times I swear I would have doubted that we were going the right way, but we were steered in the right path. Some of the roads are currently still in construction, avoid it if possible as it is rocky and unstable, difficult to ride. We fell once and scrapped my knee :( Once there at Doi Cát Bay or WSD, park at the ATV rental place. It was free parking. There's 3 different rental places, we parked at the last one, further to walk though. Many reviews cited that the serenity got destroyed and disturbed by tourists and ATVs. Though slightly true, but if you were to go alone as we did, you get to stay there as long as you want and choose where to walk. We sat and watched a little of the rising sun, and by the time we left around 7:30, it wasn't that chilly anymore and there was not a soul in sight, the great boundless dunes were deserted but us and maybe one of two Korean tourists snapping supermodel poses in the sand. Anyways, highly recommended! Much bigger and magnificent as compared to the Red Sand Dunes. P.S. We only pumped 4 litres of petrol and it cost 25,000 per litre. We did not rent an ATV. Worth it! Cheekink kaching'!!
A amazing place for visitting I should be there for sunrise. You will have very nice picuters on the top. pls ask the tour guide for good departure

If you haven't already done so, rent a scooter from your guest house. Enjoy an hour long, windy, dark, drive North. Try not to eat-it as you traverse the crater/bovine laden construction zone as you near the dunes. Park your bike at the first tourist place you see, the one with the ostrich. Pay 10k to park, pay nothing to walk up the dunes. Take some pictures, be the last ones there, eat your sack breakfast. The drive back is a beautiful one. Stop often.

Khu du lịch vô trách nhiệm, cái xích đu cho trẻ con nhưng ko chú thích và ko làm chắc chắn, có bà khách Trung Quốc ngã đập gáy xuống dưới tưởng chết, góp ý buộc dây cho chắc thì ko ai làm, chờ đến lúc có ng chết thì xác định dẹp khu vui chơi cho khỏi kinh doanh. Dịch vụ giá thì trên trời, thuê xe 800k chạy đc 2km

Ko có gì chơi , nắng thấy mẹ . Dịch vụ thuê xe cực mắc . 1 xe 800 ca . Đi bộ lên đỉnh đồi là lồi loe . Nói chung đi cho biết , biết rồi ko ghé nữa . Dưới ó có chỗ chụp ảnh điện gió miễn phí cực đẹp luôn

Giá cho 1 chuyến xe jeep lên đồi cát chưa tới 1 km là 1.000.000 VNĐ đi 2 điểm quá chát.
Cảnh đẹp nhưng cảm thấy bị làm tiền quá nhiều ở đây.
không nên đi, tốn tiền mua vé vào mà bên trong không có gì, muốn tham quan thì phải mướn xe chở đi tốn gần cả triệu đồng

I've been here twice. First time with a tour group which was great fun. Second time we came by ourselves and the quad drivers charged 800,000 VND instead of the usual 500,000 VND. Then they had the nerve to request a tip afterwards, which we did give but I saw the man smirking after we handed it over.

While it is not much money when converted into our currency, the dishonesty is very unappealing and shows bad character. Better to go with the tour group, which was also very fun.

Aside from this the ride on the sand dunes itself is very fun.

Mình di chuyển từ khách sạn - Bàu Trắng khoảng 14km bằng xe máy. Dọc đường đi mình thấy có vài đồi cát nữa và đi dọc bãi biển khá là đẹp.

- Thuê xe máy: 200k/ngày (tự đổ xăng)
- Vé vào cổng Bàu Trắng: 10k/người
- Vé gửi xe máy: 5k/xe
- Giá thuê xe jeep: 600-800k/xe (tuỳ thương lượng và di chuyển thoải mái thích khi nào về thì về)
- Giá thuê moto: 600k (chỉ đi được 2 người trong 20 phút, có lái xe đi kèm)

Mình không thích cảm giác mạnh nên mình chỉ ngồi trong xe được 20 phút vì sợ cảm giác…lật xe.
Lái xe khá an toàn nhưng đồi cát nhiều dốc khiến mình sợ hãi.

Đi Bàu Trắng đa số chỉ dừng ở 2 điểm đẹp nhất cho mọi người check in là đỉnh đồi và bãi cát cạnh hồ nước ngọt.

Nói thật là trời quá nắng nóng, mọi người nên chuẩn bị mũ đội và mang theo nước uống không thì rất mệt!

Khuyến cáo chỉ nên đi nhóm bạn bè. Người già hoặc trẻ em tốt nhất là không nên đi vì độ cao và cảm giác mạnh sẽ gây nguy hiểm hoặc sợ hãi.
Điểm than quan trải nghiệm đi xe địa hình băng qua các đồi cát.
Không có gì nổi bật, dịch vụ thuê xe địa hình chạy lên đồi cát chơi ; 2người 1 xe 20phút 800k . Theo mình giá quá cao. Đã vậy có bán vé vào cổng 15k?
Chơi thì vui nhưng thấy nguy hiểm quá, lịch xe rất nguy hiểm nên mọi người chơi yêu cầu đội nón bảo hiểm

Ở đây có dv thuê xe đi lên bãi cát chụp ảnh. Lên đó chỉ để chụp ảnh thôi chứ trượt cát thì nắng lắm ko trượt được. Giá thuê xe oto lên là 600k 1 xe cả đi cả về. Còn loại xe máy điện thì từ 500k đến 800k 1 xe chở dc 2 người tùy xe. Ở đây có phục vụ cả cơm nên bạn có thể nghỉ ăn trưa ở đây. Giá tầm 50k, 60k 1 xuất
Cảnh đẹp nhưng bị khai thác du lịch nhiều nên không còn vẻ hoang sơ như mấy năm trước mình từng có dịp ghé nữa.
Bãi biển thích hợp để nghỉ ngơi và xả stress.

Bàu trắng đẹp thì k có gì phải bàn rồi. KHÔNG có thu vé vào tham quan, hầu như xung quanh ven đường vào đồi cát có các khu du lịch khai thác bàu trắng bằng việc bán nước cho thuê xe địa hình. Mọi người có thể thuê xe địa hình hoặc vào mua nước gửi xe ủng hộ cũng được. KHÔNG BUỘC PHẢI THUÊ XE giá xe địa hình khá mắc 400-600/xe tuỳ loại nên mọi người cân nhắc. Mình chọn thuê xe jeep vì các điểm du lịch ở Bình Thuận vì các điểm DL khá xa. Khi đến mình không hề biết có thể tự đi vào và không cần thuê xe địa hình nên mình ngồi đợi, ng lái xe cũng k nói mình. đợi 15p sau họ nói mình lên xe và đi điểm khác, hỏi ra mới biết nên mình tự đi luôn. Mọi người có thể thuê xe ĐH thì đi nhanh hơn trong đồi cát, mình thấy mình đi tốt cũng không có gì quá xa nhưng tài xế của mình không ổn lắm nên mình chỉ được gần rồi về gấp thôi. Nói đi cũng nói lại a tài xế cũng giúp mình chụp được vài bức hình chứ thật sự là mình cũng hơi cáu với tài xế xe jeep (Hầu như là 2 ng mình tự chụo cho nhau thôi)

Đồi Cát Trắng còn gọi là Bàu Trắng, là điểm du lịch khám phá rất nổi tiếng tại Mũi Né Phan Thiết và được ví như sa mạc Sahara, thu hút rất nhiều du khách trong nước và quốc tế bởi vẻ đẹp thuần khiết của cát cộng với khung cảnh bao quanh tuyệt đẹp là địa điểm không thể bỏ qua khi đi du lịch Phan Thiết, Bình Thuận
Mình và gia đình có đi du lịch tại nơi này, thật tuyệt, hồ nước rộng quanh năm, mát mẻ.
Hông có gì quá đặt biệt ngoài trừ mát và checkin. Vé xe tương đối cao 800k/1 chuyến

Cảnh đẹp, nhưng dịch vụ xe lên đồi quá đắt, nếu giảm 1/2 sẽ phù hợp hơn.
Xe đậu tại địa điểm tham quan rất mất trực tự, những dạng xe địa hình 2 người nên quy định lộ trình riêng và tách biệt với những địa điểm khách đang chụp hình.
